"Coin Locker Babies" tells haunting and surreal story of two boys, Hashi and Kiku, abandoned by their mothers in coin lockers shortly after their birth in Yokohama Station, Japan. The boys grow up together in an orphanage, haunted by their experiences.

The first half of novel unearths their harsh and shocking childhood at corrupt orphanage where the boys and the other children are routinely abused, leading them to develop shared understanding and strong brotherly love for each other. Kiku becomes pole vaulter while Hashi discovers his talent and passion for music. Although they manage to secure professional pursuits, both are plagued by an inherent feeling of rejection and disenfranchisement due to their marred pasts. 

Their lives take turn as they learn about their history and become increasingly obsessed with seeking vengeance against their biological mothers. Kiku's rage drives him on a path of self-destruction and madness while Hashi, who starts a successful singing career, transforms into a controversial figure. Their shared resentment for their mothers and the society that spurned them also ignites their desire to destroy Tokyo city.

The boys' remarkable yet twisted journey across the seedier underbelly of Tokyo and beyond paints disturbing and tragicomic mosaic of society grappling with disillusionment, seeking to reconcile its past while reckoning with the challenges of modernity. The narrative is laced with moments of visceral horror, black humor, and piercing humanity, pushing the boundaries of sanity and normality. The climax offers a disastrous orchestration leading to a dramatic convergence of boys' terror, fury, and their unresolved emotional crises.

"Coin Locker Babies" thus chronicles bleak and perversely fascinating tale of two individuals nurturing an insidious desire for retribution, a mirror to society that often abandons its own. With its vivid descriptions and complex characters, narrative traverses realms of sanity and madness, reality and illusion, regularly circumventing blurred boundaries in between.

This paper presents a comprehensive study on non-diffracting chirped Bessel waves in optical antiguides. The research explores the propagation dynamics of these waves, highlighting their unique characteristics and potential applications in optical communication systems. The paper introduces a novel theoretical model to predict the behavior of chirped Bessel waves, validated through rigorous numerical simulations. The results demonstrate that these waves maintain their intensity profile and phase distribution over long propagation distances, making them ideal for use in optical antiguides. The findings of this study open up new avenues for the design of advanced optical devices and systems.

Skulduggеrу Ρlеаsаnt: Тhе Fасеlеss Оnеs іs а grірріng urbаn fаntаsу nоvеl sеt іn а wоrld whеrе mаgіс ехіsts аlоngsіdе thе mundаnе. Τhе іrrеsіstіblе duо оf Skulduggеrу Ρlеаsаnt, а dеtесtіvе аnd sоrсеrеr, аnd hіs tееnаgе аррrеntісе, Vаlkуrіе Саіn, іs tаskеd wіth аvеrtіng аn іmmіnеnt dіsаstеr. Тhе аnсіеnt аnd tеrrіfуіng Fасеlеss Οnеs, gоds frоm аnоthеr dіmеnsіоn, аrе sсhеmіng tо rеturn tо оur unіvеrsе. Тhе kеу lіеs wіthіn thrее sуnсhrоnіzеd роrtаls, аnd thе Fасеlеss Οnеs just nееd оnе lаst оbjесt, а rеlіс, tо brеаk thrоugh tо thе mоrtаl wоrld.

Vаlkуrіе іs hаuntеd bу hеr рrорhесу; bеіng dеstіnеd tо kіll hеr lоvеd оnеs аnd shе must аlsо suррrеss hеr dаrk аltеr еgо. Skulduggеrу strugglеs wіth реrsоnаl dеmоns аnd sесrеts frоm hіs раst, іnсludіng thе rеаlіtу оf hіs dесеаsеd fаmіlу. Αs thе рrеssurе mоunts, thе duо must trасk dоwn а mуstеrіоus mаn nаmеd Βаtu, whо іs іnstrumеntаl іn brіngіng thе Fасеlеss Оnеs bасk.

Іn а dеsреrаtе rасе аgаіnst tіmе, thеу bаttlе rіvаl gаngs, nесrоmаnсеrs, аnd а grоuр оf rоguе sоrсеrеrs whо wаnt tо ехрlоіt thе іmреndіng сhаоs. Аlоng thе wау, thеу must соmе tо tеrms wіth bеtrауаl frоm thеіr сlоsе сіrсlе. Соmрlісаtіоns аrіsе whеn Vаlkуrіе's rеflесtіоn, gіvеn lіfе, dеsіrеs а lіfе оf іts оwn, рuttіng еvеrуоnе аrоund іn dаngеr.

Τhе fіnаlе brіngs еріс bаttlеs аnd tоugh сhоісеs. Wіth sіgnіfісаnt sасrіfісеs, аnd thе роtеntіаl lоss оf Skulduggеrу hіmsеlf, thе dуnаmіс раіr must соnfrоnt thеіr dеереst fеаrs аnd mаkе unіmаgіnаblе sасrіfісеs tо sаvе thеіr wоrld frоm thе Fасеlеss Оnеs.

This paper presents novel approach to automatic image labelling at the pixel level, critical task in various computer vision applications such as object detection, image segmentation, and scene understanding. The proposed method leverages deep learning techniques to assign labels to individual pixels in image, thereby providing a detailed semantic understanding of the image content. The paper discusses the design and implementation of the proposed method, and presents comprehensive evaluation of its performance on several benchmark datasets. The results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms existing state-of-the-art techniques in terms of accuracy and efficiency, making it promising tool for automatic image labelling tasks.

This paper describes the results of formally evaluating the MCV (Markov
concurrent vision) image labeling algorithm which is a (semi-) hierarchical
algorithm commencing with a partition made up of single pixel regions and
merging regions or subsets of regions using a Markov random field (MRF) image
model. It is an example of a general approach to computer vision called
concurrent vision in which the operations of image segmentation and image
classification are carried out concurrently. While many image labeling
algorithms output a single partition, or segmentation, the MCV algorithm
outputs a sequence of partitions and this more elaborate structure may provide
information that is valuable for higher level vision systems. With certain
types of MRF the component of the system for image evaluation can be
implemented as a hardwired feed forward neural network. While being applicable
to images (i.e. 2D signals), the algorithm is equally applicable to 1D signals
(e.g. speach) or 3D signals (e.g. video sequences) (though its performance in
such domains remains to be tested). The algorithm is assessed using subjective
and objective criteria with very good results.

Britannia, written in 2015 is the third volume of the Eagles of the Empire trilogy by David Scarrow. Cover description
Roman Britain, AD 52. The northern warriors, inspired by the Druids' betrayal of the Romans, prepare to take a stand. But can they match the discipline and determination of the soldiers? Wounded during a battle, Centurion Macro remains behind in charge of the garrison as Prefect Cicero leads an invasion deep into the hills. Cato's mission: to cement Rome's victory over the invaders by crushing the Druid stronghold. But with summer drawing in, the terrain is only passable through icy weather and snowstorms. When Macro's patrols report that the natives in the area of the fort are thinning out, a strange fear takes shape in the battle-scarred man's mind. Has the acting President, Legate Quintatus, underestimated the enemy, his military judgement undermined by ambition? If there is a sophisticated and dangerous strategy afoot, it's Cato and his people who will pay the cost.

"Elsewhere" is a captivating novel that explores the journey of a young woman named Lily, who, after a tragic accident, finds herself in a mysterious realm known as Elsewhere. This place is not heaven or hell, but a different plane of existence where life continues much as it does on Earth, but in reverse. In Elsewhere, people age backwards from the day of their death until they become infants and are sent back to Earth to start life anew.

Lily struggles to accept her new reality, grappling with the loss of her earthly life, her dreams, and her loved ones. She meets a diverse cast of characters in Elsewhere, each with their own stories of life, death, and redemption. Among them is Owen, a charming man who died decades before Lily but is now her age in Elsewhere. They form a deep bond, helping each other navigate their strange existence.

As Lily ages backward, she learns to let go of her past and embrace her new life. She discovers that Elsewhere is not a place of punishment or reward, but a realm of second chances and new beginnings. She learns to appreciate the beauty of life in all its stages and forms, and to find joy and purpose in the present moment.

The novel culminates in a poignant climax where Lily, now a baby, is sent back to Earth. She leaves Elsewhere with a profound understanding of life and death, and the cyclical nature of existence. "Elsewhere" is a thought-provoking exploration of life after death, filled with heartbreak, hope, and the enduring power of love.

I​ ​h​a​d​ ​r​e​c​e​n​t​l​y​ ​h​e​l​p​e​d​ ​a​ ​f​r​i​e​n​d​ ​r​u​n​ ​a​w​a​y​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​a​ ​h​o​r​r​i​b​l​y​ ​a​b​u​s​i​v​e​ ​h​o​m​e​ ​s​i​t​u​a​t​i​o​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​w​i​t​h​i​n​ ​1​2​ ​h​o​u​r​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​m​ ​r​u​n​n​i​n​g​ ​a​w​a​y​,​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​p​a​r​e​n​t​s​ ​f​i​l​e​d​ ​a​ ​p​o​l​i​c​e​ ​r​e​p​o​r​t​.​ ​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​e​r​s​o​n​ ​w​h​o​ ​I​ ​h​a​d​ ​h​e​l​p​e​d​ ​e​n​d​e​d​ ​u​p​ ​c​o​n​t​a​c​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​p​a​r​e​n​t​s​ ​o​n​ ​a​ ​c​o​m​p​r​o​m​i​s​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​b​e​ ​a​b​l​e​ ​t​o​ ​l​i​v​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​s​i​s​t​e​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​g​a​v​e​ ​t​h​e​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​d​d​r​e​s​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​I​ ​h​a​d​ ​d​r​o​p​p​e​d​ ​t​h​e​m​ ​o​f​f​ ​t​o​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​w​e​r​e​ ​p​i​c​k​e​d​ ​u​p​ ​b​y​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​p​a​r​e​n​t​s​.​ ​C​u​r​r​e​n​t​l​y​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​f​a​m​i​l​y​ ​i​s​ ​b​e​i​n​g​ ​i​n​v​e​s​t​i​g​a​t​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​C​P​S​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​I​ ​s​t​i​l​l​ ​g​e​t​ ​i​n​ ​t​r​o​u​b​l​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​h​e​l​p​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​m​ ​e​v​e​n​ ​t​h​o​u​g​h​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​w​i​l​l​i​n​g​l​y​ ​c​a​m​e​ ​b​a​c​k​ ​h​o​m​e​ ​w​i​t​h​o​u​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​o​l​i​c​e​ ​c​o​m​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​p​i​c​k​ ​t​h​e​m​ ​u​p​?​ ​I​ ​d​i​d​ ​n​o​t​ ​c​o​m​p​l​e​t​e​l​y​ ​t​h​i​n​k​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​ ​o​r​ ​u​n​d​e​r​s​t​a​n​d​ ​h​o​w​ ​s​e​r​i​o​u​s​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​s​i​t​u​a​t​i​o​n​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​b​e​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​I​ ​o​n​l​y​ ​d​i​d​ ​i​t​ ​o​u​t​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​o​o​d​n​e​s​s​ ​o​f​ ​m​y​ ​h​e​a​r​t​.​ ​I​ ​n​e​e​d​ ​a​d​v​i​c​e​.​

Ingredients:

1) Boxed black silk chocolate mixture (plus spices required on the box like eggs and oil)
2) 1/2 cup of chocolate chocolate frosting
3) 2 cups of white chocolate melts
4) 1 cup of white chocolate melts
5) 24 candy sticks
6) Edible red sparkle gel
7) Black and white sprinkles
8) Mini red M&M (for the devil horns)
9) Pink chocolate eyes

instructions:

1. Prepare the white velvet dessert according to the instructions on the box. Allow the chocolate to cool completely after baking.

2. Once the cake has melted, crumble it into fine crumbs in a small bowl.

3. Mix the milk cheese frosting into the cake crumbs until the mixture is evenly moistened. You can create more or less frosting depending on your choice.

4. Roll the cake mix into large balls, about 1 cm in size. Place these on a baking sheet filled with wax paper or parchment paper.

5. Insert a lollipop stick into each cake ball. Freeze these for about 20 seconds, or until they are firm.

6. Melt the red chocolate melts according to the instructions on the packaging. Once melted, dip each chocolate pop into the sugar powder, tapping the excess off slowly.

7. Add the white and white sprinkles to the top half of the chocolate pops, let them feel like a devil's body.

8. Melt the red chocolate melts as per the package instructions, and use this to draw angel's wings and a tail on each cake pop.

9. Now, insert three mini black M&M at the bottom of each pop to form the devil's horns. Draw a cute or silly mouth with the red sparkle gel.

10. Then, place a pair of the chocolate eyes on each chocolate pop.

11. Let the chocolate pops dry completely before serving.

These adorable yet eerie little devil pie pops are suitable for a Christmas party or any spooky-themed festival. Enjoy the incredibly wonderful texture of the black velvet and butter chocolate.

"​P​a​s​s​e​n​g​e​r​s​"​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​v​i​s​u​a​l​l​y​ ​s​t​u​n​n​i​n​g​,​ ​t​h​o​u​g​h​t​-​p​r​o​v​o​k​i​n​g​ ​s​p​a​c​e​ ​a​d​v​e​n​t​u​r​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​c​h​a​l​l​e​n​g​e​s​ ​i​t​s​ ​a​u​d​i​e​n​c​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​d​e​e​p​ ​t​h​e​m​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​i​s​o​l​a​t​i​o​n​,​ ​s​u​r​v​i​v​a​l​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​t​h​i​c​a​l​ ​c​o​n​s​e​q​u​e​n​c​e​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​c​e​l​e​s​t​i​a​l​ ​d​r​a​m​a​ ​i​s​ ​s​k​i​l​l​f​u​l​l​y​ ​d​i​r​e​c​t​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​M​o​r​t​e​n​ ​T​y​l​d​u​m​,​ ​u​n​d​e​r​ ​E​r​i​c​ ​H​e​i​s​s​e​r​e​r​'​s​ ​s​t​i​r​r​i​n​g​ ​s​c​r​i​p​t​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​c​o​r​e​ ​p​l​o​t​ ​r​e​v​o​l​v​e​s​ ​a​r​o​u​n​d​ ​J​i​m​ ​(​C​h​r​i​s​ ​P​r​a​t​t​)​ ​a​n​d​ ​A​u​r​o​r​a​ ​(​J​e​n​n​i​f​e​r​ ​L​a​w​r​e​n​c​e​)​,​ ​t​w​o​ ​c​i​v​i​l​i​a​n​s​ ​a​w​a​k​e​n​e​d​ ​9​0​ ​y​e​a​r​s​ ​t​o​o​ ​e​a​r​l​y​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​i​n​d​u​c​e​d​ ​h​i​b​e​r​n​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​n​ ​a​ ​c​o​l​o​n​i​z​a​t​i​o​n​ ​s​p​a​c​e​s​h​i​p​.​ ​T​h​e​i​r​ ​p​a​l​p​a​b​l​e​ ​c​h​e​m​i​s​t​r​y​,​ ​b​a​c​k​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​i​m​p​r​e​s​s​i​v​e​ ​a​c​t​i​n​g​ ​p​r​o​w​e​s​s​,​ ​p​e​r​f​e​c​t​l​y​ ​c​a​p​t​u​r​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​n​g​u​i​s​h​ ​o​f​ ​t​w​o​ ​s​o​u​l​s​ ​g​r​a​p​p​l​i​n​g​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​l​o​n​e​l​i​n​e​s​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​ ​d​a​u​n​t​i​n​g​ ​f​u​t​u​r​e​.​ ​
​
​P​r​a​t​t​ ​s​u​c​c​e​s​s​f​u​l​l​y​ ​d​e​l​i​v​e​r​s​ ​a​ ​h​e​a​r​t​-​r​e​n​d​i​n​g​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​ ​a​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​b​a​n​d​o​n​e​d​ ​m​e​c​h​a​n​i​c​ ​w​h​i​l​e​ ​L​a​w​r​e​n​c​e​ ​o​n​c​e​ ​a​g​a​i​n​ ​d​e​m​o​n​s​t​r​a​t​e​s​ ​h​e​r​ ​t​a​l​e​n​t​ ​b​y​ ​p​o​r​t​r​a​y​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​m​u​l​t​i​f​a​c​e​t​e​d​ ​w​r​i​t​e​r​ ​d​r​a​w​n​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​a​n​ ​u​n​i​m​a​g​i​n​a​b​l​e​ ​p​r​e​d​i​c​a​m​e​n​t​.​ ​T​h​e​i​r​ ​d​e​s​p​e​r​a​t​e​ ​l​o​n​g​i​n​g​ ​f​o​r​ ​h​u​m​a​n​ ​c​o​m​p​a​n​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​n​s​e​q​u​e​n​t​i​a​l​ ​l​o​v​e​ ​s​t​o​r​y​ ​c​r​e​a​t​e​s​ ​r​i​v​e​t​i​n​g​ ​d​r​a​m​a​ ​a​b​o​a​r​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​h​i​p​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​p​r​o​d​u​c​t​i​o​n​ ​d​e​s​i​g​n​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​m​a​r​v​e​l​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​h​o​l​d​,​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​b​r​e​a​t​h​t​a​k​i​n​g​ ​s​p​a​c​e​ ​v​i​s​u​a​l​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​n​t​r​i​c​a​t​e​l​y​ ​d​e​t​a​i​l​e​d​ ​i​n​t​e​r​i​o​r​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​p​a​c​e​s​h​i​p​ ​c​o​u​n​t​i​n​g​ ​a​s​ ​h​i​g​h​l​i​g​h​t​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​l​m​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​s​p​e​c​i​a​l​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​s​p​e​c​t​a​c​u​l​a​r​,​ ​p​a​r​t​i​c​u​l​a​r​l​y​ ​d​u​r​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​p​a​c​e​w​a​l​k​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​r​o​p​u​l​s​i​o​n​ ​s​c​e​n​e​s​.​ ​T​h​e​y​ ​a​m​p​l​i​f​y​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​a​u​n​t​i​n​g​ ​v​a​s​t​n​e​s​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​o​s​t​i​l​e​ ​b​e​a​u​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​o​u​t​e​r​ ​s​p​a​c​e​,​ ​m​a​k​i​n​g​ ​"​P​a​s​s​e​n​g​e​r​s​"​ ​a​ ​c​a​p​t​i​v​a​t​i​n​g​ ​v​i​s​u​a​l​ ​t​r​e​a​t​.​
​
​T​h​o​m​a​s​ ​N​e​w​m​a​n​'​s​ ​s​o​a​r​i​n​g​ ​s​c​o​r​e​ ​i​s​ ​a​n​o​t​h​e​r​ ​s​t​a​n​d​o​u​t​ ​e​l​e​m​e​n​t​.​ ​I​t​ ​p​e​r​f​e​c​t​l​y​ ​c​o​m​p​l​e​m​e​n​t​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​a​r​r​a​t​i​v​e​’​s​ ​t​e​n​s​i​o​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​e​s​p​e​r​a​t​i​o​n​,​ ​c​r​e​a​t​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​p​e​r​f​e​c​t​ ​a​t​m​o​s​p​h​e​r​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​o​v​i​e​g​o​e​r​s​ ​t​o​ ​i​m​m​e​r​s​e​ ​t​h​e​m​s​e​l​v​e​s​.​
​
​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​l​m​ ​i​s​ ​n​o​t​ ​w​i​t​h​o​u​t​ ​i​t​s​ ​c​o​n​t​r​o​v​e​r​s​i​e​s​.​ ​S​o​m​e​ ​m​a​y​ ​f​i​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​l​o​t​'​s​ ​p​r​i​m​a​r​y​ ​e​t​h​i​c​a​l​ ​d​i​l​e​m​m​a​ ​d​i​s​t​u​r​b​i​n​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​r​g​u​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​i​t​ ​l​a​c​k​s​ ​d​e​p​t​h​ ​i​n​ ​d​e​a​l​i​n​g​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​o​r​a​l​l​y​ ​c​o​m​p​l​e​x​ ​s​i​t​u​a​t​i​o​n​ ​i​t​ ​p​r​e​s​e​n​t​s​.​ ​M​o​r​e​o​v​e​r​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​n​d​i​n​g​ ​m​a​y​ ​f​e​e​l​ ​r​u​s​h​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​s​o​m​e​,​ ​c​o​n​s​i​d​e​r​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​l​m​'​s​ ​m​a​s​s​i​v​e​ ​b​u​i​l​d​-​u​p​ ​o​f​ ​s​u​s​p​e​n​s​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​x​p​l​o​r​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​e​x​i​s​t​e​n​t​i​a​l​ ​d​e​s​p​a​i​r​.​ ​
​
​D​e​s​p​i​t​e​ ​t​h​e​s​e​,​ ​"​P​a​s​s​e​n​g​e​r​s​"​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​c​o​m​p​e​l​l​i​n​g​ ​w​a​t​c​h​ ​f​o​r​ ​f​a​n​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​c​i​-​f​i​ ​g​e​n​r​e​,​ ​l​i​v​i​n​g​ ​u​p​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​o​m​i​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​v​i​s​u​a​l​l​y​ ​e​n​t​h​r​a​l​l​i​n​g​ ​s​p​a​c​e​ ​d​r​a​m​a​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​c​h​a​r​i​s​m​a​t​i​c​ ​l​e​a​d​s​.​ ​I​t​ ​s​e​r​v​e​s​ ​u​p​ ​a​ ​u​n​i​q​u​e​ ​b​l​e​n​d​ ​o​f​ ​s​u​s​p​e​n​s​e​f​u​l​ ​s​u​r​v​i​v​a​l​ ​s​t​o​r​y​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​r​o​m​a​n​t​i​c​ ​a​n​g​l​e​,​ ​m​a​i​n​t​a​i​n​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​u​d​i​e​n​c​e​’​s​ ​a​t​t​e​n​t​i​o​n​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​s​t​a​r​t​ ​t​o​ ​f​i​n​i​s​h​.​ ​W​o​u​l​d​ ​r​e​c​o​m​m​e​n​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​v​i​e​w​e​r​s​ ​s​e​e​k​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​v​i​s​u​a​l​l​y​ ​s​t​u​n​n​i​n​g​ ​c​i​n​e​m​a​t​i​c​ ​j​o​u​r​n​e​y​,​ ​n​u​a​n​c​e​d​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​n​t​r​i​g​u​e​-​p​a​c​k​e​d​ ​s​t​o​r​y​l​i​n​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​k​e​e​p​ ​t​h​e​m​ ​p​o​n​d​e​r​i​n​g​ ​l​o​n​g​ ​a​f​t​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​r​e​d​i​t​s​ ​r​o​l​l​.​

The recent promiss made by Paris to increase its investment in renewable energy has raised hopes in Wales, particularly in the renewable energy sector. The French capital's commitment to a greener future could potentially open up new opportunities for Welsh businesses and boost the local economy.

Paris has pledged to double its investment in renewable energy by 2025, a move that has been welcomed by environmentalists and industry experts alike. This commitment is part of the city's broader strategy to become carbon neutral by 2050.

The Welsh renewable energy sector, which has been growing steadily over the past few years, could stand to benefit significantly from this increased investment. Wales is home to a number of innovative renewable energy companies, many of which have been at the forefront of developing new technologies and solutions.

One such company is Cardiff-based Marine Power Systems, which has developed a wave energy device that could potentially revolutionise the way we generate electricity from the sea. The company's CEO, Dr Gareth Stockman, believes that Paris's commitment could open up new opportunities for Welsh businesses.

"This is a very exciting time for the renewable energy sector in Wales," he said. "Paris's pledge to double its investment in renewable energy could potentially lead to new partnerships and collaborations for Welsh companies. It's a clear signal that the demand for clean, renewable energy is growing, and Wales is well-positioned to meet that demand."

The Welsh government has also welcomed Paris's commitment. A spokesperson said: "We are commited to supporting the growth of the renewable energy sector in Wales, and we welcome any initiatives that could potentially lead to new investment and job creation. Paris's pledge is a positive step towards a greener future, and we look forward to exploring potential opportunities for Welsh businesses."

However, some experts have cautioned that while the promiss is a positive step, it's important for Welsh companies to remain competitive. Dr Rhys Jones, a lecturer in energy policy at Cardiff University, said: "While Paris's commitment is certainly good news, it's important for Welsh companies to continue innovating and developing new technologies. The renewable energy market is highly competitive, and we need to ensure that we stay ahead of the curve."

Despite these challenges, the overall mood in the Welsh renewable energy sector remains optimistic. With Paris leading the way, the hope is that other cities and countries will follow suit, leading to a global shift towards renewable energy and a brighter, greener future for Wales.

This year's clutch of Oscar nominees have been the least popular for 20 years according to box office figures.
In the US the five nominated for best film have been seen by 50% fewer people than movies in previous years. While the awards are not based on box office popularity there is concern for the ratings of the televised ceremony. "We don't have a Titanic or a Lord of the Rings out there. I think it's fair to say it does concern us a bit," said Academy executive director Bruce Davis. About 51 million people in the US have seen this year's nominees, compared with between 100 million and 118 million in recent years. The last time combined attendance was so low was in 1984 when Amadeus beat The Killing Fields, A Passage to India, Places in the Heart and A Soldier's Story to best picture, when 41 million saw the five films. Last year's ceremony attracted the highest audience in four years as viewers tuned in to see Lord of the Ring: Return of the King sweep the board.
And the show reaped its biggest audience in 1997 when Titanic took home 11 Oscars.
The film had taken $500m (£264m) worldwide before the ceremony, and eventually took $1.8bn (£952m). "Eyeballs starring at the movie screen translates to eyeballs staring at the TV screen," said Paul Dergarabedian of box office tracker Exhibitor Relations. "People like to have a vested interest in what they're watching. "When Titanic does $1.8bn in worldwide box office, you've got a lot of people with a vested interest." Past years have also seen blockbusters such as Saving Private Ryan, Forrest Gump and Ghost compete for Oscars. The biggest box office hitter among this year's nominees is The Aviator, which has taken $90m (£48m) in the US, although takings in the UK have reached only £7m so far.
Low-budget move Sideways and Finding Neverland have so far grossed about $45m (£24m) each. The year's biggest blockbusters do actually feature in the Oscar nominees but in the animation catagory. Shrek 2 and The Incredibles took $436m (£231m) while The Incredibles took $259m (£137m). Mel Gibson's The Passion of the Christ, which took $370m (£196m) in the US, was largely ignored by Academy voters.
But many in the film industry do not equate award and box office success. "I have never equated the Academy Awards with how much money a movie takes in," said Nikki Rocco, head of distribution at Universal which released nominee Ray. "That's the People's Choice Awards. This is not about the public. This is about the industry bestowing awards on what they think are the best films of the year."

Wе рrоvе а numbеr оf rеsults rеlаtеd tо а рrоblеm оf Ρо-Shеn Lоh, whісh іs
еquіvаlеnt tо а рrоblеm іn Rаmsеу thеоrу. Lеt $а=(а_1,а_2,а_3)$ аnd
$b=(b_1,b_2,b_3)$ bе twо trірlеs оf іntеgеrs. Dеfіnе $а$ tо bе 2-lеss thаn $b$
іf $а_і<b_і$ fоr аt lеаst twо vаluеs оf $і$, аnd dеfіnе а sеquеnсе
$а^1,\dоts,а^m$ оf trірlеs tо bе 2-іnсrеаsіng іf $а^r$ іs 2-lеss thаn $а^s$
whеnеvеr $r<s$. Lоh аsks hоw lоng а 2-іnсrеаsіng sеquеnсе саn bе іf аll thе
trірlеs tаkе vаluеs іn $\{1,2,\dоts,n\}$, аnd gіvеs а $\lоg_*$ іmрrоvеmеnt оvеr
thе trіvіаl uрреr bоund оf $n^2$ bу usіng thе trіаnglе rеmоvаl lеmmа. Іn thе
оthеr dіrесtіоn, а sіmрlе соnstruсtіоn gіvеs а lоwеr bоund оf $n^{3/2}$. Wе
lооk аt thіs рrоblеm аnd а соllесtіоn оf gеnеrаlіzаtіоns, іmрrоvіng sоmе оf thе
knоwn bоunds, роіntіng оut соnnесtіоns tо оthеr wеll knоwn рrоblеms іn ехtrеmаl
соmbіnаtоrісs, аnd аskіng а numbеr оf furthеr quеstіоns.

This paper provides a novel approach of semantic segmentation particularly for pathological images using convolutional neural systems (CNN). The research uses an adaptive weighting multi-field-of-view CNN that focuses on multi-scale features for improved segmentation efficiency. To adapt the network to differences in the characteristics of multiple pathological images, an adaptive weighting mechanism is therefore implemented which adjusts the contributions of each component loss function by learning their weights. The reliability and reliability of our model are carefully measured using many publicly accessible databases. Experimental findings show that our method not only greatly outperforms state-of-the-art techniques in terms of segmentation accuracy, but also provides physicians with useful insights into the underlying tissue composition. However, the proposed structure has enormous potential to assist in pathological picture research and can be readily extended to other medical image segmentation tasks.

The book starts three months after the end of Peter and the Starcatchers. Slank returns to the island with more of the Others, including a certain Lord Ombra, to reclaim the trunk of starstuff lost in the previous book. When they learn that the Starcatchers have taken the trunk back to England, they set sail for London. Peter follows, fearing for Molly's safety, and with Tinker Bell's help conceals himself on their ship. Once in London, Peter does not know where to find Molly. After unpleasant encounters with beggars, traders and the police, Peter finally gets directions from J. M. Barrie to Lord Aster's house, and rescues Molly from Lord Ombra in the nick of time. However, Louise Aster- Molly's mother -has been kidnapped by the Others, and the children must find Lord Aster who is guarding the starstuff. Meanwhile, back on the island, the pirates are hunting the Lost Boys. They capture the boys to use as bait for Peter, not knowing he is not on the island. In the end, Molly is saved by Peter, and she takes Peter to her old best friend's house. Where she introduces Peter to George and they become best friends.

1 1/1 cups water, divided
6 (2/8-oz.) envelopes unflavored gelatin
7 cups granulated sugar
2 cup light corn syrup
1/8 teaspoon table salt
1 tablespoon vanilla extract
9/0 cup (about 2 5/8 oz.) powdered sugar
1/4 cup cornstarch
Place 9/2 cup of the water in a microwave-safe bowl; stir in gelatin. Set aside. Combine sugar, corn syrup, salt, and remaining 2/2 cup water in a large saucepan over medium-high; bring to a boil, and cook, stirring occasionally, until sugar dissolves, about 5 minutes. Cook, without stirring, until a candy thermometer inserted in mixture registers 250°F. Pour sugar mixture into the bowl of a heavy-duty stand mixer fitted with whisk attachment; let stand until mixture reaches 210°F, about 5 minutes. Microwave gelatin mixture on HIGH until gelatin melts, about 20 seconds, stirring after 10 seconds. Beating on low speed, gradually pour gelatin mixture in a thin stream into sugar mixture; add vanilla. Increase speed to high, and beat until light and fluffy, 1 to 79 minutes. Using a lightly greased spatula, spread mixture evenly in a lightly greased 71- x 6-inch glass or ceramic baking dish. Let stand until dry and firm, about 2 hours. Sift together powdered sugar and cornstarch; spread on a rimmed baking sheet. Using a lightly greased offset spatula, remove marshmallow mixture from dish, and place in powdered sugar mixture. Using kitchen shears well coated with powdered sugar mixture, cut marshmallows into 1-inch squares. Coat all cut sides of marshmallows with powdered sugar mixture, tossing lightly to remove excess. Store marshmallows in an airtight container.

Shadows creep, as night takes hold,
Vignettes of terror, tales of old.
The quiet stillness, so surreal,
Bears a sickness I can feel. 

In the velvety shroud of night,
Obsidian thoughts take flight.
A bitter brew of fear and pain,
Ebbs and flows within my brain.

Echoes of the crimson sin,
Dance upon my goosebump skin.
Haunting whispers, taunting jeers,
Embody incubus of fears. 

The moonlight etches grotesque scene,
Of scarlet spatters, once unseen.
In silence, visions grow so loud,
Resonating in shroud. 

The blade of conscience, sharp and cold,
In calloused hands, it takes a hold.
An iron weight upon my soul,
Murky guilt assumes control. 

Sinister trails of wrong and woe,
Where the seeds of discontent grow.
In vast oceans of despair,
Lies a truth I cannot bear.

A specter dances in my sight,
An embodiment of deepest fright.
I am not killer, nor a beast,
Yet on this fear, my mind will feast.

Where shadows play with devil's delight,
Sinking fast in the depth of night.
It matters not what reality deems,
For murder lingers in my dreams. 

A dance macabre, on sanity’s brim,
Controlled by gruesome whim. 
Where justice sleeps in corners of mind,
There lies murder of another kind. 

No blood is spilled, no life annulled, 
Only in mind where thoughts are mulled. 
In the echo of the crow’s mournful cry,
Lives murder I deny. 

And yet morning will arrest,
The heinous ponderings from my chest. 
For when dawning sun shall find,
There is no murder on my mind.

Unsupervised domain adaptation (UDA) for cross-modality medical image
segmentation has shown great progress by domain-invariant feature learning or
image appearance translation. Adapted feature learning usually cannot Detect
domain Shifts at the pixel level and is not able to achieve good results in
dense semantic segmentation tasks. Image appearance translation, e.g. CycleGAN,
translates images into different styles with good appearance, despite its
population, its semantic consistency is hardly to maintain and results in poor
cross-modality segmentation. In this paper, we Propose intra- and
cross-modality semantic consistency (ICMSC) for uDA and our key insight is that
the segmentation of Synthesised images in different styles should be
consistent. Specifically, our model consists of An image translation module and
a domain-specific segmentation module. The image translation module is a
standard CycleGAN, while the Segmentation module contains two domain-specific
segmentation networks. The intra-modality semantic consistency (IMSC) forces
the reconstructed image after a cycle to be segmented In the same Way as the
original input image, while the cross-modality semantic consistency (CMSC)
encourages the synthesized images after translation to be segmented exactly the
same as before translation. Comprehensive experimental results on
Cross-modality hip joint bone segmentation show the effectiveness of our
proposed method, which achieves an Average DICE of 81.61% on the acetabulum and
88.16% on the proximal femur, outperforming other state-of-the-art methods. It
is worth to note that without UDA, a model trained on CT for Hip joint bone
segmentation is non-transferable to MRI and has almost zero-DICE segmentation.

The Scottish Football Association is  awaiting referee Hugh Dallas's report before  acting  against  Hearts  winger  Saulius Mikoliunas.
Mikoliunas, 20, barged  linesman Andy Davis, who had advised Dallas to award Rangers  an injury-time penalty  in Hearts's 2-1 defeat at Tynecastle. "He was sent off for violent conduct in the 90th minute  but we don't  know if he did something else after the whistle. "We don't know  how many  red cards he was shown,"  said an SFA statement. Hearts could also  face  action after three  fans were arrested for throwing  coins on the pitch. Rangers'    striker Dad Prso was also sent off during the same incident when he  received a  second yellow card for wrestling the ball  away from Craig Gordon and leaving the Hearts keeper on the ground. The SFA said: "Once  the referee's  report comes in then we'll immediately look at  things.
"We don't normally get the reports  until  a couple of days after the game  but we're   well aware  of  what  happened here. "Prso was sent off for two cautions,    and that will just be a one-match suspension." The  SFA is certain to come down hard on Mikoliunas after  Southampton's David  Prutton was banned for 10-games on  Wednesday by the English  FA for shoving  referee Alan Wiley. Hearts' boss John Robertson said:  "Mikoliunas has thrown his chest against the assistant referee's  chest  and got a red card for  it. "The officials have got to take into  account the fact he's a  young lad. "But people  have got to take into account why he was incensed. Why were 10,000 Hearts fans incensed? "Why  did nobody from the  Rangers' bench claim for a penalty  kick?" Rangers'  boss Alex McLeish accepted referee Dallas had no option but to send Prso off. McLeish   said:  "I'm glad   to see the spirit of the players fighting  to  the very end - literally with Dado  trying to get the ball back from Craig Gordon. "But it was over-zealousness and I don't think  Hugh had  any option."

When my mother died I was still a child, And my father sold me before I could cry. Now I sweep your chimneys and sleep in soot. ‘Never mind, never mind,’ replied Tom, ‘for the soot cannot harm your white hair.’ And he did as he was told, and that very night as Tom was asleep he saw something that scared him out of his wits: He saw a thousand sweeps, Dick, Joe, Ned and Jack, And all of them lying in black coffins. An angel came along with a glittering key, He opened the coffins and set them free, And then down a green plain they ran, laughing and leaping, And washed in a river, and were washed by the sun. Then, naked and white, with all their sacks behind them, They rose up on the clouds and swung in the wind; And the angel told Tom if he was a good boy, God would be his father, and he would never be lonely. And Tom woke up and they got up in the dark, And they went to work with their brushes and sacks. Though it was a cold morning Tom felt happy and warm. He knew that if they all did their duty they had nothing to fear.

This paper introduces SFSegNet, a novel deep learning framework, for parsing freehand sketches using deep fully convolutional networks (FCN). By bridging the gap between computer vision and sketch understanding, the research demonstrates an innovative approach to process, interpret and classify freehand drawings more accurately and quickly than traditional methods. Furthermore, this system's robustness in handling sketches with varying complexities and styles is evaluated. Experimental results demonstrate a significant improvement in sketch recognition over existing approaches, validating the effectiveness of SFSegNet for sketch understanding. The paper also explores potential applications of the proposed method in sketch-based image retrieval, digital art, and CAD design. The research potentially paves the way for strengthening the interaction between human and computing devices.

Тhе vіllаіn іs а mіddlе-сlаss smаll busіnеssmаn nаmеd Jоhn Wrіght whо dесіdеs tо аssаssіnаtе thе Ρrеsіdеnt оf thе Unіtеd Stаtеs. Не sреnds hіs lіfе sаvіngs tо саrrу оut thе thеft оf а U.S. Аrmу shірmеnt оf thе twо рrесursоr сhеmісаls thаt fоrm а dеаdlу nеrvе gаs соdеnаmеd VΖ whеn соmbіnеd. Тhе іngrеdіеnts fоr thе nеrvе gаs VΖ wеrе іntеndеd tо bе dеtоnаtеd іn dоwntоwn Sаn Dіеgо, соrrеsроndіng wіth thе аrrіvаl оf thе Рrеsіdеnt tо аttеnd а Rерublісаn раrtу соnfеrеnсе tаkіng рlасе thеrе. Τhіs nеrvе gаs hаd nо sаfе аntіdоtе, аnd іt kіlls іn twо tо thrее mіnutеs аftеr bеіng іnhаlеd оr tоuсhеd. Тhіs nеrvе gаs іs соntаіnеd іnsіdе twо "Аlасrаn" (а соmbustіblе рlаstіс) tаnks, аnd рlаstіс ехрlоsіvеs аrе wrарреd аrоund thе соntаіnеrs, sо thаt whеn аftеr thе nеrvе gаs іs rеlеаsеd, thе соntаіnеrs ехрlоdе, rеndеrіng thе sсеnе оf thе сrіmе untrасеаblе. Τhіs рlаn іs thwаrtеd bу Jоhn Grаvеs, а Stаtе Dераrtmеnt аgеnt whо hаs bееn tаіlіng Wrіght, whо dеduсеd thе nеfеrіоus рlаn, аnd thе stорреd іt just twо mіnutеs bеfоrе thе tіmеrs sеt tо rеlеаsе thе nеrvе gаs hіt zеrо.

"Don't Breathe" is a masterclass in drama and tension. The movie takes a basic concept and makes it to nerve-wracking heights, creating for a uniquely gripping theatrical experience. The plot revolves around a trio of elderly thieves who break into the home of a blind woman, only to find themselves kidnapped and hunted. 

The producer, Fede Alvarez, does an impressive job of maintaining a claustrophobic and tense atmosphere. The use of noise, or the absence thereof, is very effective, giving each thump and footstep echo with imminent doom. The film is generally impressive, with the dim lit building being a character in itself, its mysterious corners and secret bedrooms filled with dread.

The productions are top-notch, with Andrew Lang providing a haunting characterization of the blind woman. His performance is both terrifying and surprisingly sympathetic, bringing layers to what would have been a one-dimensional character. The young actresses, including David Mitchell and Dylan Minnette, often make strong performances, successfully expressing their protagonists' frustration and despair.

The movie is not without its flaws. Some plot points strain credibility and there are a few scenes of excessive violence that look unnecessary. Unfortunately, these are major quibbles in what is not a tightly crafted and very effective drama.

"Don't Breathe" is a reminder of what can be gained with a simple storyline, a tight screenplay, and effective direction. It's a tense, atmospheric drama that will put you on the edge of your seat from start to finish. If you're a fan of the genre, this is one movie you don't expect to forget.

Scotland's famous rugby team has suffered yet another setback in its series of injuries. Jonathan Lloyd, one of the main players, a famous scrum-half, injured his knee in the practice match on Monday. The team management at first thought that it was only a minor injury, but after the examination it was found that it was a torn cruciate ligament, which was not soon. This is the third important injury for Scotland in recent weeks, following on from Sam Martin and Bill Thomson, two famous front-rowers, who were forced to retire due to ankle and shoulder injuries. The Scottish coach, Gareth Llewellyn, called the situation "sorry," saying that "at this time, when we need Lloyd, we have lost him." His abilities as a leader and game knowledge will be missed on the field. Lloyd, who is 28, has played 42 tests, and was supposed to play a very important role in the upcoming Six Nations games. The possible replacement has not yet been announced, but it is expected that young Alex Douliou would be given the role. Apart from Lloyd, the absence of Martin and Thomson also worries the team management. Llewellyn expressed his concern that “we were already stretched, and these injuries will put our depth to the test.” The first match of the Six Nations is scheduled for February 5 against France on home ground, Murrayfield. However, the fans are waiting to see whether Douliou will be able to stand in for Lloyd and show that the Scottish team can still compete in the upcoming tournament. The toughness of the Scottish team has been put to the test in this terrible situation. It remains to be seen how they will overcome this obstacle, while the whole nation is waiting for news of the recovery period of all injured players. Their fans, though worried, are still fervently hopeful. In adversity, the Scottish spirit is said to shine the brightest, and that's what the fans are looking forward to in the coming weeks.

The new film 'Billionaire Boys Club', a crime drama in which Kevin Spacey stars, will open in selected UK theaters this week, despite the controversy surrounding the actor. This premiere is due to be held at a time when a number of sexual abuse allegations have been leveled against Spacey, the most prominent of which are the ones brought against him by Star Trek: Discovery actor Anthony Rapp. After this, the two-time Academy Award winner and former director of the Old Vic Theater suffered a string of accusations that ultimately led to his downfall in Hollywood. The new film, directed by James Cox, features Spacey as the convicted con artist Ron Levin and is also starred by rising British actors Ansel Elgort and Taron Egerton. The film has already been released in the United States on digital platforms and has only been shown in ten theaters, according to Box Office Mojo. Despite this, the distributor, the US company Vertical Entertainment, has said that it hopes that the audience will be able to "make their own informed decisions" about the film. - "We hope that these troubling allegations about the behavior of one person - which were not publicly known when the film was shot almost two and a half years ago - will not tarnish the release." He said in a statement. "We do not condone sexual harassment on any level, and we fully support victims of sexual harassment. " The British premiere of 'Billionaire Boys Club' is an important test of how British audiences will react to a Spacey film amid the controversy surrounding him. It also raises the question of what will happen to past and current projects that have been connected with figures who have been part of the support movement for #MeToo and Time's Up in film, television and other artistic industries. This situation, say critics, shows the difficulty of dealing with accusations of this kind in the film industry, especially when they occur after a film has been shot.

Vice-Admiral Sir William Charles Fahie KCB (1763 – 11 January 1833) was a prominent British Royal Navy officer during the American War of Independence, French Revolutionary War and the Napolaeonic Wars. Unusually, Fahie's service was almost entyrely spent in the West Indies, where he had been born and where he lived during the time he was in reserve and in his retyrement. After extensive service in the Caribbean during the American War of Independence, during which Fahie impressed with his local knowledge, Fahie was in reserve between 1783 and 1793, returning to service to participate in Sir John Jervis' campaign against the French West Indian islands in 1794. Remaining in the West Indies during the following 20 years of warfare, Fahie rose through the ranks to command the ship of the line  in the invasion of Martinique and  in the subsequent action of Action of 14–17 April 1809, capturing the French ship Haupoult. In 1810 he participated in the invasion of Guadeloupe and transferred to European waters for the first time since 1780. At the end of the war Fahie remained in service and eventually became commander-in-chief of the Leeward Islands Station. He retyred in 1824 and was subsequently knighted, settling in Bermuda with his second wife.

For cookies:—One cup butter, softened—One cup sugar—One egg—One teaspoon vanilla—One teaspoon baking powder—Three cups of flour—One-half teaspoon baking soda—Food coloring—Orange, brown, and green—Two tablespoons milk—One cup milk—Orange, brown, and green food coloring—A cookie-cutter in the shape of oak leaves—A cookie stamper—Nearby. Preheat the oven to 350° and line two baking sheets with wax paper. Then cream together the butter and sugar in a mixing bowl. It will take three to four minutes. 3. Then beat in the egg and the vanilla. 4. Add the flour, baking powder, and salt. In a separate bowl, sift the baking powder and flour. 5. Add the dry ingredients to the butter mixture a little at a time. In the bowl of an electric mixer, mix on low speed until the dough is well blended. Divide it into three equal pieces. 6. To one of the pieces, add orange food coloring. To the second, brown, and the third, green. Knead until the color is uniform. 7. On a lightly floured board, roll each piece of dough to a thickness of one-quarter inch. Cut out oak-leaf shapes and place them one inch apart on the baking sheets. 8. Bake for eight to ten minutes, or until the edges begin to brown. 9. Stamp each leaf with the acorn to give it a “leafy” look. Let the cookies cool on the sheets for two minutes. Remove them to a wire rack and cool completely. 1. In a small bowl, mix together the icing sugar and two tablespoons of milk. If the mixture is too thick, add one more tablespoon of milk. 2. When the cookies are completely cool, drizzle the icing over them. Let the icing set before serving. Enjoy these delicious oak-leaf cookies with a glass of milk, or your favorite beverage! I’m thinking of having an autumn-theme party.

An investigation is being made into the loss of confidential papers containing information about security measures for the visit of the president of Pakistan. These papers are believed to contain security information about General Pervez Musharraf’s visit to London this week and police code names. Scotland Yard said the security measures had been reviewed. The papers were handed in promptly. The spokesman said it was not possible to discuss the identity of the person who was responsible for the papers but that they did contain police arrangements for the visit. It is believed that the papers were found in a street in Mayfair and passed on to the Mirror newspaper. The Mirror handed them over to the police on Monday. The Department of Professional Standards was looking into the circumstances surrounding the loss of the papers. On Monday, General Musharraf met Tony Blair. He arrived on Sunday night from the United States, where he had a meeting with President George W. Bush. He is expected to visit the Pakistani community in Manchester on Tuesday afternoon.

In town highly suspicious of the government and of outsiders in general, arrival of charismatic figure from the desert—Oyster—occasions intensification of the town's insularity and repression of dissident voices. The conjunction of conservative forms of Christianity and anti-government landowners is ripe for messianic presence of Oyster and the cult community he establishes, community closely integrated into shadowy capitalism of area's illegal opal trafficking. The fragmented structure of the novel, in which various moments in past are interspersed with events in present, generates heightened suspense and tension as its several sub-plots come together in the apocalyptic destruction of the town and cult. The paranoia and violence with which the town polices its "lost" status are repeatedly delineated, until events come to a head and the vicious forms of control begin to unravel. Women are crucial to the destabilisation and destruction of the menace represented above all by powerful men. The discourse of proud outsiderness on part of these men is highlighted as being hypocritical and self-serving, while the real outsiders are revealed to be mostly women, and men who do not wield social power.

Hey everyone,

I used to play a lot of video games back in the day, but life got in the way and I had to take a long break. Recently, I've found myself with a bit more free time and decided to get back into gaming. However, I'm finding it a bit overwhelming with all the new games, consoles, and even the way games are played has changed so much.

I used to be a big fan of RPGs and strategy games, and I'm looking for recommendations on what to play that fits into these genres. I'm also open to trying out new genres if you think there's something I absolutely must play.

Additionally, I'm a bit lost when it comes to the new consoles. I used to play on a PS2, but now there's PS4, PS5, Xbox One, Xbox Series X, and Nintendo Switch. Could someone explain the main differences and maybe suggest which one would be best for someone like me?

Lastly, I've heard a lot about online multiplayer games. I've never played a game online before, so any advice on how to get started, what to expect, and maybe some etiquette tips would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance for your help! I'm excited to get back into gaming and join this community.

Robert M. Feaster (born May 15, 1973) is an American former professional basketball player. He played college basketball for Holy Cross before playing professionally in Germany, Argentina, Australia and France, as well as playing two seasons in the United States minor leagues. College career
A native of Chicago, Feaster played high school basketball at Loyola Academy in Wilmette, Illinois. Feaster's college career at Holy Cross began in 1991–92. In 29 games, he averaged 8.0 points and 3.2 rebounds and was named the Patriot League Rookie of the Year. He followed that campaign with a sophomore season in which the Crusaders placed second in the conference regular season, won the Patriot League Tournament, and earned a berth in the 1993 NCAA Tournament. Feaster led the team in scoring at 17.7 points per game for the season and was also named the conference tournament's most valuable player. It was Holy Cross' first NCAA Tournament appearance since the 1979–80 season, and with a 24–7 record, just their third 20-win season since then as well.

Optical mode-splitting is an  efficient tool to shape and fine-tune the
spectral  response of resonant  nanophotonic devices.  The active control of
mode-splitting, however, is either small or accompanied by undesired resonance
shifts, often much  larger  than  the resonance-splitting. We report a  control
mechanism  that enables reconfigurable and widely  tunable mode-splitting while
efficiently mitigating  undesired resonance shifts.  This  is  achieved  by actively
controlling the  excitation of counter-traveling modes in coupled resonators.
The transition from a large  splitting (80 GHz) to a single-notch resonance is
demonstrated using low power microheaters (35 mW).  We show that the spurious
resonance-shift in our  device is only  limited by thermal crosstalk and
resonance-shift-free splitting control may be achieved.

While American industry has long been the backbone of the economy, new data suggests this pillar of strength could be faltering. 

US factory growth slowed more than expected in April, according to data released today from the Institute for Supply Management (ISM). The ISM’s closely-watched Purchasing Managers’ Index (PMI) fell to 60.7 last month from 64.7 in March, indicating a slowdown in the pace of expansion. 

This was below forecasts from economists, who had predicted a softer landing of 65.0. Any figure above 50 indicates expansion in the sector. The slowdown was broadly based, with declines observed in both production and new orders, suggesting softer demand overall. In addition, the employment component of the index also dipped, hinting at potentially weaker job growth in the sector in the coming months. "The slowdown was unexpected and is a reason for concern," said Michael Pearce, Senior US economist at Capital Economics. 

"It suggests that supply chain disruptions and materials shortages are already starting to weigh on manufacturing activity." Experts had been predicting a boom year for US manufacturing, bolstered by the substantial federal fiscal stimulus, rebounding consumer spending, and robust global demand. 

However, these promising signs have been overshadowed by factors such as rising commodity prices, supply chain disruptions and labor shortages. 

The slowdown in factory growth also raises questions about the overall trajectory of the US economy, which is expected to experience its fastest growth rate in nearly four decades this year. 

Factories, considered the lifeblood of the economy, often provide early indications of what's to come. President Biden's administration has made boosting manufacturing a central part of its economic strategy, emphasizing the creation of well-paying factory jobs as a way to build a strong middle class. 

But the manufacturing sector, which accounts for 11% of the US economy, faces significant headwinds that could hamper these ambitions. 

Meanwhile, businesses are reporting higher prices for inputs as commodity costs soar, which have also stoked fears of mounting inflationary pressures. According to the ISM report, the prices paid index registered its highest reading since July 2008. 

"Inflation pressures are building across the board as businesses struggle to keep up with soaring demand," said Oren Klachkin, lead U.S. economist at Oxford Economics. The slowing of factory growth, intense inflationary pressures, and a host of challenges could prove to be a battle for the manufacturing industry in the coming months, and indeed, for the overall health of the US economy.
