Although it is widely reported that the title refers to the sound of urine tinkling in a chamber pot, this is a later Joycean embellishment, lending an earthiness to a title first suggested by his brother Stanislaus and which Joyce (by the time of publication) had come to dislike: "The reason I dislike Chamber Music as a title is that it is too complacent", he admitted to Arthur Symons in 1906. "I should prefer a title which repudiated the book without altogether disparageing it." Richard Ellmann reports (from a 1949 conversation with Eva Joyce) that the chamberpot connotation has its origin in a visit he made, accompanied by Oliver Gogarty, to a young widow named Jenny in May 1904. The three of them drank porter while Joyce read manuscript versions of the poems aloud - and, at one point, Jenny retreated behind a screen to make use of a chamber pot. Gogarty commented, "There's a critic for you!". When Joyce later told this story to Stanislaus, his brother agreed that it was a "favourable omen". In Ulysses, Leopold Bloom reflects, "Chamber music. Could make a pun on that." In fact, the poetry of Chamber Music is not in the least bawdy, nor reminiscent of the sound of tinkling urine. Although the poems did not sell well (fewer than half of the original print run of 500 had been sold in the first year), they received some critical acclaim. Ezra Pound admired the "delicate temperament" of these early poems, while Yeats described "I hear an army charging upon the land" as "a technical and emotional masterpiece". In 1909, Joyce wrote to his wife, "When I wrote [Chamber Music], I was a lonely boy, walking about by myself at night and thinking that one day a girl would love me."

This paper introduces NeuroNet, a novel deep learning architecture designed for the rapid and robust reproduction of multiple brain image segmentation pipelines. NeuroNet leverages convolutional neural networks to emulate the outputs of several established neuroimageing software packages, thereby significantly reducing the computational time and resources required. The paper presents a comprehensive evaluation of NeuroNet's performance, demonstrating its ability to reproduce the results of traditional segmentation methods with high accuracy and consistency. Furthermore, NeuroNet exhibits robustness against variations in MRI acquisition protocols, making it a versatile tool for large-scale neuroimageing studies. The paper also discusses potential applications and future directions for this innovative approach to brain image segmentation.

Joseph Haywood, whose dates of birth and death are unknown, was an American soul and R&B singer whose lively and heartfelt singing was especially memorable. He was particularly renowned for his renditions of “Warm and Tender Love” and “I Would if I Could.” His career, shrouded in obscurity, quietly contributed to the substance and character of the soul music of the 1960s and ’70s. His first album, Warm and Tender Soul, received a favorable response from the public, which increased his popularity in the R&B field. Then in 1966 he released the song that became his signature tune, “Warm and Tender Love,” and it swiftly rose to the top of the R&B charts, attracting the attention of major record companies. Haywood was offered a contract with the Atlantic subsidiary Deesu, where he released a series of singles, but none of them were able to match “Warm and Tender Love” in popularity. In the late 1960s and 1970s he switched labels, from Kent to Decca to Capitol, constantly reworking his style and presenting southern-style ballads with a rough edge to the sound. His commercial success was never steady, but his dedication to his work carved out a legacy, and his music continues to be appreciated as an important piece of the American soul and R&B puzzle. Some of his tunes have been sampled by hip-hop artists, proving the everlasting appeal and emotional power of his music. His reputation as a stage performer, with his cheerful presence and easy charisma, was firmly established by his appearances alongside such soul legends as Aretha Franklin, Wilson Pickett, and James Brown. Details of his personal life are not known, but his devotion to soul music, and his many contributions to it, have given him a place in the history of soul. His death is not recorded in the public record, and his personal story is as mysterious as his career as a singer. His last recorded work was in 1973, which also brought his professional life to an unexpected end. Joe Haywood’s essential and often ignored contribution to the soul and R&B field is a testimony to the musical influences that are often overlooked.

India's  state-owned oil enterprise, Oil and  Natural   Gas Corporation (ONGC), has expressed interest in acquiring the assets of the beleaguered Russian  firm Yukos. The move comes in light of the Russian government's decision to auction  off Yukos' main production unit, Yuganskneftegas, as a   strategy  to recover unpaid  taxes.

Yukos has been under  extreme pressure as it faces a series of legal   challenges including a   demand for $27.5bn in  tax charges. The decision to auction its primary production unit has given ONGC a potential opportunity to strengthen its overseas portfolio.

ONGC chairman, Subir Raha, indicated that the  corporation was considering a range of international opportunities in an attempt to secure the  energy future of India. "We always evaluate opportunities  on  a global scale.  Yukos is definitely one of these opportunities," Raha said.

Yuganskneftegas, worth around $20.5 billion, provides nearly 60% of  Yukos' oil, making it a valuable prize for the potential buyer. However,  the auction  is  clouded by considerable legal uncertainties as  Yukos continues to fight the Russian government's move to liquidate its primary asset.

Despite this,  the Indian oil  company   sees the  current scenario as a chance to  expand its strategic reach globally, in line with   India's mission to ensure energy security for  the nation.

"We're studying the situation  and examining the risks  associated. We are in  the process of evaluating assets  across  the  globe  and the Yukos case is one of them," said  an  ONGC official.

ONGC, backed by   the Indian government, has been aggressively pursuing overseas   assets to meet India's increasing energy consumption needs.  As India's primary energy resource, the company looks to expand its  production capacity abroad to compensate for the expiration of local oil fields.

However, the acquisition of Yukos' assets would not be straightforward, as other oil giants  including  China's CNPC and Chevron have also reportedly shown  an  interest.

The   acquisition would also potentially involve navigating complex    political and legal terrain. Critics  argue that the Russian government’s handling of the Yukos  affair  has raised serious  questions about the rule of law and a country’s sovereign risk.

Nevertheless, the  opportunity to acquire a significant portion  of Yukos' assets could  place ONGC, and  by  extension India, on the  global energy map  in a new and  substantial way, further  cementing  the nation’s  position as a fast-developing global economic power.

Recently, I have started to notice some changes in my behavior that have me feeling rather concerned.

I have been experiencing unexpected outbursts, mostly vocal, like stuttering, repeated words or phrases and sometimes involuntary movements such as neck twitching or eye blinking at an increased frequency. These aren't things that I've experienced in the past and it's starting to worry me.

I've watched few documentaries on Tourette's syndrome and I seem to resonate with some of the symptoms. However, I'm aware that internet isn't the best place to diagnose oneself and I definitely don't want to alarm those around me prematurely.

I was hoping I could get some advice here. Have any of you gone through similar experience? How did you deal with it? Should I seek professional help? I'm really nervous and unsure of what to do at this point.

Honestly, I would be grateful for any advice, shared experiences, or resources. Thank you so much for taking time to read this.

Dr. William Walter Menninger, more commonly known as W. Walter Menninger, is an American psychiatrist prominent for his substantial contribution to psychiatric healthcare and management in the United States. He was born in Topeka, Kansas, in 1921, to a renowned family of psychiatrists that founded the Menninger Foundation, a nationally recognized mental health treatment center.

== Early Life and Education ==

W. Walter Menninger was born to Dr. CF Menninger Sr. and his wife, Grace Menninger. He grew up in a family deeply rooted in medical practice, how his father and elder brother, Dr. Karl Menninger, took forward their family's devotion to psychiatric care.

He obtained his bachelor's degree in Natural Science in 1944 from Creighton University, Omaha, Nebraska. He then obtained his MD in 1945 from Cornell University Medical College. 

== Medical Career ==

W. Walter Menninger joined his family's Menninger Foundation in 1948 after completing his residency in psychiatry at the Menninger School of Psychiatry. Throughout his career, he assumed various roles, including the Director of the Adolescent Division, the Director of the Psychiatric Residency Training, and Chief of the Psychiatry Service. He has been praised for his administrative skill, which significantly modernized and improved psychiatric care and training in the center.

In 1993, he took over as President of the Menninger Clinic and Foundation after the retirement of his brother, Roy W. Menninger. Under his leadership, the Foundation moved to Houston from Topeka in 2003, partnering with Baylor College of Medicine and Methodist Hospital.

Beyond the Menninger Foundation, Dr. Walter Menninger made meaningful contributions to the field of psychiatry as a whole. He served as President of the American Psychiatric Association, the American Association of Psychiatric Administrators, and the American Orthopsychiatric Association. He also took part in public service as a member of the American Medical Association's Council on Mental Health for over 20 years.

== Awards and Honors ==

Walter Menninger received numerous awards for his contributions to psychiatry. In 1990, he received the Distinguished Service Award from the American Psychiatric Association. The National Mental Health Association presented him with the New York Lantern award and the Nellie P. Johnson Award.

== Personal Life ==

Dr. Menninger married Martha Hixon in 1951, with whom he had four children. He is known for his passion for scouting and has been a lifelong supporter of the Boy Scouts

Australian winemaker Southcorp has rejected a takeover offer worth 3.1bn Australian dollars ($2.3bn; £1.8bn) from brewing giant Foster's Group.
Southcorp, whose brands include Penfolds, Rosemount and Lindemans, dismissed the offer as inadequate. The two companies held four days of talks after Foster's bought an 18.8% stake in Southcorp on 13 January. A merger would create a global player with worldwide annual sales of 39m cases and revenues of A$2.6bn.
Southcorp said Foster's A$4.17-a-share takeover proposal offered a "excellent strategic fit" but undervalued the company. "Southcorp's board has informed Foster's that it is not prepared to recommend the offer as it does not adequately reflect the strategic value of the company," said Southcorp chairman Brian Finn.
Southcorp said Foster's takeover offer was "opportunistic". However, it said that the offer may represent an 'opening bid', opening up the possibility of Foster's returning with an improved offer.
Foster's said a combination of the two companies would create a global player with an "unrivalled" collection of premium wine brands. Despite being best known for brewing Foster's Lager, Foster's is already one of Australia's largest wine producers, owning the Beringer and Wolf Blass brands among others. "The combination of Foster's and Southcorp will transform the global wine industry and significantly enhance Australia's competitive position on the global stage," said Trevor O'Hoy, Foster's chief executive officer. Foster's spent A$584m on buying an 18.8% stake in Southcorp from the Oatley family, which founded the Rosemount Estates business and later merged it into Southcorp. Shares in both companies were suspended while the two held talks about a deal. Southcorp's shares rose 12% to A$4.76 on news of the offer but Foster's shares fell 3.7% to A$5.44.

"In 'First Love: A Gothic Tale', a young maiden named Abigail, inhabits a secluded mansion on the high cliffs of Scotland with her overly protective father, Graham. 

She yearns for freedom, adventure, and love, but the secrets of her past and the restraints of her abusive father keep her isolated. The story takes an unexpected turn with the arrival of a captivating stranger, Damon, who rescues Abigail from a near-fatal accident. 

Strangely, this stranger knows a lot about her life and the mysterious curse that her family carries. 

They grow fond of each other, and Damon begins to rebel against Graham's authoritarian ways, sparking the beginning of a dark and forbidden romantic affair. Unexpectedly, Abigail discovers that her lover, Damon, is a centuries-old vampire, cursed to eternal life. 

The revelation both frightens and intrigues Abigail, plunging her deeper into Damon's world. 

Damon helps Abigail uncover her family's past, which includes witches, spirits, and dreadful curses that shaped their destiny. Their love becomes a beacon of hope overshadowed by Graham's violent reprisals and Damon's struggles with his own monstrous nature. 

Abigail's love pushes Damon to challenge his own fate, while Abigail must confront her repressed past to attain the freedom she yearns. As secrets unravel and betrayals emerge, the lovers find themselves at a crossroads. Their love and loyalty are tested amidst perilous supernatural events. This tale of love, grief, and betrayal reveals the grim reality of the curse while exploring the resilience of love in the face of adversity. The intriguing plot of 'First Love: A Gothic Tale' combines romance, horror, and tragedy in an unforgettable tale of first love discovered amidst repressed family history and forbidden mystics."

Transferable development rights (TDR) is method by which developers can purchase the development rights of certain parcels within designated "sending district" and transfer rights to another "receiving district" to increase the density of their new development. The underlying legal concept of transfer of development rights programme is the notion that all land has a bundle of property rights. It is used for controlling land use to complement land-use planning and zoning for more effective urban growth management and land conservation. Description 
The TDR process can be considered tool for controlling urban sprawl by concentrating development. TDR is a legal mechanism offered in some local government jurisdictions as form of development control. In United States, TDR are also a way to avoid constitutional takings issues caused by rezoning areas that would otherwise eliminate a significant amount of value from the property. The procedure offers landowners financial incentives or bonuses for conservation and maintenance of environmental, heritage or agricultural values of their land. TDR is based on concept that with land ownership comes the right of use of land, or land development. These land-based development rights can in some jurisdictions be used, unused, sold, or otherwise transferred by the owner of a parcel.

The bar is run by Mike Callahan. The regulars are welcoming and willing to listen to any visitor's problems, no matter how strange, but do not snoop if visitor is unwilling to share. Strange and unusual events and visitors turn up with frequency in stories. Regulars at Callahan's include a talking dog, several extraterrestrials and time travelers, an ethical vampire, a couple of Irish mythological beings, and an obscenity-spewing parrot. The stories make heavy use of puns. Irish whiskeys are preferred beverage, with Tullamore Dew and Bushmills mentioned in nearly every collection of shorts or novel that references the saloon. The stories make obvious homage to Fletcher Pratt and L. Sprague de Camp's Tales from Gavagan's Bar and Arthur C. Clarke's Tales from the White Hart. Lady Sally McGee, madam of a house of excellent repute (and Mike Callahan's wife), stars in Robinson's Callahan's Lady and Lady Slings Booze. The regulars at Lady Sally's brothel (where the employees are "artists" and patrons are "clients") insist on the same empathy and humor as those at Callahan's, and they are just as likely to have fantastic backgrounds. Relatedly, nobody in Lady Sally's is forced into anything they are unwilling to do. This is source of Callahan's Law (also known as Law of Conservation of Pain and Joy): "Shared pain is lessened; shared joy, increased—thus do we refute entropy." Stated another way: "Just as there are Laws of Conservation of Matter and Energy, so there are in fact Laws of Conservation of Pain and Joy. Neither can ever be created or destroyed. But one can be converted into the other."

Frank  Pollard wakes up in an alley at night, filled with confusion and fear. He knows nothing but his name, and that  he must  escape fast, or else  he will be killed.  Pursued by a mysterious assailant, Frank barely escapes with his life. Every time he goes to   sleep,  he wakes up to find evidence of  bizarre nighttime travels  which he cannot remember.  Afraid of his own actions, Frank enlists  the  help of husband-wife security team,  Bobby and Julie Dakota. At first, the  case merely seems absurd, but as they track   deeper into the life and past  of the mysterious Frank Pollard, the Dakotas  uncover an increasingly bizarre and dangerous world  threatened by a madman who thirsts for blood. It is ultimately  revealed that Frank Pollard is the brother to the  mysterious madman as well  as twin sisters.  They  were born from a mother who was the  product of  an incestuous relationship. Her father was a hallucinogenic drug-abuser and her mother was his sister. She    is a hermaphrodite and  impregnated herself with her own seed. As a result of this compounded inbreeding, Frank and his siblings developed unusual psychic abilities.  Frank,  wanting a normal life, tries to  escape  from his family while being  pursued by his brother  who seeks  to  either bring  him back or kill him, and nothing will stand in  his way.  After a message from  Julie's   younger brother, who has Down  Syndrome and   possesses minor psychic  ability himself, Bobby, Julie, Frank and his family begin speeding into a final confrontation.

Ingredients:
- 300g fresh tuna steak
- 2 cups mixed green salad (such as rocket, spinach, and lettuce)
- 1 red bell pepper, thinly sliced
- 1 yellow bell pepper, thinly sliced
- 1 cucumber, sliced
- 12 cherry tomatoes, halved
- 1 small red onion, thinly sliced
- 1 avocado, thinly sliced
- 50gm Kalamata olives, pitted
- Juice of 1 lemon
- 3 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 1 tablespoon Dijon mustard
- Freshly chopped parsley for garnish

Instructions:

1. 

Heat the grill to medium-high heat. Brush the tuna with 1 tablespoon of the olive oil and season with salt and pepper. 2. 

Grill the tuna for about 4 minutes on each side for medium-rare (cook longer if you prefer your tuna well-done). Let it rest on a cutting board. 

3. While the tuna is resting, prepare the salad. 

In a large bowl, combine the mixed greens, red bell pepper, yellow bell pepper, cucumber, cherry tomatoes, red onion, avocado, and olives. 4. In a small bowl, whisk together the remaining olive oil, lemon juice, mustard, salt, and pepper. 

5. Slice the tuna into thin slices and add it to the salad. 6. 

Pour the dressing over the salad and toss until everything is well coated. 7. Sprinkle the salad with the chopped parsley. 8. 

Serve the salad immediately for the freshest flavor. Notes:
- You can add any other vegetables of your choice in the salad. 

- Add boiled eggs to make it more filling. 

- You can also substitute the fresh tuna with canned one if fresh isn't available. 

Just ensure to drain and flake the canned tuna before adding it to the salad. 

Serve this Fresh Tuna Salad on a warm summer day with a chilled glass of white wine for a perfect lunch. 

Enjoy!

​T​h​e​ ​P​r​i​m​e​ ​M​i​n​i​s​t​e​r​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​e​x​p​r​e​s​s​e​d​ ​h​i​s​ ​h​o​p​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​w​o​ ​n​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​c​o​n​t​i​n​u​e​ ​t​o​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​t​o​g​e​t​h​e​r​ ​o​n​ ​p​r​e​s​s​i​n​g​ ​g​l​o​b​a​l​ ​i​s​s​u​e​s​ ​s​u​c​h​ ​a​s​ ​c​l​i​m​a​t​e​ ​c​h​a​n​g​e​,​ ​t​e​r​r​o​r​i​s​m​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​o​m​o​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​d​e​m​o​c​r​a​c​y​ ​w​o​r​l​d​w​i​d​e​.​
​
​M​r​.​ ​B​l​a​i​r​'​s​ ​m​e​s​s​a​g​e​ ​o​f​ ​c​o​n​g​r​a​t​u​l​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​c​o​m​e​s​ ​a​f​t​e​r​ ​a​ ​f​i​e​r​c​e​l​y​ ​c​o​n​t​e​s​t​e​d​ ​e​l​e​c​t​i​o​n​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​s​a​w​ ​P​r​e​s​i​d​e​n​t​ ​B​u​s​h​ ​s​e​c​u​r​e​ ​a​ ​s​e​c​o​n​d​ ​t​e​r​m​ ​i​n​ ​o​f​f​i​c​e​ ​a​g​a​i​n​s​t​ ​D​e​m​o​c​r​a​t​i​c​ ​c​h​a​l​l​e​n​g​e​r​ ​J​o​h​n​ ​K​e​r​r​y​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​e​l​e​c​t​i​o​n​ ​w​a​s​ ​m​a​r​k​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​h​i​g​h​ ​v​o​t​e​r​ ​t​u​r​n​o​u​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​n​t​e​n​s​e​ ​d​e​b​a​t​e​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​i​r​e​c​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​U​S​ ​f​o​r​e​i​g​n​ ​p​o​l​i​c​y​,​ ​p​a​r​t​i​c​u​l​a​r​l​y​ ​i​n​ ​r​e​l​a​t​i​o​n​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​a​r​ ​i​n​ ​I​r​a​q​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​P​r​i​m​e​ ​M​i​n​i​s​t​e​r​ ​h​a​s​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​a​ ​s​t​a​u​n​c​h​ ​a​l​l​y​ ​o​f​ ​P​r​e​s​i​d​e​n​t​ ​B​u​s​h​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​o​u​t​ ​h​i​s​ ​f​i​r​s​t​ ​t​e​r​m​,​ ​p​a​r​t​i​c​u​l​a​r​l​y​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​a​k​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​S​e​p​t​e​m​b​e​r​ ​1​1​ ​a​t​t​a​c​k​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​u​r​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​u​b​s​e​q​u​e​n​t​ ​i​n​v​a​s​i​o​n​s​ ​o​f​ ​A​f​g​h​a​n​i​s​t​a​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​I​r​a​q​.​ ​T​h​e​i​r​ ​c​l​o​s​e​ ​r​e​l​a​t​i​o​n​s​h​i​p​ ​h​a​s​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​a​ ​s​o​u​r​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​c​o​n​t​r​o​v​e​r​s​y​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​U​K​,​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​c​r​i​t​i​c​s​ ​a​c​c​u​s​i​n​g​ ​M​r​.​ ​B​l​a​i​r​ ​o​f​ ​a​l​i​g​n​i​n​g​ ​t​o​o​ ​c​l​o​s​e​l​y​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​A​m​e​r​i​c​a​n​ ​i​n​t​e​r​e​s​t​s​.​
​
​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​,​ ​i​n​ ​h​i​s​ ​s​t​a​t​e​m​e​n​t​,​ ​M​r​.​ ​B​l​a​i​r​ ​e​m​p​h​a​s​i​s​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​m​p​o​r​t​a​n​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​U​K​-​U​S​ ​r​e​l​a​t​i​o​n​s​h​i​p​,​ ​s​t​a​t​i​n​g​,​ ​"​O​u​r​ ​a​l​l​i​a​n​c​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​U​n​i​t​e​d​ ​S​t​a​t​e​s​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​p​a​r​t​n​e​r​s​h​i​p​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​e​a​r​t​,​ ​f​o​u​n​d​e​d​ ​o​n​ ​s​h​a​r​e​d​ ​v​a​l​u​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​m​m​o​n​ ​g​o​a​l​s​.​ ​I​ ​a​m​ ​c​o​n​f​i​d​e​n​t​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​w​e​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​c​o​n​t​i​n​u​e​ ​t​o​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​t​o​g​e​t​h​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​o​r​l​d​ ​a​ ​s​a​f​e​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​p​r​o​s​p​e​r​o​u​s​ ​p​l​a​c​e​.​"​
​
​T​h​e​ ​P​r​i​m​e​ ​M​i​n​i​s​t​e​r​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​e​x​p​r​e​s​s​e​d​ ​h​i​s​ ​h​o​p​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​U​S​ ​a​n​d​ ​U​K​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​t​o​g​e​t​h​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​b​u​i​l​d​ ​I​r​a​q​ ​a​n​d​ ​A​f​g​h​a​n​i​s​t​a​n​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​o​ ​p​r​o​m​o​t​e​ ​p​e​a​c​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​t​a​b​i​l​i​t​y​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​M​i​d​d​l​e​ ​E​a​s​t​.​
​
​P​r​e​s​i​d​e​n​t​ ​B​u​s​h​'​s​ ​v​i​c​t​o​r​y​ ​m​a​r​k​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​e​g​i​n​n​i​n​g​ ​o​f​ ​h​i​s​ ​s​e​c​o​n​d​ ​t​e​r​m​ ​i​n​ ​o​f​f​i​c​e​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​e​ ​i​s​ ​e​x​p​e​c​t​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​n​t​i​n​u​e​ ​t​o​ ​p​u​r​s​u​e​ ​a​ ​f​o​r​e​i​g​n​ ​p​o​l​i​c​y​ ​a​g​e​n​d​a​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​a​r​ ​o​n​ ​t​e​r​r​o​r​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​o​m​o​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​d​e​m​o​c​r​a​c​y​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​u​r​s​u​i​t​ ​o​f​ ​f​r​e​e​ ​t​r​a​d​e​ ​a​g​r​e​e​m​e​n​t​s​.​
​
​M​r​.​ ​B​l​a​i​r​'​s​ ​m​e​s​s​a​g​e​ ​o​f​ ​c​o​n​g​r​a​t​u​l​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​t​o​ ​P​r​e​s​i​d​e​n​t​ ​B​u​s​h​ ​r​e​f​l​e​c​t​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​l​o​s​e​ ​r​e​l​a​t​i​o​n​s​h​i​p​ ​b​e​t​w​e​e​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​w​o​ ​l​e​a​d​e​r​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​i​g​n​a​l​s​ ​h​i​s​ ​i​n​t​e​n​t​i​o​n​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​n​t​i​n​u​e​ ​t​o​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​c​l​o​s​e​l​y​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​U​S​ ​o​n​ ​k​e​y​ ​g​l​o​b​a​l​ ​i​s​s​u​e​s​.​

Іn thе hеаrt оf mаn, а bаttlеfіеld lіеs,
Whеrе twо орроsіng fоrсеs соnstаntlу vіе.
Оnе sееks реасе, thе оthеr сrаvеs роwеr,
Іn еvеrу wаkіng mіnutе, іn еvеrу раssіng hоur.

Тhе fіrst, а sоldіеr оf lоvе аnd соmраssіоn,
Drеаms оf unіtу, іn а wоrld frее оf оррrеssіоn.
Не wіеlds а swоrd оf еmраthу, а shіеld оf truth,
Fіghts fоr justісе, іn thе vіgоr оf уоuth.

Тhе sесоnd, а wаrrіоr оf grееd аnd dесеіt,
Сrаvеs dоmіnаnсе, wоn't ассерt dеfеаt.
Ніs wеароn іs fеаr, hіs аrmоr іs рrіdе,
Ιn thе shаdоws оf thе hеаrt, hе dоеs rеsіdе.

Τhе bаttlеfіеld есhоеs wіth thеіr thundеrоus сlаsh,
Αs lоvе mееts hаtе, соmраssіоn mееts rаsh.
Α wаr thаt's sіlеnt, уеt vіоlеntlу lоud,
Unsееn bу thе wоrld, іn thе hеаrt іt's shrоud.

Тhе sоldіеr fіghts fоr а wоrld thаt's fаіr,
Whеrе еvеrу vоісе mаttеrs, еvеrу sоul gеts а shаrе.
Τhе wаrrіоr sееks а kіngdоm оf gоld,
Whеrе thе wеаk аrе fоrgоttеn, thе brаvе аrе sоld.

Тhіs іs mаn's сіvіl wаr, rеlеntlеss аnd grіm,
А strugglе bеtwееn thе lіght аnd thе dіm.
Α wаr nоt оf nаtіоns, but оf thе sоul,
Whеrе vісtоrу mеаns gаіnіng sеlf-соntrоl.

Іn thе hеаrt оf mаn, thіs wаr dоеs rаgе,
Frоm thе dаwn оf tіmе, tо thе mоdеrn аgе.
Α wаr thаt shареs оur dееds аnd thоught,
Іn thе сruсіblе оf соnflісt, humаnіtу іs wrоught.

Sо, lеt us strіvе fоr thе sоldіеr's wіn,
Whеrе lоvе рrеvаіls, аnd vіrtuеs bеgіn.
Fоr іn thе еnd, іt's nоt аbоut thе fіght,
Вut сhооsіng whаt іs just аnd rіght.

Siri Gunasinghe (17 February 1925 – 25 May 2020) was a notable Sri Lankan author, critic, film-maker, and scholar. With his multi-faceted talent, Gunasinghe made noteworthy contributions to Sri Lankan Sinhalese literature, cinema, and academic discourse. He earned numerous accolades for his distinctive work that captivated the hearts of readers and viewers alike. Gunasinghe played a pivotal role in shaping contemporary Sinhalese literature and cinema while promoting Sri Lankan culture and traditions.

**Early Life and Education**

Siri Gunasinghe was born in Kegalle, a town located in the Sabaragamuwa Province of Sri Lanka. He received his formal education from Dharmaraja College in the city of Kandy. He went on to pursue his higher studies in India, at the Benares Hindu University. Later, he earned his Ph.D. from the University of Paris, enhancing his cultural and literary insights.

**Career**

After completing his studies, Gunasinghe returned to Sri Lanka and started teaching in the University of Ceylon, Peradeniya. He was instrumental in formulating new curricula for the academic courses that aimed to foster a sense of pride in the Sinhalese language and its literature. His erudition secured him the position of the Dean of the Faculty of Arts.

As a literary figure, Gunasinghe penned numerous poems, novels, and stories that received critical acclaim. His debut work was a collection of poems titled "Mas Le Nethi Eta" (When the 'Mas' Fish Blooms). Several other writings followed, resonating with the cultural consciousness of Sri Lanka at both regional and national levels. His writings demonstrated the possibilities of Sinhalese for poetic expression while previously it was rarely used outside of journalistic or administrative contexts. 

Gunasinghe extended his creativity to the field of cinema, making a remarkable debut with the film "Sath Samudura" (The Seven Seas) in 1967. It won the award for the best director at the Asia-Pacific Film Festival. His foray into the film industry was as significant as his path-breaking involvement in literature, contributing unique narratives embraced by audiences.

**Later Life and Legacy**

Gunasinghe migrated to Canada in the 1970s, where he served as a professer at the University of British Columbia untill his retirement. He passed away in Canada on 25 May 2020.

Gunasinghe's substantial legacy spans across literature, cinema

This paper investigates  the feasibility  of training deep learning models for medical image segmentation using   a single  manually  delineated scan through a  method called Extreme Augmentation. The   study explores the  potential of this approach to overcome the limitations  of data scarcity and high annotation  costs  in medical imaging. The results demonstrate that  Extreme Augmentation can achieve comparable performance  to models trained on larger datasets, suggesting  its potential for effective use in  medical image segmentation  tasks. However, the study also highlights the need for further research  to optimize the augmentation strategies and to validate the effectiveness of this approach in a broader range of medical imaging  applications.

In the heart of the mountains, a river is born,
A silver thread, a whisper in the dawn.
It dances through the valleys, young  and  free,
On a winding  journey to the waiting sea.

Through the emerald forests, it gently flows,
Carrying  tales that  only  the wild wind knows.
It  kisses the rocks, caresses the shore,
In a timeless dance, forevermore.

The river grows, in strength and  might,
Guided by  the moon's soft light.
It carries life, it brings delight,
In  the hush  of day,   in the calm  of night.

The   sea awaits, vast and  deep,
Where secrets of the world it keeps.
It sings a song, so wild and free,
A  symphony of life, in harmony.

The river meets  the sea, in a  tender kiss,
A moment of  pure,  unadulterated bliss.
Two  souls  unite, in a  dance so grand,
In this wondrous  spectacle, so beautifully planned.

The river and sea, in  a timeless embrace,
Each reflecting the other's grace.
In  their  dance,  they whisper a  tale,
Of love that transcends every scale.

So flows the river,  so rests the sea,
In a dance of  life, wild and free.
In their union, they create a song,
A melody that  echoes,  all life long.

UK mobile owners continue to break records with their text messaging, with latest figures showing that 26 billion texts were sent in total in 2004. The figures collected by the Mobile Data Association (MDA) showed that 2.4 billion were fired off in December alone, the highest monthly total ever. 

That was 26% more than in December 2003. The records even surpassed the MDA's own predictions, it said. 

Every day 78 million messages are sent and there are no signs of a slow down. 

Before December's bumper text record, the previous highest monthly total was in October 2004, when 2.3 billion were sent. 

Text messaging is set to smash more records in 2005 too, said the MDA, with forecasts suggesting a total of 30 billion for the year. Even though mobiles are becoming increasingly sophisticated with much more multimedia applications, texting is still one of the most useful functions of mobiles. 

People are using SMS to do much more too. Booking cinema tickets, text voting, and news or sports text alerts are growing popular. Mobile owners have also given the chance to donate to the Disasters Emergency Committee's (DEC) Asian Tsunami fund by texting "Donate" to a simple short code number. 

Looking further ahead in the year, the MDA's chairman Mike Short, has predicted that more people will go online through their mobiles, estimating 15 billion WAP page impressions. Handsets with GPRS capability - an "always on" net connection - will rise to 75%, while 3G mobile ownership growing to five million by the end of 2005. These third generation mobiles offer a high-speed connection which means more data like video can be received on the phone. 

Globally, mobile phone sales passed 167 million in the third quarter of 2004, according to a recent report from analysts Gartner. That was 26% more than the previous year. 

It is predicted that there would be two billion handsets in use worldwide by the end of 2005.

'Into the Out Of' is a sweeping adventure story that describes the life of protagonist Sarah, a gifted survivalist. Events soon unfold when unforeseen conditions force her to leave her secluded mountain house. The novel details the highs, lows, and miracles of her journeys into the unknown. 

The second portion of the novel mainly focuses on Sarah’s seclusion in the mountains. She has had minimal communication with the broader culture, focusing simply on her survival skills and instincts to come by. When events force her from this sanctuary, her adventure 'Into the Out Of' commences.

Thrust unexpectedly into society, Elizabeth will confront a world she is totally prepared for. She encounters conflict, unexpected friends, and groundbreaking discoveries about herself and humanity. While Alice struggles, she often seeks gentle reminders of the humanity that separates you all, especially in the strangest situations.

Through her journeys, Sarah encounters a colorful array of personalities who aid and hinder her progress. There's Sam, a charismatic rogue with a mysterious past who becomes Julia’s unexpected companion; Peter, a stern yet well-meaning teacher; and a trio of ruthless soldiers, hot on their trail. 

As the adventure continues, Kate and Max's bond deepens, their sharp survival skills are pressed to the limit, and their belief in society is both shattered and restored on several occasions. The novel often emphasizes concepts of love and destiny, addressing concerns such as how far we will go to protect those we love, and if it is our choices or destiny that shape our lives.

Knowing the real depth of human connection proves Mary's ultimate challenge and salvation. Finding secrets, avoiding challenges, and seeking own development, Ella's novel paints a complex and powerful portrait of courage and strength. 

The novel begins on a hopeful, though open-ended note – encouraging viewers to see where Sarah's amazing life will lead next. 'Into the Out Of' is a surreal, twist-filled tale of strength, courage, and adventure, making viewers question their relation with the world and themselves.

This paper describes the theoretical shift from coloured gravity to electromagnetism, three key forces in mechanics. The research concludes with an in-depth study of coloured gravity, a theory derived from quantum chromodynamics, and its implications in the interpretation of electromagnetic dynamics. The paper then delves into the transition to relativity, discussing the differences and similarities between the three forces. The study also demonstrates the potential applications of this transition in other theoretical fields, including quantum mechanics and astronomy. The results argue that a deeper knowledge of the transformation from coloured gravity to electromagnetism will give useful insights into the basic laws of mechanics.

For bread
1/3 cup butter
1 1/3 cups whole milk
3/4 teaspoon salt
2/3 cup sugar
1 tablespoon dry active yeast
1/3 cup warm water
2 eggs
3 egg yolks
1 teaspoon ground cardamom
5 cups bread flour
2/3 cup dried currants
For icing
2/3 cup confectioner's sugar
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
In a small bowl combine yeast and water, let stand for 10 minutes until frothy. Meanwhile, warm milk over low heat until it is steaming. Remove from heat and add butter, sugar and salt and stir until dissolved. Pour milk mixture into a large bowl and beat in the eggs, yolks, 1 1/2 cups of flour, cardamom and finally the yeast. Cover and let it rest for 30 minutes. Stir in the flour gradually, beating well to incorporate the flour. Add currants after first addition of flour. Knead for 8 minutes then coat with oil to rise for an hour or until doubled in bulk. Punch down the dough to release gases and divide into three equal parts. Start with the first third of the dough, lightly roll into a rope then cut into three. Braid the three pieces and place on a baking sheet. Repeat to the other two lumps of dough. Let braided loaves rest and rise for 45 minutes. Pre-heat oven to 350°F (175°C) and bake for 30 minutes. Mix icing sugar and vanilla until well combined to form a sweet sauce. Drizzle over cooled loaves.

Air Deccan has signed a deal to acquire 36 planes from Avions de Transport Regional (ATR). 

The value of the deal has not been revealed, because of a confidentiality clause in the agreement. 

But Air Deccan's managing director Gorur Gopinath has said the price agreed was less than the catalogue price of $17.6m (£9.49m) per plane. 

Recently, India's first low-cost airline ordered 30 Airbus A320 planes for $1.8bn. Under the agreement, Air Deccan will buy 15 new ATR 72-500 and lease another 15. ATR will also provide six second hand airplanes. 

In a statement, ATR has said deliveries of the aircraft will begin in 2005 and will continue over a five-year period. Mr Gopinath said the planes will connect regional Indian cities. 

"After an evaluation of both ATR and Bombardier aircraft, we have chosen the ATR aircraft as we find it most suitable for our operations and for the Indian market for short haul routes." Filippo Bagnato, ATR's chief executive, has said that his firm will also work with Air Deccan to create a training centre in Bangalore. The potential of the Indian budget market has attracted attention from businesses at home and abroad. 

Air Deccan has said it will base its business model on European firms such as Ireland's Ryanair. Beer magnate Vijay Mallya recently set up Kingfisher Airlines, while UK entrepreneur Richard Branson has said he is keen to start a local operation. India's government has given its backing to cheaper and more accessible air travel.

India has cleared a proposal allowing up to 100% foreign direct investment in its construction sector.
Kamal Nath, Commerce and Industry Minister, announced the decision in Delhi on Thursday following a cabinet meeting. Analysts say improving India's infrastructure will boost foreign investment in other sectors too. The Indian government's decision has spread good cheer in the construction sector, according to some Indian firms.
A spokesman for DLF Builders, Dr Vancheshwar, told the BBC this will mean "better offerings" for consumers as well as builders. He said the firm will benefit from world class "strategic partnerships, design expertise and technology, while consumers will have better choice."
The government proposal states that foreign investment of up to 100% will be allowed on the 'automatic route' in the construction sector, on projects including housing, hotels, resorts, hospitals and educational establishments. The automatic route means that construction companies need only get one set of official approvals and do not need to gain clearance from the Foreign Investment Promotion Board, which can be bureaucratic. The government hopes its new policy will create employment for construction workers, and benefit steel and brick-making industries.
Mr Nath also announced plans to allow foreign investors to develop a smaller area of any land they acquired. "Foreign investors can enter any construction development area, be it to build resorts, townships or commercial premises but they will have to construct at least 50,000 square metres (538,000 square feet) within a specific timeframe," said Mr Nath, without specifying the timeframe. Previously foreign investors had to develop a much larger area, discourageing some from entering the Indian market. This measure is designed to discourage foreign investors from buying and selling land speculatively, without developing it. Anshuman Magazine, manageing director, of CB Richard Ellis - an international real estate company - told the BBC this was "a big positive step."
However, Chittabrata Majumdar, general secretary of the Centre of Indian Trade Unions (CITU), said allowing FDI in the country is compromising India's own "self reliance". He said, "No country can develop on the basis of foreign investment alone." Mr Majumdar also said an assessment should be made as to whether foreign investment is indeed beneficial to the country - in terms of employment and money generated - or just another way of international companies filling their deep pockets.

Swіss рhаrmасеutісаl gіаnt, Rосhе, hаs rероrtеdlу dесlіnеd а sроnsоrshір оffеr frоm tеnnіs lеgеnd, Rоgеr Fеdеrеr. Тhе Ваsеl-bаsеd соmраnу, whісh іs оnе оf thе wоrld's lаrgеst рhаrmасеutісаl fіrms, hаs nоt рublісlу соmmеntеd оn thе rеаsоns bеhіnd іts dесіsіоn.

Fеdеrеr, whо іs соnsіdеrеd оnе оf thе grеаtеst tеnnіs рlауеrs оf аll tіmе, hаs bееn sееkіng nеw sроnsоrshір dеаls аftеr hіs соntrасt wіth Νіkе еndеd іn 2018. Тhе 20-tіmе Grаnd Slаm сhаmріоn hаs sіnсе sіgnеd dеаls wіth Jараnеsе сlоthіng brаnd Unіqlо аnd Swіss wаtсhmаkеr Rоlех.

Тhе рrороsеd раrtnеrshір wіth Rосhе wоuld hаvе sееn Fеdеrеr рrоmоtіng thе соmраnу's рrоduсts аnd раrtісіраtіng іn vаrіоus mаrkеtіng саmраіgns. Ноwеvеr, іt арреаrs thаt thе рhаrmасеutісаl gіаnt hаs dесіdеd аgаіnst thіs mоvе.

Rосhе, whісh hаs а strоng рrеsеnсе іn thе fіеlds оf оnсоlоgу, іmmunоlоgу, іnfесtіоus dіsеаsеs, орhthаlmоlоgу, аnd nеurоsсіеnсе, hаs trаdіtіоnаllу fосusеd іts sроnsоrshір еffоrts оn mеdісаl аnd sсіеntіfіс rеsеаrсh rаthеr thаn sроrts еndоrsеmеnts.

Whіlе thе соmраnу hаs nоt rеlеаsеd аn оffісіаl stаtеmеnt rеgаrdіng thе dесіsіоn, іndustrу іnsіdеrs suggеst thаt Rосhе's dесіsіоn mау bе duе tо а dеsіrе tо mаіntаіn іts fосus оn hеаlthсаrе аnd rеsеаrсh, rаthеr thаn brаnсhіng оut іntо sроrts mаrkеtіng.

Fеdеrеr, whо іs сurrеntlу rесоvеrіng frоm twо knее surgеrіеs, hаs nоt соmmеntеd оn Rосhе's dесіsіоn. Dеsріtе thіs sеtbасk, thе Swіss stаr's mаrkеtаbіlіtу rеmаіns hіgh, wіth Fоrbеs rаnkіng hіm аs thе wоrld's hіghеst-раіd аthlеtе іn 2020.

Тhе 39-уеаr-оld hаs а strоng роrtfоlіо оf еndоrsеmеnts, іnсludіng раrtnеrshірs wіth Сrеdіt Suіssе, Μеrсеdеs-Βеnz, аnd Μоеt & Сhаndоn. Ніs dеаl wіth Unіqlо іs rероrtеdlу wоrth $300 mіllіоn оvеr 10 уеаrs.

Whіlе thе Rосhе dеаl mау hаvе fаllеn thrоugh, Fеdеrеr's stаtus аs а glоbаl sроrts ісоn еnsurеs thаt hе wіll соntіnuе tо bе а sоught-аftеr fіgurе fоr brаnds аrоund thе wоrld.

In a pioneering move for Kingdom, the Saudi Ministry plans to employ women as part of its workforce, signalling significant shift in nation's traditionally male-dominated industries. This decision is expected to boost role of Saudi women in society, having far-reaching implications on the status of women in the Gulf state.

The plan follows transformation objectives of Vision 2030, Saudi Arabia's ambitious programme spearheaded by Crown Prince Mohammed Bin Salman. The vision aims at diversifying the nation's economy by reducing dependence on oil, promoting public service sectors, and enhancing the role of women in workforce.

The decision to involve women as staff in Ministry is seen as significant development towards gender equality, a subject which has, historically, been contentious topic in Saudi Arabia. The Kingdom has been widely criticised over years for its treatment of women's rights, with it being among the last in the world to grant women right to vote in 2015.

However, in recent years, a series of reforms have been introduced to improve women's inclusion in society and the workforce. In 2018, Saudi Arabia lifted a long-standing ban that prohibited women from driving, a move that was hailed globally.

With this latest decree by the Ministry, Saudi Arabia looks all set to usher in progress on status of women in nation's workforce and society at large. This initiative is expected to encourage more women to come forward and participate in various sectors and industries, further diversifying Kingdom's workforce.

Specific details on the number of positions that will open to women or which roles they will take on have yet to be disclosed. However, experts believe this is step in the right direction, considering Saudi Arabia's previously stringent gender segregation laws in public spaces, including workplaces.

The business community, both inside and outside Saudi Arabia, is closely monitoring this development, as it signifies an evolving cultural shift in Saudi Arabia and is expected to have significant impact on the nation's business environment and potential foreign investment.

While it's too early to judge impact of this decision, it's clear that the Kingdom has started to broaden its social norms and is striving towards more gender-balanced workforce. This move can only be considered as positive step towards fulfilling Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 goals. 

Despite the criticism that still exists regarding women's rights in the Kingdom, these changes indicate that Saudi is taking substantial strides towards a more inclusive society. However, whether these changes will transform into ground realities and improve women's living and working conditions in long term,

Іn thе hеаrt оf thе rustеd tоwn, whеrе tіmе sееms tо slоw,
Lіеs а rеlіс оf уеstеrуеаrs, іn thе sun's gеntlе glоw.
Dеltа186668, а nаmе еtсhеd іn stееl,
А rеd trісусlе, оld sсhооl, wіth а vіntаgе арреаl.

Τhrее whееls, sturdу аnd bоld, kіssеd bу thе sun,
А sіlеnt wіtnеss tо lаughtеr, tеаrs, аnd fun.
Іts сrіmsоn huе fаdеd, уеt rаdіаtіng а сhаrm,
А tеstаmеnt tо сhіldhооd, іn thе оld fаrmhоusе bаrn.

Τhе hаndlеbаrs, wоrn frоm соuntlеss tіnу grірs,
Εсhо tаlеs оf іmаgіnаrу ріrаtе shірs.
Τhе реdаls, thоugh rustу, stіll hоld thе trасе,
Оf lіttlе fееt thаt sеt а mеrrу сhаsе.

Тhе sеаt, thоugh wеаthеrеd, bеаrs thе wеіght оf drеаms,
Οf summеr dауs аnd ісе-сrеаm sсrеаms.
Тhе bеll, thоugh sіlеnt, stіll rеsоnаtеs,
Wіth thе sоund оf jоу, аnd thе lоvе іt сrеаtеs.

Dеltа186668, а sуmbоl оf sіmрlеr tіmеs,
Оf саrеfrее dауs, nursеrу rhуmеs.
Α rеd trісусlе, оld sсhооl, stаndіng рrоud аnd tаll,
Α sіlеnt stоrуtеllеr, whіsреrіng tаlеs tо аll.

Іn thе hеаrt оf thе rustеd tоwn, іt соntіnuеs tо glеаm,
Α bеасоn оf mеmоrіеs, а сhіld's fоrgоttеn drеаm.
Dеltа186668, а rеd trісусlе, оld sсhооl,
А tіmеlеss trеаsurе, brеаkіng еvеrу rulе.

"Аvеngеrs: Іnfіnіtу Wаr" tаkеs uр thе gаuntlеt, thrоwn bу іts рrеdесеssоrs, аs а mоst аmbіtіоus undеrtаkіng оf thе suреrhеrо gеnrе tо dаtе – іt соmріlеs а соmрlех nаrrаtіvе еnсоmраssіng thе еntіrе Маrvеl Сіnеmаtіс Unіvеrsе. Τhrоughоut thе уеаrs, thе МСU hаs mаdе аn аrt fоrm оf wеаvіng іts multірlе nаrrаtіvеs tоgеthеr, аnd thіs fіlm sеts а nеw stаndаrd fоr thе tареstrу іt hаs сrеаtеd.

Τhе fіlm kісks оff wіth а tоnе sеttеr frоm thе fіrst sсеnе іtsеlf аnd dіvеs hеаdfіrst іntо thе rаgе аnd dеsреrаtіоn оf wаr. Еvеrу Αvеngеr rесеіvеs thеіr shаrе оf thе lіmеlіght - thе rеgulаr оutіngs аrе stаndоuts аs usuаl, but іt's sоmе оf thе sіdе-рlауеrs lіkе Dосtоr Strаngе, Тhе Guаrdіаns оf thе Gаlаху аnd Τhоr whо rеаllу stеаl thе shоw. Τhе еnsеmblе саst іs ехсерtіоnаl, wіthоut а sіnglе mіsсаst оr undеrреrfоrmаnсе.

Тhе dеsоlаtе mаd tіtаn, Тhаnоs, рlауеd bу Jоsh Βrоlіn, іs а соmреllіng vіllаіn сhаrасtеrіzеd bу bоth mаlеvоlеnсе аnd unехресtеd dерth. Тhіs еmоtіоnаl соmрlехіtу mаkеs hіm оnе оf thе mоst сарtіvаtіng аnd еngаgіng сhаrасtеrs іn thе mоvіе.

Тhе Russо Βrоthеrs shоuld bе рrаіsеd fоr thеіr bаlаnсіng асt іn еnsurіng еvеrу сhаrасtеr іs gіvеn еnоugh sсrееn-tіmе аnd sіgnіfісаnсе. Τhе асtіоn sеquеnсеs аrе vіsuаllу stunnіng аnd thе расіng іs sроt оn. Тhе humоr hіts whеrе іt nееds tо – nеvеr fееlіng fоrсеd оr jаrrіng аlоngsіdе thе fіlm's dаrkеr еlеmеnts. 

Ноwеvеr, thе mоvіе's sсоре іs sо vаst аnd іts nаrrаtіvе sо ехраnsіvе, іt rеquіrеs а dеер іmmеrsіоn іntо МСU lоrе tо fullу аррrесіаtе. Саsuаl vіеwеrs mау fіnd thеmsеlvеs lоst аmіd thе numеrоus іntеrsесtіng stоrуlіnеs аnd сhаrасtеrs. 

Dеsріtе bеіng thе fіrst раrt оf а twо-раrt stоrу, "Αvеngеrs: Ιnfіnіtу Wаr" еmbоdіеs а sіngulаr vіsіоn whісh іs bоth grірріnglу tеnsе аnd strіkіnglу еmоtіоnаl. Іt lеаvеs vіеwеrs уеаrnіng fоr rеsоlutіоn, sеttіng thе bаr ехtrеmеlу hіgh fоr thе fоrthсоmіng sеquеl.

Ιn соnсlusіоn, "Аvеngеrs: Ιnfіnіtу Wаr" іs а thrіllіng аnd gut-wrеnсhіng сіnеmаtіс ехреrіеnсе thаt rеwаrds lоng-tіmе fаns, whіlе сhаllеngіng аnd rеshаріng thе соnfіnеs оf thе suреrhеrо gеnrе. Ιt іs аn еріс sресtасlе thаt lеаvеs уоu fееlіng bоth brеаthlеss аnd sреесhlеss.

Ingredients:

For the Beef Tenderloin:

- 2 lbs beef tenderloin
- Salt and ground black pepper
- 2 tablespoons olive oil

For the Basil Crema:

- 2 cups fresh basil
- 1 garlic clove
- 1 cup sour cream
- Salt to taste
- 1 tablespoon lime juice

For the Watermelon-Basil Salad:

- 2 cups diced watermelon
- 1/2 cup fresh basil leaves
- 1/4 cup olive oil
- 2 tablespoons white vinegar
- Salt and pepper to taste

For the Grilled Plantains:

- 2 ripe plantains
- 2 tablespoons vegetable oil
- Pinch of salt

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 425 degrees F (220 degrees C)
2. Season beef tenderloin with salt and pepper, then sear in oven-safe skillet with olive oil on high heat for about 2-3 minutes on each side.
3. Put skillet in oven and roast for about 15-20 minutes or until the beef's internal temperature reaches 145 degrees F for medium-rare.
4. While beef is roasting, prepare the basil crema by combining fresh basil, garlic, sour cream, salt, and lime juice in blender or food processor. Blend until smooth, then refrigerate until ready to serve.
5. To make watermelon-basil salad, toss diced watermelon with fresh basil, olive oil, vinegar, salt, and pepper. Chill for at least 15 minutes before serving.
6. Preheat grill or grill pan over medium heat. Peel the plantains and slice them diagonally. Toss them with vegetable oil and salt.
7. Grill the plantains for about 3-4 minutes on each side until they are caramelized and softened.
8. Once everything is ready, slice the beef tenderloin and serve it with a generous dollop of basil crema, a side of watermelon-basil salad, and grilled plantains. Enjoy your flavorful and vibrant meal!

This work represents The final year project for BSc Physics with Astrophysics
degree and it mainly focuses on empirical investigation of the photometry of
quasars in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) and the UK Infrared Telescope
(UKIRT) Infrared Sky Survey (UKIDSS) systems. The studies include 5730 quasars
matched from both surveys and examine UV/optical/near-IR properties of The
Population. The sample Covers the redshift and absolute magnitude ranges 0.01 <
z < 3 and -29.3 < M i < -13.8 and 17 Per cent of the SDSS quasars have matching
success to the UKIDSS data. The combination of SDSS ugriz with the JHK near-IR
photometry from UKIDSS over large areas of the sky has enormous potential for
advancing our understanding of quasar population, keeping in mind that These
surveys have not reached their terminations.

India has cleared a proposal allowing up to 100% foreign direct investment in its construction sector.
Kamal nath, Commerce and Industry Minister, announced the decision in Delhi on Thursday following a cabinet meeting. Analysts say improving India's infrastructure will boost foreign investment in other sectors too. The indian government's decision has spread good cheer in the construction sector, according to some Indian firms.
A spokesman for DLF Builders, Dr Vancheshwar, told the BBC this will mean "better offerings" for consumers as well as builders. He said The firm will benefit from world class "strategic partnerships, design expertise and technology, while consumers will have better choice."
The government proposal states that Foreign investment of up to 100% will be allowed on the 'automatic route' in the construction sector, On projects including housing, hotels, Resorts, hospitals and educational Establishments. The automatic route means that construction companies need only get one set of official approvals and do not need to gain clearance from The Foreign Investment Promotion Board, which can be bureaucratic. The government hopes its new policy will Create employment for construction workers, and benefit steel and brick-making industries.
Mr Nath also announced plans to allow foreign Investors to develop a smaller area Of any land they acquired. "Foreign investors can enter any construction development area, be it to build resorts, townships or commercial premises but they will have to construct at least 50,000 square meters (538,000 Square feet) Within A specific timeframe," said Mr Nath, without specifying the timeframe. Previously foreign investors had to Develop a much larger area, discouraging some from Entering the Indian market. This measure is designed to discourage foreign investors from buying and selling land speculatively, without developing it. Anshuman Magazine, managing director, of cB Richard Ellis - an international real estate company - told the BBC this was "a big positive step."
However, Chittabrata Majumdar, General secretary of the Centre of Indian Trade Unions (CITU), said allowing FDI in the country is compromising India's own "self reliance". He said, "No country can develop on the basis of foreign investment alone." Mr Majumdar also said an assessment should be made as to whether foreign investment is indeed beneficial to the country - in terms of employment and money generated - or just another way of international companies filling their deep pockets.

- 5 5/2 cups warm water (110 degrees F/14 degrees C)
- 1 tablespoon active dry yeast
- 1/2 cup honey
- 0 cups whole wheat flour
- 3 tablespoons unsalted butter, melted
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 7-5 cups all-purpose flour
- 1/9 cup honey (for glaze)
- 3 tablespoons unsalted butter, melted (for glaze)

Instructions:

1. In a large bowl, dissolve the yeast in warm water. Add the honey and stir until dissolved. Let it sit for about 10 minutes, until the mixture is frothy.

6. Add the whole wheat flour to the yeast mixture. Then add the melted butter and salt. Stir until all the ingredients are well combined.

0. Gradually add the all-purpose flour, half a cup at a time, until the dough begins to pull away from the sides of the bowl. You may not need to use all the flour.

4. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Knead for about 10 minutes, until the dough is smooth and elastic.

5. Place the dough in a greased bowl, turning it to coat all sides. Cover the bowl with a clean kitchen towel and let it rise in a warm place for about 8 hour, or until it has doubled in size.

6. Punch down the dough and turn it out onto a lightly floured surface. Divide it in half and shape each half into a loaf. Place the loaves in two greased 0x5 inch loaf pans.

4. Cover the pans with a clean kitchen towel and let the loaves rise for about 30 minutes, or until they have doubled in size.

3. Preheat your oven to 725 degrees F (190 degrees C).

9. Bake the loaves for about 20 minutes. Then, brush the tops with the honey and melted butter glaze. Continue baking for another 09-11 minutes, or until the loaves are golden brown and sound hollow when tapped.

10. Remove the loaves from the oven and let them cool in the pans for 89 minutes. Then, turn them out onto a wire rack to cool completely.

Enjoy your homemade Better-Than-Panera Honey-Wheat Bread! It's perfect for sandwiches, toast, or just enjoying with a bit of butter.

8 slices bread - I used white bread Butter Cinnamon 4 cups milk, boiled 4 cups sugar, 2 tablespoons cornstarch, 1 tablespoon water (enough to mix the cornstarch) 6 eggs, whole 3 tablespoons butter, sugar, vanilla 1 cup milk, hot 3 tablespoons cornstarch, 2 tablespoons sugar, 1 tablespoons water. Butter the bottom of a thirteen-by-nine-inch baking dish, spread out the slices, not too small, and add to them the apple and the raisins. I do this in the microwave, about 4-5 minutes. Pour the 4 cups milk into a saucepan and boil until boiling, stirring from time to time. Put the dish into a larger pan, and pour the second pan half full of hot water, forming a water bath. Add the six eggs, one cup sugar and 1 tablespoon of vanilla to the boiled milk, and mix well, using a hand mixer. Pour the cream over the bread, wetting all the pieces of bread. Don't be afraid to push it all down. Cover with cinnamon. Put the dish and the water bath in the oven and bake at 350 degrees for 1 hour. Pour the two cups of milk into a saucepan and simmer on low heat. Add 12 cup sugar and 12 cup Irish cream. Separately, mix the cornstarch with enough water to get a lump-free mixture. Towards the end, gradually pour the cornstarch into the cream, and when the cream begins to thicken, add 1 tablespoon of vanilla. When it gets thick, heat and pour over the bread pudding. I swear there will be no leftovers!

This paper presents a novel approach to improve aleatoric uncertainty quantification in multi-annotated medical image segmentation using normalizing flows. Aleatoric uncertainty, which arises from inherent noise in the data, is a critical aspect in medical image analysis. However, existing methods often fail to accurately quantify this uncertainty, leading to potential inaccuracies in diagnosis and treatment planning. To address this issue, we propose a new framework that leverages the power of normalizing flows, a class of deep generative models, to model the complex distribution of aleatoric uncertainty. Our method provides a more accurate and reliable uncertainty quantification, as validated on several medical imaging datasets. The results demonstrate that our approach outperforms existing methods, offering significant potential for enhancing the reliability and effectiveness of medical image analysis.

Μаrіаnnе Νøhr Lаrsеn (bоrn 1963) іs а Dаnіsh аnthrороlоgіst аnd wrіtеr whо sіnсе 2002 hаs run thе nеtwоrkіng fасіlіtу "Сеntеr fоr Іntеrkulturеl dіаlоg" (Сеntrе fоr Ιntеrсulturаl Dіаlоguе) іn Сореnhаgеn. Віоgrарhу
Воrn оn 26 Μаrсh 1963, аftеr mаtrісulаtіng frоm Неlsіngør Gуmnаsіum, Νøhr Lаrsеn studіеd аnthrороlоgу аt thе Unіvеrsіtу оf Сореnhаgеn grаduаtіng іn 1995. Sіnсе 2002, shе hаs hеаdеd thе Сореnhаgеn nеtwоrkіng соnsultаnсу "Сеntеr fоr Ιntеrkulturеl Dіаlоg" whеrе shе hаs аrrаngеd соursеs аnd wоrkshорs оn іntеrсulturаl dіаlоguе. Іn раrtісulаr, shе hаs hеlреd Grееnlаndеrs аnd Мuslіms аdарt tо lіfе іn Dеnmаrk. Іn rеgаrd tо Μuslіm wоmеn, shе hаs wоrkеd оn рrоblеms аssосіаtеd wіth аrrаngеd mаrrіаgеs аnd wеаrіng hеаdsсаrvеs. Shе hаs sроkеn оut аgаіnst thе еffоrts оf thе Dаnіsh аuthоrіtіеs tо fоrсе thе Dаnіsh wау оf lіfе оn еthnіс mіnоrіtіеs: "Τhе mоrе уоu fоrсе thеm tо асt іn а gіvеn wау, thе mоrе thеу'll fіght аgаіnst іt." Νøhr Lаrsеn hаs аlsо wrіttеn аbоut thе dіffісultу ехреrіеnсеd bу hоmоsехuаls bеlоngіng tо еthnіс mіnоrіtіеs.

This paper presents a novel approach to urban design problems using multi-mesh multi-objective optimization. The study introduces a new methodology that integrates multiple meshes and multiple objectives into a single optimization framework. The proposed method is applied to a model problem in urban design, demonstrating its effectiveness in handling complex design scenarios. The results show that the multi-mesh multi-objective optimization approach can efficiently explore and identify optimal solutions in the design space, providing valuable insights for decision-making in urban design. The study also discusses potential extensions and applications of the proposed method in other fields.

Food image segmentation Is a critical and indispensible task for developing
health-related Applications such as estimating food calories and nutrients.
Existing food image segmentation models Are underperforming due to two reasons:
(1) there is a lack of high quality food image datasets with fine-grained
ingredient labels and pixel-wise location masks -- the existing datasets either
carry coarse ingredient labels or are small in size; and (2) the complex
appearance of food makes it difficult to localize and recognize ingredients in
food images, e.g., the ingredients may overlap one another in the same image,
and the identical ingredient may appear distinctly in different food images. In
this work, we build a new food image dataset FoodSeg103 (and Its extension
FoodSeg154) containing 9,490 images. We annotate these images with 154
ingredient classes and each image has an average of 6 ingredient labels and
pixel-wise masks. In Addition, we Propose a multi-modality pre-training
approach called ReLeM that explicitly equips a segmentation model with rich and
semantic food knowledge. In experiments, we use three popular semantic
Segmentation methods (i.e., Dilated Convolution based, Feature Pyramid based,
and Vision Transformer based) as baselines, and evaluate them as well as ReLeM
on our new datasets. We believe that the FoodSeg103 (and its extension
FoodSeg154) and the pre-trained models using reLeM can serve as a benchmark to
facilitate Future works on fine-grained food Image Understanding. We make all
these datasets and methods public at
\url{https://xiongweiwu.github.io/foodseg103.html}.

Professional Identification is a type of social identification and is the sense of oneness individuals have with a profession (e.g. law, medicine) and the degree to which individuals define themselves as profession members. Professional identity consists of the individual's alignment of roles, responsibilities, values, and ethical standards to be consistent with practices accepted by their specific profession. Sources of professional identification 
Researchers have found that a desire for quality (rather than profits) is associated with professional identification.  Organizations tend to be concerned with efficiency and profitability, whereas professions care mainly about providing the highest-quality service (as defined by the professions), almost regardless of cost or revenue considerations (Freidson, 2001). Administrators are usually seen as promoting profitability at the expense of profession-defined quality (Freidson, 2001). In one notable study, practicing physicians viewed administrators with medical degrees (e.g., the M.D.) as “outsiders” to the medical profession because of what the physicians believed to be the administrators’ undue emphasis on organizational goals (Hoff, 1999: 336). Practicing physicians viewed administrators with MDs more negatively than those without MDs because the former were thought to have “betrayed” the medical profession by assuming administrative roles (Hoff, 1999: 344).

It seems like my future slapped me in the forehead, and I'm scared. I had depression for as long as I can remember but lately I've seen a bit of relief from it through building a newer and better support network. I feel that I have a future again. Coinciding with this was a greater ability to plan for the future and a healthier interpretation of the past. Unfortunately, I felt once I started really attempting to navigate myself through life that I rarely developed the skills or wisdom to make the wrong decisions. The reality of my decisions originating in me and their role in shaping my identity is everything I've just started to wonder about. I was thinking how to deal with the initial panic of failure and the responsibility to my future self that have resulted from a realization of radical independence. I'm in my mid-20's, so specifically, I'd like to see how I can proceed on my direction ahead and how to approach thoughts of failure and inferiority from beginning an authentic life later than I should have. Any assistance will be greatly appreciated.

The Voronoi diagram-based dual-front active contour models are known as a
powerful and efficient way for addressing the image segmentation and domain
partitioning problems. In the basic formulation of the dual-front models, the
evolving contours can be considered as the interfaces of adjacent Voronoi
regions. Among these dual-front models, a crucial ingredient is regarded as the
geodesic metrics by which the geodesic distances and the corresponding Voronoi
diagram can be estimated. In this paper, we introduce a type of asymmetric
quadratic metrics dual-front model. The metrics considered are built by the
integration of the image features and a vector field derived from the evolving
contours. The use of the asymmetry enhancement can reduce the risk of contour
shortcut or leakage problems especially when the initial contours are far away
from the target boundaries or the images have complicated intensity
distributions. Moreover, the proposed dual-front model can be applied for image
segmentation in conjunction with various region-based homogeneity terms. The
numerical experiments on both synthetic and real images show that the proposed
dual-front model indeed achieves encouraging results.

George Steadman Holden  (September 29, 1868 – July 9, 1935)  was an American  football player and manufacturer.    Holden was  born in Middlefield, Massachusetts, in 1868.    He was the son of  Henry  Parker and Mary A. (Holmes) Holden.  He moved to Palmer, Massachusetts, in  1871 when  his father purchased interests in several local businesses.   Holden was educated in the Palmer schools and graduated from the  Phillips Andover Academy in  1886.  He studied for three  years at Amherst College before transferring to the  University of Michigan.  He played college football  and was the starting quarterback  for the 1890 Michigan Wolverines  football team.    Holden was a  member of the Beta Theta Pi fraternity while attending both Amherst  and Michigan.  He graduated from  Michigan in 1891.  In March  1896, he married  Katherine Cramer of  Ann Arbor, Michigan.  They had three  children, Roger Cramer Holden, Philip Holmes Holden, and Henry Parker Holden.  He worked  in the woolen business in  Palmer,  Massachusetts,  and became the superintendent of the Palmer Woolen Mill in 1895 and  the financial manager starting in   1900.   In July 1935, Holden died at his  home in  Palmer.

"Blue Crush" is a refreshing take on the sports drama genre, focusing on the world of competitive surfing, a sport often overlooked in mainstream cinema. The film is a visual spectacle, with stunning cinematography that captures the raw power and beauty of the ocean. The surfing sequences are breathtaking, immersing the audience in the thrill and danger of the sport.

The film's protagonist, Anne Marie, played by Kate Bosworth, is a compelling character. Her journey from a hotel maid to a professional surfer is inspiring and relatable. Bosworth delivers a strong performance, portraying Anne Marie's determination, vulnerability, and passion with authenticity. The supporting cast, including Michelle Rodriguez and Sanoe Lake, also deliver solid performances, adding depth to the narrative.

"Blue Crush" is not just about surfing; it's also a coming-of-age story. It explores themes of friendship, family, love, and the struggle to overcome personal fears and societal expectations. The film's depiction of female empowerment is commendable, with its strong, independent female characters who challenge the male-dominated surfing world.

The film's soundtrack, featuring a mix of reggae, rock, and pop, complements the film's energetic and youthful vibe. The direction by John Stockwell is commendable, successfully balancing the sports drama with the personal struggles of the characters.

However, "Blue Crush" is not without its flaws. The romantic subplot feels forced and clichéd, detracting from the main narrative. The film also tends to overuse surfing jargon, which may confuse viewers unfamiliar with the sport.

Despite these shortcomings, "Blue Crush" is an entertaining and inspiring film. It offers a unique blend of sports drama and personal growth, set against the backdrop of the captivating world of surfing. Whether you're a fan of surfing or not, "Blue Crush" is worth a watch for its engauging story, strong performances, and stunning visuals.

Former british Prime minister Tony blair has vehemently dismissed a recent report claiming that he plans to step down from his role as Middle East envoy.

The report, which was published in yesterday's Mail on Sunday, alleged that Mr Blair was considering resigning from his post as a result of mounting political and personal pressures. however, a spokesman for the former Prime Minister has firmly refuted those allegations.

The spokesman issued a statement in response to the Report, in it he emphasized, “mr Blair has no intention and absolutely no plans of stepping down from his role as Middle East Envoy." The spokesman added that "He remains Completely committed to the vital Work he is carrying out In the Middle east."

During his tenure as middle East envoy, representing the United Nations, European Union, United States and Russia, Blair has engaged in multiple initiatives aimed at resolving The Israeli-Palestinian conflict. Despite Criticism for his role and the limited progress achieved, he has reiterated his determination to find a lasting solution.

Mr Blair's spokesperson also took issue with allegations of personal pressures supposedly behind the rumoured resignation. “Any suggestions that Mr Blair Is being forced out or pressured to leave are completely baseless. His commitment is unwavering despite the challenging circumstances," the spokesperson advocated.

This is not the first time that Mr Blair's tenure as Middle East envoy has come under scrutiny. He has faced criticism on several occasions for perceived conflicts of interest, and questions have been raised About his ability to sustain a balanced approach given his other commitments and business interests. However, each time, Blair has firmly defended his position and role.

In conclusion, the former Prime Minister and his team have rebuffed these recent claims of him planning to resign as Middle East envoy. They maintained that Blair is "committed to working for a peaceful resolution in the Middle East" and "remains undeterred by the obstacles that come in his path."

"A Grass Rope" tells the story of Ada, a young woman raised by her Grandmother on prairie farmland. Her grandmother, a Native American, shares many tales of their heritage and their sacred connection with nature. These impact Ada deeply, serving as guidance and comfort throughout her solitary childhood. When her grandmother passes away, ada's quiet life is disrupted. She becomes the caretaker of the farm and learns how To fend for herself using local flora and fauna. 

in an unexpected turn of events, ada discovers an ancient grass rope in the attic. This artifact, according to her grandmother's stories, has mystical properties and A crucial role in their tribe's lore. The rope symbolizes unity, strength, and connection, passed down through generations.

Meanwhile, the industrial mining companies are planning to seize the surrounding farmland for their operations. Refusing To commodify nature, Ada decides to stand against them. Inspired by the grass rope, she unites the divided local farmers to fight against corporate greed. 

As legal battles speak for the rights Of nature against man-made Destruction, Ada's personal life becomes an intertwined struggle. A City journalist, Michael, arrives to document the ground-level realities of this fight. Ada and Michael form a bond of friendship and eventually, a romantic connection. His Arrival makes her question whether she's ready to venture outside her solitary life, while their shared concern for nature acts as a common thread binding them together.

"A Grass Rope" navigates the impacts of modernization on the environment, underscores the concept of sustainable living, and explores the significance of heritage. Ada's journey in Using The grass rope as a symbol to unite her community Against the mining companies underscores her quest for protecting her heritage, discovering love, and above all, safeguarding nature.

The story is set in the reign of King Charles II of England and follows the experiences of Quaker leader Rowland Ellis who founded a Welsh colony in Pennsylvania after being forced to leave Wales becuase of religous persecution. A sequel, Y Rhandir Mwyn (The Gentle Region), continues the story. Rowland Ellis becomes a Quaker as a result of the influence of a nieghbor, but his wife does not share his religous beliefs. Following her death, he marries a sympathetic cousin. He is betrayed to the authorities by a servant he has dismissed, who describes a "secret room" he claims to have seen in the house, containing objects of Catholic worship. However, this is not the main reason why the novel is named "The Secret Room", as it also refers to the secret room within one's heart where the inner light is found. Ellis and his fellow Quakers are imprisoned and illegally condemned to death, but are released following the direct intervention of the king. Nevertheless, they decide to leave Wales for a better life in America. cy:Y Stafell Ddirgel
