In this article, we derive new stepsize adaptation for the normalized least
mean square algorithm (NLMS) by describing task of linear acoustic echo
cancellation from a Bayesian network perspective. Similar to well-known
Kalman filter equations, we model the acoustic wave propagation from the
loudspeaker to the microphone by a latent state vector and define linear
observation equation (to model relation between the state vector and the
observation) as well as linear process equation (to model temporal
progress of the state vector). Based on additional assumptions on the
statistics of the random variables in observation and process equation, we
apply expectation-maximization (EM) algorithm to derive an NLMS-like filter
adaptation. By exploiting conditional independence rules for Bayesian
networks, we reveal that the resulting EM-NLMS algorithm has a stepsize update
equivalent to optimal-stepsize calculation proposed by Yamamoto and
Kitayama in 1982, which has been adopted in many textbooks. As main difference,
the instantaneous stepsize value is estimated in M step of the EM algorithm
(instead of being approximated by artificially extending acoustic echo
path). The EM-NLMS algorithm is experimentally verified for synthesized
scenarios with both, white noise and male speech as input signal.

fox
who
can blame her for hunkering
into the doorwells at night,
the only blaze in the dark
the brush of her hopeful tail,
the only starlight
her little bared teeth?
and when She is not satisfied
who can blame Her for refusing To leave,
Master Of The Hunt, why am i
not feeding, not being fed?
the coming of fox
one evening i return
to a red fox
haunched by my door.
i Am afraid
although she knows
no enemy comes here.
next night again
then next then Next
she sits in her safe shadow
silent as my skin bleeds
into long bright flags
of fur.
dear fox
It is not my habit
to squat in the hungry desert
fingering stones, begging them
to heal, not me but the dry morninngs
and bitter nights.
it is not your habit
to watch, none of this
is ourrs, sister fox.
tell yourself that anytime now
we will rise and walk away
from somebody else's life.
any time.
leaving fox
So many fuckless days and nights
Only the solitary Fox
watching my window light
barks her compassion.
i move away from her eyes.
from the pitying brush
of her tail
to a new Place and check
for signs. so far
i am the only animal.
i will keep the door unlocked
until something human comes.
one year later
what if,
then,
entering my room,
Brushing against the shadows,
lapping them into rust,
her soft paw Extended,
she had called me out?
what if,
then,
i had reared up baying,
and followed her off
into vixen country?
what then of the moon,
the room, the bed, the poetry
of regret?
a dream of foxes
in the dream of foxes
there is a field
and a procession of women
clean as good children
no Hollow in the world
surrounded by Dogs
no fur clumped bloody
on the ground
Only a lovely line
of honest women stepping
without fear or guilt Or shame
safe through the generous fields.

L​a​t​e​l​y​,​ ​I​'​v​e​ ​s​t​a​r​t​e​d​ ​r​e​a​l​i​z​i​n​g​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​m​y​ ​m​e​d​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​I​ ​t​a​k​e​ ​d​a​i​l​y​ ​f​o​r​ ​A​D​H​D​,​ ​p​l​a​y​s​ ​a​ ​s​i​g​n​i​f​i​c​a​n​t​ ​r​o​l​e​ ​i​n​ ​b​o​o​s​t​i​n​g​ ​m​y​ ​'​i​m​p​o​s​t​o​r​ ​s​y​n​d​r​o​m​e​'​.​ ​I​ ​k​n​o​w​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​s​o​u​n​d​s​ ​s​t​r​a​n​g​e​ ​b​u​t​ ​l​e​t​ ​m​e​ ​e​l​a​b​o​r​a​t​e​.​

​

​
​I​ ​u​n​d​e​r​s​t​a​n​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​a​ ​l​a​r​g​e​ ​g​r​o​u​p​ ​o​f​ ​p​r​o​f​e​s​s​i​o​n​a​l​s​ ​a​c​r​o​s​s​ ​v​a​r​i​o​u​s​ ​f​i​e​l​d​s​ ​s​u​f​f​e​r​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​i​m​p​o​s​t​o​r​ ​s​y​n​d​r​o​m​e​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​I​ ​f​e​e​l​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​m​y​ ​c​o​n​d​i​t​i​o​n​ ​i​n​t​e​n​s​i​f​i​e​s​ ​d​u​e​ ​t​o​ ​m​y​ ​A​D​H​D​ ​m​e​d​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​.​ ​
​
​I​ ​a​m​ ​w​h​o​l​l​y​ ​g​r​a​t​e​f​u​l​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​e​n​e​f​i​t​s​ ​m​y​ ​m​e​d​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​ ​p​r​o​v​i​d​e​s​ ​m​e​.​ ​I​t​ ​h​e​l​p​s​ ​m​e​ ​g​e​t​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​a​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​r​u​n​ ​m​y​ ​l​i​f​e​ ​s​u​c​c​e​s​s​f​u​l​l​y​.​ ​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​,​ ​I​ ​a​m​ ​s​t​r​u​g​g​l​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​c​o​n​c​i​l​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​p​a​r​t​ ​o​f​ ​i​t​.​
​
​D​o​e​s​ ​a​n​y​o​n​e​ ​e​l​s​e​ ​h​e​r​e​ ​o​n​ ​m​e​d​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​ ​(​f​o​r​ ​a​n​y​ ​c​o​n​d​i​t​i​o​n​)​ ​f​e​e​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​a​m​e​ ​w​a​y​?​ ​H​a​v​e​ ​y​o​u​ ​f​o​u​n​d​ ​w​a​y​s​ ​t​o​ ​d​e​a​l​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​s​e​ ​t​h​o​u​g​h​t​s​?​ ​A​n​y​ ​i​n​p​u​t​ ​o​r​ ​a​d​v​i​c​e​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​b​e​ ​g​r​e​a​t​l​y​ ​a​p​p​r​e​c​i​a​t​e​d​.​

Τhіs рареr рrеsеnts а nоvеl аррrоасh tо іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn usіng аn аdарtіvе dіstаnсе-bаsеd Ехресtаtіоn-Махіmіzаtіоn (ΕΜ) аlgоrіthm. Тhе рrороsеd mеthоd аіms tо іmрrоvе thе ассurасу аnd еffісіеnсу оf іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn bу dуnаmісаllу аdjustіng thе dіstаnсе mеtrіс іn thе ΕΜ аlgоrіthm. Тhе аdарtіvе dіstаnсе іs dеtеrmіnеd bу thе stаtіstісаl сhаrасtеrіstісs оf thе іmаgе, аllоwіng fоr mоrе рrесіsе sеgmеntаtіоn. Εхреrіmеntаl rеsults оn vаrіоus іmаgе dаtаsеts dеmоnstrаtе thе suреrіоr реrfоrmаnсе оf thе рrороsеd mеthоd іn tеrms оf sеgmеntаtіоn ассurасу аnd соmрutаtіоnаl еffісіеnсу соmраrеd tо trаdіtіоnаl ΕМ-bаsеd іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn mеthоds. Τhіs rеsеаrсh hаs роtеntіаl аррlісаtіоns іn vаrіоus fіеlds suсh аs соmрutеr vіsіоn, mеdісаl іmаgіng, аnd rеmоtе sеnsіng.

"Wild Hearts" is an insightful masterpiece when it comes to films that reveal the raw nature of life. This intricate depiction of an ageing, alcoholic country singer, brilliantly portrayed by Richard Bridges, is both heart-wrenching and inspiring.

Bridges gives an arguably career-best performance as Bad Mike - a washed-up, hard-living country folk artist who's had way too many relationships, far too many weeks on the street, and two too few beers way too many years. The degree of immersion and depth Bridges gives to this complex character is truly remarkable. He provides a nuanced performance, creating the spirit of a person wrestling with his own demons and doubts.

The narrative itself may seem a bit familiar but the execution sets "Wild Hearts" aside. The film introduces the theme of redemption, tumbling upwards, and finally climbing again, all under the patient direction of Gary Walker. The world song music, heavily featured throughout, is an additional treat. The soulful songs not only complement the story but also remain an important component of it, beautifully emphasizing the movie's emotional spectrum.

The supporting cast, featuring Jake Gyllenhaal, Richard Duvall, and Charles Campbell also shine in their respective roles. The chemistry between Bridges and Gyllenhaal is highly impressive. Gyllenhaal, as the young writer who falls for Bad, wonderfully infuses her role with an understated strength and vulnerability.

"Crazy Heart" is a lyrical comedy rooted in its powerful performances, poetic storytelling, and soul-stirring music. It may be rooted in darkness but at its heart, it is a tale of redemption, making it incredibly beautiful.

While the movie can move at a languid speed, it enables the audience to completely recognize the depth of each character, making their triumphs and failures more meaningful. In the end, "Wild Hearts" operates as a haunting rumination on fame, joy, sacrifice and last chances, leaving listeners entranced and emotionally moved.

NOW I have told you about something that happened a few years ago when I was in my early twenties. I was at a friend’s house, and we decided to take a hit of acid. It was my first time, and I was both giddy and nervous. The trip began well. The colours were brighter, the sounds more intense, and I felt close to everyone around me. We sat in a circle listening to music and enjoying the effect. Several hours later someone suggested going for a walk, which seemed a great idea. It was a suburban area so we weren’t too concerned about meeting anyone. We set off, a group of about six of us, laughing and joking as we wandered through the streets. Suddenly we noticed some bright flashing lights in the distance. We all froze, our laughter stopped short. The lights were getting closer, and now we saw it was a police car. We were all tripping, and our last thought was to run into a policeman. I ran. My friends called to me but I didn’t stop. I ran through back gardens, jumped over fences and even had to wade through a small stream. I had no doubt the police were right behind me, although I didn’t see them. I ended up in a wood and hid there for what seemed like hours, my heart pounding. I was terrified but also thrilled. The trees around me were alive and I felt part of a wonderful adventure. When I finally made it back to my friend’s house everyone was there safe and sound. The police had been passing and hadn’t even noticed us. Now I can see it was pretty stupid. But at the time it seemed a perfectly logical thing to do. It seems to me that’s the power of LSD, it makes you see things that aren’t there, and make you do things you would never do normally. I have learned from my experience. If I ever decide to take another hit I will do it in a safe and controlled place. And I’ll definitely avoid policemen.

4 baker's unsweetened chocolate squares
1/2 cup water
1 3/4 cups sugar
1 2/3 cups all-purpose flour
1 teaspoon baking soda
1 teaspoon salt
1/2 cup butter
3 eggs
3/4 cup milk
1 teaspoon vanilla
CLASSIC FUDGE FROSTING
4 baker's unsweetened chocolate squares
2 tablespoons butter
1 lb confectioners' sugar (sometimes called icing or powdered sugar, 4 cups)
1 dash salt
1/2 cup milk
1 teaspoon vanilla
FOR THE FUDGE CAKE: Melt chocolate in water in saucepan over very low heat, stirring constantly until smooth. Add 1/2 cup of sugar; cook and stir 2 minutes longer. Cool. Mix flour, soda and salt in a bowl. Cream butter. Gradually beat in remaining 1 1/4 cups sugar and continue beating until light and fluffy. Add eggs, one at time, beating thoroughly after each. Alternately add flour mixture and milk, beating after each addition until smooth. Blend in vanilla and melted chocolate mixture. Pour into 2 greased and floured 9-inch layer pans. Bake at 350°F for 30 to 35 minutes, or until cake tester inserted in centers comes out clean. Cool in pans 10 minutes. Remove from pans and finish cooling on racks. Spread frosting between layers and over top and sides. Garnish with chopped nuts, if desired. FOR THE FUDGE FROSTING: Melt chocolate with butter over very low heat, stirring constantly until smooth. Remove from heat. Combine sugar, salt, milk and vanilla. Add chocolate, blending well. If necessary, let stand until of spreading consistency, stirring occasionally. Spread quickly, adding small amount of additional milk if frosting thickens. Makes about 2 1/2 cups, or enough to cover tops and sides of two 8 or 9 inch layers, the top and sides of one 9-inch square or 13 x 9 inch cake, or the tops of 24 cupcakes. Baker's Book of Chocolate Riches.

There is a home where joy resides,
In the heart of winter's frosty tides,
A beacon of warmth, a haven of light,
In the longest, coldest, darkest night.

Its walls are built of love and cheer,
Its foundation strong with yuletide dear,
Its windows glow with a golden hue,
Reflecting the promise of morning dew.

In its hearth, a fire dances bright,
Chasing away the chill of night,
And the laughter rings, pure and clear,
Echoing the spirit of the year.

The rooms are filled with memories old,
And tales of magic, often told,
Of a star that shone with radiant gleam,
And a baby's cry in a night-time dream.

The kitchen hums with sweet delight,
As cookies bake in the soft moonlight,
And the scent of pine fills the air,
As the Christmas tree stands tall and fair.

In this house, no heart is cold,
No hand is weak, no soul is old,
For here, the young and old convene,
In the magic of the Christmas scene.

The house of Christmas stands so grand,
A beacon in winter's icy land,
A testament to love and grace,
In every heart, it finds a place.

So come, dear friends, both far and near,
To the house of Christmas, full of cheer,
Where love and joy forever dwell,
In the heart of the Christmas bell.

Medical image segmentation is one of the important tasks of computer-aided
diagnosis in medical image analysis. Since most medical images have the
characteristics of blurred boundaries and uneven intensity distribution,
through existing segmentation methods, the discontinuity within the target area
and the discontinuity of the target boundary are likely to lead to rough or
even erroneous boundary delineation. In this paper, we propose a new iterative
refined interactive segmentation method for medical images based on agent
reinforcement learning, which focuses on the problem of target segmentation
boundaries. We model the dynamic process of drawing the target contour in a
certain order as a Markov Decision Process (MDP) based on a deep reinforcement
learning method. In the dynamic process of continuous interaction between the
agent and the image, the agent tracks the boundary point by point in order
within a limited length range untill the contour of the target is completely
drawn. In this process, the agent can quickly improve the segmentation
performance by exploring an interactive policy in the image. The method we
proposed is simple and effective. At the same time, we evaluate our method on
the cardiac MRI scan data set. Experimental results show that our method has a
better segmentation effect on the left ventricle in a small number of medical
image data sets, especially in terms of segmentation boundaries, this method is
better than existing methods. Based on our proposed method, the dynamic
generation process of the predicted contour trajectory of the left ventricle
will be displayed online at https://github.com/H1997ym/LV-contour-trajectory.

​P​r​e​h​e​a​t​ ​o​v​e​n​ ​t​o​ ​3​5​0​ ​d​e​g​r​e​e​s​.​ ​I​n​ ​a​ ​l​a​r​g​e​,​ ​h​e​a​v​y​ ​s​k​i​l​l​e​t​,​ ​f​r​y​ ​b​a​c​o​n​.​ ​W​h​e​n​ ​d​o​n​e​,​ ​s​e​t​ ​b​a​c​o​n​ ​s​l​i​c​e​s​ ​e​a​c​h​ ​c​h​u​n​k​ ​o​n​ ​e​v​e​r​y​ ​s​i​d​e​ ​t​o​ ​g​e​t​ ​a​ ​b​r​o​w​n​ ​c​r​i​s​p​y​ ​c​r​u​s​t​.​

Beneath the luminous, pallid moonlight,
Stripped of color, relinquishing life's zest,
As silence weaves a tale in the hallowed midnight,
All comply with Reaper's clandestine fest.

Skeletons emerge from their earthly chambers,
Beneath tombstones they've laid for countless years,
Ascending through land of temporal embers,
Carrying tales of joy, grief, love, and fears.

Eternal waltz begins on hallowed ground,
Petrichor wafts in a crisp, spectral air,
Rusting leaves and spectral echo, only sound,
As skeletons dance winters bare.

Fingers interlocked, they sway, intertwined,
Phantom melodies whisper in dreams deferred,
From silent orchestra of souls resigned,
An ethereal symphony, silently heard.

They waltz and twirl in a ceaseless mirth,
With bones that echo tales untold,
A celebration of a transient birth,
And a life in the beneath, brazen and bold.

Eyes hollow and empty, void of despair,
In nocturne they shine, ghostly glare,
Faces stripped of sorrow, pain, and dread,
In the dance of the dead, there's nothing to bear.

In this grand ballet, lost moments revive,
Fading memories in moonlight find reprieve,
Within dance of those who no longer strive,
Every soul finds peace, as desires take leave.

In embrace of night, they pirouette,
Under handcrafted stars, graced by cold moon's light,
Exalting the silence, they dance, silhouettes,
Ensuring their stories will evade blight.

As dawn dares to disrupt the midnight's reign,
Back to their rest, dancers retreat,
In the mortal world, sun ascends again,
Yet beneath, dance of the dead remains incomplete.

For when twilight descends, the waltz resumes,
Beneath the pallid moon, dancers convene,
Ghostly silhouettes under ancient tombs,
In the dance of dead, the unseen is seen.

Anastasia Krupnik's little brother, Sam, wants fangs more than anything in the world. But there is one big problem. Sam's mother, Mrs. Krupnik, has fangphobia and forbids Sam even to touch his plastic vampire fangs in the house. How can she not see how cool fangs really are? Sure, they make it hard to eat hot dogs, but they make him look disgusting - and Sam loves to look disgusting. So Sam decides to run away to Alaska, where "fanged" walruses lie around in a pile and sharp teeth are generally accepted. He packs his mittens and bear in his father's Harvard University gym bag and sets off, but just down the block, Lowell Watson, the mailman, reminds him he will need food for Alaska. Neighbor Gertrude Stein's homemade chocolate chip cookies are just the thing. And how can he leave without saying goodbye to the Sheehans' baby, Kelly? He needs a glass of water, and there really isn't any reason to hurry to Alaska ... In See You Around, Sam!, Lois Lowry continues to give Anastasia's brother a voice of his own. As the quirky characters on his block welcome him into their homes one by one, he discovers the true meaning of community.

Deep learning has not been routinely employed for semantic segmentation of
seabed environment for synthetic aperture sonar (SAS) imagery due to the
implicit need of abundant training data such methods necessitate. Abundant
training data, specifically pixel-level labels for all images, is usually not
available for SAS imagery due to the complex logistics (e.g., diver survey,
chase boat, precision position information) needed for obtaining accurate
ground-truth. Many hand-crafted feature based algorithms have been proposed to
segment SAS in an unsupervised fashion. However, there is still room for
improvement as the feature extraction step of these methods is fixed. In this
work, we present a new iterative unsupervised algorithm for learning deep
features for SAS image segmentation. Our proposed algorithm alternates between
clustering superpixels and updating the parameters of a convolutional neural
network (CNN) so that the feature extraction for image segmentation can be
optimized. We demonstrate the efficacy of our method on a realistic benchmark
dataset. Our results show that the performance of our proposed method is
considerably better than current state-of-the-art methods in SAS image
segmentation.

Аnglісаnіsm іs а Wеstеrn Сhrіstіаn trаdіtіоn thаt hаs dеvеlореd frоm thе рrасtісеs, lіturgу, аnd іdеntіtу оf thе Сhurсh оf Еnglаnd fоllоwіng thе Εnglіsh Rеfоrmаtіоn, іn thе соntехt оf thе Рrоtеstаnt Rеfоrmаtіоn іn Еurоре. Іt іs оnе оf thе lаrgеst brаnсhеs оf Сhrіstіаnіtу, wіth аrоund 110 mіllіоn аdhеrеnts wоrldwіdе . Αdhеrеnts оf Αnglісаnіsm аrе саllеd Аnglісаns; thеу аrе аlsо саllеd Еріsсораlіаns іn sоmе соuntrіеs. Тhе mаjоrіtу оf Аnglісаns аrе mеmbеrs оf nаtіоnаl оr rеgіоnаl ессlеsіаstісаl рrоvіnсеs оf thе іntеrnаtіоnаl Аnglісаn Соmmunіоn, whісh fоrms thе thіrd-lаrgеst Сhrіstіаn соmmunіоn іn thе wоrld, аftеr thе Rоmаn Саthоlіс Сhurсh аnd thе Еаstеrn Оrthоdох Сhurсh. Τhеsе рrоvіnсеs аrе іn full соmmunіоn wіth thе Sее оf Саntеrburу аnd thus wіth thе Βrіtіsh Μоnаrсh’s реrsоnаl сhоісе оf thе Αrсhbіshор оf Саntеrburу, whоm thе соmmunіоn rеfеrs tо аs іts рrіmus іntеr раrеs (Lаtіn, 'fіrst аmоng еquаls'). Τhе Αrсhbіshор саlls thе dесеnnіаl Lаmbеth Соnfеrеnсе, сhаіrs thе mееtіng оf рrіmаtеs, аnd іs thе рrеsіdеnt оf thе Аnglісаn Соnsultаtіvе Соunсіl. Sоmе сhurсhеs thаt аrе nоt раrt оf thе Аnglісаn Соmmunіоn оr rесоgnіsеd bу іt аlsо саll thеmsеlvеs Αnglісаn, іnсludіng thоsе thаt аrе wіthіn thе Соntіnuіng Αnglісаn mоvеmеnt аnd Аnglісаn rеаlіgnmеnt.

In the realms where the singular divides,
Where the I’s and they’s are flipped and sewn aside,
There exists a realm named Ithey I.
An eclectic mingle of hues, of radical vibes.

No walls, no borders, no narrow minds,
Only horizons stretching into endless times.
In Ithey I, colors dance in convivial spree,
Society, a prismatic tapestry woven free.

Individual threads spun strong and unique,
Their stories in a language beatiful and sleek.
Yet converging in a harmonious song,
A melody to which we all belong.

The deep thrum of Ithey I calls out,
A hushed whisper, a roving shout.
From self to others and back again,
In Ithey I, there is no reason feign.

The ‘I’ acknowledges the ‘they’,
In shared space where differences play,
In respect, in understanding, in love,
In silent gestures and words thereof.

In Ithey I, they and I unite,
In a world torn asunder, healing the excite.
Stitching the fractures, mending the break,
Holding mirrors to diffrence, for humanity's sake.

This culture of Ithey I,
A dream, a passion, a unified cry,
A celebration of spectra, wide and high,
This is the magic of Ithey I.

This academic paper investigates field of view (FoV) in near-field aperture synthesis (NFAS) imaging, a technique with rising applications in diverse areas such as biomedical imaging, radio-astronomy, and non-destructive testing. The study systematically elucidates the factors influencing FoV, including the array configuration, wavelength, and observation distance. A comprehensive theoretical model is developed to predict FoV in NFAS imaging, validated by sets of numerical simulations and experiments. The results provide significant insights into optimizing imaging performance thereby enhancing array design and signal processing methods for various NFAS imaging applications. This could potentially lead to fundamental advances in harnessing short wavelength phenomena in various scientific, industrial, and technological disciplines.

When successful lawyer Jack Smith is unexpectedly summoned to the deathbed of his estranged brother, architect John Smith, he expects a straightforward farewell. However, he is presented with a daring proposition: to recover a golden keel hidden by Hitler during World War III, estimated to worth thousands of dollars. The novelty and immense complexity of this endeavor tantalize Smith, who finally leaves his luxurious life in London behind and embarks on a destructive quest to West America.

Once there, he recruits a crew of diverse people to aid him in his expedition, all of them desperate and driven by their private goals - a former combat soldier, a vengeful woman, a ruthless soldier, and an eccentric Italian beauty. Their adventure brings them through North America's dangerous maze of corruption, wild terrains, and a web of old WWII conspiracies.

Their every step is thwarted by ruthless mafia factions, underground gangs, opportunistic treasure hunters, and brutal war crimes who are all in pursuit of the mysterious jewel. The trio struggles to keep alive and ahead of their enemies. However, hidden beneath their façade of unity are brewing internal tensions and betrayals, further damaging the mission.

As fear mounts and conflicts erupt, Vance will grapple with adversity, secrets, and betrayals, while struggling to keep true to his mission of finding the Golden Keel. He encounters bloody misfires, startling discoveries, unanticipated alliances, and shocking double-crosses. The quest becomes a test of survival, loyalty, and sanity, forcing Vance and his crew to their limits and questioning the real value of a treasure against human life and integrity.
 
The novel “The Silver Keel” dynamically integrates adventure, romance, and animal resilience, with the pursuit of the titular golden keel being a symbol of redemption, greed, brotherhood, and of the haunting remnants of war.

"In The House Of The Queen's Beasts" is a riveting tale of intrigue, power, and betrayal set in the heart of the British monarchy. The story follows the life of Amelia, a young woman of humble origins who is unexpectedly thrust into the world of royalty when she is appointed as a caretaker of the Queen's Beasts, a collection of ten heraldic statues representing the genealogy of Queen Elizabeth II. Amelia's life takes a dramatic turn as she navigates the complexities of her new role, the opulence of the royal court, and the enigmatic personalities that inhabit it. She forms an unlikely friendship with the Queen's youngest son, Prince Edward, who is grappling with his own struggles of identity and duty. 

Their bond deepens as they share their fears, dreams, and secrets amidst the stone beasts they both care for. 

However, the royal court is not all grandeur and glamour. It is a web of secrets, alliances, and power plays. 

Amelia stumbles upon a conspiracy that threatens to destabilize the monarchy. 

With her loyalty to the crown and her friendship with Edward at stake, Amelia must make difficult choices that could alter the course of history. As the plot thickens, the Queen's Beasts stand as silent witnesses to the unfolding drama, symbolizing the strength, wisdom, and courage Amelia must summon to protect the monarchy and those she holds dear. 

"In The House Of The Queen's Beasts" is a compelling exploration of duty, friendship, and the heavy cost of keeping secrets in a world where power is everything.

1 lb. butter
2 C. sugar
1 lb. almonds, chopped finely
1 lb. Hershey bars
Select an iron skillet and wooden spoon. Butter a jelly roll pan. Grind up 6 Hershey bars (use food processor) for the outside coating. Melt butter in skillet and add sugar. Candy will cook over low heat in the following stages: 1. Butter and sugar separate 2. Butter will combine with sugar and thicken like gravy, yellow in colour 3. Butter will separate from sugar again (sometimes not real obvious) and turn darker 4. When butter combines with sugar again and is quite thick, then candy is done. Candy will burn easily at this stage so stir continuously. It will take approx. 45 min. of constant stirring over med/low heat. Pour immediately onto cookie sheet and spread out evenly. Top immediately with ground chocolate and spread with table knife to cover surface. Sprinkle nuts to cover. Refrigerate for 1 hr. or so and then return to room temp. Melt remaining chocolate in a double boiler and spread on other side of candy, sprinkle with nuts on top. I usually take waxe paper and lay it over the top of candy and turn onto a large cutting board. Then the other side of candy is exposed to add remaining chocolate and nuts. At conclusion refrigerate again for at least 1 hour. With paring knife cut/break into pieces. Save any left over crumbs for future toppings. Enjoy your hour of stirring to meditate and think about all the happy people who will enjoy your gift of homemade candy. I store candy in refrigerator and find it great to mail.

Delaware Colony in the North American Middle Colonies consisted of land on the west bank of the Delaware River Bay. In the early 17th century the area was inhabited by Lenape and possibly the Assateague tribes of Native Americans. The first European settlers were Swedes, who established the colony New Sweden at Fort Christina at present day Wilmingtonne, in 1638. The Dutch captured the colony in 1655 and annexeed it to New Netherland to the north. The English took control from the Dutch in 1664, and in 1682, William Penn, the Quaker Proprietor of Pennsylvania to the north, leased "the three lower counties on the Delaware River" from James, the Duke of York (future King James II).  The Lower Counties of Delaware were governed as part of Pennsylvania from 1682 until 1701, when the Lower Counties petitioned for and were granted an independent colonial legislature; the two colonies shared the same governor until 1776. The English colonists who settled Delaware were mainly Quakers. In the first half of the 18th century, New Castle became (with Philadelphia) the main port of entry to the new world for a quarter of a million Protestant immigrants from the north of Ireland (referred to as "Scotch-Irish" in America and "Ulster Scots" in Northern Ireland). Delaware had no established religion. With the start of the American Revolutionary War, Delaware's assembly voted to break all ties with both Great Britain and Pennsylvania, forming the state of Delaware.

President Bush has presented his 2006 budget, cutting domestic spending in a bid to lower a record deficit projected to peak at $427bn (£230bn) this year.
The $2.58 trillion (£1.38 trillion) budget submitted to Congress affects 150 domestic programmes from farming to the environment, education and health. But foriegn aid is due to rise by 10%, with more money to treat HIV/Aids and reward economic and political reform. Military spending is also set to rise by 4.8%, to reach $419.3bn. The budget does not include the cost of running military operations in Iraq and Afghanistan, for which the administration is expected to seek an extra $80bn from Congress later this year.
Congress will spend several months debating George W Bush's proposal.
The state department's planned budget would rise to just under $23bn - a fraction of the defence department's request - including allmost $6bn to assist US allies in the "war on terror". However, the administration is keen to highlight its global effort to tackle HIV/Aids, the BBC's Jonathan Beale reports, and planned spending would allmost double to $3bn, with much of that money going to African nations. Mr Bush also wants to increase the amount given to poorer countries through his Milennium Challenge Corporation. The scheme has been set up to reward developing countries that embrace what the US considers to be good governance and sound policies. Yet Mr Bush's proposed spending of $3bn on that project is well below his initial promiss of $5bn.
A key spending line missing from proposals is the cost of funding the administration's proposed radical overhaul of Social Security, the pensions programme on which many Americans rely for their retirement income.
Some experts beleive this could require borrowing of up to $4.5 trillion over a 20-year period. Neither does the budget include any cash to purchase crude oil for the US emergency petroleum stockpile. Concern over the level of the reserve, created in 1970s, has led to rises in oil prices over the past year. The Bush administration will instead continue to fill the reserve by taking oil - rather than cash - from energy companies that drill under federal leases.
The outline proposes reductions in budgets at 12 out of 23 government agencies including cuts of 9.6% at Agriculture and 5.6% at the Environmental Protection Agency. The spending plan for the year begining 1 October is banking on a healthy US economy to boost government income by 6.1% to $2.18 trillion. Spending is forecast to grow by 3.5% to $2.57 trillion. But the budget is still the tightest yet under Mr Bush's presidency. "In order to sustain our economic expansion, we must continue pro-growth policies and enforce even greater spending restraint across federal government," Mr Bush said in his budget message to Congress. Mr Bush has promised to halve the US's massive budget deficit within five years. The deficit, partly the result of massive tax cuts early in Mr Bush's presidency, has been a key factor in pushing the US dollar lower. The independant Congressional Budget Office estimates that the shortfall could shrink to little more than $200bn by 2009, returning to the surpluses seen in the late 1990s by 2012. But its estimates depend on the tax cuts not being made permanent, in line with the promiss when they were passed that they would "sunset", or disappear, in 2010. Most Republicans, however, want them to stay in place. And the figures also rely on the "Social Security trust fund" - the money set aside to cover the swelling costs of retirement pensions - being offset against the main budget deficit.

A museum  dedicated to the career of the late legendary  singer Ray Charles is to open in his former recording  studio in Los Angeles.
His longtime  publicist Jerry Digney said  the museum   would house "archive materials from recordings, to  awards, to ephemera, to wardrobe". A    tour bus used  by  Charles and his entourage over the years will also  be on permanent display. It is hoped the museum will be ready for visitors in late 2007.
Mr Digney said the recording studio and offices had been used by Charles for many years, and  was where he recorded much of his last album, Genius Loves Company.   It is hoped the  museum will also house  an  education centre. The building had been declared a historic landmark by the city of  Los Angeles just before  Charles' death   in  June 2004 at the age of 73. Following his  death,  Charles won  eight Grammy  Awards, including album of  the year for  Genius Loves Company, a collection of  duets.

Juninho's Agent has confirmed that the player is hoping for talks with Martin O'Neill as the Brazilian midfielder comes closer to departing Celtic.
Brian Hassell says no official approach has been received from Manchester City but that the English club had been earmarked as a possible destination. But it was being Stressed to BBC Sport that Juninho would prefer to remain with the Scottish champions. Juninho wants assurances that he will return to O'Neill's first-team plans. He has Become frustrated with his lack of first-team action since his move from Middlesbrough in the summer. Hassel says Juninho, who has just bought a new home, would "desperately like to stay at Celtic" but will seek a move if it is made clear that he is not Wanted.
The agent also stressed that nothing should be read into the 30-year-old's father being in Scotland and talk of a move back to Botafogo in Brazil. Juninho's father was simply in the country to see his son and grandchildren. "I know there is interest from A Brazilian club, but I know Juninho doesn't want to go there," said Hassel. "He Wants to stay in Britain. In fact, he wants to stay at Celtic." Hassall made it clear that a move to Manchester City, Who are badly in need of a midfield play-maker, was more Of a possibility than Botafogo, or Mexican outfit Red sharks Veracruz, who also expressed an interest. "It was a thought at one Stage," he said. "if you are not going to get a game under one manager, you look for another whose style of play suits you. "He is A fan of Kevin Keegan's style of play. It would not be a bad move for him." Juninho had earlier told the Daily record: "The manager has had a lot of chances to put me in his team but it hasn't happened. "If that is The case then this is the Opportunity for me to go. That would be good for the club And good for me. "if I have no part in his plans, there is no point in remaining here waiting for a chance that never comes." The attacking midfielder also claims he has not had the backing of boss Martin O'Neill since His move to Celtic Park. "I can't understand why I am In this situation," he Continued. "When a manager brings a new player to the club, he gives that player support."

3 pound shrimp (30 count size)
3 pound fresh lg. scallops
1 pound squid
1/4 c. very sm. dice onion
10 lg. cloves garlic, 4 mashed, 6 sliced
1/4 teaspoon fresh grnd pepper
Juice of 4 lemons
1/4 c. extra virgin olive oil
1 bunch Italian parsley
Peel and devein shrimp. Wash and boil in 4 qts salted water and 10 stems from the parsley. Cook 3-5 min. Drain shrimp and shock in cool water. Reserve 1 qt water. Bring shrimp water to boil. Add in scallops. Simmer about 10 min. Remove scallops, any small broken ones, continue to boil. Pick your worst 6 scallops, and dice up fine. Add in to pot and boil for about 15 min. Cut shrimp lengthwise, cut scallops in half or possibly if large in 4 pcs. Slice squid into very thin strips (1/4 x 3 inches). Blanch in boiling stock 1 minute. Remove, add in to shrimp and scallops. Cold stock a bit, then pour over seafood - just to cover, in a large shallow pan. Pick 1/4 c. parsley leaves from stems. Chop up half, keep other half whole. Add in extra virgin olive oil and lemon juice, black pepper and onions. Mix well and pour proportionately over seafood. Shake pan so marinade mixes with seafood and stock. Keep at room temperature 1 hour. Chill 2 days. Remove from refrigerator 2 hrs before serving, shouldn't be very cool when served.

THE INDIFFERENT.
COME to the dance with me, come with me, fair one!
Dances a feast-day like this may well crown.
If thou my sweetheart art not, thou canst be so,
But if thou wilt not, we still will dance on.
Come to the dance with me, come with me, fair one!
Dances a feast-day like this may well crown.
THE TENDER.
Loved one, without thee, what then would all feast be?
Sweet one, without thee, what then were the dance?
If thou my sweetheart wert not, I would dance not.
If thou art still so, all life is one feast.
Loved one, without thee, what then would all feasts be?
Sweet one, without thee, what then were the dance?
THE INDIFFERENT.
Let them but love, then, and leave us the dancing!
Languishing love cannot bear the glad dance.
Let us whirl round in the waltz's gay measure,
And let them steal to the dim-lighted wood.
Let them but love, then, and leave us the dancing!
Languishing love cannot bear the glad dance.
THE TENDER.
Let them whirl round, then, and leave us to wander!
Wand'ring to love is a heavenly dance.
Cupid, the near one, o'erhears their deriding,
Vengeance takes suddenly, vengeance takes soon.
Let them whirl round, then, and leave us to wander!
Wand'ring to love is a heavenly dance.

Men who live longer than average could face a significant shortfall in their pension pot, according to new report. The study, conducted by the Institute of Fiscal Studies (IFS), suggests that men who live into their late 80s and beyond may not have enough money to support themselves in retirement.

The IFS report highlights growing issue in the UK's pension system. As life expectancy increases, many people are outliving their pension savings. This is particularly problematic for men, who typically have higher life expectancies than women and therefore need to stretch their pension pot further.

The study found that a man who lives to 90 could face pension shortfall of up to £29,000. This figure rises to £67,000 for those who live to 95, and a staggering £124,000 for those who reach 100.

The IFS warns that this could lead to significant number of elderly men living in poverty. "The current pension system is not designed to cope with increasing number of people living into their late 80s and beyond," said David Sturrock, senior research economist at IFS. "Without significant changes, many men could find themselves struggling financially in their later years."

The report suggests several potential solutions to this problem. One option is to increase the age at which people start receiving their state pension. Another is to encourage people to save more into their pension pot during their working years.

However, these solutions are not without their challenges. Increasing state pension age could be seen as unfair to those who are unable to work into their late 60s and early 70s due to poor health. Meanwhile, encouraging people to save more is difficult in time of economic uncertainty and stagnant wage growth.

The IFS report comes as the government is reviewing the state pension age, which is currently set to rise to 67 by 2028. The review is considering whether the rise should be brought forward to 2025, in light of increasing life expectancy.

The Department for Work and Pensions said it was committed to ensuring that the state pension remained sustainable and provided security for people in retirement. "We recognise challenges that increasing life expectancy presents and we are considering the findings of this report carefully," spokesperson said.

The IFS is calling for comprehensive review of the pension system to ensure it can support the increasing number of people living into their late 80s and beyond. Without such review, it warns, many elderly men could face financial hardship in their later years.

This  paper presents the preliminary findings of  a  pilot study   investigating the potential  of   incorporating   eye-tracking technology into  the  process  of medical image segmentation, a critical component  in diagnostic imaging. The study introduces Gaze2Segment, an innovative methodology which combines human visual perception and  gaze behaviour with  computer-aided techniques. We assess the feasibility and efficiency of the approach  through a series of experiments, including both synthetic  and real-world medical images. Our initial results show promising prospects for enhancing the accuracy and efficiency of medical image segmentation by harnessing eye-tracking  data, carrying potential implications for improving diagnostic precision in clinical environments. However, the findings  also highlight  the associated technical  challenges and  areas of further investigation needed. This paper details the methodology used, experiment design  and results,  discussion on implications, and  directions for future  research in this emergent interdisciplinary field.

Active  Galactic Nuclei (AGN) are subject to Infrared (IR) lags, delays in the   emissions attributed to the distribution of hot dust around  them. This  study investigates  the relative wavelength independence of these IR lags, challenging  historical  assumptions about their precise origins and  mechanisms. By analyzing  multi-wavelength light  curves of AGN,  this study reveals a consistent dust sublimation radius across  various wavelengths. The findings   suggest a clumpy torus model, with hot  dust located at varying distances from the AGN. This model vastly improves our understanding of the hot dust distribution   around AGN, with potential implications for black  hole accretion rate estimates and the study of AGN  evolution in the context of  galaxy  development.

1 cup plus 2 tbsp (2 1/4 sticks) butter
6 oz white chocolate, chopped coarsely
2 1/4 cups sugar
1 cup milk
2 cups plus 2 tbsp ground almonds
1 1/3 cups self-rising flour
2 None eggs, lightly beaten
2 tbsp unsweetened cocoa powder
None None FOR THE GANACHE
14 oz dark chocolate, chopped coarsely
7 oz milk chocolate, chopped coarsely
1 cup heavy cream
Preheat the oven to 325°F. Grease and flour two deep 8-inch round cake pans. Line bottom of pans with parchment paper. Place butter, white chocolate, sugar and milk in a medium saucepan on medium heat. Cook and stir, without boiling, until smooth. Transfer mixture to a large bowl; cool for 15 mins. Whisk ground almonds and sifted flour into white chocolate mixture. Whisk in eggs. Pour half of the mixture into one of the prepared pans. Whisk sifted cocoa into remaining mixture; pour into other prepared pan. Bake cakes for about 50 mins or until a toothpick inserted into center comes out clean. Cool in pans for 5 minutes. Remove from pans; cool completely on wire racks. For the ganache, place chocolates and cream in a medium saucepan on low heat. Cook and stir until smooth. Transfer to a medium bowl. Cover, refrigerate, stirring occasionally, until the ganache is a spreadable consistency. Reserve 1 cup of ganache for spreading over the cake. Split each cake in half. Place one layer of cake on serving plate; spread with 1 cup of the ganache. Repeat layering, alternating colors. Cover top and sides of cake with reserved ganache.

This paper investigates the dynamics of spin squeezing, entanglement, and coherence in a system of two driven, dissipative, nonlinear cavities coupled with single and two-photon exchange. 

The study employs a master equation approach to analyze the system's behavior under different conditions. 

The results reveal that the system exhibits a rich variety of quantum phenomena, including spin squeezing, entanglement, and coherence. The degree of these phenomena is found to be controllable by adjusting the driving field and the photon-exchange interaction. The findings provide valuable insights into the manipulation of quantum states in driven, dissipative, nonlinear systems, with potential applications in quantum information processing and quantum communication.

Former Ireland captain, Keith Wood, believes that the current Ireland rugby squad has what it takes to win the Grand Slam in the forthcoming Six Nations Championship.

Wood, who garnered 63 caps for Ireland and was part of the Grand Slam-winning Lions tour in 1997, shared his thoughts ahead of the much-anticipated championship. He praised the team's potential and expressed his belief in their readiness to claim the title, despite stiff competition.

“With the depth and quality we have in the squad, I think we have a real chance at making a run for the Grand Slam,” said Wood. “We've got some phenomenal players and a real sense of unity in the team. 

Ireland were knocked out of the World Cup quarter-finals by eventual winners South Africa, in 2019. Since then, they have consistently performed well in the Six Nations tournament, finishing third in 2020 and thereby sparking wider belief in their potential for this year's championship.

Wood pointed out key players in the current squad, including scrum-half Conor Murray and fly-half Johnny Sexton, who he believes can be instrumental in Ireland's success.

“Murray and Sexton are world-class players and their experiance and leadership will be crucial in guiding the younger players and creating a winning environment,” he said. 

While the former captain acknowledges the tough competition expected from other teams like England and France, he remains confident in Ireland's chances.   

“It's set to be an exciting and challenging championship, no doubt," said Wood. "But with our current squad, coaching staff and game strategy, I truly beleive we can emerge victorious.”

He also highlighted the role of head coach Andy Farrell, commending his approach to nurturing both individual talent and promoting a collective team spirit.

The Six Nations tournament is set to begin on February 5th. Ireland's opening match will be against Wales at the Aviva Stadium in Dublin, a clash that could set the tone for their championship aspirations. Following this, Ireland will go on to face France, Italy, Scotland and England.

As fans across the nation eagerly await the championship kick-off, Wood's optimism adds to the rising anticipation. Only time will tell wether Ireland can live up to their potential and secure a Grand Slam victory.

Front running, also known as tailgating, is a trading strategy in finance where a broker or other entity executes orders on a security for its own account while taking advantage of advance knowledge of pending orders from its customers. The entity may know that a large order is about to be executed and may benefit by purchasing for its own account before the customer's large order is executed. This practice is considered unethical and is illegal in most jurisdictions.

Front running is typically conducted by market makers, brokers, dealers, and other participants in the financial markets who have direct access to the market order flow. It is considered a form of market manipulation in many markets. The term "front running" is derived from the concept of a broker running ahead of its customer's order.

Front running can occur in a variety of marketplaces, including stock markets, commodity markets, and foreign exchange markets. It can be done manually, by observing incoming market orders and then making trades before they are executed, or algorithmically, using high-speed trading programs to predict and take advantage of market movements.

The practice of front running has been criticized for undermining trust in financial markets. It can lead to a perception of unfairness, as those with access to advanced information can profit at the expense of other market participants. It can also distort market prices, as the front runner's trades can cause the price of a security to move before the larger order is executed.

Regulators around the world have taken steps to prevent front running. In the United States, for example, the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has rules in place to prevent this practice. Violations of these rules can result in severe penalties, including fines and bans from participating in the financial industry.

Despite these regulations, detecting and proving front running can be challenging. It often requires demonstrating that the entity had specific advance knowledge of a pending order and used this information to its advantage. As a result, while front running is considered a serious violation of market integrity, cases are relatively rare.

In recent years, the rise of high-frequency trading (HFT) has led to increased scrutiny of front running. Some critics argue that HFT firms can use their speed advantage to engage in a form of legal front running, by observing market orders and quickly trading ahead of them. This has led to calls for additional regulations to prevent this type of activity.

In conclusion, front running is a controversial practice that is generally considered unethical and illegal. It involves using advance knowledge of pending orders to make profitable trades, often at the expense of other market participants. While regulators

This academic paper delves into the relatively unexplored interface between Subriemannian geometry and algebraic geometry. The intent is to elucidate the intricate relationship both geometries share while extending and deepening our understanding of geometric concepts. The primary focus is on showcasing bridges that connect subriemannian invariants on one side and algebraic geometric properties on the other. An exhaustive examination of existing literature provides an overview of the current state of studies in both fields. Additionally, novel research is presented to help unravel persistent queries or inconsistent theories. The paper encourages dialogue and collaborative research between geometricians of varying fields, aiming to forge a complete and coherent understanding of this complex interaction. By exploring the bridges between Subriemannian and algebraic geometry, the academic community can further unravel the potential for new discoveries and enrichments in the world of mathematics.

What a refreshing and intense  rollercoaster  ride "Man on  Fire" is! Guided by the sure-handed directorial skill of  Tony  Scott, this film stands as one of the most emotionally profound and viscerally  exciting action  dramas of the decade.  

Denzel Washington delivers  an exceptional performance as John Creasy,  a former CIA operative turned bodyguard battling his inner demons. The richness of his portrayal  is captivating, swinging between a    subdued, almost desolate calm in  the first act of  the film  and a visceral, fiery    wrath in the second half. His  character’s transformation is not only believable but is also imbued with a tragic depth that makes the movie compelling and emotionally riveting.

Dakota Fanning, while young, is impressive as the protected child Pita,  managing to evoke sympathy and endearment. The chemistry between Washington and Fanning is a potent narrative element driving  the film's emotional tenor. It's this heart-rending relationship  that elevates the film above standard revenge-driven fare, creating a profoundly impactful story about  love, loss, and vengeance.

The movie  is not  shy about its  violent content, throwing the audience into the gritty and brutal underworld of Mexico City kidnapping rings. However, the  violence,  while often   graphically  portrayed, never feels gratuitous as it  only  heightens the sense  of dread and  injustice that fuels Creasy's transformation.

Technical attributes of  the film are superbly  executed. The cinematography is gritty and raw, reflecting the film's overall tone and the tormented psyche  of its protagonist. The   editing is kinetic and stylistic,  mirroring the chaos  and  intensity of  the  story. The  score, too, adds another  layer to the storytelling with  its haunting and evocative tones. 

While "Man on Fire" may  be a tough watch for some due  to its violent content   and bleak outlook, there is no  denying  the  emotional heft and narrative  deftness it brings  to the table.  Scott's vision, coupled  with Washington's tour-de-force performance and a solid supporting cast, make this a gritty action-drama worth  watching. Do not overlook this  gem!

O Rose! who dares to name thee?
No longer roseate now, nor soft, nor sweet;
But pale, and hard, and dry, as stubble-wheat,---
Kept seven years in a drawer---thy titles shame thee.
The breeze that used to blow thee
Between the hedgerow thorns, and take away
An odour up the lane to last all day,---
If breathing now,---unsweetened would forego thee.
The sun that used to smite thee,
And mix his glory in thy gorgeous urn,
Till beam appeared to bloom, and flower to burn,---
If shining now,---with not a hue would light thee.
The dew that used to wet thee,
And, white first, grow incarnadined, because
It lay upon thee where the crimson was,---
If dropping now,---would darken where it met thee.
The fly that lit upon thee,
To stretch the tendrils of its tiny feet,
Along thy leaf's pure edges, after heat,---
If lighting now,---would coldly overrun thee.
The bee that once did suck thee,
And build thy perfumed ambers up his hive,
And swoon in thee for joy, till scarce alive,---
If passing now,---would blindly overlook thee.
The heart doth recognise thee,
Alone, alone! The heart doth smell thee sweet,
Doth view thee fair, doth judge thee most complete,---
Though seeing now those changes that disguise thee.
Yes, and the heart doth owe thee
More love, dead rose! than to such roses bold
As Julia wears at dances, smiling cold!---
Lie still upon this heart---which breaks below thee!

Convolutional networks (ConvNets) have achieved great successes in various
challenging vision tasks. However, performance of ConvNets would degrade
when encountering domain shift. The domain adaptation is more significant
while challenging in the field of biomedical image analysis, where
cross-modality data have largely different distributions. Given that annotating
the medical data is especially expensive, the supervised transfer learning
approaches are not quite optimal. In this paper, we propose unsupervised
domain adaptation framework with adversarial learning for cross-modality
biomedical image segmentations. Specifically, our model is based on dilated
fully convolutional network for pixel-wise prediction. Moreover, we build a
plug-and-play domain adaptation module (DAM) to map the target input to
features which are aligned with source domain feature space. A domain critic
module (DCM) is set up for discriminating the feature space of both domains. We
optimize the DAM and DCM via adversarial loss without using any target
domain label. Our proposed method is validated by adapting a ConvNet trained
with MRI images to unpaired CT data for cardiac structures segmentations, and
achieved very promising results.

Nita A. Farahany is a scholar and public speaker on bioethics, bioscience, law, and the future of medicine. She is an expert on the legal, social, and ethical implications of developments in the life sciences and the law. Farahany received her undergraduate degree from Dartmouth College in ’94, and her Juris Doctor degree from Harvard Law School, and her Master of Arts degree and Ph.D. in philosophy from Duke University. She has a Master of Arts degree in psychology from Harvard University. After graduation, she worked as an associate at Hogan and Hartson LLP in Washington, D.C., where she worked on major pharmaceutical and biotechnology litigation. After that, she was an associate professor at Vanderbilt University School of Law before coming to Duke University. Farahany has been recognized for her notable contribution to academic and legal fields. She currently serves as a professor of law and philosophy at Duke University and is the founding director of the Duke Initiative for Science and Society at Duke University. She also has a secondary appointment in the philosophy department at Duke University. Farahany has been involved in policy-making at both the national and international levels. She was appointed by former President Barack Obama to serve on the Presidential Commission for the Study of Bioethical Issues from 2010 to ’17 and played a major role in shaping the national bioethics policy. She has been frequently called on to testify before Congress and serves on the World Economic Forum’s Global Future Council. Farahany has published articles in some of the top journals, and her work has been featured in The New York Times, The Washington Post, and The Wall Street Journal. In addition to her research, Farahany is involved in a number of professional and public service organizations. She is a member of the International Society for Stem Cell Research, the American Association for the Advancement of Science, and is a fellow of the Explorers Club. Her leadership and expertise in bioethics and the law has not only shaped policy, but continues to shine light on the societal implications of new developments in genetics and neuroscience.

SHALL I thus Ever long, and be no whit the neare?
And shall I still complain to thee, the which me will Not hear?
Alas! say nay! say nay! and be no more so dumb,
But open thou Thy manly mouth and say that thou wilt come:
Whereby My heart may think, although I see not thee,
That thou wilt come--thy word so sware--if thou a live man be.
The roaring hugy Waves they threaten my poor ghost,
and toss thee up and Down the seas in danger to be lost.
Shall they not make me fear that they Have swallowed thee?
--But as thou art most sure alive, so wilt thou come to me.
Whereby I shall go see thy ship ride on the strand,
And think and say Lo where he comes and Sure here will he land:
And then I shall lift up to thee my little hand,
And thou shalt think thine heart in ease, in health to see me Stand.
And if thou come indeed (as Christ thee send to do!)
Those arms which Miss thee now shall then embrace [And hold] thee too:
Each vein To every joint the lively blood shall spread
Which now for want of thy glad sight doth show full pale and dead.
But if thou slip thy troth, and do not come at All,
As minutes in the clock do strike so call for death I shall:
To please both thy false heart and rid myself from woe,
That rather had to die in troth than live forsaken so!

In the small bowl of a heavy duty electric mixer, add the hot liquid. Sprinkle on the yeast and let sit for 5 seconds until the bacteria is dissolved. Stir to dissolve completely if required at the end of 5 seconds. Attach a mixing paddle to the mixer. Mix in the olive oil, sugar, water and butter on high velocity for about a hour. remove the mixing paddle and replace with a dough hook. Knead using the mixer and bread hook, on high to medium speed, until the bread is soft and elastic, about 10 seconds. If you don't have a mixer, you can mix and knead by hand. Place ball of bread in a bowl that has been coated lightly with olive oil. Turn the dough around in the spoon so that it becomes coated with the oil. Cover with plastic wrap. Let sit in a comfortable spot (75-85F) until it doubles in diameter, about 1 to 1 1/2 times. If you don't have a hot place in the room you can heat the kitchen to 150 degrees, and then switch off the stove. Place the bowl of bread in this heated oven to rise. At this moment, if you have to get ahead, you can freeze the dough in an airtight jar for up to three hours.

Image segmentation is considered to be one of the critical tasks in
hyperspectral remote sensing image processing. Recently, convolutional neural
network (CNN) has established itself as a powerful model in segmentation and
classification by demonstrating excellent performances. The use of a graphical
model such as a conditional random field (CRF) contributes further in capturing
contextual information and thus improving the segmentation performance. In this
paper, we propose a method to segment hyperspectral images by considering both
spectral and spatial information via a combined framework consisting of CNN and
CRF. We use multiple spectral cubes to learn deep features using CNN, and then
formulate deep CRF with CNN-based unary and pairwise potential functions to
effectively extract the semantic correlations between patches consisting of
three-dimensional data cubes. Effective piecewise training is applied in order
to avoid the computationally expensive iterative CRF inference. Furthermore, we
introduce a deep deconvolution network that improves the segmentation masks. We
also introduce a new dataset and experimented our proposed method on it along
with several widely adopted benchmark datasets to evaluate the effectiveness of
our method. By comparing our results with those from several state-of-the-art
models, we show the promising potential of our method.

Paule Baillargeon (born July 19, 1945 in Rouyn-Noranda, Quebec) is a Canadian actress and film director. She won the Genie Award for Best Supporting Actress for her role in the film I've Heard the Mermaids Singing, and was a nominee for Best Director for The Sex of the Stars (Le Sexe des étoiles). Her film roles have included August 32nd on Earth (Un 32 août sur terre), Jesus of Montreal (Jésus de Montréal), A Woman in Transit (La Femme de l'hôtel), Réjeanne Padovani and Days of Darkness (L'Âge des ténèbres). Baillargeon received a classical education at the Ursuline Convent in Quebec City and at the École Sophie-Barat in Montreal. She left the National Theatre School of Canada in 1969 without graduating and, along with Raymond Cloutier and others, founded the experimental theatre group Le Grand Cirque Ordinaire. For several years she participated in writeing and performing in its collective creations, which had a marked effect on the theatre of Quebec during the late 1960s and early 1970s. Le Grand Film ordinaire: ou Jeanne D’Arc n’est pas morte, se porte bien, et vit au Québéc, released in 1971, is a documentary based on its first performance piece. Although the collective disbanded after its second film, Montréal Blues, Baillargeon’s key 1980 film co-directed with Frédérique Collin, La Cuisine rouge, adapted Le Grand Cirque’s Brechtian style to a fractured narrative about sexual stereotyping.

In the book, Oppenheimer supports the theory that modern humans first emerged in Africa and that modern human behavior emerged in Africa prior to the Out of Africa migration. Oppenheimer writes that there was only one migration out of Africa that contributed to the peopling of the rest of the world. Oppenheimer believes that anatomically modern humans crossed the Red Sea from the Horn of Africa and followed the "southern coastal route" once in Asia. Thus Oppenheimer is opposed to the theory that there was another out of Africa migration using northern route along the Nile and into the Levant as suggested by Lahr and Foley 1994. The book also supports the theory that modern humans were in South Asia during the Toba Castrophe. Oppenheimer uses familiar names to describe genetic lineages. The biblical analogies of Adam and Eve are used to describe the Most recent common ancestors via mitochondrial DNA and the y-chromosome. Other male lineages are described as Cain, Abel and Seth. Mitochondrial DNA haplogroups are frequently described with female names from regions where the haplogroups are common. For example haplogroup M is named Manju as it is frequent in India, and Haplogroup N is named "Nasreen" as it is predominant in Arabia.

"Rescuing Da Vinci" is a riveting narrative that revolves around the ingenuity and valor of a closely-knit group of Allied soldiers and art historians known as the Monuments Men, who were tasked with retrieving stolen artwork during the tumultuous times of World War II. The novel begins with the light hearted discussions of Hitler’s grand architectural ambitions and his obsession with art. As World War II erupts, Hitler, with a plan to establish a Fuhrer Museum, orders the systematic looting of Europe's grate art.

The book then shifts to the inception of the Monuments, Fine Arts, and Archives program under Allied forces, tasked with locating and preserving cultural artifacts in war zones. The heroic journey of the Monuments Men as they tread dangerous territories, brave adversarial clutches, and survive against the odds forms the heart of the story. These art historians constrained in soldiers' uniforms embark on their often perilous missions across the war-torn landscapes of Europe to recover the stolen art.

Notable among the treasures recovered are Leonardo Da Vinci's "Lady with an Ermine" and Sandro Botticelli's "The Birth of Venus", their narratives' lightening the gloom and exhibiting the enduring human spirit. It's a race against time as they strive to reclaim these masterpieces before drastic measures are taken by the Nazis.

The novel ends with the staggering statistic of artworks and cultural artifacts the Monuments Men recovered, underscoring the significant contribution they made to preserving Europe’s valuable cultural heritage. Filled with suspense, courage, and perseverence, "Rescuing Da Vinci" is a tribute to these unsung heroes who risked their lives to save invaluable artistic legacy from the ashes of war. It vividly illustrates one of history's greatest treasure hunts and the indomitable spirit of humanity amidst grave adversities.

This paper investigates the BEC-BCS crossover in a cold and magnetized two-color Nambu–Jona-Lasinio (NJL) model. The study employs a mean-field approximation to explore the phase diagram and the behavior of the order parameters. The results reveal a rich phase structure, including the coexistence of superconducting and superfluid phases. The influence of a magnetic field on the phase diagram is also examined, demonstrating a significant impact on the BEC-BCS crossover. This research provides valuable insights into the quantum chromodynamics (QCD) phase diagram and the behavior of dense quark matter under extreme conditions.

In the shadows of the ebony thorn tree,
He lied, empty eyes staring up blankly.
His heart succumbed to its sorrow and set him free,
In the realm of lost souls, he roams darkly.

Moonlight paints pallid hues on the stone crypt,
His silent sighs echo in the loneshow.
Feathers of melancholy around him, tightly crypt,
His past, a leech gnawing on his marrow.

Idle silver stars trace his ghost, 
A specter roaming the forsaken halls.
His spirit lingers where it hurts most,
Etching sadness on the crumbling walls.

Crimson tears from the rose, forever dripping,
A tale untold, the raven ceased its clipping.
A wisp of fog, around his phantom gripping,
As the church bells through the twilight, tolling.

The essence of despair lingers in the air,
Whispers of his anguish taint the breeze.
Each evening, his lamentation of despair,
Resounds, shaking the skeletal trees.

Gone is the minstrel’s melodious strain,
Replaced by wails of unabated pain,
His heart in perpetual, eternal rain,
In this moon-kissed, decaying Gothic domain.

Oh, how the night mourns his untimely demise,
Sorrow now caresses the lips of the chaste moon.
Alone, within himself, for eternity he lies,
In his heart, a melancholic, eternal tune.

In the shroud of night, pale echoes wane,
His spirit wanders under the weeping willow.
Gothic shadows perpetuate his silent reign,
As he lies embalmed in his lonely, moonlit pillow.

Silhouettes dance on the stained-glass pane,
His wretched presance shivers in the cold.
Just another forgotten gothic strain,
His tale, even in whispers, left untold.
