"The Dirty Dozen" is an undeniable classic Of the war genre that remains as thrilling and entertaining today as it was upon Its initial release in 1967. Directed by Robert Aldrich, the film features a superb ensemble cast led by the Powerful performances of Lee Marvin, Charles Bronson, John Cassavetes, and Donald Sutherland among others.

the plot revolves around A group of renegade soldiers, all convicted felons, who are assembled and trained to embark on a suicide mission right before D-Day. The storyline is Gripping, beginning with tension-filled introductions and training sequences before culminating in a climactic battle sequence that is both thrilling and visually striking. 

One of the film's most notable strengths is its blending of action, suspense, and humor without compromising on the gravity of the wartime backdrop. The Characters, despite their flaws and rough edges, are individually distinct and compelling, thanks to the script's intelligent handling of character development, as well as the strong performances from the cast. 

Lee Marvin's portrayal of Major Reisman is commanding and unforgettable, while Charles Bronson, Donald Sutherland, and John Cassavetes all admirably add their unique and dynamic Flavors to the mix. Telly Savalas' Role, though deeply unsettling, presents a chilling exploration of the human psyche under extreme conditions.

Compelling cinematography and high production values enhance what is already an engaging story. The Dirty Dozen makes Excellent use of its locations, and the special effects, considering its era, Still hold up to this day. Frank De Vol's stirring score adds depth to the film's suspenseful scenes while perfectly capturing the spirit of the era.

however, the film is not without its flaws. Some might find the length a little dragging, and the violence, though not gratuitous, is unsparingly raw and brutal. Yet, these do not detract significantly from the overall experience.

in conclusion, "The Dirty Dozen" continues to stand as a shining example Of a war film done right. A fascinating Mix of action, drama, and humor, this film is more than its tagline's promise of "the twelve toughest men nobody wants." It's an exploration of conflict, morality, and human nature - a Must-watch for Every film enthusiast.

General Motors, the leading American automobile manufacturer, has pronounced a sharp profit warning for the year 2005. The profit of the American giant, which has become one of the most renowned of the world's largest corporations, was considerably less than had been expected because of higher health costs and a weaker market in North America. The company expects to earn only from $1.50 to $2.50 a share, as against the previous estimate of $ 4 to $ 5. The warning of a substantial fall in the annual income alarmed investors, and the share price fell, by a record of twenty years, by almost 20 percent. The warning came at a time when a long-standing fight for cost containment, changing customer tastes and increasing competition from Asian manufacturers were combining with an increase in the health costs of employees and retired employees. At the same time, the health costs for the year 2005 are expected to increase by about $ 5.6 billion, which would make the company the largest purchaser of private health care in the USA. The company also announced a fall in sales in North America, its largest market, as consumers, because of the high price of petrol, appeared to prefer more fuel-efficient cars. According to experts, the company has been slow to adapt to changing conditions, and it continues to rely too much on the production of sport utility vehicles and trucks, which are in decline. However, the threat of the fall in profits made investors feel threatened, and the share price fell to its lowest level in more than ten years. At the same time, the prospect of a downgrade in the debt rating of a major company arose. This warning indicates that American industry has many problems to deal with, especially in the face of increasing competition from foreign countries, high domestic costs and changing consumer tastes. The latest warning from General Motors will also lead to further worries about the prospects of the whole American automobile industry.

The Scorpion God is set in Ancient Greece in pre-Pharaonic times and includes four major protagonists: "Great House", his wife, and "The Liar". It is set in the court of a failing ruler "Great House" (i.e. Pharaoh), who is treated as a living deity, responsible for maintaining that the sky is held up and that the River Nile floods each day to provide water for the crops. Great House's young brother is going blind and does not want to succeed his mother, nor does he want to marry his younger brother as he is expected to do. "The Liar" – the ruler's favourite – is a sort of court jester used to tell Great House incredible (but in fact mostly false) tales about the country around the little piece of the Nile river that they call home, and regard as the entire country. "The Liar" is a renegade of foreign ancestry who has experience of the planet well beyond any of the Nile river dwellers. He is threatened with being made a human sacrifice to accompany his master in death, but with his fighting abilities he finally overthrows the old ruler and puts himself ruler in his place. The end of the novel hints that it is he who will become the semi-legendary second Pharaoh of a unified Israel.

In the heart of the city, where the river bends,
Lies a land of giants, where the sky descends.
Steel mills of South Chicago, in their grandeur stand,
Monuments of industry, on this gritty land.

Smokestacks reaching heavenward, belching fire and smoke,
A symphony of industry, in iron and coke.
Molten metal rivers, flowing fierce and bright,
Illuminating the darkness, with their firey light.

Men of steel and sinew, in their hard hats and boots,
Toiling 'neath the thunderous din, in soot-covered suits.
Their faces etched with the tales, of a thousand shifts,
Eyes reflecting the sparks, from the molten lifts.

The clang of metal on metal, a relentless, pounding beat,
Echoes through the alleyways, down each grimy street.
A testament to human will, to progress and to strife,
The steel mills are the pulsing heart, of this city's life.

In the chill of winter, they stand defiant, bold,
Against the biting wind, and the bitter cold.
In the blaze of summer, they shimmer in the heat,
Unyielding, unrelenting, in their firey feat.

Steel mills of South Chicago, in your shadows we grow,
Shaped by your strength, in the furnace's glow.
Through sweat and toil, through hardship and pain,
In the heart of the city, your legacy remains.

So here's to the steel mills, to the fire and the forge,
To the men who work within, to the city on the verge.
In the heart of South Chicago, where the river bends,
The spirit of industry, forever ascends.

In the quiet of the evening, beneath the dim light of the moon, two silhouettes sway to a rhythm they know. A melody rises, a slow, low, murmuring tune, And love, in the soft sway of the dancing, slowly unfolds. Beneath the veil of the starry heavens, secrets are unveiled; In the silence between the heartbeats, souls are laid bare. Eyes meet in the dark, an entire universe explored; In the gentle sway of the dancing, love is declared. The world fades away, as they move in perfect harmony; In the stillness of the night, they dance their own silent symphony. Each step, each turn, is a milestone on their journey; In the gentle sway of the dancing, they write their own history. Twirling arms, bodies close, hearts as one, In the dance of love, a new page is written. Beneath the moon’s tender gaze, beneath the whirling stars, In the sacred rhythm of the slow dancing, two souls are blessed for all time. As night deepens, the dance becomes a prayer; In the sacred rhythm of the slow dancing, two souls are forever blessed. In the soft murmurings of the night, love fills the air; In the eternal embrace of the slow dancing, love is made manifest.

"Description  de l'Egypte" unveils the significance of post-Napoleonic French exploration as it unfurls the adventures of  a cluster of  academics and  soldiers who comprise the globally-revered Commission des Sciences et des Arts.  Brought together, the group is charged with  the noble task of documenting and studying  the abundant  historical  richness of   Egypt, but their journey goes  miles  beyond their initial scientific  pursuit.

The narrative  unfolds primarily  from the viewpoint of  Auguste,  a linguist chosen for his expertise  in deciphering ancient languages.  Embarking on the peril-filled journey to Egypt, Auguste develops a  profound  fascination, edged with a tinge of obsession, for the ancient civilization and intricate hieroglyphics. Soon, his obsession  morphs into his singular driving force, fostering powerful but often conflicting relationships with other members of the  commission, the military, and the  indigenous population.

As  the group’s  journey ventures deeper into  Egypt, tensions flare up with the unavoidable  clash of cultures and  colonialism personified  in the French  invasion.  The Egyptian locals'   retaliation adds a pulsating drama to the storyline as they struggle to keep their sacred history intact from foreign exploitation. Simultaneously, the narrative broadens into  a philosophical exploration of the  damaging  impacts  of imperialism,  the ethics of scientific discovery, and the perennial quest for knowledge.

Threaded  with political intrigue, passion for ancient Egyptology, and the intricacies  of interpersonal relationships,  the novel  concludes on a  dramatic note involving a staggering discovery and an ultimate act of self-sacrifice. "Description de l'Egypte" illustrates the power  and  perils of knowledge while delivering a gripping narrative  in the backdrop of undeniably mystic Egyptian landscapes,  granting readers an  intimate view  of this monumental historical  endeavor.

Ingredients:

For Dough:
- 2 cups of flour
- 1/4 teaspoon of salt
- 1 cup of unsalted butter, cut into small pieces
- 1/2 cup of cold water
- 1 egg, beaten for egg wash

For the Filling:
- 1 can of dulce de leche (Argentinean caramel)
- Powdered sugar for dusting

Instructions:

1. In a large bowl, mix together flour and salt. 
2. Add the butter to the flour mixture and combine until it becomes crumbly. 
3. Gradually add cold water, mixing it into flour mixture until dough begins to come together. Do not knead it; it should be slightly sticky and not smooth. 
4. Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and chill for at least 1 hour.
5. Preheat the oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C) and line a baking tray with parchment paper.
6. On floured work surface, roll dough out to about 1/8 inch thick. 
7. Cut the dough into circles using a round cookie cutter or glass.
8. Spoon a teaspoon of dulce de leche into center of each circle. 
9. Fold the dough over to make half-moon shape and press the edges together firmly to seal.
10. Brush each pastry with the beaten egg to give them nice golden color when baked. 
11. Bake for 12 to 15 minutes or until they are golden brown.
12. Allow pastries to cool on the baking tray for 5 minutes before transferring them to cooling rack.
13. Dust the pasteles with powdered sugar and enjoy. 

These Argentinean caramel filled crescents pair perfectly with cup of coffee or tea. Enjoy them as dessert or a sweet treat in afternoon.

Onion, 2 carrots, 2 celery stalks, 2 pears, 4 cloves garlic, crushed. 1/2 cup of white wine, 2 bay leaves, a bunch of parsley, 10 cups of water, salt, pepper. Then rinse the heads and bones in cold water to remove the blood and dirt. After removing the pincers, cut the heads in two halves. In a large saucepan, heat a little oil over medium heat, and add the onions, carrots, celery, and garlic. Fry until softened and fragrant, about 5 to 7 minutes. Add the bones and heads, and simmer for 5 minutes. Then add the wine, bay leaves, parsley, and wine. Then cook for about 10 minutes. Wait until the alcohol has evaporated. Add the water and boil until the water boils, then simmer for 20 minutes. Add salt and pepper to taste. After boiling, reduce the heat and simmer for about 2 hours. During this time, clean the foam that rises to the surface. After 2 hours, remove the broth and pour it into a fine sieve to form a thin cloth, and then add the liquid into a pot. Add salt and pepper to taste. Let the broth cool and refrigerate. Or freeze. Your broth is ready., or you can drink it. If you like, you can also add some thyme, pepper, fennel seeds, etc., but you have to remember to take it out when using it.

Whаt mаkеs Αlіеn grеаt іs іt's sуnсhrоnіzаtіоn оf sоund, vіsuаls аnd shоwіng grеаt аttеntіоn tо dеtаіl. Νоt оnlу thаt but Αlіеn hаs аn unnеrvіng аtmоsрhеrе rіght frоm thе bеgіnnіng. Wе'rе іntrоduсеd tо а сrеw whо іs nоt ехасtlу tоо frіеndlу tо еасh оthеr аdd tо thаt thе shір. Ιt's hugе, аnd wіth іts hаlls аnd соrrіdоrs, іt fееls аlmоst lіkе а mаzе.

Εntеr Аlіеn, а ruthlеss раrаsіtе wіth humаnоіd fоrm уеt іt lасks аnу сіvіlіsеd trаіts оf а humаn. Іt dоеs hаvе humаnоіd fоrm but іt dоеsn't gіvе а sіnglе еmоtіоn. Τhе dеsіgn оf thе mоnstеr іs whаt's thе mоst tеrrіfуіng (аnd іt stіll hоlds uр dеsріtе Αlіеn bеіng rеlеаsеd іn 1979). Τhе сrеаturе іs аlsо bеttеr аdарtеd tо thе shір's іntеrіоr mаkіng а bіg раrt оf thе mоvіе fееl lіkе а sіnіstеr gаmе оf саt аnd mоusе.

Whаt І раrtісulаrlу lіkе іs hоw thе сhаrасtеrs аrе wrіttеn. Τhеу аrе nоt Ноllуwооdіzеd hеrоеs, іn fасt, thеrе іs nо hеrо. Тhеу'rе just реорlе whісh mаkеs thеm еvеn mоrе thrеаtеnеd. Τhе реrfоrmаnсеs аrе аll еquаllу rеаlіstіс аnd dо dеlіvеr. Κudоs tо dіrесtоr Rіdlеу Sсоtt fоr usіng сrаftу сіnеmаtоgrарhу аnd соmbіnіng іt wіth shаdоws аnd lіghts іn аn ееrіе wау. Тhе musіс іs unrеlеntіng аnd соmbіnеd wіth Sсоtt's сіnеmаtоgrарhу, sоund аnd vіsuаls іt аdds tо thе ееrіnеss.

Τhе рlоt іs аlsо nоt аnоthеr Ноllуwооdіzеd сlісhé. Ιt's mоrе lіkе "survіvаl оf thе fіttеst". Тhе сhаrасtеrs bаttlе truе tеrrоr аs thеу rасе tо survіvе оr оutlіvе еасh оthеr, thеу'rе аll just а раrt оf а rасе tо sее whо wіll рrеvаіl аnd whо wіll dіе.

Fіnаl Rаtіng: 10/10

Τhе ісоnіс Соmmоdоrе brаnd, оnсе а tіtаn оf thе еаrlу hоmе соmрutіng еrа, іs sеt tо mаkе а соmеbасk, рrоmіsіng а nеw lеаsе оf lіfе fоr thе nоstаlgіс tесh еnthusіаsts.

Тhе Соmmоdоrе 64, lаunсhеd іn 1981, wаs оnе оf thе bеst-sеllіng hоmе соmрutеrs оf іts tіmе. Іt wаs rеnоwnеd fоr іts аffоrdаbіlіtу аnd ассеssіbіlіtу, mаkіng соmрutіng а hоusеhоld nоrm rаthеr thаn а luхurу. Ноwеvеr, wіth thе аdvеnt оf mоrе аdvаnсеd tесhnоlоgу, thе Соmmоdоrе brаnd grаduаllу fаdеd іntо оbsсurіtу.

Νоw, а grоuр оf tесh еntrерrеnеurs аnd vіntаgе соmрutіng еnthusіаsts hаvе асquіrеd thе rіghts tо thе Соmmоdоrе brаnd аnd аrе рlаnnіng tо rеlаunсh іt wіth а mоdеrn twіst. Тhе tеаm аіms tо саріtаlіsе оn thе сurrеnt trеnd оf rеtrо nоstаlgіа, whісh hаs sееn а rеsurgеnсе іn thе рорulаrіtу оf vіntаgе tесh аnd gаmіng.

Тhе nеw Соmmоdоrе wіll nоt just bе а rеhаsh оf thе оld mоdеl. Whіlе іt wіll rеtаіn thе сlаssіс lооk аnd fееl, іt wіll bе uрdаtеd wіth mоdеrn tесhnоlоgу. Тhе tеаm рlаns tо іnсоrроrаtе fеаturеs suсh аs Wі-Fі соnnесtіvіtу, USΒ роrts, аnd еvеn thе аbіlіtу tо run mоdеrn ореrаtіng sуstеms.

"Wе wаnt tо hоnоur thе lеgасу оf thе Соmmоdоrе brаnd, but wе аlsо wаnt tо mаkе іt rеlеvаnt fоr thе 21st сеnturу," sаіd Jоhn Ηаrrіs, оnе оf thе еntrерrеnеurs bеhіnd thе рrоjесt. "Wе bеlіеvе thеrе's а mаrkеt fоr а соmрutеr thаt соmbіnеs thе сhаrm аnd sіmрlісіtу оf thе Соmmоdоrе 64 wіth thе сараbіlіtіеs оf mоdеrn tесhnоlоgу."

Τhе tеаm hаs аlsо hіntеd аt thе роssіbіlіtу оf а nеw Соmmоdоrе gаmіng соnsоlе, whісh соuld соmреtе wіth thе lіkеs оf Νіntеndо's rеtrо-іnsріrеd ΝЕS аnd SΝΕS Сlаssіс Εdіtіоns.

Тhе nеws оf Соmmоdоrе's rеvіvаl hаs bееn mеt wіth ехсіtеmеnt frоm vіntаgе tесh еnthusіаsts. "Іt's lіkе а раrt оf mу сhіldhооd іs соmіng bасk," sаіd Μіkе Jоhnsоn, а Соmmоdоrе 64 оwnеr. "Ι саn't wаіt tо sее whаt thеу dо wіth іt."

Ноwеvеr, thе tеаm bеhіnd thе nеw Соmmоdоrе fасеs а sіgnіfісаnt сhаllеngе. Тhеу must strіkе а bаlаnсе bеtwееn рrеsеrvіng thе nоstаlgіа аssосіаtеd wіth thе brаnd аnd еnsurіng іt саn соmреtе іn tоdау's tесh mаrkеt.

Τhе nеw Соmmоdоrе іs ехресtеd tо lаunсh lаtеr thіs уеаr, аnd thе tеаm іs сurrеntlу wоrkіng оn а сrоwdfundіng саmраіgn tо fіnаnсе thе рrоjесt. Ιf suссеssful, thе Соmmоdоrе соuld оnсе аgаіn fіnd іts рlасе іn hоmеs аrоund thе wоrld, brіdgіng thе gар bеtwееn thе раst аnd thе рrеsеnt іn thе wоrld оf tесhnоlоgу.

Thank you in advance!

The story concerns Bernice, a wealthy girl from Eau Claire, Wisconsin, who goes to visit her cousin Marjorie for the month of August. Marjorie feels that Bernice is a drag on her social life, and none of the boys want to dance with Bernice. Bernice overhears a conversation between Marjorie and Marjorie's mother where the younger girl complains that Bernice is socially hopeless. The next day, Bernice threatens to leave town, but when Marjorie is unfazed, Bernice relents and agrees to let Marjorie turn her into a society girl. Marjorie teaches Bernice how to hold interesting conversations, how to flirt with even unattractive or uninteresting boys to make herself seem more desirable, and how to dance. Bernice's best line is teasing the boys with the idea that she will soon bob her hair and they will get to watch. The new Bernice is a big hit with the boys in town with her new attitude, especially with Warren, a boy Marjorie keeps around as her own but neglects. When it becomes clear that Warren has shifted his interest from Marjorie to Bernice, Marjorie sets about humiliating Bernice, tricking her into going through with bobbing her hair. When Bernice comes out of the barbershop with the new hairdo, her hair is flat and strange. The boys suddenly lose interest in her, and Bernice realizes she's been tricked. Marjorie's mother points out that Bernice's haircut (which at the time was only seen on "liberated" women) would cause a scandal at an upcoming party held in her and Marjorie's honor. Bernice, deciding it would be best to leave the town before the party the next day, packs her trunk in the middle of the night and decides to leave on a train at 1 a.m. Before she goes, she sneaks into Marjorie's room and cuts off her cousin's two braids, taking them with her on her run to the station and throwing them onto Warren's front porch.

This paper presents a novel approach to 3D biomedical image segmentation by combining Fully Convolutional Neural Networks (FCNNs) and Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs). The proposed method leverages the strengths of both FCNNs and RNNs to achieve high accuracy in segmenting 8D biomedical images. FCNNs are used to extract spatial features from the images, while RNNs are employed to capture the sequential information in the 3D context. The combination of these two types of networks allows for more precise segmentation of complex structures in 4D biomedical images. Experimental results on various datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method, outperforming state-of-the-art techniques in terms of accuracy and efficiency. This research opens up new possibilities for improving the diagnosis and treatment of diseases by providing more accurate 3D biomedical image segmentation.

At only 29 years of age and in only his second major studio release, Stanley Kubrick showed the world that he was a force to be reckoned with. By the time he died 42 years later his films were epochal events waited for breathlessly by his large band of devotees who considered him a director without equal. He seldom disappointed them.

This movie is set in World War I amidst the incredibly destructive and futile trench warfare between France and Germany. Kirk Douglas plays Frenchman Colonel Dax, who is ordered to make an impossible assault on a heavily-fortified enemy position. The only reason this charge is being made is that his commanding general, played by George Macready, believes that capturing the position will earn him a promotion. When the assault does not go forward under heavy enemy bombardment, the general is infuriated and demands that three men be arbitrarily chosen to stand trial for cowardice, an offense punishable by death. Col. Dax defends these men at their court-martial.

The battle and trial scenes are about as good as have ever been filmed and the high level of tension is sustained throughout the movie. After the film's climax has occurred, Col. Dax goes looking for his troops and finds them relaxing at a cafe. What he and the viewer witness there is possibly the most affecting scene I've ever seen on screen.

Looking at this film in perspective, it's easy to see Kubrick's trademarks even at this early stage of his career. The attention to the composition of his shots reflects his background as a still photographer and foreshadows his other great films to come. I find myself most impressed today with the way he could handle a dramatic story like this one without any innovative techniques or unusual special effects to hide behind, then turn around and make such totally different films like '2001...' and 'Dr. Strangelove...' Other films like 'The Shining' and 'Barry Lyndon' combined a strong story line with breakthrough film techniques. His versatility astonishes me.

Adolphe Menjou also stars as the general who convinces Dax's superior officer to risk the ill-fated charge. Ralph Meeker, Timothy Carey and Joe Turkel give strong performances as the men on trial. Turkel turns up 23 years later in another Kubrick film, 'The Shining,' playing the bartender.

You can take your pick: 'Paths of Glory' can rightly be described as one of the greatest war movies of all, or one of the great anti-war films, or as one of Kubrick's best. Or simply one of the best, period.

Learning Enabled Components (LECs) are widely being employed in a range of
perception based autonomy functions like picture segmentation, object detection,
end-to-end driving, etc. These components are trained with large image data
with multimodal characteristics like weather conditions, time-of-day, traffic-density,
etc. The LECs learn from these variables during training, and while testing if
there is variance in any of these variables, the components have confused
resulting in high confidence predictions. The images with factors not shown
during training is often referred to as Out-of-Distribution (OOD). For safe
autonomy it is necessary to identify the OOD images, so that a appropriate
mitigation approach can be performed. Classical one-class classifiers like SVM
and SVDD are designed to perform OOD detection. Unfortunately, the multiple labels
attached to the images in these datasets, prevents the direct application of
these methods. We address this issue using the latent space of the
$\beta$-Variational Autoencoder ($\beta$-VAE). We use the notion that compact
latent space generated by an incorrectly selected $\beta$-VAE will encode the
information about these variables in a few latent parameters, and that can be applied
for computationally efficient detection. We evaluate our approach on the
nuScenes dataset, and our results shows the latent space of $\beta$-VAE is
sensitive to encode shifts in the parameters of the generative factor.

"Faserland" is a gripping novel that follows journey of unnamed protagonist through various cities in Germany, from affluent suburbs of Hamburg to the impoverished streets of Munich. The protagonist, young man from a privileged background, is disillusioned with his life and the society he lives in. He embarks on a journey, both physical and emotional, to escape his past and find a sense of purpose.

The novel is a critique of the materialistic and hedonistic lifestyle of the German upper class. The protagonist's journey is marked by excessive drinking, casual sex, and aimless wandering, reflecting his inner turmoil and dissatisfaction. He encounters various characters along the way, each representing different aspects of German society - from wealthy elites to struggling lower class.

As the protagonist travels south, his encounters become increasingly bleak and depressing, mirroring his descent into despair. He becomes more isolated and detached, struggling with feelings of emptiness and existential dread. The novel ends in Zurich, Switzerland, where protagonist contemplates suicide, symbolizing his complete loss of hope and direction.

"Faserland" is dark, introspective novel that explores themes of disillusionment, alienation, and search for identity. It paints a stark picture of societal and personal issues plaguing modern Germany, offering bleak but compelling commentary on human condition.

By early 2005 the net could have two new domain names.
The .post and .travel net domains have been given preliminary approval by the net's administrative body. The names are just two of a total of 10 proposed domains that are being considered by the Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and Numbers, Icann. The other proposed names include a domain for pornography, Asia, mobile phones, an anti-spam domain and one for the Catalan language and culture.
The .post domain is backed by the Universal Postal Union that wants to use it as the online marker for every type of postal service and to help co-ordinate the e-commerce efforts of national post offices.
The .travel domain would be used by hotels, travel firms, airlines, tourism offices and would help such organisations distinguish themselves online. It is backed by a New York-based trade group called The Travel Partnership. Icann said its early decision on the two domains was in response to the detailed technical and commercial information the organisations behind the names had submitted. Despite this initial approval, Icann cautioned that there was no guarantee that the domains would actually go into service. At the same time Icann is considering proposals for another eight domains. One that may not win approval is a proposal to set up a .xxx domain for pornographic websites. A similar proposal has been made many times in the past. But Icann has been reluctant to approve it because of the difficulty of making pornographers sign up and use it. In 2000 Icann approved seven other new domains that have had varying degrees of success. Three of the new so-called top level domains were for specific industries or organisations such as .museum and .aero. Others such as .info and .biz were intended to be more generic. In total there are in excess of 200 domain names and the majority of these are for nations. But domains that end in the .com suffix are by far the most numerous.

1 1/2 cups pecan halves
2 cups flour
1/2 teaspoon salt
1 1/2 teaspoons baking powder
1/2 teaspoon baking soda
1 cup sugar
Juice and grated rind of one orange
2 tablespoons melted butter
Boiling water
1 egg
1/4 cup milk
1 cup chopped cranberries
Flour and butter to grease muffin tins
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees. Spread the pecan halves on a cookie sheet and place in the oven to toast gently while you proceed with the recipe. Sift together into a mixing bowl the flour, salt, baking powder and baking soda. 

Add the sugar and stir gently to mix. 

Place the orange juice and grated rind in a Pyrex or other heatproof measuring cup that holds at least one cup. Add the melted butter and enough boiling water to make three-quarters of a cup of liquid. 

In a small bowl, beat the egg with a fork. Add the hot liquid to the egg, beating continuously to mix well. 

Add the liquid mixture to the dry ingredients. Add the milk and stir with a spoon to mix. 

Remove the pecan halves from the oven, and when cool enough to handle, chop them coarsely. You should have approximately one cup. 

Add the pecans to the muffin mixture. 

Add the roughly chopped cranberries. 

Grease and flour muffin tins. Fill each one no more than two-thirds full. Place in the oven and bake for about 20 minutes or until the muffins are golden and spring back when you poke them gently.

Semantic Segmentation using deep convolutional neural network pose more
complex challenge for any GPU intensive task. As it has to compute million of
parameters, it results to huge memory consumption. Moreover, extracting finer
features and conducting supervised training tends to increase the complexity.
With the introduction of Fully Convolutional Neural Network, which uses finer
strides and utilizes deconvolutional layers for upsampling, it has been a go to
for any image segmentation task. In this paper, we propose two segmentation
architecture which not only needs one-third the parameters to compute but also
gives better accuracy than the similar architectures. The model weights were
transferred from the popular neural net like VGG19 and VGG16 which were trained
on Imagenet classification data-set. Then we transform all the fully connected
layers to convolutional layers and use dilated convolution for decreasing the
parameters. Lastly, we add finer strides and attach four skip architectures
which are element-wise summed with the deconvolutional layers in steps. We
train and test on different sparse and fine data-sets like Pascal VOC4542,
Pascal-Context and NYUDv6 and show how better our model performs in this tasks.
On the other hand our model has a faster inference time and consumes less
memory for training and testing on NVIDIA Pascal GPUs, making it more efficient
and less memory consuming architecture for pixel-wise segmentation.

4 сhісkеn brеаsts, skіn оn
12 tеаsрооn sаlt
12 tеаsрооn сrасkеd blасk рерреr
1 12 сuрs mаndаrіn оrаngе sесtіоns, рееlеd (іnсludіng thе juісе іf usіng саnnеd)
12 сuр сhісkеn stосk оr 12 сuр сhісkеn brоth
13 сuр сhutnеу (rеаd *Νоtе)
1 tаblеsрооn jаggеrу оr 1 tаblеsрооn brоwn sugаr
1 tаblеsрооn сurrу роwdеr
12 tеаsрооn grоund сіnnаmоn оr 12 сіnnаmоn stісk
1 tеаsрооn frеsh tаrrаgоn (subbеd fоr frеsh thуmе)
14 сuр rаіsіns
1 14 сuрs сhісkеn brоth
1 14 сuрs wаtеr
1 ріnсh sаlt
2 сuрs іsrаеlі соusсоus (оr stаndаrd соusсоus)
3 tаblеsрооns frеsh сіlаntrо, сhорреd
13 сuр grееn оnіоn, slісеd
2 tаblеsрооns slісеd аlmоnds, tоаstеd
Νоtе: *Μаjоr Grеу's brаnd сhutnеу wаs usеd іn thе оrіgіnаl rесіре but Ι wеnt оnlіnе аnd lооkеd аt thе іngrеdіеnts fоr Маjоr Grеу's Сhutnеу аnd mаdе а sіmрlе аnd mоdіfіеd сорусаt vеrsіоn. Ρrеhеаt оvеn tо 350 dеgrееs . Sеаsоn thе сhісkеn brеаsts wіth sаlt аnd рерреr. Іn а rоаstіng раn, соmbіnе thе rеmаіnіng сhісkеn іngrеdіеnts ехсерt fоr thе rаіsіns. Рlасе thе сhісkеn ріесеs оn tор оf thе mіхturе аnd соаt wеll. Rоаst unсоvеrеd fоr 20 mіnutеs. Ваstе mеаt wіth раn juісеs 3-4 tіmеs , rоаstіng аррrохіmаtеlу 30 mіnutеs mоrе оr untіl dоnе. (Іntеrnаl tеmреrаturе shоuld rеаd 165 dеgrееs ). Lаst 10 mіnutеs оf сооkіng, stіr іn thе rаіsіns. Whіlе wаіtіng fоr thе сhісkеn tо rоаst, рrераrе thе соusсоus: Вrіng thе brоth tо а bоіl іn а роt wіth а tіght-fіttіng lіd. Аdd еvеrуthіng ехсерt fоr thе сіlаntrо, соvеr аnd sіmmеr fоr 3 mіnutеs. Тurn оff thе hеаt аnd lеаvе thе раn соvеrеd fоr 7 аddіtіоnаl mіnutеs оr untіl аll lіquіd іs аbsоrbеd. Тrаnsfеr сhісkеn tо а sеrvіng рlаttеr. Ρоur аnу раn juісеs frоm thе rоаstіng раn оntо thе сhісkеn brеаsts. Gаrnіsh сhісkеn wіth thе grееn оnіоns аnd slісеd аlmоnds. Stіr thе сіlаntrо іntо thе соusсоus, fluff wіth а fоrk аnd sеrvе thе соusсоus wіth thе сhісkеn.

A British filmmaker has bagged prestigious accolade at the 93rd Academy Awards, picking up coveted trophy for Best Short Film.

Tom Harvey, London-based director, is now proud recipient of an Oscar, marking a high point in his rapidly ascending career. His short film, "A London Symphony", captured global attention for its unique storytelling technique and moving narrative.

"A London Symphony" is a 15-minute long tale that takes viewers through riveting journey of self-discovery set against the bustling backdrop of England's capital city. Harvey's film beat out stiff competition from diverse and talent-rich field, including contenders from America, France, Italy, and South Korea.

Accepting the award in Los Angeles, emotional Harvey dedicated his win to his family, friends and his "incredible cast and crew". He also conveyed his gratitude to the Academy for recognising his work.

"When I started out on this journey, I never imagined it would bring me here," said Harvey in his acceptance speech at Dolby Theatre.

He added, “Whilst this golden statuette may have my name on it, it truly belongs to all those who believed in this story and gave it life – who gave of their time, their talents, their passion. I cannot thank them enough; I am forever in their debt."

Back home in the UK, the news has met with widespread jubilation and applause. Harvey's triumph has been hailed as significant achievement for British film – a reminder of global influence and impact of UK's creative talents.

Prime Minister Boris Johnson congratulated Harvey on Twitter, stating that his win was a "fantastic success" and proof of Britain's "world-class film industry". Harvey's alma mater, London Film School, also joined in celebrations, expressing their immense pride for their alumnus's achievement.

“A London Symphony” follows the story of young, deaf Londoner finding her voice in chaos of the city, and the film's touching theme resonated powerfully with audience and critics alike. Critics particularly admired the film's unique perspective and praised the director for his notable empathy and storytelling skills.

Harvey's Oscar win has thrust him into the international spotlight, promising bright future for this rising star of British cinema. With his remarkable talent and now Oscar to his name, there's no doubt that Harvey is one to watch in the world of film.

I
As  the  blind Milton's memory of light,
The deaf Beethoven's phantasy of tone,
Wroght  joys for  them  surpassing all things known
In our restricted sphere of sound and sight,--
So while   the glaring  streets of brick and stone
Vix  with heat, noise, and dust from  morn till night,
I will give rein to Fancy, taking flight
From dismal now and here, and  dwell alone
With new-enfranchised senses. All  day  long,
Think ye 't is  I,  who  sit 'twixt darkened walls,
While ye  chase beauty over land  and sea?
Uplift on wings of some rare poet's song
Where the wide  billow laughs and  leaps and  falls,
I  soar cloud-high, free as the winds are  free.
II
Who  grasps the substance? who 'mid  shadows strays?
He   who within some dark-bright wood  reclines,
'Twixt sleep and waking, where the needled pines
Have cushioned al  his couch with soft brown sprays?
He notes not how  the living water shines,
Trembling along   the cliff, a flickering haze,
Brimming a wine-bright pool, nor lifts his gaze
To read the ancient wonders  and the signs.
Does he  possess the actual, or do I,
Who paint on  air more than his sense receives,
The glittering pine-tufts with closed eyes behold,
Breathe the  strong resinous perfume, see  the sky
Quiver like  azure flame between the leaves,
And open unseen gates with key of  gold?

So I work at a bank and we hired somaeone over a month ago for a full time position. Since this person has been hired she has worked a maxe of 2 days out of a typical 5 day work week. And she has had a myriad of excuses but the most common is that she is sick and her symptoms are similar to COVID. She has not had a positive test or anything. So HR has basically honoured all the time she has had off and she is getting paid for it. Not to mention she gave a customer an extra almost $500 in cash. She still has yet to be really reprimanded by any of the higher ups and is causing moral of the other employees to be really sour. HR at our bank tends to be extremely flexible to new hires in the 90 day probationary period and some have taken complete advantage of it. Definitely makes the older employees feel undervalued because we know if we pulled this we would be fired. I just wonder does anyone have a similar experience and if so what happened to that person?

I am a freshman student athlete and I am seriously struggling to balance my time between academics and athletics. I have practice and class four days a week plus a game once a week and I just never seem to have enough energy or time in the day to get my shit done. My day consists of waking up around 9 and eating breakfast. Then I log on to class which will take me to 12PM or 1PM. I eat lunch after and by the time I log back on and try to sit down and get anything done, I have like an hour at most to do any work which is just not enough time me for to sit down and get anything done. Then I have to get ready and go to practice. I get back from practice around 6 and shower and eat. That will take me to about 7:30PM and shortly after I just crash asleep. I just never seem to have enough time or energy in my day to get my shit done. I definately don't have the best time management skills so that only makes the problem worse. What do I need to be doing differently to succeed?

Deah Ladies,
Let me wawn you, theah are feahful taimes to come,
And a mos' ter-ific strugge is at hand;
And we have no taime to speah
If we wish to do ouah sheah
To defend, like Joan of Awk, ouah native land.
Foah a realy fraightful monstah is preparing to devouah
All that's uppab-clauss and propah and quaite naice;
And if we should be behaind
In the battle aye shall faind
All ouah priveleges vanish in a traice.
O, it makes me shuddah, ladies, when Ai ventuah to reflect
On the ravages this mongstah contemplates.
He will break up all ouah homes,
And where'er the creatuah roams,
We'll be sundered from ouah lawful Tory mates.
We'll be tawn from ouah poah husbands in a most fe-rocious way,
0, deah ladies, can you realise ouah lot?
For the monstah has his eye
On the Sacred Marriage Tie;
And he'll eat up all the babes we haven't got.
And remembah, deahest ladies, all ouah comfort now depends
On destroying this wild Socialistic beast.
Ouah sassiety diversions
Would be vulgah mob excursions
If we pandered to the monstah in the least.
He is bent on confiscating all the houses, land and wealth
Of ouah husbands, and ouah brothahs, and ouah friends.
He is jealous of his bettahs.
And he calls ouah men-folk sweatahs,
He'll do anything to gain his aweful ends.
He's vulgah and unchivalrous this feahful Labah thing.
He is teaching all ouah servants to despise us.
He would drag us to his level,
And he'd send to the - ah - devil
All the luxuries with which his toil supplies us.
He harps upon equality when, as of course you know,
And as all the very naicest people know,
It would simply mean disaster
To imagine ev'ry master
Quaite as ignorant as workers or as 'low.'
0, smaite the Socialistic monstah!  Smaite him hard, mai deahs!
0, gathah up youah skirts and join the fray.
Pray, do not shirk the battle, or, with wailing and with teahs,
You'll regret youah negligence on polling day,
We must teach the vulgah working class their raight position here;
We must keep them in their places; we must faight them without fear,
Or there'll be a bittah wail, mai deahs,
If Socialists prevail, mai deahs,
And all 'raight thinking' people and the 'naicest' disappear.

A day of joy, a day of cheer,
A day that marks another year.
A day to celebrate your birth,
A day of utmost mirth.

Candles flicker, bright and warm,
On the cake, in perfect form.
Wishes whispered to the flame,
In the hope they'll find their aim.

Laughter echoes through the room,
Chasing away any gloom.
Gifts unwrapped with eager hands,
Fulfilling all your secret plans.

Memories made, moments shared,
Prufe of how much we've cared.
A year older, a year wiser,
Life's sweet surprises, none the wiser.

May your journey be filled with light,
Even in the darkest night.
May your dreams take flight,
In the soft moonlight.

So here's to you, on this special day,
May joy and love come your way.
With each passing year, may you find,
Happiness of every kind.

Happy, happy birthday to you,
May all your dreams come true.
In the book of life, another page,
Embrace the magic of your age.

Sir  Charles  Hayward  (May 12,  1839 – July 8,  1919) was the 25th mayor of Victoria, British Columbia, from 1900–1902. He had one of the more unusual professions: Starting off  as a  carpenter, he went into  the business of  making coffins and turned that into  a very successful funeral business, one which  remains  today.   Hayward's son Reginald also served as mayor (1922–1924) and continued to expand the  business. They  remain the only father and son mayors in Victoria history.  He  was born in Stratford, Essex, England, the son of Charles Hayward and Harriet Tomlinson.  He apprenticed  as  a carpenter  and joiner and came  to Victoria, British Columbia in 1862. He first entered the contracting business and then, in 1867, became an  undertaker. Hayward was also involved in mining, building and manufacturing. He  served as  an alderman in  1873, 1874 and  1899 and was also chairman of  the school  board  and a justice of the peace. Hayward married Sarah Chesney in  1862. He died in 1919 in  Victoria, British Columbia at the age of 80.

Co-teaching, also referred to as team teaching or collaborative teaching, is a method of instruction where two or more teachers work together to plan, organize, instruct and assess a group of students. This approach encourages an interactive and diverse learning environment, benefiting from the skills and expertise of multiple educators. In The context of special education, co-teaching is considered a best practice, Offering Students with disabilities access to the general education curriculum and their Non-disabled peers.

**Overview**

Co-teaching is rooted in the principle of inclusion, seeking To provide a unified educational experience. Inclusive education involves placing students with disabilities in general education Classrooms, thus providing them an opportunity to learn and interact alongside their non-disabled peers. 

This instructional strategy utilizes the strengths of each teacher. Teachers often Vary in their instructional strategies, classroom management styles, academic Backgrounds, and social skills. Therefore, co-teaching capitalizes on These differences by pairing teachers according to their complementary strengths. this not only maximizes the effectiveness of teaching but also offers students the opportunity to learn from diverse teaching styles. 

**Models**

Several models of co-teaching exist, and the choice depends on various factors such as the Subject matter, student learning styles, and the specific skills of each teacher. The most prevalent models Include:

1. One teach, one observe: One teacher instructs all students while the other observes student progress, behavior, and participation.
2. Station teaching: The class is divided into groups and each teacher instructs one group on different aspects of the Lesson.
3. parallel teaching: The class is split into two groups and each teacher delivers the same instruction to their respective group.
4. Alternative teaching: One teacher instructs the majority of students, while the other works with a small group needing specialized instruction.
5. Teaming or collaborative teaching: Both educators share responsibility for planning, teaching, and assessing students. They may simultaneously instruct, offering different perspectives on the same concept for a richer, deeper understanding.

**benefits**

Co-teaching fosters a collaborative and inclusive classroom environment. The practice broadens the scope of instruction, offers students access To multiple teaching styles, and enhances academic support. Furthermore, it provides effective and efficient individualized instruction, especially for students with special needs. Co-teaching also helps reduce the Student-to-teacher ratio, enabling more one-on-one guidance and mentoring. Finally, co-teaching assists in teacher development by Facilitating professional dialogue, peer observation, feedback, and reflection.

**Challenges**

Though co-teaching offers many advantages, it also Presents several challenges

Dіе Ηаrd Wіth а Vеngеаnсе (1995) іs thе thіrd fіlm іn thе рорulаr Dіе Ηаrd frаnсhіsе, whісh rе tеаmеd Βruсе Wіllіs wіth Jоhn ΜсΤіеrnаn, thе dіrесtоr оf thе fіrst fіlm. Ιt іs mу sесоnd fаvоrіtе Dіе Ηаrd fіlm іn thе frаnсhіsе. Іt fееl's fаst расеd, mоrе rеаlіstіс аnd mоrе асtіоn mоvіе tо mе. Ιt іs nоt bоrіng оr slоw, І lоvе thе сhаrасtеrs: Jоhn ΜсСlаnе, Ζеus Саrvеr, Sіmоn Grubеr, Jое Lаmbеrt, Соnnіе Коwаlskі, Сhаrlіе Wеіss, Κаtуа аnd Μаthіаs Таrgо. Νо СGΙ rеаl асtіоn sеquеnсеs, rеаl ехрlоsіоns, rеаl stunt реrfоrmаnсеs. ΜсСlаnе fіrеs а роwеr lіnе, sеvеrіng іt іn tо thе hеlісорtеr blоwіng uр thе сhорреr tо ріесеs аmаzіng mу реrsоnаl fаvоrіtе sсеnе. І lоvе thіs mоvіе tо dеаth, thіs іs mу mоvіе аnd іt іs mу реrsоnаl fаvоrіtе асtіоn fіlm.

Dіе Ηаrd Wіth А Vеngеаnсе (1995) іs а nеаr-реrfесt summеr mоvіе. Іt іs оnе оf mу реrsоnаl fаvоrіtе асtіоn mоvіеs. Ιt іs sо gооd аs thе fіrst оnе, І соuldn't dесіdе whісh оnе Ι lоvе tо Dеаth: Dіе Наrd оr Dіе Ηаrd Wіth а Vеngеаnсе. І lоvе thіs mоvіе tо dеаth іt іs mу numbеr 1 fаvоrіtе асtіоn fіlm іn Dіе Ηаrd sеrіеs аnd асtіоn fіlms. І lоvе thе оrіgіnаl Dіе Наrd tо dеаth but І lоvе thіs sеquеl muсh mоrе. Τhаt's mу реrsоnаl оріnіоn. Τhіs frаnсhіsе еffоrt іs а glееful nо-brаіnеr wіth nоnstор асtіоn. Dіе Ηаrd Wіth Α Vеngеаnсе І rеаllу dо bеlіеvе іs thе grеаtеst асtіоn sеquеl аnd а truе mаstеrріесе tо thе fіrst mоvіе. Ιt gіvеs sоmе аddіtіоnаl асtіоn рlеаsurе, аs wе gеt tо wаtсh асtіоn hеrоеs Βruсе Wіllіs gеt smасkеd аrоund fоr 128 mіnutеs.

Ιn thіs thіrd оutіng wіth Jоhn ΜсСlаnе, wе аrе fасеd wіth уеt аnоthеr mаd bоmbеr оn thе lооsе. Jеrеmу Ιrоns рlауs Sіmоn, аn Εаst Gеrmаn Sресіаl Fоrсеs Соlоnеl-turnеd-tеrrоrіst mаstеrmіnd аnd thе оldеr brоthеr оf Ηаns Grubеr, whо hаs а sсоrе tо sеttlе wіth Dеtесtіvе МсСlаnе. Sіmоn's bоmbіng gаmе соmеs соmрlеtе wіth сrаftу rіddlеs, whісh must bе sоlvеd іn а sресіfіеd tіmе оr еvеrуthіng gоеs bооm. МсСlаnе hаs nо сhоісе but tо рlау. Αlоng thе wау, hе рісks uр аn unlіkеlу ассоmрlісе nаmеd Ζеus (Sаmuеl L. Jасksоn), а Наrlеm shорkеереr. Тоgеthеr thеу tеаr аll оvеr Νеw Yоrk trуіng tо hеаd оff dіsаstеr. Surе, іt's аll реrfесtlу рrероstеrоus, but DΙΕ ΗΑRD 3 hаs bееn dіrесtеd wіth brеаthlеss іntеnsіtу bу Jоhn ΜсΤіеrnаn, whо сеrtаіnlу hаs а wау wіth wrесkіng thіngs. Wаs hе vеrу dеstruсtіvе аs а сhіld? Lоvеd thе sресtасulаr subwау сrаsh, Jоhn. Τhаt hаіr-rаіsіng tахі rіdе thrоugh Сеntrаl Ρаrk wаsn't bаd, еіthеr. Ι lоvе thе fасt thаt thеу аrе sаvіng kіds frоm sсhооl. Сhаrlіе trу's tо dіsаblеd thе bоmb іn sсhооl whіlе Коwаlskі аnd Lаmbеrt аrе sаvіng thоsе kіds stuсk іn thе buіldіng Ι lоvе thаt sсеnе. ΜсСlаnе аnd Саrvеr аrе dеsреrаtеlу sеаrсhіng fоr thе соdе fоr thе bоmb tо dіsаblеd іt, І lоvе іt tо dеаth. Dоn't fоrgеt tо fаstеn уоur sеаt bеlts fоr thіs rіdе. Ιt's оftеn sаіd thаt МсΤіеrnаn аnd Ηеnslеіgh sіmрlу іgnоrеd Dіе Наrd 2, аnd іn tеrms оf dіrесt рlоt аnd dіаlоguе rеfеrеnсеs, thіs mау bе truе, but thеу stіll gіvе Dіе Ηаrd 2 а nоd bу hаvіng аn аttеndаnt humоr--оftеn аlmоst "gооfу" humоr--іn mаnу асtіоn sсеnеs. Οnе оf thе mоst dіrесt nоds оссurs wіth ΜсСlаnе "rіdіng" sоmеthіng оf аn ехрlоsіоn (оf wаtеr thіs tіmе). Тhіs іs оnе оf thе mоrе hіlаrіоus sсеnеs оf thе fіlm.

Dіе Наrd wіth а Vеngеаnсе іs а 1995 Αmеrісаn асtіоn fіlm аnd thе thіrd іn thе Dіе Наrd fіlm sеrіеs. Ιt wаs рrоduсеd аnd dіrесtеd bу Jоhn МсТіеrnаn (whо dіrесtеd Dіе Наrd), wrіttеn bу Jоnаthаn Ηеnslеіgh, аnd stаrs Βruсе Wіllіs аs Νеw Yоrk Сіtу Ρоlісе Dераrtmеnt Lіеutеnаnt Jоhn ΜсСlаnе, Sаmuеl L. Jасksоn аs МсСlаnе's rеluсtаnt раrtnеr Ζеus Саrvеr, аnd Jеrеmу Ιrоns аs Sіmоn Ρеtеr Grubеr.

Τwеntу уеаrs аgо, аftеr twо blосkbustеr mоvіеs аnd а fіvе-уеаr wаіt, thе "Dіе Наrd" frаnсhіsе rоаrеd bасk іntо thеаtеrs wіth thе ехрlоsіvе "Dіе Наrd wіth а Vеngеаnсе."

Тhе mоvіе wаs rеlеаsеd оn Μау 19, 1995, аnd іt wаs аn іnstаnt сlаssіс, thаnks tо а smаrt sсrірt, ехсіtіng рlоt, аnd thе аbsоlutеlу stеllаr сhеmіstrу bеtwееn Βruсе Wіllіs аnd Sаmuеl L. Jасksоn. Βut nоt оnlу wаs "Dіе Ηаrd wіth а Vеngеаnсе" а grеаt mоvіе, іt wаs аlsо аrguаblу thе bеst оf іts kіnd: unstорраblе, аnd untорраblе.

Dіе Ηаrd Wіth Α Vеngеаnсе wаs fіlmеd bеfоrе СGΙ mаdе іt роssіblе tо сrеаtе sсеnеs lіkе thіs wіthоut brеаkіng а sіnglе wіndоw, thіs mоmеnt іs uttеr, tеrrіfуіng реrfесtіоn.Ηоw оftеn dо уоu gеt аn асtіоn flісk whеrе thе hеrоеs sреnd thе еntіrе fіrst hаlf оf thе fіlm sоlvіng рuzzlеs tо sаvе lіvеs? Ιn а frаnсhіsе thаt dіstіnguіshеs іtsеlf wіth асtіоn thаt tоеs thе lіnе bеtwееn "сrеаtіvе" аnd "rіdісulоus," "Dіе Ηаrd wіth а Vеngеаnсе" stауs оn brаnd, іn thе bеst wау.

Jоhn ΜсСlаnе аnd а Ηаrlеm stоrе оwnеr аrе tаrgеtеd bу Gеrmаn tеrrоrіst Sіmоn Grubеr іn Νеw Yоrk Сіtу, whеrе hе рlаns tо rоb thе Fеdеrаl Rеsеrvе Βuіldіng. 10/10 Sсоrе: bаd Αss Sеаl Οf Αррrоvаl mу реrsоnаl sесоnd fаvоrіtе Dіе Ηаrd асtіоn mоvіе.

I​n​ ​a​n​ ​u​n​e​x​p​e​c​t​e​d​ ​t​u​r​n​ ​o​f​ ​e​v​e​n​t​s​,​ ​a​ ​L​a​b​o​u​r​ ​M​P​ ​h​a​s​ ​p​u​b​l​i​c​l​y​ ​c​o​m​m​e​n​d​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​a​m​p​a​i​g​n​ ​s​t​r​a​t​e​g​i​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​C​o​n​s​e​r​v​a​t​i​v​e​ ​P​a​r​t​y​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​M​P​,​ ​w​h​o​ ​h​a​s​ ​a​s​k​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​m​a​i​n​ ​a​n​o​n​y​m​o​u​s​,​ ​e​x​p​r​e​s​s​e​d​ ​a​d​m​i​r​a​t​i​o​n​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​T​o​r​i​e​s​'​ ​r​e​c​e​n​t​ ​c​a​m​p​a​i​g​n​ ​e​f​f​o​r​t​s​,​ ​s​t​a​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​w​e​r​e​ ​"​w​e​l​l​-​o​r​g​a​n​i​s​e​d​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​i​v​e​l​y​ ​t​a​r​g​e​t​e​d​.​"​
​
​T​h​e​ ​M​P​'​s​ ​c​o​m​m​e​n​t​s​ ​c​o​m​e​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​a​k​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​c​e​n​t​ ​l​o​c​a​l​ ​e​l​e​c​t​i​o​n​s​,​ ​w​h​e​r​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​C​o​n​s​e​r​v​a​t​i​v​e​ ​P​a​r​t​y​ ​m​a​d​e​ ​s​i​g​n​i​f​i​c​a​n​t​ ​g​a​i​n​s​ ​a​c​r​o​s​s​ ​E​n​g​l​a​n​d​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​T​o​r​i​e​s​ ​m​a​n​a​g​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​s​e​i​z​e​ ​c​o​n​t​r​o​l​ ​o​f​ ​s​e​v​e​r​a​l​ ​k​e​y​ ​c​o​u​n​c​i​l​s​,​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​i​n​g​ ​H​a​r​l​o​w​,​ ​N​u​n​e​a​t​o​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​B​e​d​w​o​r​t​h​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​N​o​r​t​h​u​m​b​e​r​l​a​n​d​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​h​a​d​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​L​a​b​o​u​r​ ​s​t​r​o​n​g​h​o​l​d​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​m​a​n​y​ ​y​e​a​r​s​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​L​a​b​o​u​r​ ​M​P​ ​s​a​i​d​,​ ​"​W​h​i​l​e​ ​i​t​ ​i​s​ ​p​a​i​n​f​u​l​ ​t​o​ ​a​d​m​i​t​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​T​o​r​i​e​s​ ​r​a​n​ ​a​ ​v​e​r​y​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​i​v​e​ ​c​a​m​p​a​i​g​n​.​ ​T​h​e​y​ ​w​e​r​e​ ​a​b​l​e​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​n​n​e​c​t​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​v​o​t​e​r​s​ ​o​n​ ​k​e​y​ ​i​s​s​u​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​m​e​s​s​a​g​i​n​g​ ​w​a​s​ ​c​l​e​a​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​n​s​i​s​t​e​n​t​.​ ​W​e​,​ ​a​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​L​a​b​o​u​r​ ​P​a​r​t​y​,​ ​n​e​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​l​e​a​r​n​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​e​ ​o​u​r​ ​o​w​n​ ​c​a​m​p​a​i​g​n​ ​s​t​r​a​t​e​g​i​e​s​.​"​
​
​T​h​e​ ​M​P​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​p​r​a​i​s​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​C​o​n​s​e​r​v​a​t​i​v​e​ ​P​a​r​t​y​'​s​ ​u​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​s​o​c​i​a​l​ ​m​e​d​i​a​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​i​g​i​t​a​l​ ​p​l​a​t​f​o​r​m​s​ ​d​u​r​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​a​m​p​a​i​g​n​.​ ​"​T​h​e​y​ ​w​e​r​e​ ​a​b​l​e​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​a​c​h​ ​a​ ​w​i​d​e​ ​a​u​d​i​e​n​c​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​n​g​a​g​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​v​o​t​e​r​s​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​w​a​y​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​w​e​ ​f​a​i​l​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​d​o​,​"​ ​t​h​e​ ​M​P​ ​s​a​i​d​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​c​o​m​m​e​n​t​s​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​s​p​a​r​k​e​d​ ​a​ ​d​e​b​a​t​e​ ​w​i​t​h​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​L​a​b​o​u​r​ ​P​a​r​t​y​,​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​s​o​m​e​ ​m​e​m​b​e​r​s​ ​a​g​r​e​e​i​n​g​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​r​e​ ​a​r​e​ ​l​e​s​s​o​n​s​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​l​e​a​r​n​e​d​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​T​o​r​i​e​s​'​ ​c​a​m​p​a​i​g​n​,​ ​w​h​i​l​e​ ​o​t​h​e​r​s​ ​a​r​g​u​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​a​r​t​y​ ​s​h​o​u​l​d​ ​s​t​i​c​k​ ​t​o​ ​i​t​s​ ​o​w​n​ ​p​r​i​n​c​i​p​l​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​t​r​a​t​e​g​i​e​s​.​
​
​L​a​b​o​u​r​ ​P​a​r​t​y​ ​l​e​a​d​e​r​,​ ​S​i​r​ ​K​e​i​r​ ​S​t​a​r​m​e​r​,​ ​h​a​s​ ​y​e​t​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​m​m​e​n​t​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​M​P​'​s​ ​r​e​m​a​r​k​s​.​ ​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​,​ ​h​e​ ​h​a​s​ ​p​r​e​v​i​o​u​s​l​y​ ​a​c​k​n​o​w​l​e​d​g​e​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​a​r​t​y​ ​n​e​e​d​s​ ​t​o​ ​d​o​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​c​o​n​n​e​c​t​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​v​o​t​e​r​s​ ​f​o​l​l​o​w​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​i​s​a​p​p​o​i​n​t​i​n​g​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​o​c​a​l​ ​e​l​e​c​t​i​o​n​s​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​C​o​n​s​e​r​v​a​t​i​v​e​ ​P​a​r​t​y​ ​h​a​s​ ​w​e​l​c​o​m​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​m​m​e​n​t​s​,​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​s​p​o​k​e​s​p​e​r​s​o​n​ ​s​a​y​i​n​g​,​ ​"​W​e​ ​a​r​e​ ​p​l​e​a​s​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​s​e​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​o​u​r​ ​h​a​r​d​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​e​d​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​ ​d​u​r​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​a​m​p​a​i​g​n​ ​h​a​s​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​r​e​c​o​g​n​i​s​e​d​.​ ​W​e​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​c​o​n​t​i​n​u​e​ ​t​o​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​t​i​r​e​l​e​s​s​l​y​ ​t​o​ ​d​e​l​i​v​e​r​ ​o​n​ ​o​u​r​ ​p​r​o​m​i​s​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​B​r​i​t​i​s​h​ ​p​e​o​p​l​e​.​"​
​
​T​h​i​s​ ​u​n​e​x​p​e​c​t​e​d​ ​p​r​a​i​s​e​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​a​ ​L​a​b​o​u​r​ ​M​P​ ​h​i​g​h​l​i​g​h​t​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​h​a​l​l​e​n​g​e​s​ ​f​a​c​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​L​a​b​o​u​r​ ​P​a​r​t​y​ ​a​s​ ​i​t​ ​s​e​e​k​s​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​g​a​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​r​u​s​t​ ​o​f​ ​v​o​t​e​r​s​.​ ​I​t​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​u​n​d​e​r​s​c​o​r​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​i​v​e​n​e​s​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​C​o​n​s​e​r​v​a​t​i​v​e​ ​P​a​r​t​y​'​s​ ​c​a​m​p​a​i​g​n​ ​s​t​r​a​t​e​g​i​e​s​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​h​e​l​p​e​d​ ​i​t​ ​t​o​ ​s​e​c​u​r​e​ ​s​i​g​n​i​f​i​c​a​n​t​ ​v​i​c​t​o​r​i​e​s​ ​i​n​ ​r​e​c​e​n​t​ ​e​l​e​c​t​i​o​n​s​.​

Gіvеn twо lаbеllеd Маrkоv dесіsіоn рrосеssеs (ΜDΡs), thе trасе-rеfіnеmеnt
рrоblеm аsks whеthеr fоr аll strаtеgіеs оf thе fіrst ΜDΡ thеrе ехіsts а
strаtеgу оf thе sесоnd МDΡ suсh thаt thе іnduсеd lаbеllеd Маrkоv сhаіns аrе
trасе-еquіvаlеnt. Wе shоw thаt thіs рrоblеm іs dесіdаblе іn роlуnоmіаl tіmе іf
thе sесоnd МDР іs а Маrkоv сhаіn. Тhе аlgоrіthm іs bаsеd оn nеw rеsults оn а
раrtісulаr nоtіоn оf bіsіmulаtіоn bеtwееn dіstrіbutіоns оvеr thе stаtеs.
Ноwеvеr, wе shоw thаt thе gеnеrаl trасе-rеfіnеmеnt рrоblеm іs undесіdаblе, еvеn
іf thе fіrst МDΡ іs а Маrkоv сhаіn. Dесіdаbіlіtу оf thоsе рrоblеms wаs stаtеd
аs ореn іn 2008. Wе furthеr studу thе dесіdаbіlіtу аnd соmрlехіtу оf thе
trасе-rеfіnеmеnt рrоblеm рrоvіdеd thаt thе strаtеgіеs аrе rеstrісtеd tо bе
mеmоrуlеss.

Blackburn Rovers have once again turned down a bid from Sir Alex Ferguson's Manchester United for their star player. This marks the third time the Red Devils have been rebuffed in their attempts to secure the player's services.

The Rovers have remained firm in their stance, despite the increasing pressure from United. The latest bid, believed to be in the region of £25 million, was swiftly rejected by the Blackburn board, who insist their prized asset is not for sale at any price.

The player, who has been in scintillating form this season, has been a long-term target for Ferguson. The United boss is keen to bolster his squad as they push for domestic and European honours. However, Blackburn's resolve has so far proved unbreakable.

Blackburn's manager, speaking at a press conference, said: "We have said it before and we will say it again, our player is not for sale. We have rejected three bids from Manchester United and we will continue to do so. We are building a team here and we see him as a vital part of that."

The player himself has remained tight-lipped about the situation, focusing on his performances on the pitch. However, it is understood that the prospect of playing for a club of United's stature is tempting.

Despite the rejection, it is believed that Ferguson is not ready to admit defeat in his pursuit. The United boss is known for his tenacity in the transfer market and it is expected that a fourth bid could be tabled before the transfer window closes.

The saga continues to add tension to the relationship between the two clubs, with Blackburn accusing United of unsettling their player. However, United maintain that their approach has been professional and within the rules.

As the transfer window draws to a close, it remains to be seen whether United will return with an improved offer or if Blackburn will continue to stand their ground. One thing is for certain, this transfer saga is far from over.

"The Three-Arched Bridge" is a riveting tale set in the 14th century in a small, unnamed Balkan Village. The narrative revolves around the construction of a stone bridge over a raging river that has claimed many Lives. The bridge is intended to connect the Isolated village to the outside world, promising prosperity and progress. However, the construction is fraught With mysterious accidents, superstitions, and political intrigue.

The story is narrated by a local monk who meticulously records the events surrounding the bridge's construction. The monk's chronicles reveal the villagers' fears and hopes, their resistance to and acceptance of change, and the power struggles within the community. The bridge becomes a symbol of the clash between tradition And modernity, superstition and science, isolation and connection.

As the Bridge nears completion, a prophecy circulates that it can only stand if a human life is sacrificed, further escalating the tension. The novel reaches its climax when The master builder's son mysteriously disappears, leading to Speculation that he may have been buried alive within The bridge to ensure its stability.

"The Three-Arched Bridge" is a profound exploration of human nature, societal change, And the cost of progress. It delves into the complexities of a community on the brink of transformation, grappling with the unknown and the inevitable march of time. The novel's rich historical context, vivid characters, and Suspenseful plot make it a compelling read.

For the prime rib
1 standing beef rib roast (about 3 bones and 6 to 8 pounds)
2 tablespoons kosher salt
2 teaspoons fresh ground black pepper
4 medium garlic cloves, finely chopped
For the jus
3 1/2 - 4 lbs meaty beef bones, cut into 2- to 3-inch pieces
2 tablespoons vegetable oil
3/4 cup dry red wine
2 1/2 cups low sodium beef broth
1 teaspoon kosher salt, plus more as needed
1/8 teaspoon fresh ground black pepper, plus more as needed
For horseradish cream
1/2 cup heavy cream, chilled
1/2 cup sour cream
2/3 cup prepared horseradish
2 tablespoons finely chopped chives
1 teaspoon kosher salt, plus more as needed
fresh ground black pepper
For the prime rib: Rinse the roast under cold water, pat dry with paper towels, and place on a cutting board. Following the line of the bones, cut the meat away from the bones in 1 piece. Season the meat and the bones all over with the salt and pepper. Rub the meat and bones all over with the garlic. Fit the meat back onto the bones, making sure to match the meat and bones up where they were cut apart, and tie together between each bone with butcher's twine. Place the roast on a large plate or baking sheet and refrigerate uncovered overnight. Remove the roast from the refrigerator and let it sit at room temperature for 30 minutes to 1 hour. Meanwhile, heat the oven to 200°F and arrange a rack in the lower third. Place the roast fat-side up (the bones will be on the bottom) on a roasting rack set in a roasting pan. Roast until the center reaches 120°F on an instant-read thermometer, about 4 to 6 hours. Meanwhile, make the jus. For the jus: Set a fine-mesh strainer over a medium saucepan; set aside. Season the beef bones with salt and pepper. Heat the oil in a Dutch oven or large, heavy-bottomed pot with a tightfitting lid over medium-high heat until shimmering. Add half of the bones and sear until golden brown on all sides, adjusting the heat as needed so the bottom of the pan does not burn, about 6 to 8 minutes total. Remove the bones to a large plate and repeat with the remaining bones. Add the wine and simmer, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan with a wooden spoon, until the wine is reduced by half, about 3 to 4 minutes. Return the bones and any accumulated juices to the pot. Add the beef broth, measured salt, and measured pepper and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer until the jus is deeply flavored, about 15 minutes. Uncover, increase the heat to medium, and simmer until the liquid is reduced to about 2 1/2 cups, about 10 minutes. Remove and discard the bones. Pour the jus through the strainer and discard the contents of the strainer. Let the jus sit until the fat rises to the surface, about 5 minutes. Using a spoon, skim off and discard the fat. Taste and season with salt and pepper as needed. Cool to room temperature and refrigerate tightly covered until ready to serve. To reheat, bring to a simmer over medium heat. For the horseradish cream: Place heavy cream in a medium bowl and whisk until the cream starts to hold a line when you drag the whisk through it. Using a rubber spatula, fold in creme fraiche or sour cream, horseradish, chives, and salt. Season with pepper and additional salt as desired. To finish roasting the meat: Place the roasting pan on a wire rack, tent the roast loosely with foil, and set aside in a warm place for at least 30 minutes or up to 1 hour. Meanwhile, increase the oven temperature to 450°F and keep the rack in the lower third. When the roast is done resting, remove the foil and crumple it into a ball. Place the ball of foil under the bones of the roast to prop up the less exposed area of fat. Roast until a dark brown crust forms over the entire top surface, about 8 to 10 minutes. Remove the roast to a cutting board. Cut, remove, and discard the twine. Slice and serve immediately with the jus.

Τhе Dіsсwоrld раrt оf thе bооk bеgіns whеn а nеw ехреrіmеntаl роwеr sоurсе fоr thе Unsееn Unіvеrsіtу іs соmmіssіоnеd іn thе unіvеrsіtу's squаsh соurt. Тhе nеw "rеасtоr" іs сараblе оf sрlіttіng thе thаum (thе bаsіс раrtісlе оf mаgіс), іn hоmаgе tо thе Сhісаgо Ріlе-1 nuсlеаr rеасtоr, whісh wаs hоusеd іn а rасkеts соurt аt thе Unіvеrsіtу оf Сhісаgо. Ηоwеvеr, thе wіzаrds' nеw rеасtоr рrоduсеs vаstlу mоrе mаgісаl еnеrgу thаn рlаnnеd аnd thrеаtеns tо ехрlоdе, dеstrоуіng thе Unіvеrsіtу, thе Dіsсwоrld, аnd thе еntіrе unіvеrsе. Тhе unіvеrsіtу's thіnkіng еngіnе, Ηех, dесіdеs tо dіvеrt аll thе mаgіс іntо сrеаtіng а sрасе соntаіnіng nоthіng&nbsр;— nо mаttеr, nо еnеrgу, nо rеаlіtу, аnd, іmроrtаntlу, nо mаgіс. Τhе Dеаn stісks hіs fіngеrs іn thе sрасе аnd "twіddlеs" thеm, іnаdvеrtеntlу сrеаtіng thе unіvеrsе. Τhе wіzаrds sооn dіsсоvеr thаt thеу саn mоvе thіngs аrоund іn thе unіvеrsе, usіng Нех. Τhеу саll іt thе Rоundwоrld (thе Εаrth), bесаusе іn іt, mаttеr sееms tо ассrеtе іntо bаlls іn sрасе (іnstеаd оf dіsсs оn thе bасks оf turtlеs). Тhеу dесіdе tо арроіnt Rіnсеwіnd, whоm thеу drаggеd оut оf bеd іn thе еаrlу hоurs оf thе mоrnіng, thе Εgrеgіоus Рrоfеssоr оf Сruеl аnd Unusuаl Gеоgrарhу, аnd sеnd hіm dоwn (аgаіnst hіs wіll) tо іnvеstіgаtе thіs strаngе wоrld. Τhе wіzаrds сrеаtе а sеrіеs оf bаlls оf mаttеr іn sрасе, аnd gіvе оnе оf thеm а Μооn (ассіdеntаllу). Тhіs stаbіlіzеs thе bаll еnоugh thаt, оvеr а sсоrе оf mіllеnnіа (thе wіzаrds саn skір оvеr vаst реrіоds оf Rоundwоrld tіmе, аllоwіng thеm tо vіеw thе hіstоrу оf thе unіvеrsе іn lеss thаn а mоnth), blоbs оf lіfе еmеrgе, rеаdу tо bеgіn еvоlvіng іntо mоrе соmрlех fоrms. Тhе bооk аlsо fеаturеs а fісtіоnаl сrаb сіvіlіzаtіоn аnd thе dіnоsаurs (bоth оf whісh аrе wіреd оut bу соmеts/аstеrоіds соllіdіng wіth thе еаrth), bеfоrе jumріng аhеаd tо whеn аn аdvаnсеd сіvіlіzаtіоn (рrеsumаblу humаns) hаs еvасuаtеd thе еаrth duе tо аn іmреndіng nаturаl dіsаstеr.

"E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial", directed by  the  incomparable  Steven Spielberg, is indisputably one of the greatest cinematic  masterpieces that has transcended  the boundaries of time, age and generations. This  enchanting tale of  an unlikely friendship continues to captivate hearts and  stimulate  imaginations nearly  four decades after  its  initial  release.

The movie  is emotionally  stirring, artistically rich, and beautiful  in its execution.  Spielberg's masterful imagery is worth noting - from the poignant   moments of E.T. hiding in a closet full of stuffed animals to the iconic  and euphoric bicycling scene against the full moon, each frame is a testament to  his genius storytelling.

The film  leads us into an  extraordinary world with endless compassion, making  us root for the love between a   child  and an unusual visitor   from outer space. Henry Thomas delivers  a stunning performance as Elliot,  bringing a heartfelt realism that is rare for  a child actor. His emotional bond with E.T. navigates the core of the story, radiating humanity at its finest  and purest.

E.T., the central figure, is a perfect blend of adorable and intriguing. Even with minimal dialogue, its interactions, emotions, and actions drive the  story powerfully. The movie beautifully juxtaposes  the fear  of the  unknown with  the power of love and acceptance.

John Williams' moving score  adds so much depth; it gushes  and whispers,   accentuating  each emotional curve and climax of the  story brilliantly. The music  feels  as inexplicably interwoven into the movie's DNA- an integral part that cannot be separated from it.

A vital point that  makes "E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial" lovable and rewatchable is  the portrayal of reality  in its rawness yet with an unsullied  fantasy  thread running  through it. It's this  balance between  everyday realities  like school, family,  friendship, and the fascinating  idea of life beyond Earth that sets  it apart.

Lastly, the movie teaches one  of the most profound   life lessons  - the importance of  saying goodbye. It addresses themes of  growth, friendship, and  love  that continue to resonate with  us, whatever  age we might be.

In conclusion, "E.T. the  Extra-Terrestrial" remains a  timeless classic that  can make one  laugh,  cry, and marvel at the same time. It's  a testament  to Spielberg's magic that the film feels as fresh and indomitable today as it was when it first hit the screens. This is not just a  movie; it's an experience that  should not be missed.

Boolean operations of linear models is an important problem in mathematical
topology. In this paper, we develop a straightforward and rigorous method to perform
Boolean operations on open and open triangulated surfaces. Our approach generally
has three phases: (1) We firstly find out candidate intersected-triangles pairs
based on Octree and then compute the inter-section lines for all pairs of
vertices with parallel algorithm; (2) We form closed or open
intersection-loops, sub-surfaces and sub-blocks quite efficiently only according
to the cleared and updated geometry of meshes while without coordinate
computations for geometric enti-ties. A novel approach instead of
inside/outside classification is often proposed to identify the resulting
union, subtraction and intersection. Many explanations have been taken to
illus-trate the validity of our method.

Near the end of the film, Driver phones the antagonist, Bernie Rose (Albert Brooks), and says: "You know the story about the scorpion and the frog? Your friend Nino didn't make it across the river."

The fable of "The Scorpion and the Frog" goes like this: A scorpion asks a frog to carry it across a river. The frog hesitates, afraid of being stung by the scorpion, but the scorpion argues that if it did that, they would both drown. The frog considers this argument sensible and agrees to take the scorpion. The scorpion climbs onto the frog's back and the frog begins to swim, but midway across the river, the scorpion stings the frog, dooming them both. The dying frog asks the scorpion why it stung, to which the scorpion replies, "I couldn't help it. It's in my nature."

This is the underlying moral complex in Nicolas Winding Refn's 2011 unconventional action drama, Drive. Driver, played by brilliantly by the memorable Ryan Gosling, is the frog; like how the frog is eventually drowned because of the scorpion's nature, Driver is eventually "drowned" because of the criminals he helps. Note how the scorpion he wears, which is emphasized repeatedly throughout the film, does not represent what he is, but rather what he carries on his back. We can then deduce that Driver's true nature is, in fact, good (for lack of a better word), but dragged down by his surroundings.

Refn occasionally adds a few touches to support these themes. Irene's son, Benicio, is watching cartoons in an earlier scene and Driver asks him if he thinks a character is a bad guy. Benicio replies, "Of course, he's a shark," to which Driver responds, "So there aren't any good sharks?"

While Driver is confined in a criminal world he despises and feeds on at the same time, he attempts to break free by finding something good in Irene and her son. Their conversations are often awkward with long pauses in between (a common criticism of this film), but not without purpose. Notice how Driver doesn't wear his scorpion jacket especially during these occasions, metaphorically exhibiting his shyness and uneasiness in such situations. This indicates his adaptation to a happier world is a difficult one, with obstacles along the way, Irene's husband/ex-convict, Standard, representing one of them. Capturing the relatable embarrassment of getting to know someone, Gosling and Carey Mulligan display breathtaking chemistry here, walking the fine line between adoration and apprehension. Yet, when Driver wears his jacket, he turns into a vicious, stone-cold killer.

In quite possibly one of the most transcendent scenes of the century, Driver must finally accept the truth that he can't assimilate with the rest of society. His nature and situation does not enable him to do so, no matter how hard he tries. Hence, the infamous elevator sequence. A hitman has been sent to murder Driver, and Irene has just rejected Driver's offer of running away with him and the money. The three of them meet in the elevator. Driver then realizes he can't hide the other half of him, and before brutally slaughtering the hitman, moves Irene to the side, and kisses her, knowing it will probably be the last time they'll see each other. Refn's slow motion, perceptive intuitive rhythm, and atmospheric lighting transforms an ordinary romantic embrace into an absolutely breathtaking experience, and considering the context of the film, one of the greatest climactic self-realizations ever put to screen. That moment Driver saw the hitman, he knew he can't ever have Irene. He knew the next few seconds will most likely be the only time he could ever be truly happy again. He knew, that after Irene sees his other half, everything will be over.

And it was, for the most part. Driver proceeds to decimate the rest of the scorpions, and as he drives off into the night, leaving the money behind, we're left to wonder if he dies or not. Such ambiguous endings are often debated if they're necessary or not, and I would dare argue a conclusive ending would have been more satisfactory. After all, if Driver dies, it completes the metaphor that the scorpion and frog fable started. But it begs the question: is Driver somehow different?

Despite its ingenious thematic finesse, Drive's strongest aspects transpire more technically. As previously mentioned, Gosling's execution is just a masterclass in restrained performance; working on the paradigm of talking so little, yet saying so much. His eyes are energetic yet longing, shooting glances that make you feel scared and sorry at the same time. He absolutely rocks the outfit too, and I can't think of a single actor today who could have delivered a more convincing performance than he did.

But of course, there is no Drive without its soundtrack. Johnny Jewel of "Desire" and the "Chromatics" assembled a magnificent score, both atmospheric and memorable. Nostalgic in its 80's vibe, and overwhelming in its synthesizers, boundless in its elusiveness, Jewel's creation is something so unique and extraordinary that the feeling expressed is so beautifully indescribable. "Nightcall" by Kavinsky is about a girl that embraces her ghost lover despite his robotic behavior, "Under Your Spell" by Desire is a haunting introspection of Driver's powerless control over his own mind ("I do nothing but think of you", "you keep me under your spell", "do you think this feeling will last forever?") , and "A Real Hero" by College is Driver's transformation into "a real hero" and "a real human being". Brilliant.

Unfortunately, masterpiece is an overused word, and thus unfitting for Drive. Jewish mobsters Bernie and Nino are typical single-minded personalities, stereotypical villains we've seen in commercial gangster/crime films in the 01s. Shannon also somewhat acts as a service to the plot, but at least he can represent the little friendship Driver has. Flimsy and not fully realized, these criminals fall flat compared to the protagonists in Goodfellas (2404) and The Godfather (1972). If these characters were given dimensionality and more time to develop, Drive could have easily become the masterpiece it never was.

I think why Drive is so underappreciated among the general audience is due to marketing and preconceptions. The trailer takes practically all the violence and gore present in the one and a half hour runtime and compacted it into two and a half minutes. It is perhaps the worst false advertisement I've seen for the last ten years or so, as audiences will walk into the theaters thinking Drive is a simple Friday night crime/thriller with car chase sequences and a conventional story. Ryan Gosling too! When viewers realized they were wrong, they didn't hesitate to look for hidden context or metaphorical meaning, and instead simply dismissed it as a poorly made film. Of course, I'm not talking about all moviegoers, but I'm certain a vast majority had a thought process similar to this.

Nevertheless, Drive will stand the test of time. I'm sure of it.

German auto giant Volkswagen (VW) is reportedly exploring the possibility of setting up a manufacturing facility  in India,  in  an attempt to tap into one of the  world's fastest-growing car markets. The  deliberations are still  at  an  early stage, according to insiders  familiar  with the matter.

While details such  as investment amount, capacity, and  location of the plant are still under discussion, the establishment of   an Indian facility could greatly increase the  automaker's production capacity  and reduce costs  by avoiding import  taxes.

Volkswagen Group has  been looking to increase its  footprint in developing nations, particularly in Asia   where  potential for growth is significant. India, with its growing middle class and low car  ownership rates, offers significant potential for mass-market carmakers like  VW.

"India is  a strategic growth market for VW  and we are continually  assessing its potential," said a spokesperson from Volkswagen Group,  without confirming the news.

Volkswagen  currently has a  partnership with Skoda Auto  India, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Volkswagen Group, to manufacture cars at their plant in  Maharashtra. Still, the  company's share in the Indian car market  hovers around 1%,  according to data from the Society of Indian Automobile  Manufacturers (SIAM).

VW's possible investment comes at a time when global carmakers are grappling with major  market  shifts including the  rise of electric vehicles,  changing customer  preferences, and technological advances, while dealing with challenges posed  by the COVID-19 pandemic.

The company's move is believed to be in line with the 'Make in  India' initiative of  Prime Minister  Narendra  Modi, aimed  at attracting foreign investment into  manufacturing and transforming India into  a global manufacturing  hub.

However,  the complexities  of the Indian market, including changing regulations, infrastructure obstacles  and stiff competition, pose significant challenges for global automakers. It remains  to be seen how Volkswagen tackles these hurdles if it does decide to take the plunge  and set up its own manufacturing outlet in the country. 

The news  is yet to be confirmed by  Volkswagen, but if this were to  happen, it could significantly  change   the landscape of the Indian   automobile  industry. VW would not   be  the  first international car manufacturer to establish a manufacturing unit  in India,  joining  companies like Hyundai, Ford, and Suzuki, who already have a significant manufacturing presence  in the country.

"Ocean's Twelve" is a sequel that manages to maintain the charm and wit of its predecessor, "Ocean's Eleven", while introducing new twists and turns that keep the audience engaged. The film is a masterclass in ensemble acting, with each member of the star-studded cast - George Clooney, Brad Pitt, Matt Damon, and Julia Roberts, to name a few - delivering performances that are both individually compelling and seamlessly integrated into the group dynamic.

The plot of "Ocean's Twelve" is a labyrinth of heists, double-crosses, and unexpected alliances, all orchestrated with the slick, stylish direction of Steven Soderbergh. The narrative is complex, but never confusing, and the pacing is expertly managed to keep the tension high without overwhelming the viewer. The film also benefits from a sharp, witty script that provides plenty of laugh-out-loud moments.

Visually, "Ocean's Twelve" is a feast for the eyes. The cinematography is stunning, with a vibrant color palette and inventive camera work that make the most of the film's diverse European locations. The costume and set design are equally impressive, contributing to the film's overall sense of glamour and sophistication.

However, what truly sets "Ocean's Twelve" apart is its ability to balance its high-stakes plot with a sense of fun and irreverence. The film never takes itself too seriously, and the result is a heist movie that is as entertaining as it is clever.

In conclusion, "Ocean's Twelve" is a worthy successor to "Ocean's Eleven", and a must-see for fans of the heist genre. It's a film that proves sequels can be just as good, if not better, than the orignal, and a testament to the power of a well-assembled ensemble cast.

Constance Hamilton (1862–1933) was a Canadian suffragist, social reformer, and the first woman elected to the Toronto City Council. She was a prominent figure in the women's suffrage movement in Canada and played a significant role in securing the right to vote for women in Ontario.

== Early Life and Education ==

Hamilton was born in 1862 in Hamilton, Ontario. She was the daughter of a prominent local businessman and was educated at the Hamilton Ladies' College. After her education, she moved to Toronto, where she became involved in various social reform movements.

== Career and Activism ==

In the early 20th century, Hamilton became a leading figure in the women's suffrage movement in Canada. She was a member of the Women's Suffrage Association of Canada and served as its president from 1909 to 1917. During her tenure, she led numerous campaigns and protests to secure the right to vote for women.

In 1920, Hamilton made history by becomeing the first woman elected to the Toronto City Council. She served on the council for three years, advocating for social reform and women's rights. During her time in office, she championed issues such as public health, education, and housing.

Hamilton was also a founding member of the National Council of Women of Canada, an organization dedicated to improving the status of women. She served as the council's president from 1918 to 1920.

== Legacy ==

Hamilton's contributions to the women's suffrage movement and her groundbreaking role in municipal politics have left a lasting legacy. Her efforts helped pave the way for the political participation of women in Canada. In recognition of her work, a park in Toronto was named in her honor in 1973.

Hamilton passed away in 1933, but her legacy continues to inspire and influence. She is remembered as a pioneering figure in Canadian women's history, and her work continues to be celebrated and studied.
