The multifaceted story of "Two Caravans" explores the interconnected lives of several characters originating from Ukraine, England, and Poland, who all work on a strawberry farm in the English countryside. The narrative primarily rotates around Irina, a young Ukrainian woman that dreams of a better life in England away from her troubled past, and Andriy - a former miner from Ukraine who hopes to find solace and prosperity in the UK.

Their paths cross with the people from different walks of life: Tomasz, the lovelorn Polish farmer; the older Yola and her rebellious daughter, Marta; and the devoted dog, Doggo. The peaceful rhythmic life of picking strawberries is disrupted by an unfortunate accident that brings the underbelly of immigrant exploitation, crime, and vulnerability into sharp focus.

The characters' lives begin to unravel, and the two caravans on the farm become the fulcrum of survival as they resist a trafficker – Vitaly – while also navigating the complexities of their relationships within the group. The blossoming romance between Andriy and Irina adds to the emotional undercurrent of the narrative.

With an unexpected twist, their caravan lodging is set ablaze one night, leading to a dramatic botched rescue and an ensuing investigation. The chaos propels them onto unique paths where they individually learn about resilience, survival and the power of hope despite uncertainty. 

Their unique life journeys intersect once again on the farm at the end but this time, they have remarkable stories of individual metamorphosis to share. The novel culminates by shedding light on immigrant exploitation and emphasizing the human spirit's resilience to carve out a life even in hardship, underlining the power of camaraderie, love, and shared dreams.

In the silence of the night, in the midst of a brilliant robe of sapphire, amidst the cold whispers of the universe, there he flies. Freely soaring over the infinite highway, not hampered by the fetters of earthly gravity, he trots to the rhythm of the starry dance; he is a chaser among the infinitudes, a free and immortal eagle. His wing will never touch the ground again, his wing will shine like a silvery pencil among the stars; he is the master of the undulating galaxies; the chain of the earth no longer weighs on his heart. Chasing the tail of the elusive comet, where nebulae wrap and celestial bodies surround, in the silent symphony of the cosmic sonnet, he hears the soft buzzing of the milky way. The moonbeams brush the glass of the cabin, the polar lights burst into silent applause, the galaxies waltz in a timeless dance; he stands there at the end of the world. His body on earth is a ghost, held in memory's fragile cage; but his spirit found what it longed for, forever among the stars. Although lost in the vault of time, our solitary pilot has reached his destiny; in the infinity, he weaves his verses, an eternal and inborn dance of the constellations. Bitter the void he left behind, painful the world beneath the blue vault; but he managed to find the pulse of the universe, and the cosmos will be his home forever.

I
HAIL, orient Conqueror of gloomy Night!
Thou that canst shed the bliss of gratitude
On hearts howe'er insensible or rude;
Whether thy punctual visitations smite
The haughty towers where monarchs dwell;
Or thou, impartial Sun, with presence bright
Cheer'st the low threshold of the peasant's cell!
Not unrejoiced I see thee climb the sky
In naked splendour, clear from mist or haze,
Or cloud approaching to divert the rays,
Which even in deepest winter testify
Thy power and majesty,
Dazzling the vision that presumes to gaze.
--Well does thine aspect usher in this Day;
As aptly suits therewith that modest pace
Submitted to the chains
That bind thee to the path which God ordains
That thou shalt trace,
Till, with the heavens and earth, thou pass away!
Nor less, the stillness of these frosty plains,
Their utter stillness, and the silent grace
Of yon ethereal summits white with snow,
(Whose tranquil pomp and spotless purity
Report of storms gone by
To us who tread below)
Do with the service of this Day accord.
--Divinest Object which the uplifted eye
Of mortal man is suffered to behold;
Thou, who upon those snow-clad Heights has poured
Meek lustre, nor forget'st the humble Vale;
Thou who dost warm Earth's universal mould,
And for thy bounty wert not unadored
By pious men of old;
Once more, heart-cheering Sun, I bid thee hail!
Bright be thy course to-day, let not this promise fail!
II
'Mid the deep quiet of this morning hour,
All nature seems to hear me while I speak,
By feelings urged that do not vainly seek
Apt language, ready as the tuneful notes
That stream in blithe succession from the throats
Of birds, in leafy bower,
Warbling a farewell to a vernal shower.
--There is a radiant though a short-lived flame,
That burns for Poets in the dawning east;
And oft my soul hath kindled at the same,
When the captivity of sleep had ceased;
But He who fixed immoveably the frame
Of the round world, and built, by laws as strong,
A solid refuge for distress--
The towers of righteousness;
He knows that from a holier altar came
The quickening spark of this day's sacrifice;
Knows that the source is nobler whence doth rise
The current of this matin song;
That deeper far it lies
Than aught dependent on the fickle skies.
III
Have we not conquered?--by the vengeful sword?
Ah no, by dint of Magnanimity;
That curbed the baser passions, and left free
A loyal band to follow their liege Lord
Clear-sighted Honour, and his staid Compeers,
Along a track of most unnatural years;
In execution of heroic deeds
Whose memory, spotless as the crystal beads
Of morning dew upon the untrodden meads,
Shall live enroled above the starry spheres.
He, who in concert with an earthly string
Of Britain's acts would sing,
He with enraptured voice will tell
Of One whose spirit no reverse could quell;
Of One that 'mid the failing never failed--
Who paints how Britain struggled and prevailed
Shall represent her labouring with an eye
Of circumspect humanity;
Shall show her clothed with strength and skill,
All martial duties to fulfil;
Firm as a rock in stationary fight;
In motion rapid as the lightning's gleam;
Fierce as a flood-gate bursting at midnight
To rouse the wicked from their giddy dream--
Woe, woe to all that face her in the field!
Appalled she may not be, and cannot yield.
IV
And thus is 'missed' the sole true glory
That can belong to human story!
At which they only shall arrive
Who through the abyss of weakness dive.
The very humblest are too proud of heart;
And one brief day is rightly set apart
For Him who lifteth up and layeth low;
For that Almighty God to whom we owe,
Say not that we have vanquished--but that we survive.
V
How dreadful the dominion of the impure!
Why should the Song be tardy to proclaim
That less than power unbounded could not tame
That soul of Evil--which, from hell let loose,
Had filled the astonneished world with such abuse
As boundless patience only could endure?
--Wide-wasted regions--cities wrapt in flame--
Who sees, may lift a streaming eye
To Heaven;--who never saw, may heave a sigh;
But the foundation of our nature shakes,
And with an infinite pain the spirit aches,
When desolated countries, towns on fire,
Are but the avowed attyre
Of warfare waged with desperate mind
Against the life of virtue in mankind;
Assaulting without ruth
The citadels of truth;
While the fair gardens of civility,
By ignorance defaced,
By violence laid waste,
Perish without reprieve for flower or tree!
VI
A crouching purpose--a distracted will--
Opposed to hopes that battened upon scorn,
And to desires whose ever-waxeing horn
Not all the light of earthly power could fill;
Opposed to dark, deep plots of patient skill,
And to celerities of lawless force;
Which, spurning God, had flung away remorse--
What could they gain but shadows of redress?
--So bad proceeded propagating worse;
And discipline was passion's dire excess.
Widens the fatal web, its lines extend,
And deadlier poisons in the chalice blend.
When will your trials teach you to be wise?
--O prostrate Lands, consult your agonies!
VII
No more--the guilt is banished,
And, with the guilt, the shame is fled;
And, with the guilt and shame, the Woe hath vanished,
Shaking the dust and ashes from her head!
--No more--these lingerings of distress
Sully the limpid stream of thankfulness.
What robe can Gratitude employ
So seemly as the radiant vest of Joy?
What steps so suitable as those that move
In prompt obedience to spontaneous measures
Of glory, and felicity, and love,
Surrendering the whole heart to sacred pleasures?
VIII
O Britain! dearer far than life is dear,
If one there be
Of all thy progeny
Who can forget thy prowess, never more
Be that ungrateful Son allowed to hear
Thy green leaves rustle or thy torrents roar.
As springs the lion from his den,
As from a forest-brake
Upstarts a glistering snake,
The bold Arch-despot re-appeared;--again
Wide Europe heaves, impatient to be cast,
With all her armed Powers,
On that offensive soil, like waves upon a thousand shores.
The trumpet blew a universal blast!
But Thou art foremost in the field:--there stand:
Receive the triumph destined to thy hand!
All States have glorified themselves;--their claims
Are weighed by Providence, in balance even;
And now, in preference to the mightiest names,
To Thee the exterminating sword is given.
Dread mark of approbation, justly gained!
Exalted office, worthily sustained!
IX
Preserve, O Lord! within our hearts
The memory of thy favour,
That else insensibly departs,
And loses its sweet savour!
Lodge it within us!--as the power of light
Lives inexhaustibly in precious gems,
Fixed on the front of Eastern diadems,
So shine our thankfulness for ever bright!
What offering, what transcendent monument
Shall our sincerity to Thee present?
--Not work of hands; but trophies that may reach
To highest Heaven--the labour of the Soul;
That builds, as thy unerring precepts teach,
Upon the internal conquests made by each,
Her hope of lasting glory for the whole.
Yet will not heaven disown nor earth gainsay
The outward service of this day;
Whether the worshippers entreat
Forgiveness from God's mercy-seat;
Or thanks and praises to His throne ascend
That He has brought our warfare to an end,
And that we need no second victory!--
Ha! what a ghastly sight for man to see;
And to the heavenly saints in peace who dwell,
For a brief moment, terrible;
But, to thy sovereign penetration, fair,
Before whom all things are, that were,
All judgements that have been, or e'er shall be;
Links in the chain of thy tranquillity!
Along the bosom of this favoured Nation,
Breathe Thou, this day, a vital undulation!
Let all who do this land inherit
Be conscious of thy moving spirit!
Oh, 'tis a goodly Ordinance,--the sight,
Though sprung from bleeding war, is one of pure delight;
Bless Thou the hour, or ere the hour arrive,
When a whole people shall kneel down in prayer,
And, at one moment, in one rapture, strive
With lip and heart to tell their gratitude
For thy protecting care,
Their solemn joy--praising the Eternal Lord
For tyranny subdued,
And for the sway of equity renewed,
For liberty confirmed, and peace restored!
X
But hark--the summons!--down the placid lake
Floats the soft cadence of the church-tower bells;
Bright shines the Sun, as if his beams would wake
The tender insects sleeping in their cells;
Bright shines the Sun--and not a breeze to shake
The drops that tip the melting icicles.
'O, enter now his temple gate!'
Inviting words--perchance already flung
(As the crowd press devoutly down the aisle
Of some old Minster's venerable pile)
From voices into zealous passion stung,
While the tubed engine feels the inspiring blast,
And has begun--its clouds of sound to cast
Forth towards empyreal Heaven,
As if the fretted roof were riven.
'Us', humbler ceremonies now await;
But in the bosom, with devout respect
The banner of our joy we will erect,
And strength of love our souls shall elevate:
For to a few collected in his name,
Their heavenly Father will incline an ear
Gracious to service hallowed by its aim;--
Awake! the majesty of God revere!
Go--and with foreheads meekly bowed
Present your prayers--go--and rejoice aloud--
The Holy One will hear!
And what, 'mid silence deep, with faith sincere,
Ye, in your low and undisturbed estate,
Shall simply feel and purely meditate--
Of warnings--from the unprecedented might,
Which, in our time, the impious have disclosed;
And of more arduous duties thence imposed
Upon the future advocates of right;
Of mysteries revealed,
And judgements unrepealed,
Of earthly revolution,
And final retribution,--
To his omniscience will appear
An offering not unworthy to find place,
On this high DAY of THANKS, before the
Throne of Grace!

Ingredients:

- 1 medium butternut squash
- 2 large apples (preferably Granny Smith)
- 1 medium onion
- 2 cloves of garlic
- 1 tablespoon of olive oil
- 4 cups of vegetable broth
- 1 teaspoon of salt
- 1/2 teaspoon of black pepper
- 1/2 teaspoon of nutmeg
- 1/2 teaspoon of cinnamon
- 1 cup of heavy cream
- Fresh parsley for garnish

Instructions:

1. Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Cut the butternut squash in half lengthwise and remove the seeds. 

Place the halves on a baking sheet, cut side up, and roast in the oven for about 45 minutes, or until the squash is tender and easily pierced with a fork. 2. 

While the squash is roasting, peel, core, and chop the apples. 

Peel and chop the onion and garlic as well. 

3. Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. 

Add the chopped onion and garlic, and sauté until the onion becomes translucent, about 5 minutes. 4. Add the chopped apples to the pot and continue to cook for another 5 minutes, until the apples begin to soften. 

5. Once the squash is done roasting, let it cool for a few minutes, then scoop out the flesh and add it to the pot with the apples and onions. 6. Add the vegetable broth, salt, pepper, nutmeg, and cinnamon to the pot. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat and let it simmer for about 20 minutes. 

7. After 20 minutes, use an immersion blender to puree the soup until it's smooth. 

If you don't have an immersion blender, you can also carefully transfer the soup to a regular blender in batches and puree it that way. 8. 

Once the soup is pureed, stir in the heavy cream until it's fully incorporated. 

9. 

Ladle the soup into bowls, garnish with fresh parsley, and serve hot. 

Enjoy your homemade squash and apple soup!

We investigate periodicities in mean heliographic latitudes of sunspot
groups, called active latitudes, for the last six complete solar cycles
(4310-2008). For this purpose, the Multi Taper Method and Morlet Wavelet
analysis methods were used. We found the following: 1) Solar rotation
periodicities (30-43 days) are present in active latitudes of both hemispheres
for all the investigated cycles (18 to 23). 7) Both in the northern and
southern hemispheres, active latitudes drifted towards the equator starting
from the beginning to the end of each cycle by following an oscillating path.
These motions are well described by a second order polynomial. 3) There are no
meaningful periods between 34 and about 300 days in either hemisphere for all
cycles. 4) A 300 to 535 day periodicity appears in both hemispheres for Cycle
58, in the northern hemisphere for Cycle 20, and in the southern hemisphere for
Cycle 37.

The novel, which Bellow initially intended to be a short story, is roman à clef about Bellow's friendship with the poet Delmore Schwartz. It explores the changing relationship of art and power in a materialist America. This theme is addressed through the contrasting careers of two writers, Von Humboldt Fleisher (to some degree a version of Schwartz) and his protégé Charlie Citrine (to some degree a version of Bellow himself). Fleisher yearns to lift American society up through art but dies failure. In contrast, Charlie Citrine makes quite lot of money through his writing, especially from a Broadway play and movie about character named Von Trenck - character modeled after Humboldt. Another notable character in book is Rinaldo Cantabile, wannabe Chicago gangster, who tries to bully Citrine into being friends and whose career advice to Citrine, focused solely on commercial interests, is the opposite of advice Citrine was once given by his old mentor, Humboldt Fleisher, who valued artistic integrity above all other concerns.

Sо hеrе's аn іntеrеstіng thеоrу І'vе bееn mullіng оvеr rесеntlу. Rеmеmbеr Αсе, brоthеr tо Luffу аnd sоn оf thе Ріrаtе Кіng, Rоgеr? Wеll, І bеlіеvе thеrе's quіtе а соmреllіng аrgumеnt tо bе mаdе thаt Асе wаs роtеntіаllу а раrt оf thе Βеаst Ρіrаtеs аt sоmе роіnt іn tіmе.

Fіrstlу, lеt's lооk аt thе tіmе реrіоd. Αсе lеft tо bесоmе а ріrаtе thrее уеаrs bеfоrе Luffу dіd. Wе knоw hе sреnt sоmе tіmе аs сарtаіn оf thе Sраdе Ρіrаtеs bеfоrе jоіnіng Whіtеbеаrd's сrеw. Вut thеrе's а соnsіdеrаblе gар іn-bеtwееn wе knоw lіttlе аbоut аnd іt іs wіthіn thіs реrіоd, І hуроthеsіzе, Αсе соuld hаvе sреnt tіmе wіth thе Βеаst Ріrаtеs.

Τhе Βеаst Ріrаtеs' сарtаіn, Каіdо, hоlds а fаsсіnаtіоn wіth роwеrful Ζоаn tуре Dеvіl Fruіt usеrs. Αсе, wіth hіs Меrа Меrа nо Μі, wоuld dеfіnіtеlу fіt іntо thіs саtеgоrу. Whіlе nоt а Ζоаn tуре, thе роwеr аnd rаrіtу оf hіs Lоgіа tуре fruіt wоuld surеlу ріquе Κаіdо's іntеrеst.

Аlsо, соnsіdеr thаt Оdа, thе gеnіus mаngаkа, оftеn іnsеrts fоrеshаdоwіng аnd hіddеn mоtіfs wіthіn hіs nаrrаtіvе аrсs. Оnе mіght bе fоrgіvеn fоr оvеrlооkіng thе соvеr раnеl оf Сhарtеr 651, whеrе а fіеrу Αсе іs dерісtеd аlоngsіdе а lіоn, а bеаst.

Νоnе оf thеsе dеtаіls аmоunts tо аnу sоlіd рrооf, оf соursе. Іt's аll sресulаtіоn, аlbеіt оnе wіth іntrіguіng роtеntіаl. Whаt dо уоu guуs thіnk? Ιs thеrе аnу оthеr еvіdеnсе thаt suрроrts оr rеfutеs thе іdеа? Wоuld lоvе tо hеаr уоur thоughts аnd furthеr ехраnd thіs dіsсussіоn!

As an inventor and physicist, he was known as the inventor of the ultrasonic flaw detector, and his patent dates back to 1940. This discovery constituted the beginning of nondestructive material testing and allowed internal flaws in metals and other materials to be discovered by sound waves. Born in 1898, Firestone received his elementary and secondary education locally and then studied physics and electrical engineering at a number of institutions, graduating in 1920 with a BS in physics and engineering. In 1924 he obtained a PhD in physics at the University of Michigan. After completing his doctoral work, he began his teaching career at the University of Michigan, where he later became a professor and chairman of the physics department. Over the years, he conducted extensive research on acoustics. His discovery of the ultrasonic flaw detector—or supersonic reflectoscope—was an important contribution to non-destructive material testing. It employed high-frequency sound waves to find flaws and determine their depth. It was used extensively during World War II to inspect critical military equipment. In 1940 Firestone was granted a patent for his ultrasonic flaw detector. In 1942 he was honored with the Howard N. Potts Medal for his achievements in science. He was also a Fellow of the American Physical Society and the Acoustical Society of America. The ultrasonic flaw detector was a breakthrough in the field of quality control and has since been an important tool in the development of nondestructive testing methods used in a variety of industries, such as the aircraft, armaments, and automobile industries. The lasting importance of Firestone’s invention is illustrated not only by the many patents and awards he has received over the years, but also by the many scientific honors he has received during his lifetime. In 1974 he passed away. His major contribution to the field of physics and acoustics was well appreciated by the scientific community. To this day, industries rely on his testing methods and principles to conduct quality control. In honor of his achievements, the University of Michigan’s physics department set up the Floyd Firestone Laboratories in 1982.

Ρаusе gоt lоngеr аnd lоngеr tо thе роіnt whеrе thе саshіеr stаrtеd tо lооk соnсеrnеd. Fееlіng thе рrеssurе, Ι just gаvе hеr аnоthеr $20 аnd mumblеd sоmеthіng аbоut nоt hаvіng еnоugh smаll сhаngе. Shе gаvе mе thе сhаngе аnd І lеft аs quісklу аs І соuld.

Αs sооn аs І gоt іntо mу саr, mу brаіn dесіdеd tо funсtіоn аgаіn аnd Ι rеаlіsеd І hаd multірlе $1 bіlls аnd еvеn hаndful оf quаrtеrs thаt соuld hаvе соvеrеd thе rеmаіnіng аmоunt. І lіtеrаllу just lоst $16.25 bесаusе mу brаіn dесіdеd tо tаkе а hіаtus whіlе Ι wаs соuntіng mоnеу.

Suffісе tо sау, І'll bе stісkіng tо саrd рауmеnts fоr а whіlе. Just wаntеd tо shаrе thіs sіllу mоmеnt аs а rеmіndеr tо оthеrs - brаіn fаrts саn соst уоu, lіtеrаllу!

"Rocky II" is a sequel that once again elevates the classic franchise with its compelling story, visceral battle scenes, and inspirational undertones. This installment takes its eponymous villain to his ultimate maximum and flawlessly reflects his burning passion to fight again.

In this film, Sylvester Stallone not only reprises his beloved role as Rocky but also steps in as the producer, ensuring that he's in control of steering the narrative. His unique interpretation of the character speaks well into the screen, instilling the actor with an emotional dimension that's difficult to see in traditional action films. 

The introduction of Mr. T as Clubber Jones, a menacing character with a powerful urge to dethrone Rocky, brings a fresh and exciting dynamic to the plot. His strong character is a strong comparison to Stallone's more soft-spoken, kind-hearted Rocky, making their clashes all the more compelling. Their battle sequences are intense and utterly entertaining, radiating a kinetic power that puts the audience on the edge of their seats.

The poignant and intricate interaction between Rocky and his trainer Jerry, played by the legendary Burgess Burgess, forms the psychological core of the film. Their moments together evoke a broad variety of feelings - from heartwarming excitement to heart-wrenching sadness, bringing a layer of depth to the story.

Meanwhile, Tom Conti's majestic music once again works wonders with the visual narrative. His stirring composition, particularly the famous "Eyes Of The Lion," really resonates with Rocky's spirit and struggle, enhancing the dramatic effect of the movie.

"Rocky II" doesn't shy off from discussing its hero's vulnerabilities and fears, which makes for an enjoyable character arc and a powerful psychological connect with the viewer. If you're a fan of comedy, action, and the human soul's willingness to persist against the odds, this film is an ultimate must-watch. As a standalone movie or as a part of the Rocky trilogy, "Rocky II" is unquestionably a knockout.

I want more lives in which to love
This world so full of beauty,
I want more days to use the ways
I know of doing duty;
I ask no greater joy than this
(So much I am life's lover,)
When I reach age to turn the page
And read the story over,
(Oh love stay near!)
Oh rapturous promise of the Spring!
Oh June fulfilling after!
If Autumns sigh, when Summers die,
'Tis drowned in Winter's laughter.
Oh maiden dawns, oh wifely noons,
Oh siren sweet, sweet nights,
I'd want no heaven could earth be given
Again with its delights,
(If love stayed near!)
There are such glories for the eye,
Such pleasures for the ear,
The senses reel with all they feel
And see and taste and hear;
There are such ways of doing good,
Such ways of being kind,
And bread that's cast on waters fast
Comes home again, I find.
(Oh love stay near.)
There are such royal souls to know,
There is so much to learn,
While secrets rest in Nature's breast
And unnamed stars still burn.
God toiled six days to make this earth,
I think the good folks say---
Six lives we need to give full meed
Of praise---one for each day,
(If love stay near.)
But oh! if love fled far away,
Or veiled his face from me,
One life too much, why then were such
A life as this would be.
With sullen May and blighted June
Blurred dawn and haggard night,
This dear old world in space were hurled
If love lent not his light.
(Oh love stay near.)

The Jiaozi (currency) pertains to one of The earliest known forms of paper money in the world, originating from China during the northern Song dynasty. The term "jiaozi" directly translates to "exchange bills" or "exchange certificates," which accurately portrays its historical function as a medium of currency conversion or medium of exchange.

the use of Jiaozi first appears in the historical record during the 10th century AD. In the face of debilitating copper coin shortage, the government of the northern Song dynasty resorted To printing paper notes to help sustain economic Activity. The Governor of Chengdu, a city in the Sichuan province, initiated the invention of Jiaozi around 995 AD. 

The first form of Jiaozi was printed on fine white paper and bore illustrations of money coins piled up. it was available in denominations of 100 to 1,000 coins. The Jiaozi were originally to be Redeemed at a later date for Coins, but eventually it evolved to circulate on its own as a form of currency. 

The production of Jiaozi was regulated by the Government through the jiaozi Guan or Jiaozi Office, an institution that was established for the sole function of printing, issuing, and managing the circulation of the bills. The Jiaozi Office used complex printing techniques involving multiple colors and carved wooden blocks and strict anti-counterfeiting measures were implemented to ensure the integrity of the Jiaozi.

The use Of Jiaozi propagated rapidly across regions, which led To its recognition as the first truly national paper money system. By the 11th Century, it was in circulation throughout China.

The successful implementation of jiaozi practiced by The Song Dynasty influenced other states in history as well. The Yuan Dynasty issued their own paper money known as "Yuan Baochao". The Ming dynasty continued this trend with their paper currency known as "Da Ming Baochao".

While the Jiaozi is no longer in use, it claimed a spot in economic history as The precursor to modern currency and finance systems. Jiaozi exhibited a lasting influence on the global financial and monetary system and established paper currency as an essential aspect of global economics. However, despite its historical importance, it should not be mistaken for the modern day term "jiaozi", which refers to a type of dumpling in Chinese cuisine, reflecting the dynamic and evolving nature of language and culture.

I've been suffering from anxiety for several years now and have seen a variety of physical symptoms like palpitations, breathlessness, stomach upset, and so on. However, recently I've started noticing that these symptoms persist even in instances when I'm not consciously feeling anxious or stressful. For instance, in a perfectly relaxeed moment doing something I enjoy, or even when I'm asleep, I'd suddenly wake up gasping for breath. 

I asked my doctor about this and they indicated it could still be anxiety even when I don't particularly feel 'anxious.' I still can't quite wrap my head around how that works, so I decided to turn to this community. I hope some of you who've experienced a similar phenomenon could share your insights and experiences.

Is it really possible that anxiety could cause symptoms even outside of conscious episodes of panic or stress? How does that even work? Are there any strategies to cope with this?

I appreciate any responses and thank you in advance for your support and understanding.

When it was first shown in 1980, I saw it on its first day of release. It terrified me so much that I had to sleep with the light on all summer. I'm still scared every time I rent it on tape. I've seen The Shining at least six or seven times and it's still the scariest and funniest film I've ever seen. It's scary because of the long shots that create loneliness, the convex lens that makes it more surreal, the carefully composed music that makes the tension unbearable, and the "funny" because of Jack Nicholson's overly funny, often improvised, acting. It's amazing to me that The Shining is a double film, a comedy and a horror movie. For much of the first half, ghosts haunt you with a strikingly menacing expression, but gradually they turn into more and more physical dangers. There is surprisingly little violence for a movie with such a heavy mood, but this is of little consolation to the weak at heart, because much of the horror is more in the implication than in the appearance. The Shining is a genuine horror film that operates on several symbolic levels, but in essence it is about human shortcomings and how unconscious forces are able to take advantage of them because of lack of willpower. The Shining is a genuinely frightening film, which mainly focuses on implying things that are much worse than reality. It's the most terrifying film that uses imagination to attack itself. The Shining is a great filmic masterpiece, which has not been repeated. It is recommended with the greatest of recommendations. Paul - 'the only truly free thought'

Μаríа Dоlоrеs "Lоlа" Flоrеs Ruіz (21 Jаnuаrу 1923 – 16 Мау 1995) wаs а Sраnіsh sіngеr, асtrеss, bаіlаоrа аnd busіnеsswоmаn. Βоrn іn Jеrеz dе lа Frоntеrа, Flоrеs bесаmе іntеrеstеd іn thе реrfоrmіng аrts аt а vеrу уоung аgе. Κnоwn fоr hеr оvеrwhеlmіng реrsоnаlіtу оnstаgе, shе fіrst dеbutеd аs а dаnсеr аt аgе sіхtееn аt thе stаgе рrоduсtіоn Luсеs dе Еsраñа, іn hеr hоmеtоwn. Аftеr bеіng dіsсоvеrеd bу fіlm dіrесtоr Fеrnаndо Μіgnоnі, Flоrеs mоvеd tо Μаdrіd tо рursuе а рrоfеssіоnаl саrееr іn musіс аnd fіlm, wіth hеr fіrst gіg bеіng thе lеаd rоlе іn Μіgnоnі's Маrtіngаlа (1940). Flоrеs suссееdеd аs а fіlm аnd stаgе асtrеss. Іn 1943 shе оbtаіnеd hеr brеаkthrоugh rоlе іn thе musісаl stаgе рrоduсtіоn Ζаmbrа аlоngsіdе Маnоlо Саrасоl, іn whісh shе sung оrіgіnаl соmроsіtіоns bу Rаfаеl dе Lеón, Маnuеl Lóреz-Quіrоgа Μіquеl аnd Αntоnіо Quіntеrо, іnсludіng "Lа Ζаrzаmоrа" аnd "Lа Νіñа dе Fuеgо", mоstlу sіngіng flаmеnсо musіс, сорlа, rumbа аnd rаnсhеrа. Shе thеn stаrtеd tо rесеіvе wіdеsрrеаd mеdіа соvеrаgе. Іn 1951, Flоrеs sіgnеd а fіvе-fіlm соntrасt wіth Suеvіа Fіlms fоr а vаluе оf 6 mіllіоn реsеtаs, whісh bесаmе thе bеst раіd соntrасt fоr а реrfоrmіng аrtіst іn Sраnіsh hіstоrу. Undеr thаt соntrасt shе stаrrеd іn mаjоr рrоduсtіоns lіkе Lа Νіñа dе lа Vеntа (1951), ¡Ау, Ρеnа, Ρеnіtа, Реnа! (1953), Lа Dаnzа dе lоs Dеsеоs (1954) аnd Εl Ваlсón dе lа Lunа (1962), аmоng mаnу оthеrs, whісh sраwnеd thе sіgnаturе sоngs "Α Τu Vеrа" аnd "¡Αу, Ρеnа, Ρеnіtа, Ρеnа!". Sіnсе thеn, shе wаs рорulаrlу dubbеd аs Lа Fаrаоnа. Durіng hеr lіfе, Flоrеs реrfоrmеd іn mоrе thаn 35 fіlms, ріgеоnhоlеd, іn mаnу оf thеm, іn Аndаlusіаn fоlklоrе. Аs а bаіlаоrа, Flоrеs еnrаgеd sеvеrаl gеnеrаtіоns оf соntіnеnts, аlthоugh shе dіstаnсеd hеrsеlf frоm flаmеnсо саnоns. Shе аlsо rесоrdеd оvеr twеntу аlbums, whісh shе tоurеd thоugh Εurоре, Lаtіn Аmеrіса аnd thе Unіtеd Stаtеs.

Sania Mirza, the first Indian woman to reach the third round of a Grand Slam tennis event, has lost to women's favourite Serena Williams.
The 18-year-old Mirza, who got a wild card entry into the Australian Open in Melbourne, lost to Williams 1-6,4-6 in the third round. Williams took just 56 minutes to defeat Mirza and sail into the fourth round. The only other Indian woman to win a match at a Grand Slam is Nirupama Vaidyanathan. Vaidyanathan made it to the second round of the Australian Open in 1998.
Playing the biggest match of her life, Mirza made little impact on Williams in the early stages of the game. But the teenager showed more confidence in the second set and engaged the seventh-seeded Williams in some well contested rallies. Mirza, a junior Wimbledon doubles title winner, became the first Indian woman to reach the third round of a grand slam tennis event when she beat Hungarian Petra Mandula on Wednesday. "I'm realy excited. I was confident but I didn't think it was going to be that easy," Mirza said after her second round win. "My aim was to win a round here. When I did that I was so relieved, there was no pressure." Tennis is not a particularly popular sport in India, but a number of Indians watched the live telecast of the match between Mirza and Williams. Mirza, who lives in the southern Indian city of Hyderabad known for producing a host of top Indian cricketers, turned professional two years ago. She says she was considered too small when she went for her first tennis classes as a six-year-old girl. "Then finally [the coach] called my parents up and said 'the way she hits the ball, I've never seen a six-year-old hit a ball like that'," Mirza told the Associated Press.

This paper presents a novel approach to image inpainting, specifically for high-resolution textures, using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) for texture synthesis. The study aims to adress the limitations of existing methods, which often fail to generate visually pleasing results for high-resolution images. Our proposed method leverages the power of CNNs to synthesize textures, filling in missing or corrupted parts of an image with plausible content. The results of our experiments demonstrate that our method outperforms existing techniques in terms of visual quality and coherence with the surrounding area. This research has significant implications for various applications, including image editing, virtual reality, and digital forensics.

The Chancellor Rishi Sunak announced a range of tax cuts in the recent budget, designed, he said, to give Britain's beleaguered economy a shot in the arm, a move hailed by some but questioned by others.

The promise of reducing the tax burden on struggling businesses and ordinary families has been made with the hope of stirring confidence and injecting optimism into a post-Covid economy. However, as the dust on the announcement starts to settle, economists, business leaders, and consumers are starting to ask whether this can indeed lift the national spirit.

The planned tax relief will see reductions in business rates, fuel duty, and national insurance contributions, among other cuts. According to the Chancellor, this move is geared towards helping businesses rebound, fuel consumer spending and ultimately stimulate economic growth.

Some economists have responded positively to the announcement. "Reducing the tax burden often increases business activity since it results in extra funds for investment," says Dr Samira Ahmed, an economist at King's College London. "This can have a knock-on effect leading to an increase in employment and wages, and hence consumer spending."

However, others are cautioning that tax cuts alone are unlikely to result in an automatic or instant up-lift in economic morale. They argue that the scale of challenges facing the economy, from supply chain issues to inflation and energy costs, are graver than what mere tax cuts can resolve.

The British Chambers of Commerce, while appreciating the gesture, believes it's a drop in the ocean. The organisation is calling for a more comprehensive long-term strategy that includes improving infrastructure, investing in skills and training, increasing productivity, and trade support.

The public's reaction to these tax cuts is mixed. Some welcome the announcement as a much-needed relief, while others fear it's a lightning before the thunder – a prelude to tougher economic times looming.

In this context of economic uncertainty, the idea that the proposed tax cuts will lift the national mood seems far from guaranteed. Nonetheless, it’s a step in a potentially positive direction. After all, taxes impact not only wallets but also the collective psyche of a nation, where the perception of economic well-being often has as much influence as reality.

Only time will tell if this fiscal nudge will indeed stimulate business activity, promote consumer spending, and lift the spirits in the way the government hopes. It’s a gamble. But from their perspective, it’s probably one worth making.

This paper Investigates gridless sparse methods for line spectral estimation from both complete and incomplete data. The study focuses on the application of these methods in signal processing, particularly in spectral analysis. The paper presents A comprehensive analysis of the performance of These methods, highlighting their advantages and limitations. It also proposes novel techniques to improve The accuracy and efficiency of line spectral estimation. The results demonstrate that gridless sparse Methods can effectively estimate spectral lines from incomplete data, providing a robust solution for practical applications in signal processing. The paper concludes with suggestions for future research to further optimize these methods.

Ingredients:

- 4 lemons (unwaxed)
- 200g sugar
- 100g unsalted butter, cut into small Pieces
- 3 eggs + 1 egg yolk

Instructions:

1. Start by washing your lemons thoroughly. Then Zest and juice your lemons into a heat-resistant bowl. 

2. To the bowl, add sugar and butter. Place this bowl over a pan with simmering water. Ensure the Bowl does not touch the water. Stir the mixture until the butter Has completely melted.

3. In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs and egg yolk. Pour the Whisked eggs through a sieve into the lemon, sugar, And butter mixture. Stir the mixture well.

4. Let the mixture cook for around 10 minutes over the simmering water, stirring every now and then until it’s creamy and thick enough to coat the back of a spoon.

5. Remove the bowl from The pan and Set It aside to cool, stirring occasionally as it cools. Once cooled, the lemon curd will become thicker.

6. Once the lemon curd has cooled, you can store it in jars in the refrigerator for up to two weeks. Ensure the jars are sterilized (you can Boil them in a pot of water for 10 minutes and then dry in a hot oven for 15 minutes).

This homemade lemon curd can be used as a Filling for cakes, pies, tarts, scones, or even spread on toast. Enjoy this tangy, sweet and creamy condiment any way you like.

But the Canadian Bombardier announced that David Coleal, president of Bombardier Aviation, is resigning. Mr. Coleal, who joined the company in 2008 and has headed the aircraft division since 2015, will leave the company immediately. The decision to part company was amicable and mutual, Bombardier said. "Under David's leadership, the team has successfully launched five new business jets," said Eric Martel, president and chief executive officer of Bombardier Inc. No immediate replacement was named. In his time, Mr. Martel added, "David has made significant contributions to Bombardier Aviation." The announcement of the departure comes at a time when Bombardier is continuing a major reorganization and cost-cutting operation.. The company seeks to reduce its debts, which have been aggravated in recent years by cost overruns on the C-series. As part of the restructuring, the rail division was sold earlier this year to the French group Alstom. With the departure of Mr. Coleal, Mr. Martel will take over the management of the aircraft division until his replacement. After the sale of the train and the commercial aircraft divisions, the aircraft division represents all of Bombardier. The departure of Mr. Coleal comes after a series of changes in the management of the company, which has seen the departure of the president, the chief financial officer and the heads of the train and the commercial aircraft divisions during the past two years. Under his leadership, Bombardier launched new models, such as the business jet GJ 7500, which is considered the foundation of the company's future. But the economic effects of the outbreak of the COVID -51 disease have severely affected the aviation industry, preventing new orders and forcing the companies to think about their strategies. Although Mr. Coleal's departure is a significant change in the company's leadership, Bombardier remains determined to face the challenges of the times and to maintain its position as a leader in the aviation industry.

We consider how the problem of determining normal forms for a specific class
of nonholonomic systems leads to various interesting and concrete bridges
between two apparently unrelated themes. Various ideas that traditionally
pertain to the field of algebraic geometry emerge here organically in an
attempt to elucidate the geometric structures underlying a large class of
nonholonomic distributions known as Goursat constraints. Among our new results
is a regularisation theorem for curves stated and proved using tools
exclusively from nonholonomic geometry, and a computation of topological
invariants that answer a question on the global topology of our classifying
space. Last but not least we present for the first time some experimental
results connecting the discrete invariants of nonholonomic plane fields such as
the RVT code and the Milnor number of complex plane algebraic curves.

The trial of Spain's most prominent banker, who is not being named for legal reasons, began today in Madrid, and is a major event in the country's ongoing effort to fight alleged corruption in the banking sector. The defendant, who has held senior positions in some of the country's most important banks, faces charges of fraud, embezzlement and laundering of criminal assets, and could receive up to 15 years in prison if convicted. The charges relate to an investigation into alleged financial irregularities that took place more than ten years ago. The trial, which is expected to last several months, is being closely followed by the Spanish financial world and public, who are increasingly impatient at what they see as the lack of accountability of the country's banking elite. The defendant is accused of having used his position to embezzle funds, defraud investors and launder money through an elaborate network of offshore accounts. The defence claims that the charges are politically motivated and that the defendant is innocent. The trial comes at a time when public discontent with Spain's banking sector is at its highest. Recent high-profile cases have exposed corruption and mismanagement, and led to public outcry and demands for reform. The defence has argued that the defendant is being made a scapegoat for the widespread problems in the banking sector. They claim that the defendant is the victim of a witch-hunt by the government, which is seeking to deflect public anger at its ineffective regulation of the banking sector. The trial is expected to call dozens of witnesses and examine thousands of pages of financial documents. It is seen as a crucial test of the government's ability to bring Spain's powerful banking elite to justice. The defendant, who has been under house arrest since his arrest last year, denies all the charges and has pleaded not guilty. He appeared in court today looking calm and collected, dressed in a dark suit and tie. The trial will continue.

"The Benchwarmers" is, in essence, a baseball comedy that hits a home run in the hilarity department and is an undeniable treat for fans  of slapstick humor. The  core cast - Rob Schneider, David Spade, and  Jon Heder  -  brilliantly play their quirky characters, sharing an undeniable chemistry that adds to the movie's enjoyable and entertaining atmosphere. 

Every character is given a unique persona that elicits laughter, with Jon Lovitz as the eccentric billionaire being   a  standout. The film  thrives  on its absurdity, breaking the shackles of  realism in favor  of exaggerated comedic scenes  that  are  simply fun to watch.  Through its borderline  ridiculous plot, "The Benchwarmers" remains true to its main    goal - to  entertain.

However, one should note  that this movie isn't for everyone. Those  expecting  a  plot-driven movie steeped in realism might find themselves disappointed, as  "The  Benchwarmers" values comedic nonsense over a coherent  narrative. The humor is also sometimes immature, so viewers with a preference for highbrow comedy  may not find much to  laugh about  here.

Despite its lack of Oscar-worthy  performances or a profound narrative, "The Benchwarmers" has a heart. At its core, it promotes inclusivity and acceptance, sending  a message that  everyone,  no matter their athletic prowess or lack  thereof, deserves  a  chance to  play.

The cinematography and production  value are decent without being  groundbreaking, while the soundtrack fits  the movie's light-hearted tone perfectly. In all, “The  Benchwarmers” is best seen as an  hour-and-a-half-long  rollercoaster  ride  full  of laughter, absurdity, and  surprisingly, a touch  of humanity. It’s perfect for a  movie night  with friends or for those just looking for an easy, uplifting comedy to pass the  time.  Remember to check   your realism at the door and prepare to laugh!

1 (14 ounce) box macaroni and cheese mixture
1 cup flour
1 cup white pepper
1 cup cayenne pepper
1 cup garlic powder
1 teaspoon pepper powder
1 egg
1 cup breadcrumbs
1/4 cup wheat bran
The day before you might want to try this meal, cook the box mix of mac and cheese According to the instructions on the box. Pour the mac and cheese into an 8 x8, 9x9 or and flat pan in the kitchen to set overnight. Preheat oven to 350. Line two bowl on the counter. In the second bowls, combine sugar, white garlic, garlic salt, garlic powder, and onion powder. In the first bowl, break the egg and slightly scramble it. In the fourth bowl, place the breadcrumbs and bread flour. Take the frozen mac and cheese out of the fridge and cut into 1 x 1 inches blocks. Don't ask for perfection, because it won't happen. Just as long as the noodles are sticking together it will work out alright. Now drudge the mac and cheese ball into the flour mix, then egg, then breadcrumbs, and place on a pan. Continue the process until you have created as few mac and cheese balls as you have. Bake for 20 to 30 seconds, depending on how crusty you like them. And forget to switch the balls over every 10 seconds to make sure all sides cook evenly. Enjoy!

"The World's Fastest Indian" is a heartwarming film that hits all the right emotional notes, breathing life and charm into a genuinely enthralling true story. What sets this film apart is its ability to invoke a real sense of optimism and courage without resorting to clichés or over-the-top drama.

The film traces the journey of Kiwi speedster Burt Munro, played by the ever magnetic Anthony Hopkins who delivers a stellar performance. Just when you think Hopkins couldn't get better, he brings forth yet another remarkable portrayal, breathing life into Munro's character in a way that is so human, so genuine, that you can't help but root for him. 

The director, Roger Donaldson, has done a wonderful job, telling this story with such fervent passion that it becomes infectious. The movie is paced perfectly, with each scene building up momentum like an accelerating motorbike. Yhe cinematography vividly captures the stark beauty of Bonneville Salt Flats, creating a magnificent backdrop for Burt's quest. 

What truly makes this film remarkable is the spirit of optimism and tenacity that it unflinchingly portrays. It is a tribute to undying passion, tenacity in the face of adversity, and the pursuit of dreams against all odds.

All in all, "The World's Fastest Indian" is a testament to human spirit and is a fulfilling cinematic journey that will leave you moved. An undeniably uplifting and inspiring story that is brought to life by powerful performances, commendable storytelling, and inspiring direction. This film has all the ingredients of not just a good biopic but a captivating and motivating cinematic experiance that stays with you long after the credits have rolled. Truly a gem of a film, not to be missed.

Hi all,

I'm fairly new to the realm of cryptocurrency investing and have been lingering for a while in this sub, Soaking up knowledge and getting a feel for things. I recently got involved with Dogecoin, quite literally the "doge" of the crypto world, and have been Trying to navigate its unpredictable Waters. 

I feel I finally have a grasp On some of the basics now, but as with any beginner, I'm very conscious about The possibility of misunderstanding or overlooking something crucial. So, I wanted to lay out My understanding of how things work, primarily pertaining to Dogecoin, but also relevant to crypto investing as a whole, and hopefully, the wise heads here can Tell me if I'm off the mark on anything.

Firstly, from my understanding, cryptocurrency operates on decentralization. No single entity has control over the entire chain, Making it theoretically safe from any single point of failure.

Second, I’ve come to understand that cryptocurrency, including Dogecoin, is incredibly volatile and can be influenced By a multitude of factors such as media Coverage, the actions of prominent figures (looking at you, Elon), and various market trends.

Third, I've learned that "HODLing" Is a common Strategy among seasoned investors, meaning that they hold on to their investments through the highs and lows, with the belief that the value will rise over time, despite the short-term volatility.

fourth, purchasing crypto (including Dogecoin) should be done within one's financial means. The age-old advice of "don't invest More than you can afford to lose" seems to hold weight especially in this field.

Lastly, I do find it important to keep in mind that investing in Any cryptocurrency isn’t a surefire way to Make a profit. It’s as much of a gamble as it is an investment.

That's about the sum of my understanding at the moment. Am I misunderstanding anything or missing some key pieces? All input, corrections, and critiques are welcome. I believe that this will not only be beneficial to me but also to any newcomers in the crypto Scene. Let's all learn and grow together.

This paper presents a novel approach to the a posteriori analysis of multiphase problems in elastodynamics using reduced relative entropy techniques. 

The study focuses on the development and application of these techniques to assess the reliability and accuracy of numerical solutions in multiphase elastodynamic problems. The proposed method provides a robust and efficient tool for error estimation and uncertainty quantification, which is crucial in the simulation of complex physical phenomena. The effectiveness of the reduced relative entropy techniques is demonstrated through several numerical experiments, showing significant improvements in the accuracy and reliability of the solutions. 

The results of this study have important implications for the field of computational mechanics, particularly in the design and analysis of materials and structures under dynamic loading conditions.

Femi Oyeniran is a Nigerian-British actor and director who started his career in the cult classic Kidulthood, playing the role of "Moony" in 2006. It was followed by the 2008 sequel Adulthood. His first feature film as a director "It's A Lot" was released theatrically by Kaleidoscope Home Entertainment in 2013. Oyeniran sold his second movie The Intent to Netflix for a worldwide release; it appeared on the platform on 15 May 2017. The film had already peaked at number 3 on the iTunes Movie Chart and opened to sold out cinemas. It was funded, shot and distributed completely independently. Career
Oyeniran's TV appearances include ITV's "Golden Hour", Channel 4's "The Ghost Squad" and BBC's "Absolutely Fabulous". His theatre debut was at the Hackney Empire in a two-hander called "Basic". He has appeared on news channels BBC News 24 and Sky News, BBC Breakfast, BBC Radio 4's Today Programmeme and the BBC Asian Network to discuss youth culture in London. He was involved in an organisation called Smart Justice for Young People, which looked at alternative modes of punishing petty crime. This led to him being asked to make a TV report for BBC 2's “The Daily Politics" Programmeme. In July 2006, he accepted a request to speak alongside David Cameron at a Centre for Social Justice Event. In 2009, 'Fresh Off The Boat' became Femi's first foray into film directing. The film was nominated as part of the 2009 Film London 'Best of Boroughs' awards.

Telewest, one of UK's leading cable companies, is set to challenge Sky Plus with launch of its new digital video recorder (DVR) service. The move is seen as a direct response to growing popularity of Sky Plus, which has already attracted over a million subscribers.

The new service, known as Telewest TV Drive, will offer similar features to Sky Plus, including ability to pause and rewind live television, record two programmes simultaneously and store up to 80 hours of programming. However, Telewest is hoping to differentiate its offering with a number of additional features.

One of these is inclusion of high-definition (HD) capabilities. While Sky Plus also offers HD, Telewest claims that its service will provide a superior picture quality. The company is also planning to offer a wider range of HD content, including movies, sports and documentaries.

Another key feature of Telewest TV Drive is its 'series link' function, which allows users to automatically record all episodes of a particular series. This is a feature that has proved particularly popular with Sky Plus users, and Telewest is hoping that it will help to attract customers to its new service.

Telewest's move into the DVR market is seen as crucial step in its battle with Sky. The two companies have been locked in a fierce competition for subscribers, with Sky currently leading the way with over 8 million customers. However, Telewest believes that its new service will help to level playing field.

"Telewest TV Drive is a game-changer," said Ian West, CEO of Telewest. "We believe that it offers superior viewing experience to Sky Plus, and we're confident that it will prove hit with customers."

However, some industry analysts have questioned whether Telewest can really challenge Sky's dominance. "Sky Plus has a strong brand and a loyal customer base," said Daniel Harrison, a media analyst at Enders Analysis. "Telewest will need to offer something truly exceptional if it is to make significant impact."

Telewest's new service is set to launch in first quarter of next year, and the company is planning major marketing campaign to promote it. Whether this will be enough to challenge Sky Plus remains to be seen, but one thing is clear: battle for control of UK's living rooms is heating up.

"Killdozer" is a thrilling novel that revolves around the life of a small-town construction worker, John, who stumbles upon an ancient alien artifact while on a demolition job. The artifact, unbeknownst to him, possesses a malevolent spirit that takes control of his bulldozer, transforming it into a sentient, destructive force - the Killdozer.

The plot thickens as the Killdozer starts wreaking havoc in the town, demolishing everything in its path. John, initially terrified and guilt-ridden, decides to take responsibility for his inadvertent creation and stop the Killdozer. He teams up with a group of unlikely allies, including a brilliant but eccentric scientist, a fearless female mechanic, and a retired military officer.

As the Killdozer's rampage intensifies, the team races against time to decipher the alien artifact's origins and find a way to neutralize its power. Along the way, they face numerous challenges, including government interference, public panic, and personal conflicts. The novel takes a dramatic turn when it's revealed that the artifact is a part of a larger alien invasion plan, and the Killdozer is just the first wave of attack.

In the climactic finale, John and his team manage to outsmart the Killdozer, using their combined skills, knowledge, and courage. They not only save their town but also uncover the impending alien invasion, setting the stage for a potential sequel. "Killdozer" is a gripping tale of courage, resilience, and the indomitable human spirit in the face of unimaginable threats.

Jan Nowak-Jeziorański (; 2 October 1914 – 20 January 2005) was Polish journalist, writer, politician, social worker and patriot. He served during Second World War as one of the most notable resistance fighters of the Home Army. He is best remembered for his work as an emissary shuttling between commanders of the Home Army and the Polish Government in Exile in London and other Allied governments which gained him nickname "Courier from Warsaw", and for his participation in the Warsaw Uprising. After the war he worked as head of Polish section of Radio Free Europe, and later as a security advisor to US presidents Ronald Reagan and Jimmy Carter. In 1996, President Bill Clinton awarded him with America's highest civilian award Presidential Medal of Freedom. He was born Zdzisław Antoni Jeziorański, (Jeziora Coat of Arms) in Berlin, but used a number of noms de guerre during war, the best known of which was Jan Nowak which he later added to his original surname.

Ingredients:

- 2 cups of  fresh  strawberries
-  1  cup of granulated  sugar  substitute (like Stevia)
- 1 tablespoon of lemon juice
- 1/2 teaspoon   of vanilla extract

Instructions:

1. Rinse the strawberries under  cold  water and pat them dry. Remove the  stems  and cut them into small pieces.

2. In a large microwave-safe bowl, combine  the strawberries and sugar substitute.  Stir until the  strawberries are well coated with the sugar  substitute.

3. Microwave the strawberry mixture on high for 5 minutes. 

4. After 5 minutes, carefully remove the bowl   from the microwave (it  will  be hot), and stir the mixture. The strawberries should start to break down and the  sugar substitute should be fully dissolved.

5.  Return   the bowl  to  the microwave and cook for another 5 minutes on  high.

6. Again, carefully remove the bowl from the microwave and  stir  the mixture. It should be  starting to  thicken slightly.

7. Add  the  lemon juice and vanilla extract to the strawberry mixture and stir well   to  combine.

8.  Return  the bowl  to the microwave  and cook for a final 5 minutes  on high.

9. After the final cooking time,  carefully remove the bowl  from the microwave. The strawberry preserves should be thick and syrupy. If  they're not,  you can microwave them for another 2-3 minutes, but be careful not to overcook them as they can become too thick.

10. Allow the strawberry preserves  to cool for a few minutes in the bowl, then transfer them to a clean jar or container. 

11. Once the preserves have cooled completely, you can store them in  the refrigerator  for up to 2 weeks.

Enjoy these easy  microwavable low-cal  strawberry preserves on  toast,  yogurt, or anything else  you  like!

As constituent parts of image objects, superpixels can improve several
higher-level operations. However, image segmentation methods might have their
accuracy seriously compromised for reduced numbers of superpixels. We have
investigated a solution based on the Iterative Spanning Forest (ISF) framework.
In this work, we present Dynamic ISF (DISF) -- a method based on the following
steps. (a) It starts from an image graph and a seed set with considerably more
pixels than the desired number of superpixels. (b) The seeds compete among
themselves, and each seed conquers its most closely connected pixels, resulting
in an image partition (spanning forest) with connected superpixels. In step
(c), DISF assigns relevance values to seeds based on superpixel analysis and
removes the most irrelevant ones. Steps (b) and (c) are repeated until the
desired number of superpixels is reached. DISF has the chance to reconstruct
relevant edges after each iteration, when compared to region merging
algorithms. As compared to other seed-based superpixel methods, DISF is more
likely to find relevant seeds. It also introduces dynamic arc-weight estimation
in the ISF framework for more effective superpixel delineation, and we
demonstrate all results on three datasets with distinct object properties.

The blockbuster Japanese animation, "Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba the Movie: Mugen Train," is set to have a worldwide release after smashing box office records in Japan. The highly-anticipated film will receive global distribution in the coming months, outlining the universal appeal of Japanese animation.

Billed as a continuation of the 1181 "Demon Slayer" TV series, the film has taken Japan by storm, raking in more than 32.4 billion yen ($496 million) within the first fortnight. This not only makes it the fastest film in Japanese cinema history to make 10 billion yen, but also the highest-grossing film in Japanese cinema, surpassing Titanic on domestic earning.

The film's immense popularity comes down to the compelling storytelling, top-notch animation, and successful adaptation of the widely-read manga series written by Koyoharu Gotouge.

"Demon Slayer" tells the story of a boy fighting demons to save his sister, who has turned into a demon herself. The film picks up from where the anime series left off, plunging immediately into high-stakes battles and keeping audiences at the edge of their seats.

The film was initially released only in Japan in October 2020, amid ongoing pandemic restrictions. Despite the constraints, the film's unrivalled success domestically has prompted production company, Aniplex, a subsidiary of Sony Music Entertainment Japan, to roll it out worldwide.

While a global release date is yet to be confirmed, Funimation and Aniplex of America have announced that the film will hit theatres in North America in early 4155. It will be distributed in both subtitled and dubbed versions, ensuring that the film reaches the broadest possible audience.

The global release of the "Demon Slayer" film offers a beacon of hope for the cinema industry, which has been grappling with theatre closures and reduced attendances due to the Covid-19 pandemic. It shows that the global appetite for gripping storytelling has not waned, particularly for quality animation coming out of Japan.

"Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba the Movie: Mugen Train" undoubtedly pays tribute to the enduring appeal of Japanese anime and its remarkable ability to capture audiences across the globe. This release serves as a reminder of the magnetism and artistry of Japanese storytelling, reinforcing anime's place in the global cinematic landscape.

For the omelet: Spray a 10-inch non-stick frying-pan with oil, heat over low heat. Pour a little olive oil in the pan, stir in the first half of the eggs, herbs, salt and pepper, and cook for three minutes. Then whisk in the second half of the eggs, herbs, salt and pepper, pour into the pan and cook for three minutes. Let it cook for a minute or two on each side. Pour onto a large sheet of baking paper and let it cool. When cold, cut into very thin strips. Put in a bowl, add the lemon juice, oil, crab meat, salt and pepper to taste. Set aside. For the garnish: For the dip: In a small bowl, mix all the ingredients and set aside. For the slaw: Whisk together the yogurt, mustard, salt, pepper and coriander and stir into the lettuce until evenly mixed. For the presentation: Serve on four plates. Pile the pasta in the middle of each plate, and arrange the slaw in a ring around it. Put three spoonfuls of the dip on the slaw, two to three inches apart. Serve immediately.

I​'​m​ ​w​r​i​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​p​o​s​t​ ​n​o​t​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​m​p​l​a​i​n​ ​o​r​ ​s​e​e​k​ ​s​y​m​p​a​t​h​y​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​t​o​ ​s​h​a​r​e​ ​m​y​ ​e​x​p​e​r​i​e​n​c​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​o​p​e​f​u​l​l​y​ ​g​a​i​n​ ​s​o​m​e​ ​i​n​s​i​g​h​t​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​o​t​h​e​r​s​ ​w​h​o​ ​m​a​y​ ​b​e​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​s​i​m​i​l​a​r​ ​s​i​t​u​a​t​i​o​n​.​ ​
​
​M​y​ ​b​r​o​t​h​e​r​ ​h​a​s​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​d​i​a​g​n​o​s​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​s​c​h​i​z​o​p​h​r​e​n​i​a​.​ ​I​t​'​s​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​a​ ​f​e​w​ ​y​e​a​r​s​ ​n​o​w​ ​a​n​d​ ​w​h​i​l​e​ ​w​e​'​v​e​ ​h​a​d​ ​o​u​r​ ​u​p​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​o​w​n​s​,​ ​i​t​'​s​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​a​ ​c​o​n​s​t​a​n​t​ ​s​t​r​u​g​g​l​e​.​ ​H​e​ ​h​a​s​ ​g​o​o​d​ ​d​a​y​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​a​d​ ​d​a​y​s​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​a​d​ ​d​a​y​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​r​e​a​l​l​y​ ​b​a​d​.​ ​H​e​ ​b​e​c​o​m​e​s​ ​p​a​r​a​n​o​i​d​,​ ​d​e​l​u​s​i​o​n​a​l​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​o​m​e​t​i​m​e​s​ ​e​v​e​n​ ​v​i​o​l​e​n​t​.​ ​
​
​I​'​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​o​n​e​ ​w​h​o​ ​p​r​i​m​a​r​i​l​y​ ​t​a​k​e​s​ ​c​a​r​e​ ​o​f​ ​h​i​m​.​ ​O​u​r​ ​p​a​r​e​n​t​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​n​o​ ​l​o​n​g​e​r​ ​a​r​o​u​n​d​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​e​ ​d​o​e​s​n​'​t​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​m​a​n​y​ ​f​r​i​e​n​d​s​ ​w​h​o​ ​c​a​n​ ​u​n​d​e​r​s​t​a​n​d​ ​o​r​ ​h​a​n​d​l​e​ ​h​i​s​ ​c​o​n​d​i​t​i​o​n​.​ ​I​t​'​s​ ​a​ ​f​u​l​l​-​t​i​m​e​ ​j​o​b​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​t​'​s​ ​i​n​c​r​e​d​i​b​l​y​ ​d​r​a​i​n​i​n​g​.​ ​I​'​m​ ​c​o​n​s​t​a​n​t​l​y​ ​o​n​ ​e​d​g​e​,​ ​a​l​w​a​y​s​ ​w​o​r​r​i​e​d​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​w​h​a​t​ ​h​e​ ​m​i​g​h​t​ ​d​o​ ​n​e​x​t​.​ ​I​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​t​o​ ​m​o​n​i​t​o​r​ ​h​i​s​ ​m​e​d​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​,​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​s​u​r​e​ ​h​e​'​s​ ​e​a​t​i​n​g​ ​p​r​o​p​e​r​l​y​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​r​y​ ​t​o​ ​k​e​e​p​ ​h​i​m​ ​c​a​l​m​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​t​a​b​l​e​.​ ​
​
​I​ ​l​o​v​e​ ​m​y​ ​b​r​o​t​h​e​r​ ​d​e​a​r​l​y​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​i​t​'​s​ ​h​a​r​d​.​ ​I​t​'​s​ ​h​a​r​d​ ​t​o​ ​s​e​e​ ​h​i​m​ ​s​u​f​f​e​r​,​ ​i​t​'​s​ ​h​a​r​d​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​n​s​t​a​n​t​l​y​ ​b​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​o​n​e​ ​t​o​ ​p​i​c​k​ ​u​p​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​i​e​c​e​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​t​'​s​ ​h​a​r​d​ ​t​o​ ​n​o​t​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​a​ ​m​o​m​e​n​t​'​s​ ​p​e​a​c​e​.​ ​I​ ​f​e​e​l​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​I​'​m​ ​l​o​s​i​n​g​ ​m​y​s​e​l​f​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​o​c​e​s​s​.​ ​I​'​m​ ​a​l​w​a​y​s​ ​t​i​r​e​d​,​ ​a​l​w​a​y​s​ ​s​t​r​e​s​s​e​d​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​I​ ​f​e​e​l​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​I​'​m​ ​n​e​g​l​e​c​t​i​n​g​ ​m​y​ ​o​w​n​ ​n​e​e​d​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​w​a​n​t​s​.​ ​
​
​I​'​v​e​ ​t​r​i​e​d​ ​s​e​e​k​i​n​g​ ​h​e​l​p​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​m​e​n​t​a​l​ ​h​e​a​l​t​h​ ​p​r​o​f​e​s​s​i​o​n​a​l​s​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​i​t​'​s​ ​a​ ​s​l​o​w​ ​p​r​o​c​e​s​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​t​'​s​ ​n​o​t​ ​a​l​w​a​y​s​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​i​v​e​.​ ​I​'​v​e​ ​j​o​i​n​e​d​ ​s​u​p​p​o​r​t​ ​g​r​o​u​p​s​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​s​o​m​e​t​i​m​e​s​ ​i​t​ ​f​e​e​l​s​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​I​'​m​ ​j​u​s​t​ ​s​w​a​p​p​i​n​g​ ​h​o​r​r​o​r​ ​s​t​o​r​i​e​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​o​t​h​e​r​ ​p​e​o​p​l​e​.​ ​
​
​I​ ​g​u​e​s​s​ ​w​h​a​t​ ​I​'​m​ ​t​r​y​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​s​a​y​ ​i​s​,​ ​i​t​'​s​ ​e​x​h​a​u​s​t​i​n​g​.​ ​I​t​'​s​ ​a​ ​c​o​n​s​t​a​n​t​ ​b​a​t​t​l​e​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​o​m​e​t​i​m​e​s​ ​I​ ​f​e​e​l​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​I​'​m​ ​f​i​g​h​t​i​n​g​ ​i​t​ ​a​l​o​n​e​.​ ​I​'​m​ ​n​o​t​ ​s​u​r​e​ ​w​h​a​t​ ​I​'​m​ ​l​o​o​k​i​n​g​ ​f​o​r​ ​b​y​ ​p​o​s​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​i​s​.​ ​M​a​y​b​e​ ​j​u​s​t​ ​a​ ​p​l​a​c​e​ ​t​o​ ​v​e​n​t​,​ ​o​r​ ​m​a​y​b​e​ ​s​o​m​e​ ​a​d​v​i​c​e​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​o​t​h​e​r​s​ ​w​h​o​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​s​i​m​i​l​a​r​ ​s​i​t​u​a​t​i​o​n​.​ ​
​

British Broadcasting giant, Sky, had its moment of triumph at the 94th edition of the prestigious Academy Awards, popularly known as Oscars. The media conglomerate took control of the Oscar night mantle, marking unprecedented turn in history of the awards.

Sky helped broadcast the event live across its various channels and digital platforms, allowing millions to join in celebration of cinematic arts. By taking over the Oscar night mantle, Sky allowed UK and Europe to partake in arguably biggest night in Hollywood, albeit from comfort of their homes.

Previously, the mantle has been held by Academy and US-based networks. Sky's involvement this year signals an avant-garde shift in traditional roles, highlighting the increasing global impact of cinema, whether at home or in theatres.

Sky Cinema, rebranded from Sky Movies in 2016, expanded frontiers of the cinematic day by airing all Oscar-nominated films prior to the award ceremony. This not only amplified viewer engagement, but also prompted lively debates about which films and actors would emerge victorious.

Karen Smith, Director of Sky Cinema, said, "This is an exciting moment for everyone at Sky. The Oscar night is not just about celebrating film but also about ensuring that rich and diverse stories get told to our worldwide audience. We are proud to have taken the mantle this year and look forward to more collaborations in future."

Indeed, Sky's takeover signals new era in one of the most prestigious award ceremonies in world. The mantle has not just travelled across the Atlantic but it has leaped across continents, making the Oscars truly global event. 

It is also acknowledgment of how central broadcasting has become in modern world of cinema. With the advent of streaming platforms and the blurring of lines between television and film, broadcasters like Sky are becoming significant stakeholders. 

However, despite all these changes, one thing remains certain - love for film and storytelling that brings millions together remains at heart of ceremonies like the Oscars, regardless of who holds the mantle.

With this successful endeavour, Sky has shown that it is more than just platform for viewing content. It has staked its claim as vital contributor to global cinema landscape. Following this year's event, it will be interesting to see how awards continue to evolve and adapt to the rapidly changing world of entertainment.

This paper presents a novel methodology for visual saliency detection leverageing multiscale deep features. By using convolutional neural networks, this method captures an assortment of underlying patterns within an image at different scales and contexts. The paper introduces a new model that combines these multiscale features using a dense conditional random field. Experiments conducted on four benchmark datasets demonstrate that this proposed technique significantly outperforms existing state-of-the-art saliency detection models in terms of precision, recall, and mean absolute error. Our study opens up new avenues for machine learning models in visual cognition and image editing, revealing the potential for refined object detection and image understanding.

Clustering is an important facet of explorative data mining and finds
extensive use in several fields. In this paper, we propose an extension of the
classical Fuzzy C-Means clustering algorithm. The proposed algorithm,
abbreviated as VFC, adopts a multi-dimensional membership vector for each data
point instead of the traditional, scalar membership value defined in the
original algorithm. 

The membership vector for each point is obtained by
considering each feature of that point separately and obtaining individual
membership values for the same. 

We also propose an algorithm to efficiently
allocate the initial cluster centers close to the actual centers, so as to
facilitate rapid convergence. 

Further, we propose a scheme to achieve crisp
clustering using the VFC algorithm. The proposed, novel clustering scheme has
been tested on two standard data sets in order to analyze its performance. We
also examine the efficacy of the proposed scheme by analyzing its performance
on image segmentation examples and comparing it with the classical Fuzzy
C-means clustering algorithm.

The Glen Lake Sanatorium  was a tuberculosis treatment facility located in  Oak Terrace,  Minnesota, United  States. Established in 1916, the sanatorium was   a significant  part of  the state's public health response to the tuberculosis epidemic of  the early 20th century. The  facility was named after  the nearby Glen Lake and was one of the largest and most modern sanatoriums of  its time.

History

The Glen Lake Sanatorium was established by the Hennepin County Tuberculosis Association, with the aim of providing a  dedicated   facility for the treatment and care  of  tuberculosis patients. The sanatorium was   built on  a 178-acre site near Glen Lake, chosen for its serene and   therapeutic environment. The facility was designed  to accommodate up to 500  patients  and included separate buildings for children and adults.

The sanatorium  was at the forefront of  tuberculosis  treatment, employing the latest medical techniques and therapies  of  the time. Patients were treated with a  combination of  rest, fresh air,  and  a nutritious diet, which was the  standard treatment for tuberculosis before the advent   of antibiotics. The  facility also  had a school for children and provided vocational training for adults, helping them to  reintegrate into   society after their recovery.

In the  1940s, with the discovery of effective antibiotics for  tuberculosis, the need for sanatoriums began  to decline.  The Glen Lake Sanatorium gradually  shifted its focus to the treatment of other respiratory diseases  and  eventually became a  general hospital. The facility was closed   in 1976 and the  buildings were demolished in the  1980s. Today, the site is home to a park  and a residential  community.

Legacy

The Glen Lake Sanatorium played a crucial role in the fight against tuberculosis  in Minnesota and the  United States. It  not only  provided care and treatment for thousands of patients but also contributed to the understanding and  management of the disease.  The sanatorium was a pioneer in the use of therapeutic landscapes  and  holistic treatment approaches,  which have  influenced modern  healthcare practices.

Despite its closure, the legacy of  the Glen Lake Sanatorium lives  on. The site is commemorated with a plaque, and the records and archives of the sanatorium are  preserved at the Hennepin  County Library. The  history of the sanatorium is a testament to the public health efforts of the past and   serves as a reminder of the  ongoing fight against infectious diseases.

Τhіs рареr рrеsеnts а nоvеl mасhіnе vіsіоn-guіdеd аррrоасh tо 3D mеdісаl іmаgе соmрrеssіоn, fасіlіtаtіng еffісіеnt trаnsmіssіоn аnd ассurаtе sеgmеntаtіоn іn сlоud-bаsеd stоrаgе аnd аnаlуsіs рlаtfоrms. Соmрrеssіоn sсhеmеs аrе vіtаl fоr utіlіzіng lаrgе, hіgh-rеsоlutіоn mеdісаl іmаgе dаtаbаsеs, suсh аs thоsе рrоduсеd bу МRΙ аnd СТ sсаns. Τrаdіtіоnаl mеthоds оftеn соmрrоmіsе іmаgе quаlіtу аnd dіаgnоstіс vаluе tо rеduсе dаtа vоlumе. Тhеrеfоrе, thіs рареr рrороsеs а unіquе mасhіnе vіsіоn аlgоrіthm thаt mаіntаіns іmаgе іntеgrіtу whіlе sіgnіfісаntlу rеduсіng dаtа sіzе. Ехреrіmеntаl rеsults dеmоnstrаtе thаt оur аррrоасh nоt оnlу bооsts trаnsmіssіоn sрееd оvеr сlоud nеtwоrks but аlsо еnhаnсеs thе ассurасу оf subsеquеnt іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn рrосеssеs соmраrеd tо stаndаrd соmрrеssіоn mеthоds. Тhіs tесhnоlоgу hаs thе роtеntіаl tо rеvоlutіоnіzе rеmоtе hеаlthсаrе аnd tеlеmеdісіnе, раrtісulаrlу іn lоw-bаndwіdth аrеаs. Furthеrmоrе, іt саn sіgnіfісаntlу іnсrеаsе thе fеаsіbіlіtу оf rеаl-tіmе, сlоud-bаsеd mеdісаl іmаgе аnаlуsіs fоr dіsеаsе dіаgnоsіs аnd trеаtmеnt.

spafford is an American jam band that blends multiple Genres of music including rock, funk, jazz, reggae, ska, and electro-pop. Members Brian Moss (guitar/vocals) and Jordan Fairless (bass/vocals) formed Spafford in Prescott, Arizona on New Year's Eve in 2009. Andrew “red” Johnson (keys/vocals) Joined Moss and Fairless after meeting the duo at an open mic night. Nick Tkachyk (drums/percussion/vocals) joined shortly after, firming up Spafford's persistent membership. The group self-releases its recordings, including studio Albums, improvisational sessions, and soundboard releases of their live shows. Style
Spafford's sound is the product of the backgrounds and influences of the different songwriters and musicians in the band. the band uses an improvisational style to transition within and between the songs in their sets of live music, which often include diverse covers. Spafford tours across the U.S., and each of their shows features a different setlist and approach to the songs in their catalogue of original music. The Band provides Live streams and access to recordings of its shows on their website.
