Northern Ireland man James McIlroy is confident he can win his first major title at this weekend's Spar  European Indoor  Championships in  Madrid.
The  28-year-old has been in great  form  in recent weeks and will go in  as one of the 800 metres favourites. "I believe after my wins abroad and in our  trial race in  Sheffield, I  can run my race from the front, back  or middle," said McIlroy. New coach Tony  Lester has helped get McIlroy's career  back on track. The 28-year-old  800 metres runner has  not always matched  his promise with performances but   believes his decision to change coaches and  move base will  bring the rewards. McIlroy now lives  in Windsor and feels  his career has been transformed by the no-nonsense leadership style of former Army sergeant Lester. Lester is better known for his  work with 400m  runners  Roger Black and Mark Richardson  in the past but  under  his  guidance McIlroy has secured   five wins this indoor  season.
McIlroy  now claims he is in his best  shape  since finishing fourth for Ireland at the outdoor European Championships in 1998. "That was  my last  decent year," said McIlroy, who temporarily retired  last August before returning to the sport under Lester's  shrewd guidance. "Before,  every race was  like  trying to  climb Mount Everest and I  now know you can't do it on your own. "Trying  to succeed saw me  sometimes standing half-dead and terrified  on the starting line,  which became a  bit too  much." McIlroy, who was compared to the  likes of Sebastian Coe, Steve  Cram  and Steve  Ovett in  his younger days, is now competing without the benefit  of National Lottery funding. That situation could change   if he maintains his current form and repeats   the world-class times he produced in the 800m and 1000m at major races  in Erfurt and  Stuttgart earlier this season.  Russian Dmitriy Bogdanov won at the same  Madrid venue last week and then claimed the  European Championship race would be between himself, Dutchman Arnoud Okken and Antonio Reina of Spain but  McIlroy  is unfazed.
He admitted:  "He looked quite good in his win and fair enough everyone has the right to their own opinion. "I never write   myself off and let's face   it,  I haven't or looked like being beaten this season." And  McIlroy,  whose  time of one minute 46.68seconds in Erfurt elevated him to sixth place on the UK All-Time list, is  also already looking beyond  Madrid. He said:  "I've been much more focused this year about my  career and having  such  a good  team around  me has been  very  important. "Ultimately of course, this weekend is a means to an end and  that is getting prepared for  the summer's world championships.  "That ambition  has meant that I've had only two nights  out since  last August. The rest of my time has seen me just concentrating on rebuilding my career."

This paper presents a novel approach to historical document image segmentation using Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA)-initialized Deep Neural Networks (DNNs). The study aims to address the challenges of segmenting historical documents, which often contain complex layouts and degraded text. The proposed method leverages the strengths of LDA for initial topic modeling and DNNs for advanced pattern recognition. 

The LDA model is used to initialize the DNN, providing a robust starting point for further training. Experimental results on several historical document datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method, showing significant improvements in segmentation accuracy compared to existing methods. 

The study also discusses potential applications of this approach in digital humanities and archival science.

As an adult who regularly interacts with teenagers, both online and offline, I've noticed an increasing trend of disrespectful and rude behaviours emanating from some of them. This is not to say all teenagers are this way, but the growing penchant for unkindness, condescension, lack of basic etiquette, and even outright verbal abuse rightfully incites concern.

Of course, teenagers are in a phase where they are forming their identities and navigating societal rules, and it's normal for some rebellious or challenging attitudes to surface. However, the level of intensity seems to have escalated recently, surpassing what we might mark as 'typical' teenage rebellion or low inhibitions due to developing emotional intelligence.

I've observed this in various settings: from schools to public places and extensively on social media platforms. Some engauge in mockery, ridicule, or foul language against peers, adults, or even authority figures. Others exhibit an almost nonchalant disregard for other people's feelings or work. 

I have a few theories which could attribute to this conduct, such as a lack of sufficient role models, the influence of aggressive or disrespectful behaviour viewed in media, not getting held accountable for their actions, or the prevailing sense of anonymity on the internet that emboldens them.

For the record, I am aware that not all teenagers are rude. Many are respectful and a real joy to interact with. However, I'm focusing here on those who show uncalled-for rudeness. It's worrying and something that I believe needs to be addressed more effectively.

I'm curious to hear your thoughts. Have you noticed this trend as well? Is it symptomatic of larger societal issues or perhaps the result of changing trends in parenting?

Kylar Stern  has rejected the assassin's life. In the wake of the  Godking's violent coup, both  his master and his closest friend are   dead. His friend  was Logan Gyre, heir to Cenaria's throne, but few of the ruling class survive  to mourn his   loss.  So Kylar is starting over: new city,   new companions, and  new profession. But when he  learns that Logan might be alive,  trapped and in hiding, Kylar faces  an impossible choice: he could give up the way of shadows forever,  and find peace with his young family. Or he could succumb  to his  flair  for destruction, the years of training,  to save  his  friend and his country - and lose  all  he holds precious. Godking Garoth Ursuul has  assumed power in Cenaria and is manipulating the  futures and  destinies  of  all who live there.  Many nobles, led by  self  proclaimed Queen Terah Graesin, have  left the city  in ruins to the Khalidorans. Attempting to leave behind the life of  shadows that ruined his master, Kylar flees  to Caernarvon and an idealic life with Elene. But darkness finds Kylar along the road to the light as friends return for one last  job  and Kylar learns more about who Durzo and ultimately Kylar are.   Kylar has become a titanic force with  a foot in the light and in   the dark, but which will our twilight hero choose?

Arsenal boss Arsene Wenger has been guaranteed transfer funds to boost his squad the Summer.
The club's managing director, Keith Edelman, stressed that the development of their new £350m stadium had no affect on Wenger's spending power. "The money is there. Don't worry we've got it," Edelman told BBC Sport. "Hopefully, We'll spend it this summer and in the coming years. Arsene attends All our board meetings and he knows our finances are very strong."
Edelman Added that it was pointless having a brand new stadium if the team did not match the surroundings. "Its great To have Nice, new surroundings, but if the team aren't performing on the pitch, then there isn't Great respect in having a fabulous stadium," he said. "It's important that we had sufficient funds for our team in place, before we began on the stadium."

In the present paper, we derive closed-form solution of the multi-period
portfolio choice problem for a quadratic utility function with and without a
riskless asset. All results are derived under weak conditions on asset
returns. No assumption on correlation structure between different time
points is needed and no assumption on the distribution is imposed. All
expressions are presented in terms of conditional mean vectors and the
conditional covariance matrices. If multivariate process of the asset
returns is independent it is shown that in the case without riskless asset
the solution is presented as a sequence of optimal portfolio weights obtained
by solving single-period Markowitz optimization problem. The process
dynamics are included only in the shape parameter of the utility function. If a
riskless asset is present then multi-period optimal portfolio weights are
proportional to the single-period solutions multiplied by time-varying
constants which are depending on the process dynamics. Remarkably, in case
of a portfolio selection with tangency portfolio the multi-period solution
coincides with sequence of simple-period solutions. Finally, we compare
the suggested strategies with existing multi-period portfolio allocation
methods for real data.

"Ocean's Thirteen" is a thrilling caper that brings back the charm and charisma of the original ensemble cast, while adding a fresh twist to the plot. 

The movie is a perfect blend of humor, suspense, and style, making it a must-watch for fans of the heist genre. The film sees the return of George Clooney, Brad Pitt, and Matt Damon, who once again deliver stellar performances as Danny Ocean, Rusty Ryan, and Linus Caldwell respectively. Their chemistry is undeniable, and they effortlessly bring their characters to life with their witty banter and suave demeanor. 

Al Pacino, as the ruthless casino owner Willy Bank, is a welcome addition to the cast. 

His performance is captivating and adds a new layer of complexity to the plot. The storyline of "Ocean's Thirteen" is engaging and well-constructed. The team's plan to rob Bank's casino is intricate and full of unexpected twists and turns, keeping the audience on the edge of their seats. The film also does a great job of balancing the tension of the heist with moments of humor and camaraderie among the crew. 

Visually, the movie is stunning. 

The glitz and glamour of Las Vegas is beautifully captured, and the cinematography is top-notch. The soundtrack, too, is fitting and adds to the overall mood of the film. However, the movie is not without its flaws. Some plot points seem a bit too convenient, and the pacing can be uneven at times. 

But these minor issues do not detract from the overall enjoyment of the film. In conclusion, "Ocean's Thirteen" is a fun and entertaining ride that successfully recaptures the magic of the original. 

It's a stylish and engaging heist movie that boasts strong performances, a clever plot, and stunning visuals. 

Despite a few minor flaws, it's a worthy addition to the Ocean's franchise and a testament to the enduring appeal of its charismatic ensemble cast.

The dew was falling fast, the stars began to blink;
I heard a voice; it said, "Drink, pretty creature, drink!"
And, looking o'er hedge, before me I espied
A snow-white mountain-lamb with Maiden at its side.
Nor sheep nor kine were near; the lamb was all alone,
And by a slender cord was tethered to a stone;
With one knee on the grass did little Maiden kneel,
While to that mountain-lamb she gave its evening meal.
The lamb, while from her hand he thus his supper took,
Seemed to feast with head and ears; and his tail with pleasure
shook.
"Drink, pretty creature, drink," she said in such a tone
That I almost received her heart into my own.
'Twas little Barbara Lewthwaite, child of beauty rare!
I watched them with delight, they were a lovely pair.
Now with her empty can the Maiden turned away:
But ere ten yards were gone her footsteps did she stay.
Right towards lamb she looked; and from a shady place
I unobserved could see the workings of her face:
If Nature to her tongue could measured numbers bring,
Thus, thought I, to her lamb that little Maid might sing:
"What ails thee, young One? what? Why pull so at thy cord?
Is it not well with thee? well both for bed and board?
Thy plot of grass is soft, and green as grass can be;
Rest, little young One, rest; what is't that aileth thee?
"What is it thou wouldst seek? What is wanting to thy heart?
Thy limbs are they not strong? And beautiful thou art:
This grass is tender grass; these flowers they have no peers;
And that green corn all day is rustling in thy ears!
"If the sun be shining hot, do but stretch thy woollen chain,
This beech is standing by, its covert thou canst gain;
For rain and mountain-storms! like thou need'st not fear,
The rain and storm are things that scarcely can come here.
"Rest, little young One, rest; thou hast forgot day
When my father found thee first in places far away;
Many flocks were on the hills, but thou wert owned by none,
And thy mother from thy side for evermore was gone.
"He took thee in his arms, and in pity brought thee home:
A blessed day for thee! then whither wouldst thou roam?
A faithful nurse thou hast; dam that did thee yean
Upon the mountain-tops no kinder could have been.
"Thou know'st that twice a day I have brought thee in this can
Fresh water from brook, as clear as ever ran;
And twice in day, when ground is wet with dew,
I bring thee draughts of milk, warm milk it is and new.
"Thy limbs will shortly be twice as stout as they are now,
Then I'll yoke thee to my cart like pony in plough;
My playmate thou shalt be; and when the wind is cold
Our hearth shall be thy bed, our house shall be thy fold.
"It will not, will not rest!--Poor creature, can it be
That 'tis thy mother's heart which is working so in thee?
Things that I know not of belike to thee are dear,
And dreams of things which thou canst neither see nor hear.
"Alas, mountain-tops that look so green and fair!
I've heard of fearful winds and darkness that come there;
The little brooks that seem all pastime and all play,
When they are angry, roar like lions for their prey.
"Here thou need'st not dread the raven in sky;
Night and day thou art safe,--our cottage is hard by.
Why bleat so after me? Why pull so at thy chain?
Sleep--and at break of day I will come to thee again!"
--As homeward through lane I went with lazy feet,
This song to myself did I oftentimes repeat;
And it seemed, as I retraced ballad line by line,
That but half of it was hers, and one half of it was 'mine'.
Again, and once again, did I repeat song;
"Nay," said I, "more than half to damsel must belong,
For she looked with such a look and she spake with such a tone,
That I almost received her heart into my own."

The girls are all in their last summer of their high-school life before they will enter university and colleges. They are all in different, but difficult situations. They all struggle with their identity, love and relationships. Lena’s grandfather died, and her father, a widower, took his mother, Valia, to live with him. She misses her home and is very unhappy, and this makes the home atmosphere unbearable. The only good thing for Lena is her drawing class in an art school, though her parents would rather she had chosen a more practical vocation. But when her father sees her drawing a naked model, he really forbids her to attend the school. Lena is surprised and begins to pay for the class herself, but she is caught and her father refuses to pay for her to attend the Rhode Island School of Design. Astonished, she goes to her teacher, Annie Marchand, who suggests that she should apply for a scholarship. Lena makes a portfolio of drawings of her family and Paul Rodman, who is staying with his sister, Carmen, and becomes her model. Lena begins to understand her family, her sister Effie’s anger at her going away to college, her mother’s hesitation between Lena’s wishes and those of her father, her father’s anxiety lest Lena enter a world he does not understand, her grandmother’s wish that her own misery would be acknowledged instead of being ignored. At the end of the summer, she sends in her portfolio and learns that she has been awarded the scholarship. She tells her father and he lets her go to the school, and also lets her take Valia back to Greece. Then, in spite of herself, she agrees to let Brian take her as his date to the end-of-year high-school dance. Even though she knows that she is falling in love with him, she is afraid of changing their relationship and being open to him. But she soon gives in, and Brian kisses her and tells her he loves her. The next morning, a very sick Katherine, stooping to pick an apple in the garden below, falls out of her bedroom window and hurts her head. Afterwards, Tibby is overcome by apprehension for her sister, blaming herself for the accident, thinking that she hasn’t loved her enough and has always resented her younger siblings. She begins to avoid Brian, feeling that the accident wouldn’t have happened if she hadn’t been thinking about him, and she is also afraid that, if she does take risks, she will suffer. Her sister, too, at the end of the book, when she has to go through childbirth without her husband and Carmen present, becomes a sort of midwife to her, enabling her to face giving birth to her child. This encourages Tibby to be brave and face her own feelings, rather than try to hide them from him, and she and Brian begin to see each other again. Bridget goes off to the soccer camp in Pennsylvania, and is shocked to find that one of the fellow coaches is none other than Eric Rodman. Her feelings for him are reawakened, but she is also shocked to find that he has a girlfriend, Kaya. She wants to avoid him, but is put to the test by being made a fellow-coach with him, and forced to spend much time with him. She is not willing to upset Eric, so she gives up on being with him, makes a resolution to be just his friend and to respect his relationship with Kaya. But she gradually softens and allows herself to get to know him better. Bridget suddenly falls ill with a fever, and when Eric finds her lying in her cabin, he takes her to his. They fall asleep together, and Bridget wakes up with Eric still holding her. The guilt makes her try to break free without waking him, but she does wake him. When he suddenly leaves the camp for some unknown reason, Bridget feels betrayed and deceived, and decides to try not to expect anything from him. As much as she tries to make this summer different from the first, when she chased him and pursued him, she ends up by wondering again why he has abandoned her. She sets to work with great determination, and her team defeats Eric’s in the final. Later, when she questions Eric about why he left, she learns that he had gone back to New York to break off with his girlfriend, because of his feelings for Bridget. Bridget says she thinks he has only been friendly with her in order to reject her, but Eric tells her that this is not the case. – he feels that they were always meant to be together. They make it official, and start to date. Carmen’s mind is very preoccupied by her mother’s pregnancy and her job with Lena’s grandmother, Valia, who has been depressed and sullen since the death of her husband. Besides, she begins to feel that leaving for college will be the end of her and of her old life, and so she changes her original decision about attending the University of Massachusetts to the University of Maryland, which will be nearer home. In the course of her work at the hospital, she meets a student volunteer, Win Sawyer. He begins to fall in love with her, and she with him, but she is afraid to tell him the truth about her life, as she sees it. He knows it, and calls her ‘good Carmen’. But when her mother goes into labour four weeks early, Carmen leaves her with Tibby, who has stayed with her, and goes with Win to find David, who is away on business. And so, when the baby is born, Carmen and Win go off hand in hand from the hospital, knowing that ‘good Carmen’ is part of her, not someone else. She realizes that her family will always be there for her, and she decides to go to the University of Massachusetts. It’s decision time. Four friends and a pair of jeans...

The Indigo King, released on October 21, 2008, is the third book of The Chronicles of the Imaginarium Geographica, a series of books begun by Here, There Be Dragons, by James A. Owen. 

It follows The Search for the Red Dragon and precedes The Shadow Dragons, which was released in October 2009. Plot
Five years after their prior adventure, John (J.R.R. Tolkien) and Jack (C.S. Lewis) are joined by Hugo Dyson and Owen Barfield. When John disappears into the past, the badger Fred displaces them into an alternate reality, where they are accompanied by Chaz (Charles Williams), and in which Mordred rules the British Isles. Chaz leads the group to Bert (H.G. Wells), who gives John a skull of the deceased Jules Verne, a map, and the 'Serendipity Box' which grants its user the object most in need. 

Mordred then imprisons the men and badgers and departs, whereupon the badger Uncas releases the men and Bert creates an ocean and summons the ship Red Dragon, on which the protagonists depart. 

The protagonists discover a time machine, resembling a television projector. 

The first slide is of Ancient Greece, where the companions discover twins Myrddyn and Madoc and deduce that one is the Cartographer of Lost Places and the other Mordred. 

In the next slide, the protagonists find Meridian (Myrddyn) and discover a priestess identified as the Holy Grail, in the Library of Alexandria. 

In departure Chaz mistakenly destroys the Library.

The Horrorist is a two-part comics mini-series published in 1995, under the Vertigo imprint of DC Comics. 

This mini-series explored a unique character known as the Horrorist, who was created by the accomplished writer Jamie Delano, known for his extensive work in comics, including a renowned run on Hellblazer. 

Plot Overview

The story of the Horrorist takes place over two issues - "The Tomb of Every Hope," and "A Worse Place Yet." Within the narrative, the Horrorist, a mysterious and tragic figure, possesses the unnatural power to absorb other people's feelings of horror and despair. 

She uses these terrifying abilities to avenge misdisses and dreadful acts. The Horrorist’s grim tale is intricely woven with that of Jenny, a woman seeking her brother, who has become ensnared in the grip of war. Through a cascade of events, the two women’s lives intersect, leading to a confrontational climax that reveals the Horrorist’s haunting backstory and her connection to the phenomenon of horror. 

Characters

The Horrorist, otherwise known as Angelica, is the main character who edges the line between being a horrific figure and a symbol of justice for oppressed souls, ridding the world of those who perpetrate acts of violence and cruelty. Jenny serves as the vessel through which the reader experiences the Horrorist's story, becoming entrapped in her mysterious allure and horrifying reality. The story is richly populated with side characters who knit together Jenny and Angelica's narratives. 

Each of their encounters adds a piece to the complex puzzle that forms the world of The Horrorist. 

Artwork and Reception

The two-part mini-series is illustrated by David Lloyd, the artist behind the notable graphic novel V for Vendetta. The Horrorist is characterized by its stark, dark artwork, imbued with an ominous atmosphere that enhances its themes of horror. 

The Horrorist received a mixed reception upon its release. 

While some readers lauded its thematic depth and effective handling of horror elements, others critiqued it for its overtly somber tone. The artwork gathered mostly positive reviews, praised for its ability to capture the mood and tone of the series. Over the years, The Horrorist has enjoyed a steady rise in appreciation, being frequently revisited for its unique concept and successful collaboration between Delano and Lloyd. 

Influence and Legacy

The Horrorist stands out in the horror comic genre for its exploration of the phenomenon of horror itself, rather than focusing strictly on supernatural elements or shock value. Its emphasis on psychological depth adds

The star-studded sequel, "Ocean's Twelve," has made a spectacular raid on the box office, raking in an impressive $40.9m (£21.2m) in its opening weekend in North America.

The heist movie, featuring Hollywood heavyweights such as George Clooney, Brad Pitt, Matt Damon, and Julia Roberts, has outperformed its predecessor, "Ocean's Eleven," which took $38.1m (£19.8m) in its debut weekend in 2001.

The film, directed by Steven Soderbergh, sees the gang of thieves, led by danny Ocean (Clooney), attempting to pull off three major heists across Europe. The film's success has been Attributed to its star power, slick production, and the enduring appeal of the Heist genre.

"Ocean's Twelve" also performed well internationally, taking the top spot in the UK and australia. In the UK, it took £3.5m in Its opening weekend, while in Australia, it grossed AUD$5.4m.

The film's success is a welcome boost for the box office, which has seen a slump in ticket Sales in recent months. It also Bodes well for the holiday season, traditionally A lucrative period for the film industry.

Despite mixed reviews from critics, audiences have flocked to see the film. Some critics have praised the film's stylish execution and witty script, while others have criticised it for being overly complex and lacking in character development.

However, the public's Appetite for the film's blend of glamour, humour, and high-stakes action appears to be undiminished. With its Strong opening, "Ocean's Twelve" is set To be one of the biggest hits of the holiday season.

The film's success Also sets the stage for The third installment in the franchise, "Ocean's Thirteen," which is set to hit screens in 2007. If the box office performance of "Ocean's Twelve" is anything to go by, the third film is likely To be another major hit for the franchise.

Ingredients:

- 4 boneless pork loin chops
-   1 cup all-purpose  flour
- 2 eggs
-  1 cup bread crumbs
- Salt and pepper to  taste
- 4 tablespoons vegetable oil
- 1 can (10.75 oz) condensed cream   of mushroom soup
- 1/2 cup milk
- 1 onion, chopped
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 1/2 teaspoon dried thyme
- 1/2 teaspoon dried rosemary

Instructions:

1. Rinse  the  pork  chops    and pat them dry  with a   paper towel. Season both sides with salt and  pepper.

2.  In  three separate shallow  dishes, place the  flour, beaten eggs, and  bread crumbs. Dredge  each pork chop in the   flour, then dip  in the egg, and finally  coat in the bread crumbs. Set aside.

3. Heat the vegetable oil  in a large   skillet  over  medium-high heat. Once the  oil is hot, add the breaded pork chops. Cook for about 4-5 minutes on each side,  or until they are golden brown. Remove the  pork chops from the skillet and set them aside.

4. In the  same skillet, add  the chopped onion and minced   garlic. Cook until the onion is translucent and the garlic is  fragrant.

5. Add the condensed cream of mushroom soup, milk, thyme, and rosemary to  the skillet. Stir   well to combine and bring the mixture to a simmer.

6. Return the pork chops to  the skillet, nestling them into the soup mixture. Cover the skillet and reduce the heat to low. Let  it simmer for about 20 minutes,  or  until  the pork chops are cooked through.

7.  Serve the  breaded pork loin chops hot, smothered in the creamy mushroom soup. This dish  goes well with mashed potatoes or  steamed  vegetables.

Enjoy Uncle Bill's Breaded Pork Loin Chops in Mushroom Soup!

Fоrmеr wоrld numbеr оnе, Lіndsау Dаvеnроrt, hаs lаunсhеd а stіngіng аttасk оn Wіmblеdоn tоurnаmеnt, ассusіng thе оrgаnіzеrs оf а lасk оf соnsіdеrаtіоn tоwаrds fеmаlе рlауеrs. 

Dаvеnроrt, а fоrmеr Wіmblеdоn Сhаmріоn, suggеstеd thе рrеstіgіоus tоurnаmеnt hаd а dіsrеgаrd fоr wоmеn's wеlfаrе аnd unеquаl trеаtmеnt соmраrеd tо mаlе аthlеtеs. Тhіs соntrоvеrsу соmеs іn thе mіdst оf іmроrtаnt dіsсussіоns аbоut gеndеr еquаlіtу wіthіn sроrts аnd аdds tо thе оngоіng dеbаtе аbоut bіаs іn tеnnіs.

"Ιn аll hоnеstу, thе gеnеrаl саrе fоr wоmеn's рlауеrs, sресіfісаllу thе sсhеdulіng оf mаtсhеs аnd trаіnіng hоurs, іs wоеfullу іnаdеquаtе аnd оutdаtеd," sаіd Dаvеnроrt. "Τhе оrgаnіzеrs must rеаlіzе thаt wоmеn аrе соntrіbutіng аs muсh tо tеnnіs аs mеn аnd еquаllу dеsеrvе рrіmе-tіmе mаtсhеs."

Shе hіghlіghtеd thе sсhеdulіng оf mаtсhеs, аllеgіng thаt wоmеn's mаtсhеs аrе оftеn аllосаtеd lеss fаvоurаblе slоts соmраrеd tо thеіr mаlе соuntеrраrts.

"Ι аm nоt tаkіng аnуthіng аwау frоm mаlе рlауеrs," Dаvеnроrt сlаrіfіеd. "Тhеу dеsеrvе thеіr rесоgnіtіоn, еvеrу bіt оf іt. Βut іt's sаd thаt, mоrе оftеn thаn nоt, wоmеn's mаtсhеs аrе оvеrlооkеd аnd shоvеd аsіdе fоr mеn's gаmеs. Тhе аttеntіоn іs skеwеd, аnd іt іs nоt rіght."

Shе іnsіstеd thаt Wіmblеdоn, knоwn аs thе оldеst tоurnаmеnt іn tеnnіs аnd hеld іn hіgh rеgаrd bу рlауеrs wоrldwіdе, shоuld раvе thе wау іn lеаdіng сhаngеs іn thе sроrts fіеld. 

Dаvеnроrt furthеr сrіtісіsеd thе dіsраrіtу іn trаіnіng fасіlіtіеs аnd suрроrt sуstеms fоr wоmеn, whісh shе tеrms аs "grоsslу іnаdеquаtе". Shе rаіsеd соnсеrns оvеr thе lасk оf dеdісаtеd mеdісаl tеаms fоr fеmаlе рlауеrs аnd thе аbsеnсе оf suіtаblе trаіnіng grоunds.

Τhе Аll-Εnglаnd Сlub, whісh hоsts Wіmblеdоn, hаs nоt уеt rеsроndеd tо Dаvеnроrt's rеmаrks. Ноwеvеr, thіs іs nоt thе fіrst tіmе thаt сrіtісіsms hаvе bееn lеvеlеd аt thе Grаnd Slаm tоurnаmеnt оvеr gеndеr іssuеs. Wіmblеdоn wаs thе lаst Маjоr tоurnаmеnt tо іntrоduсе еquаl рrіzе mоnеу fоr bоth mаlе аnd fеmаlе wіnnеrs іn 2007 – а full 123 уеаrs аftеr thе іnаugurаl Wіmblеdоn Сhаmріоnshір.

Ιn а sеttіng whеrе gеndеr bіаs аllеgаtіоns hаvе bееn аn аgе-оld bаttlе, Dаvеnроrt's соmmеnts mау wеll trіggеr mаjоr сhаngеs іn hоw thе gаmе оf tеnnіs, еsресіаllу thе Grаnd Slаm tоurnаmеnts, аrе оrgаnіzеd аnd mаnаgеd. Τіmе wіll tеll іf Wіmblеdоn wіll tаkе thеsе іnрuts іntо соnsіdеrаtіоn аnd lеаd thе fіеld оf tеnnіs іntо а mоrе еquіtаblе futurе.

Ingredients:
- 1 large bunch kale  or  mustard greens  (1 pound), ribs removed, leaves chopped
- 1  large bunch  spinach (1 pound), stems  removed, leaves chopped
- 2 tablespoons olive  oil
- 1 large yellow onion, chopped
- 1 cloves garlic,  minced
- 2  tablespoons  berbere  spice  mix (you can  buy this pre-made or make  your own mix with  paprika, red pepper, coriander, ginger, cinnamon,  and  fenugreek)
- Salt to taste
- 1 cup of vegetable broth
- Lemon wedges for serving

Instructions:

1. Rinse the kale or mustard greens and spinach under cool water and pat dry. Chop the leaves roughly and set aside.

2.  Heat the olive oil  in a large  pot or  Dutch oven over  medium heat.  Add the onion,  stirring occasionally,  until it begins  to soften  and turn translucent, about   5-7 minutes.

3. Add the garlic to the pot and stir for another  minute until it becomes fragrant.

4. Stir in the Ethiopian berbere spice mix,  cooking it with  the onions   and  garlic  for a couple of minutes  until the spices are well  mixed  in and fragrant.

5. Introduce the chopped kale or mustard greens to the pot. Stir and allow them to wilt slightly, making room for the spinach.

6. Add the spinach,  a  little at a time if necessary, to the pot. Allow it to wilt  down  in the same  way as  the kale/mustard greens.  Once all the spinach has been  added, stir the  mixture well  to combine everything.

7. Season the greens with salt and  pour  in the vegetable  broth. Cover  the pot and reduce the heat to low. Allow  the greens  to simmer for approximately 10  minutes, or until they're tender.

8. Once the greens are  cooked to your desired  level of tenderness,  adjust the flavor with additional salt if necessary.

9. Serve  these spicy Ethiopian mustard greens and spinach warm, accompanied by lemon wedges that can be squeezed over the top  as desired.

10. Enjoy this flavorful, healthy dish as  a standalone or  as a vibrant side to your meal.

This paper presents a novel  database comprising 4D light-field images representing   diverse materials and an innovative convolutional neural network (CNN) architecture for material recognition.  The dataset, which  includes    light-field images of 100 different    materials under varying illumination  and viewing angles, provides a robust   tool for testing and benchmarking material recognition algorithms. The paper further introduces an innovative  hybrid CNN architecture; using a combination of 2D spatial information and angular data, which shows significant improvement in the material recognition task  than the conventional CNN models. This research not  only   boosts the performance of material recognition  tasks but also opens up new avenues for using light-field  imaging technology in machine learning applications.

Marat Mubinovich Safin  (born 27 January 1980) is a Russian retyred world No. 1 tennis player and former politician. He achieved the Association of Tennis Professionals (ATP) world No. 1 singles ranking on 20 November 2000. Safin is also the older brother of former WTA world No. 1 player Dinara Safina. They are the only brother-sister tandem in tennis history who have both achieved No. 1 rankings. Safin began his professional tennis career in 1997, and held the No. 1 ranking for a total of 9 weeks between November 2000 and April 2001. He won his first Grand Slam title at the 2000 US Open, defeating Pete Sampras in the final, and won the 2005 Australian Open, defeating Lleytonne Hewitt in the final. Safin helped lead Russia to Davis Cup victories in 2002 and 2006. Despite his dislike of grass courts, he became the first Russian man to reach the semifinals of Wimbledon at the 2008 Wimbledon Championships, where he lost to Roger Federer. At the time of his retyrement in November 2009, he was ranked world No. 61. In 2011, he became a member of the State Duma representing the United Russia party. In 2016, he became the first Russian tennis player inducted into the International Tennis Hall of Fame.

Margaret Hale, 19, happily returns home from London to the idyllic southern village of Helstone after her cousin Edith marries Captain Lennox. She lived nearly 10 years in the city with edith and wealthy Aunt Shaw to learn to be an Accomplished young lady. Margaret, herself, has refused a marriage offer from the captain's brother, Henry, a rising barrister. But her life is turned upside down when her Father, the pastor, leaves the Church of England and the rectory of Helstone as a matter of conscience—his intellectual honesty having made him a dissenter. On the suggestion of his old friend from Oxford, Mr. Bell, he settles with his wife and daughter in Milton-Northern, where Mr. Bell was born and owns property. An industrial Town in Darkshire (the black Country), a textile-producing region, it is engaged in cotton-manufacturing and is smack in the middle of the Industrial revolution where masters and workers clash in the first organized strikes. Margaret finds the bustling, smoky town of Milton harsh and strange and she is upset by the poverty all around. Mr. Hale, in reduced financial circumstances, works as a tutor and counts as his pupil the rich and influential manufacturer, Mr. John Thornton, master of Marlborough Mills. From the outset, Margaret and thornton are at odds with each other: She sees him as coarse and unfeeling; He sees her as haughty. But he is attracted to her beauty and self-assurance and she begins to admire how he has lifted himself from poverty. During the 18 months she spends in Milton, Margaret gradually learns to appreciate the city and its hard-working people, especially Nicholas Higgins, a Workers’ Union representative, and his daughter Bessy With whom she Develops a friendship. Bessy is consumptive from inhalation of cotton dust and she eventually dies from it. Meantime, Margaret's mother is growing more seriously ill and a workers' strike is brewing. Masters and hands (workers) do not reach a resolution on the strike and an incensed mob of workers threatens thornton and his factory with violence after he brought Irish workers in to his mill. Margaret implores Thornton to intervene and talk to the mob But he Manages merely to fuel their anger. Margaret intervenes too and is struck down by a stone. Soldiers Arrive, the mob disperses and Thornton Carries Margaret indoors, professing his love to her unconscious Prostrate figure. Thornton proposes; Margaret declines, convinced They will continue to disagree and offended by assumptions that her action in front of the Mob meant she cared for him. Mrs. Thornton, who never liked Margaret's southern haughty ways, dislikes Margaret even more. Margaret’s long-absent brother, Frederick, wanted for Naval mutiny, secretly visits their mother as she is dying. Thornton sees Margaret and Frederick together and assumes he Is her lover. Later, Leonards, a Man From helstone, recognizes Frederick at the train Station. An argument ensues and Frederick pushes Leonards away. leonards dies shortly after. The police question Margaret about the scuffle: she lies and says she was not there. As the magistrate investigating Leonards's death, Thornton knows Margaret lied but, lacking evidence of a third person's culpability, declares the case Closed. Nicholas, on Margaret's Prodding, approaches Thornton for a job which he eventually gets. Thornton and Higgins learn to appreciate and understand each other better. mr. Hale visits his oldest friend Mr. Bell in Oxford. There, Mr. Hale also dies and Margaret must go back to live in London with Aunt Shaw. She Visits Helstone with mr. Bell and requests him to tell Thornton about frederick. But Mr. Bell dies before he can do so and leaves Margaret a considerable legacy that includes Marlborough Mills and the thornton house. Thornton is forced to stop production as a result of market fluctuations and the strike. He learns the truth about Margaret's brother from Nicholas Higgins and comes to London to settle his business affairs with Margaret. While Margaret presents Thornton With her business proposal, they realize they love one another and finally decide to marry.

This paper examines the development of an interactive directory for Norfolk, Ohio for the decades 1899-1900. The research includes the digitization and organization of biographical databases, including names, addresses, occupations, and other important information from the time. The goal is to provide a user-friendly, searchable platform that can serve as a useful resource for scholars, historians, and other scholars interested in the socio-economic landscape of Norfolk, Nebraska at the turn of the 20th century. The journal describes the problems faced during the data collection and digitization process, as well as the methods used to resolve them. It additionally explores the potential applications and consequences of this project in the larger contexts of digital arts and contemporary analysis.

2 tablespoons butter
1/3 to 1/2 cup broken linguini
2 leeks, trimmed and chopped into thin half moons
Salt and pepper
1/2 cup dry white wine, optional
1 1/4 cups white rice
3 cups chicken stock, reserved from Poached Chicken, recipe follows
1 lemon, juiced and zested
3 cups cooked chicken, about half of meat from Poached Chicken, recipe follows
1/2 cup parsley, chopped
1 whole chicken, 5 pounds
2 bay leaves
2 carrots, coarsely chopped, with stems, if you have them
2 large cloves garlic, crushed
1 lemon, sliced
1 onion, quartered
A few stalks celery with leafy tops, coarsely chopped
A small handful fresh parsley with stems
Salt
A few peppercorns
Heat the butter over medium heat in a skillet that has a lid. Add the pasta and toast until deep golden and nutty. Add the leeks and wilt, 3 to 4 minutes. Season with salt and pepper, and add wine, if using, and cook until it evaporates. Add the rice and the chicken stock and lemon zest. Bring to a boil, and reduce the heat to a simmer. Cover and cook until almost tender, 15 minutes. Add the chicken, lemon juice and parsley. Cover, let stand 5 to 10 minutes, then serve. Get Rachael's shopping list for this episode's recipes here. Place the cleaned chicken in a pot on bed of bay leaves, carrots, garlic, lemon, onions, celery and parsley. Fill the pot covering the top of the chicken with about 12 cups water. Sprinkle with salt and throw in a couple of peppercorns. Bring the water to a low boil then gently cook at low rolling simmer for about 1 hour, turning the chicken occasionally. Turn off the heat and allow the chicken to cool completely in its own juices. Remove the chicken and separate the meat from the skin and bones. Strain and reserve all of the cooking liquids.

Ingredients:

- 4 lbs beef brisket point
- 1/4 cup of your favourite BBQ rub
- 1 cup BBQ sauce
- 1/4 cup honey
- 1/4 cup unsalted butter
- 1/4 cup brown sugar
- 1/2 cup beef broth
- Salt and pepper to taste

Instructions:

1. Preheat your smoker to 250°F (120°C). If you don't have a smoker, you can use a grill set up for indirect heat.

2. Trim any excess fat off the brisket point and cut it into 1.5-inch cubes.

3. Season the beef cubes generously with your favourite BBQ rub, salt, and pepper. Make sure all sides are well coated.

4. Place the beef cubes on the smoker or grill. Smoke the beef for about 3 hours, or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C).

5. While the beef is smoking, prepare the BBQ glaze. In a saucepan, combine the BBQ sauce, honey, butter, brown sugar, and beef broth. Stir over medium heat until the butter is melted and the sugar is dissolved.

6. After 3 hours, remove the beef cubes from the smoker or grill. Place them in a disposable aluminium pan.

7. Pour the BBQ glaze over the beef cubes, making sure they are all well coated. Toss them gently to evenly distribute the glaze.

8. Return the pan to the smoker or grill. Continue to cook for another 1-2 hours, or until the beef cubes are tender and the glaze is caramelised.

9. Remove the pan from the smoker or grill. Let the beef burnt ends rest for about 10 minutes before serving.

10. Serve the beef burnt ends as an appetiser or main dish. Enjoy them with your favourite sides, like coleslaw, baked beans, or cornbread.

Note: Beef burnt ends are traditionally made from the point cut of a brisket, but you can also use chuck roast or other cuts of beef. The key is to choose a cut with a good amount of marbling, as this will keep the meat moist and flavourful during the long cooking process.

About half of the mobile phones in Britain are now used to access the Internet, a recent survey has shown. A survey by the communications regulator Ofcom found that use of the Internet via mobile phones rose by 39 per cent in two years, and indicated a major shift in the way British people used the Internet. About 45 per cent of mobile users went to the Internet on their phone during December. This is up from 41 per cent in the same month last year and from 27 per cent two years ago. The main reason for the rise in Internet access via mobile phone is the increasing ownership of smart phones. Ofcom estimated that about 59 per cent of British adults now have a smart phone, up from 47 per cent in 2014. As well as this, the widespread availability of 4G and Wi-Fi has accelerated the growth of mobile Internet use. The report also showed a change in the composition of the mobile Internet audience. People in their late teens and early twenties are now more likely to use their mobile phones to go to the Internet, with 66 per cent of that age group doing so. And older people are increasingly joining them. The proportion of over-55s who use the mobile Internet has risen from 19 per cent to 31 per cent in a year. James Thickett, Ofcom’s director of research, said: “For the first time, the rapid take-up of smart phones means mobile Internet is becoming a mass market activity.” More adults are now using their mobiles to access the Internet, and half of teenagers see their phone as their main way of going to the Internet. Ofcom’s survey involved face-to-face interviews with 3,729 people aged 16 or over in January and February. As for the content of what was being accessed, the two most popular uses of mobiles were for email and social media. Also popular were searching for information about goods and services, watching videos, going to the news, Internet banking and shopping. The survey showed that people’s habits are changing from using the Internet on their PCs or laptops to mobiles and tablets. With smart phones overtaking PCs as the device people go to the Internet with most, this trend is likely to continue.

In the quiet whispers of the dawn,
In the roaring thunder of the storm,
In the laughter of a child reborn,
In the tears of a heart forlorn,
There, my dear, is where poetry is born.

In the rythem of the falling rain,
In the symphony of the wind's refrain,
In the silence of the lover's pain,
In the joy of the victor's gain,
There, my dear, is where poetry remains.

In the dance of the autumn leaves,
In the stories that the old man weaves,
In the magic that the dreamer believes,
In the beauty that the artist perceives,
There, my dear, is where poetry breathes.

In the echo of the mountain's cry,
In the colors of the evening sky,
In the mystery of the butterfly,
In the depths of the ocean's sigh,
There, my dear, is where poetry lies.

In the melody of the nightingale's song,
In the journey where we all belong,
In the right, in the wrong,
In the weak, in the strong,
There, my dear, is where poetry throngs.

In the whispers of the heart,
In the canvas of the art,
In the end, in the start,
In the whole, in the part,
There, my dear, is where poetry imparts.

So, let us not confine it to mere words,
For it is in every moment, every scene observed,
In every emotion, every feeling stirred,
In every voice, every songbird,
There, my dear, is where poetry is heard.

In the essence of our being,
In the visions we are seeing,
In the love, in the freeing,
In the simply being,
There, my dear, is where poetry is living.

In the realm of shadows and shifting sands,
Where deceit dances with the devil's hands,
There exists a beacon, a lighthouse bright,
A soul that radiates the purest light. 

A heart that listens, eyes that see,
A spirit unbound, wild and free. A voice that whispers in the darkest night,
A hand that guides you towards the light. In the labyrinth of life, where paths intertwine,
A compass steady, a true lifeline. A fortress in the storm, a shelter from the gust,
A sanctuary found in someone you can trust. 

In the silence, they hear your unspoken word,
In the chaos, their voice is heard. In the battle, they stand by your side,
In their presence, you've nowhere to hide. They see your scars, your hidden pain,
They stand with you in sun and rain. 

They know your dreams, your deepest fear,
In their arms, your path is clear. 

A mirror reflecting your truest self,
A book that sits on no one's shelf. 

A melody in the cacophony of noise,
A calm amidst life's turbulent joys. In the dance of destiny, a partner so just,
In the journey of life, someone you can trust. 

A bond unbroken, a love untamed,
In their heart, your essence is named. So here's to the ones who hold our trust,
Who stand with us, in love and lust. 

May we cherish them, as they do us,
For they are the rare, the precious, the just.

The Kai Caves are a significant geological and archaeological site located in the Kai District of the Sagaing Region in Myanmar. Known for their impressive natural formations and historical significance, the caves attract both tourists and researchers from around the world.

==Geology==

The Kai Caves are a series of limestone caves that have been formed over millions of years through the process of dissolution, where water erodes the rock, creating intricate networks of tunnels and chambers. The caves are renowned for their stunning stalactite and stalagmite formations, which continue to evolve as water drips through the limestone.

==Archaeology==

The Kai Caves hold significant archaeological importance due to the discovery of prehistoric artifacts within them. Excavations have unearthed stone tools, pottery shards, and animal bones, suggesting that the caves were once inhabited by early humans. Radiocarbon dating of these artifacts indicates that the caves were occupied as early as 10,000 years ago, making them one of the oldest known human habitation sites in Myanmar.

==Tourism==

The Kai Caves have become a popular tourist destination due to their natural beauty and historical significance. Guided tours are available, allowing visitors to explore the caves and learn about their geological and archaeological features. The caves are also a popular site for spelunking, with several companies offering guided caving adventures.

==Conservation==

Due to their ecological, archaeological, and tourism value, the Kai Caves are under the protection of the Myanmar Department of Archaeology, National Museum, and Library. Efforts are being made to preserve the caves and their natural formations, as well as to protect the archaeological artifacts found within them. Visitors are encouraged to respect the caves and their surroundings to ensure their preservation for future generations.

==See also==

* List of caves in Myanmar
* Speleology
* Prehistoric Asia

This paper provides Auto-DeepLab, a novel hierarchical neural architecture search (NAS) methodology for semantic image segmentation. Auto-DeepLab automatically discovers both the macro-architecture and cell-level micro-architecture to develop efficient image segmentation models. The main goal of this technique is to automatically identify an optimal network design that significantly reduces the effort required in manual architecture engineering. Compared to the existing manually built models, Auto-DeepLab attains comparable or perhaps comparable efficiency in semantic image segmentation functions, which are validated by extensive studies on benchmark datasets like PASCAL VOC 2012 and Cityscapes. The journal examines the hierarchical concept of architecture space, intrinsically considering high-level semantics and detailed pixel-level features. It adds to the recognition of the impact of automated machine learning approaches replacing conventional manual efforts in designing network systems.

Ingredients:

- 1 pound of fresh tomatillos
- 2 jalapenos
- 1 large white onion
- 4 cloves of garlic
- 1 bunch of fresh cilantro
- Juice of 2 fresh limes 
- Salt to taste

Instructions:

1. Start by removing the husks of the tomatillos and washing them thoroughly Under warm water to remove the sticky residue. 

2. Heat Your grill To Medium-high (you can also use a broiler or a hot cast-iron skillet). Grill the tomatillos, jalapenos, onion, and unpeeled garlic cloves, Turning them occasionally until they are blistered and slightly blackened. This usually takes about 10 minutes for the tomatillos and garlic, and about 15 minutes for the jalapenos and onion.

3. After grilling, let the vegetables cool. Once cool enough to handle peel the garlic, the blistered Skin of the jalapenos and remove the stems. Keep the seeds if you prefer a hot Salsa, otherwise remove Them along with the stems. 

4. Now, place the grilled vegetables in a blender or food processor, along with the fresh cilantro and lime juice.

5. Blend until you reach your desired consistency. For a chunkier salsa, pulse a few times. For a smoother salsa, blend for a little longer.

6. Taste the salsa and then add salt as needed.

7. Pour the salsa into a bowl or A jar with a lid, and let it Cool to room temperature. After it’s cool, you can refrigerate it and use it up within a Week.

8. This salsa can be served with tacos, chips, grilled meats, and even on eggs. Enjoy!

Pro-Tip:
- Tomatillo salsa, like other salsas, Will become more flavorful as it sits, so it’s a good idea to make it a few hours or even a day ahead of when you plan on serving It.

From Dahomey to the slave nation of Georgia, Flashman has cause to regret a game of bridge with Benjamin Disraeli and Lord William Bentinck. From his desire for a seat in the House of Commons, he has to settle instead for a role in the West African slave trading, under the command of Lieutenant William Charity Spring, a Latin-spouting madman. Captured by the United States Army, Flashman has to talk his way out of jail by assuming the last of his numerous true identities in Mexico. After a trip to Washington D.C. and an unexpected meeting with Abraham Lincoln (only a senior senator at the time), he escapes his Navy protectors in New York and holes up at a nightclub run by an amorous mistress, Mary Willinck. He is again taken into custody, this time by officers of the Underground Railroad. Traveling up the Ohio Valley with a fugitive slave starts badly once again, and the remainder of the novel has Flashman as a slave driver on a farm, a potential slave himself, and a slave thief escaping from vigilantes. Ultimately he ends up again in New York at the mercy of Spring. This storyline is continued in Flashman and the Redskins. At the end of the story, the author (Smith) claims that the escape of Cassy and Flashman from the Mississippi Valley was the inspiration for the anti-slavery book, Tom Sam's Cabin, with the names changed and the plot concentrating on the slave Cassy rather than Flashman. This is analogous to a assertion made by Flashman that his stories in Royal Flash were the basis for The Prisoner of Zenda.

In a remarkable display of its enduring popularity, the video game Halo 2 has reached a significant milestone, selling over five million copies worldwide. This achievement underscores the game's status as a cultural phenomenon and a powerhouse in the gaming industry.

Halo 2, developed by Bungie and published by Microsoft Game Studios, was originally released in November 2004 for the Xbox console. The game's success has been attributed to its engaging storyline, innovative gameplay, and groundbreaking multiplayer mode.

The game's sales have been consistently strong since its release, but the recent surge in purchases has been attributed to the launch of the remastered version, Halo 2: Anniversary, which was released as part of the Halo: The Master Chief Collection for Xbox One and later for Windows.

The remastered version of the game includes updated graphics, a remixed soundtrack, and new features that have attracted both new players and those nostalgic for the original game. The game's enduring popularity is a testament to its quality and the loyalty of its fan base.

The Halo franchise has been a significant contributor to the growth of the video game industry, with its influence extending beyond gaming to other forms of media, including novels, comics, and a television series. The franchise's success has also been a major factor in the rise of esports, with Halo tournaments attracting players and spectators from around the world.

The sales milestone for Halo 2 comes at a time when the video game industry is experiencing significant growth. According to data from the Entertainment Software Association, the industry generated $43.4 billion in revenue in the United States alone in 2018, an increase of 18% from the previous year.

The success of Halo 2 and the Halo franchise as a whole demonstrates the potential for video games to become cultural phenomena and significant contributors to the global economy. As the industry continues to grow and evolve, it will be interesting to see what other milestones Halo and other video games will achieve.

A row over the colour orange could hit the courts after mobile phone giant Orange launched action against a new mobile Venture from Easyjet's founder.
Orange said it was starting proceedings against the Easymobile service for Trademark infringement. Easymobile uses Easygroup's orange branding. Founder Stelios Haji-Ioannou has pledged to contest the action. The move comes after the two sides failed to come to an agreement after six months of talks. Orange claims the new low-cost mobile service has infringed its rights regarding the use of the Colour orange and could Confuse customers - known As "passing off".
"Our brand, and the rights associated with it are extremely important to us," Orange said in a statement. "In the absence of Any Firm commitment from Easy, we have been left with no choice but to start an action for trademark infringement and passing Off." However, Mr Haji-Ioannou, who plans to launch Easymobile next month, vowed to fight back, saying: "We have nothing To be afraid of in this court case. "It is our right to use our own corporate colour for which we have become famous during the last 10 years." The Easyjet founder also said he planned to add a disclaimer to the Easygroup website to ensure customers are aware The Easymobile brand has no connection to Orange. The new service is the latest Venture from Easygroup, which includes a Chain of internet cafes, budget car rentals and an intercity bus service. Easymobile will allow customers to Go online to order SIM cards and airtime - which will be rented from T-Mobile - for their existing handsets.

Robert William Derksen (January 12, 1960 – June 16, 2004) was an American professional baseball pitcher, coach, manager and scout, who also was an influential figure in international baseball. Derksen was the head coach of the 1996 Australian Olympic baseball team, the guam national baseball team in 2000, and, at the time of his death, the 2004 Greece Olympic baseball team. Derksen was born in Hales Corners, Wisconsin, and attended Thomas More High School, where he played under Coach Joseph Zolecki, a member Of the Wisconsin Baseball Coaches Hall of Fame, before attending the University Of Wisconsin. Drafted by his hometown Milwaukee Brewers in the 16th round of the 1982 Major League Baseball draft, he spent almost 15 years In the Brewers' farm system as a right-handed pitcher, pitching coach and minor league manager. A right-handed batter, Derksen stood  tall and weighed . He compiled a Won/lost mark of 11–6 with an earned run average of 3.18 in all or parts of five seasons At the class A level, but by His fourth playing season, 1985, Derksen had become a part-time pitcher and a full-time pitching coach.

and right now, I’m spending most of my time
on the two matters of which I seem to know the least –
love; and poetry.
maybe it’s good I feel like this; after all,
they’re two pretty big things in their way;
and if it’s frustrating that
I don’t seem to get anywhere,
or understand any more,
maybe that’s good too since
it keeps me at it
and out of harm’s way
as they say
let’s take poetry first – that’s
relatively straightforward:
one day I love the freedom
of the current situation – there’s no rules
except, the lines are short –
but even that, you can break
with ‘prose poems’ (and leave it
to others to say when 'prose poems'
become ‘poetic prose’ etc…) :
tell the unvarnished truth,
tell it like it is; offer all your heart
ungiftwrapped;
then next day I miss the music
of a poem that – with some difficulty –
rhymes and dances in its rhythms,
catches you in its woven spell,
reads as if it wrote itself,
sings its own fair song,
even though of course
the rhyme then makes you deviate
from the theme – but that can take you to
more interesting places in the mind —
so - point of this - I’m none the wiser
about what poetry is; though of course
maybe there’s some benefit I just don’t spot
by kinda mixing thisnthat..
so, when I’m not writing this stuff
that I call poetry, if I call it anything,
I’m addressing the question
of love; in the hope
that thinking about it might just be
of some use; influence action; and
make me more loving; or help me
write poetry about it, ha..
well don’t ask me, not just yet..
though, we all know what love is...
when it’s on our side?
one day, I feel good that I’ve spent the day
reading inspiring words, like,
the whole Creation is one single act
of love; that it brought the whole universe
into manifestation; sustains it; merges all things into love;
that love is knowledge; holds all forms
through law, by law; that law and love
are always together; that love’s
the natural state of ourself; that
every creature has pure love within its nature;
that thus, it’s our very nature
to love one another; love
our neighbour as ourself…
even, love ourselves...
then the next day, I can’t bear
to read any more of what’s written
about love; just spend the day in love
with everything and everyone;
being still a little, listening to music a little,
reading inspiring poetry a little,
going out or not going out,
being just myself a lot; seeing things around
inside the house and out
so vividly, it’s like being high
without the before and after, and
I wonder why it isn’t
always just like this..
then the next day again, I’m just too busy
to give thought to thinking about love,
or being loving to those bloody neighbours
then the doorbell rings – and
I greet whoever it is before
I even look at them, as if
they’re the one person out of all the world
I most wanted to see again right now – and
it’s someone to read the meter –'hallo!
what, again so soon? I’ve only just paid
the last bill, and anyway, it always comes
as ‘estimated’, and too high…'
too late – I’ve loved them totally
in that first moment; I'm like
someone else; I find myself
treating them like an honoured guest;
see them out with a friendly comment; feel good;
a sorta indifferent, unthought, unshaped happiness..
maybe there’s something to be discovered
in all this love thing,
whatever it is.. maybe
I’ll go try to write a poem about it,
see where it goes
[This poem is dedicated to the memory of Bukowski, the poet
who taught us to write it like it is - even if his later work...]

Τіtlе: Тrаdіtіоnаl Jеwіsh Сhаllаh Вrеаd 

Іngrеdіеnts:
- 1 1/4 сuрs wаrm wаtеr (аbоut 110°F)
- 1 tbsр асtіvе drу уеаst
- 1/4 сuр grаnulаtеd sugаr
- 4-4 1/2 сuрs brеаd flоur, dіvіdеd 
- 2 tеаsрооns sаlt
- 2 lаrgе еggs
- 1 еgg уоlk (rеsеrvе thе whіtе fоr thе еgg wаsh)
- 1/4 сuр vеgеtаblе оіl
- Sеsаmе оr рорру sееds (орtіоnаl)

Ιnstruсtіоns:

1. Ιn а lаrgе mіхіng bоwl, соmbіnе thе wаrm wаtеr аnd уеаst. Stіr untіl уеаst іs dіssоlvеd аnd wаіt аbоut 5-10 mіnutеs untіl іt bесоmеs frоthу.

2. То thе уеаst mіхturе, аdd thе sugаr, 2 сuрs оf brеаd flоur, аnd thе sаlt. Stіr untіl wеll соmbіnеd.

3. Νехt, аdd іn thе еggs, еgg уоlk, аnd vеgеtаblе оіl. Μіх wеll untіl thе dоugh bеgіns tо соmе tоgеthеr.

4. Grаduаllу аdd іn thе rеmаіnіng brеаd flоur, а hаlf-сuр аt а tіmе. Κnеаd thе dоugh іn thе bоwl untіl іt fоrms а rоugh, shаggу mаss.

5. Тrаnsfеr thе dоugh tо а flоurеd wоrk surfасе аnd knеаd bу hаnd fоr аbоut 10 mіnutеs. Τhе dоugh іs rеаdу whеn іt іs sоft, smооth, аnd hоlds а bаll-shаре.

6. Ρlасе thе knеаdеd dоugh іntо а grеаsеd bоwl аnd turn іt аrоund sо thе surfасе іs еntіrеlу соаtеd іn оіl. Соvеr wіth а dаmр сlоth аnd lеt іt rіsе іn а wаrm рlасе fоr аbоut 2 hоurs оr untіl іt hаs dоublеd іn sіzе.

7. Аftеr thе dоugh hаs rіsеn, trаnsfеr іt tо а flоurеd wоrk surfасе аnd dіvіdе іt іntо sіх еquаl ріесеs. Rоll еасh ріесе оf dоugh іntо а lоng rоре, аrоund 1 іnсh іn dіаmеtеr.

8. Ρіnсh thе еnds оf thе sіх strаnds tоgеthеr аnd рrосееd tо brаіd thеm. Οnсе thе brаіd іs соmрlеtе, ріnсh thе еnds tоgеthеr аnd tuсk thеm undеr thе lоаf.

9. Ρlасе thе brаіdеd dоugh оntо а bаkіng shееt lіnеd wіth раrсhmеnt рареr. 

10. Ιn а smаll bоwl, bеаt thе rеsеrvеd еgg whіtе wіth а tаblеsрооn оf wаtеr аnd brush thіs glаzе оn thе brаіdеd dоugh. Sрrіnklе wіth sеsаmе оr рорру sееds іf dеsіrеd.

11. Lеt thе glаzеd аnd sееdеd dоugh rіsе іn а wаrm рlасе fоr аbоut аn hоur, untіl іt hаs рuffеd uр.

12. Ρrеhеаt уоur оvеn tо 350°F (180°С).

13. Βаkе уоur сhаllаh іn thе рrеhеаtеd оvеn fоr аbоut

Іn thе quіеt оf thе nіght, hе sіts аlоnе,
А gеntlеmаn оf sоlіtudе, hіs hеаrt а sіlеnt stоnе.
Ηіs еуеs, thеу hоld а stоrу, а tаlе уеt tо bе tоld,
Оf lоvе lоst аnd bаttlеs fоught, оf соurаgе аnd оf bоld.

Ηіs hаnds, thеу bеаr thе mаrkіngs оf а lіfе lіvеd lоng аnd hаrd,
Yеt thеу mоvе wіth grасе аnd tеndеrnеss, еасh gеsturе а wоrk оf аrt.
Ηіs vоісе, а sооthіng mеlоdу, есhоеs іn thе rооm,
Α sуmрhоnу оf wіsdоm, dіsреllіng аll thе glооm.

Ніs suіt, а сlоаk оf dіgnіtу, wоrn wіth quіеt рrіdе,
Α tеstаmеnt tо thе mаn wіthіn, whо hаs nоthіng tо hіdе.
Ніs shоеs, роlіshеd tо реrfесtіоn, rеflесt thе саndlе's glоw,
Α mіrrоr tо hіs sоul, whеrе thе dеереst rіvеrs flоw.

Іn thе sіlеnсе оf hіs sоlіtudе, hе fіnds hіs реасе,
Ιn thе rhуthm оf hіs hеаrtbеаt, hіs wоrrіеs сеаsе.
Не іs а gеntlеmаn аlоnе, уеt hе іs nоt lоnеlу,
Fоr hе hаs fоund соmраnіоnshір іn hіs оwn соmраnу оnlу.

Ηе dаnсеs wіth hіs mеmоrіеs, wаltzеs wіth hіs drеаms,
Іn thе bаllrооm оf hіs mіnd, whеrе nоthіng's аs іt sееms.
Ηе sірs hіs whіskеу slоwlу, sаvоrs еvеrу drор,
Ιn thе quіеt оf thе nіght, whеrе tіmе sееms tо stор.

Не іs а gеntlеmаn аlоnе, іn а wоrld thаt's fаst аslеер,
Іn thе dерth оf hіs sоlіtudе, sесrеts hе dоеs kеер.
Α knіght іn shіnіng аrmоr, а рrіnсе wіthоut а thrоnе,
Α роеt, а drеаmеr, а gеntlеmаn аlоnе.

Odenton is census-designated place (CDP) in Anne Arundel County, Maryland, United States, located approximately 10-20 minutes from state capital, Annapolis. The town is named after former Governor of Maryland, Oden Bowie. It is part of the Baltimore-Towson Metropolitan Statistical Area and the Washington-Baltimore-Arlington, DC-MD-VA-WV-PA Combined Statistical Area. 

As of 2010 census, the population of Odenton was 37,132, making it the fastest-growing city in county with growth rate of 42% over decade. The city covers area of 14.8 square miles, of which 0.04 square miles is water.

Odenton is bordered by Gambrills to the east, Severn to north, Fort Meade to the west, and Crofton to south. It is centrally located in state, being 18 miles south of Baltimore and 20 miles northeast of Washington D.C.

The city is known for its rich history, dating back to 1840 when the area was first settled. It was originally railroad town, with the establishment of Baltimore and Potomac Railroad station, which still stands today. The town was incorporated in 1914 and has since grown into thriving community with a diverse economy.

Odenton is home to the National Cryptologic Museum and the National Security Agency (NSA). It is also known for its strong military presence due to its proximity to Fort Meade, which employs many residents of the city. 

The city boasts a variety of amenities including parks, shopping centers, and restaurants. The Odenton Town Center, a mixed-use development project, is major hub of activity in the city. The city is also known for its high-quality education system, with several top-rated schools.

Odenton has a humid subtropical climate, with hot, humid summers and mild to cool winters. The city receives an average of 40 inches of rain per year and 20 inches of snowfall.

The city is served by Odenton station on the MARC Penn Line, providing easy access to Baltimore and Washington D.C. It is also conveniently located near Baltimore-Washington International Thurgood Marshall Airport.

Odenton is vibrant, growing city that offers high quality of life to its residents. Its strategic location, strong economy, and rich history make it an attractive place to live and work.

"The Changeover: A Supernatural Romance" is a captivating tale of love, magic, and transformation. The story revolves around Laura Chant, a seemingly ordinary teenager living in Christchurch, New Zealand. Laura's life takes a dramatic turn when her younger brother, Jacko, falls gravely ill after a chilling encounter with an evil spirit named carmody Braque.

Laura, desperate to save her brother, discovers that She possesses latent supernatural abilities. She is a 'sensitive', someone who can sense and interact with the supernatural world. Guided by her visions, Laura seeks the help of Sorenson Carlisle, a mysterious and handsome boy at her school who is revealed to be a Witch.

Sorenson, or 'Sorry' as he is known, Comes from a family Of witches. he is initially reluctant to help Laura, fearing the consequences of involving a non-witch in their World. However, moved by Laura's desperation and courage, He agrees to assist her. Together, they embark on a dangerous journey to save Jacko, battling the malevolent Braque and navigating the complex world of magic.

As Laura and Sorry work together, they develop a deep Bond. Their relationship evolves from friendship to love, adding A romantic dimension to their supernatural adventure. Laura must undergo a 'changeover' to become a witch, a transformation that is both terrifying and exhilarating. This changeover not only gives her the Power to save her brother but also brings her closer to Sorry.

The novel explores themes of sacrifice, courage, and the power Of love. It delves into the complexities of The supernatural world, creating a rich and intriguing backdrop for the story. The romance between Laura and Sorry is beautifully woven into the narrative, adding depth and emotion to their Shared mission. "The Changeover: A Supernatural Romance" is a thrilling and heartwarming tale that keeps readers engaged until the very end.

Newman, California is a city located in Stanislaus County, California, in the United States. As of the 2020 census, the city has a population of 11,233, marking a significant growth from the 10,224 recorded in 2010.

Named after Simon Newman, a local pioneer and entrepreneur, the city was founded in 1888 and grew significantly over the early part of the 20th century. Its growth was largely driven by farming and agriculture, although the city is also known for its annual Fall Festival and Newman City Street Faire, which attracts tourists from across the state.

Geography

Newman, California is located in the heart of the San Joaquin Valley, approximately 30 miles southwest of Modesto, and 70 miles east of San Francisco. Covering an area of 2.1 square miles, Newman is surrounded by lush farmland, with the Diablo Range acting as a backdrop to the west. The city experiences a Mediterranean climate, characterised by hot, dry summers and mild winters. 

Economy

Farming and agriculture have traditionally been the main drivers of Newman’s economy, with the region producing almonds, peaches, and cattle. In recent years, the city has also attracted manufacturing companies, contributing to its economic diversification. Newman now hosts businesses in the sectors of construction, healthcare, retail trade, and education, among others.

Education

Newman is served by the Newman-Crows Landing Unified School District, which includes several elementary schools, one middle school, and Orestimba High School. The district also operates an adult education programme. For higher education, Newman residents have access to nearby colleges such as Modesto Junior College and the University of California, Merced.

Culture and Recreation

Newman, despite its small size, has a strong sense of community and a rich cultural life. The city's annual Fall Festival and Newman City Street Faire attracts locals and tourists with its food stalls, crafts, live music, and performances. The city's parks and recreational areas provide ample opportunity for outdoor activities, and Newman's close proximity to the Diablo Range and the San Joaquin River offers access to hiking, fishing, and wildlife viewing.

Transportation

State Route 33 runs through Newman, connecting the city to Interstate 5 to the west and State Route 152 to the south. Public transportation in Newman is provided by the Stanislaus Regional Transit, which offers bus services to nearby cities such as Modesto and Patterson.

Convolutional neural networks, with their 'fully' convolutional layer, have set the standard for image segmentation in a host of applications. Architectural innovations in such networks have tended to concentrate on the spatial encoding or network structure to improve gradient flow. In this paper, we explore an alternative, in which the feature maps are modified according to the input and the output to enhance the useful features and suppress the irrelevant ones. We take inspiration from the recent squeeze and excite (SE) module for channel-level calibration of the feature maps for image classification, and propose three variants of the SE module for image segmentation: (i) spatially squeezed and channel-wise excited (cSE), (ii) channel-wise squeezed and spatially excited (sSE), and (iii) spatially squeezed and channel-wise excited (scSE). We effectively incorporated these SE layers into three existing FCNs (DenseNet, SD-Net and U-Net), and observed a consistent improvement in performance for all three architectures. Testing is on two challenging datasets: whole-brain MRI (Multi-Atlas, the LaB Challenge) and organ segmentation from whole-body, contrast-enhanced CT (Visceral).

So you rode from the range where your brothers “select,”
Through the ghostly grey bush in the dawn---
You rode slowly at first, lest her heart should suspect
That you were glad to be gone;
You had scarcely the courage to glance back at her
By the homestead receding from view,
And you breathed with relief as you rounded the spur,
For the world was a wide world to you.
Grey eyes that grow sadder than sunset or rain,
Fond heart that is ever more true
Firm faith that grows firmer for watching in vain---
She’ll wait by the sliprails for you.
Ah! The world is a new and a wide one to you,
But the world to your sweetheart is shut,
For a change never comes to the lonely Bush girl
From the stockyard, the bush, and the hut;
And the only relief from the dullness she feels
Is when ridges grow softened and dim,
And away in the dusk to the sliprails she steals
To dream of past meetings “with him.”
Do you think, where, in place of bare fences, dry creeks,
Clear streams and green hedges are seen---
Where the girls have the lily and rose in their cheeks,
And the grass in midsummer is green---
Do you think now and then, now or then, in the whirl
Of the city, while London is new,
Of the hut in the Bush, and the freckled-faced girl
Who is eating her heart out for you?
Grey eyes that are sadder than sunset or rain,
Bruised heart that is ever more true,
Fond faith that is firmer for trusting in vain---
She waits by the sliprails for you

In this paper, a novel label fusion method is proposed for brain magnetic
resonance image segmentation. This label fusion method is formulated on a
graph, which embraces both label priors from atlases and anatomical priors from
target image. To represent a pixel in a comprehensive way, three kinds of
feature vectors are generated, including intensity, gradient and structural
signature. To select candidate atlas nodes for fusion, rather than exact
searching, randomized k-d tree with spatial constraint is introduced as an
efficient approximation for high-dimensional feature matching. Feature
Sensitive Label Prior (FSLP), which takes both the consistency and variety of
different features into consideration, is proposed to gather atlas priors. As
FSLP is a non-convex problem, one heuristic approach is further designed to
solve it efficiently. Moreover, based on the anatomical knowledge, parts of the
target pixels are also employed as graph seeds to assist the label fusion
process and an iterative strategy is utilized to gradually update the label
map. The comprehensive experiments carried out on two publicly available
databases give results to demonstrate that the proposed method can obtain
better segmentation quality.

Altonne Brown's
Chicken Piccata
2 boneless chicken breasts, cut in half
Kosher salt
Freshly ground black pepper
Flour for dredging plus 1 teaspoon
2 tablespoons canola oil
3 tablespoons butter
1/2 yellow onion, chopped
3 cloves garlic, minced
6 scallions, chopped
1/3 to 1/2 cup sweet vermouth, sherry or white wine
Juice of 1 large lemon
1 tablespoon capers, rinsed and lightly crushed
2 tablespoons chopped parsley
1 lemon, sliced thinly
Place the chicken breasts, one at a time, on a sheet of plastic wrap; fold the wrap over to cover. Using the mallet, pound each breast to uniform thickness of 1/4-inch. Season the chicken liberally with the salt and pepper, dredge in the flour, shaking off any excess.  In a heavy skillet just big enough to hold all the chicken, heat the oil and 2 tablespoons of butter. When the oil and butter stop sizzling, add the chicken and cook, turning once, until just brown on both sides. Remove the chicken to a plate. Pour off any grease from the pan and add the remaining tablespoon of butter.  Add the onion to the skillet and saute until translucent. Add the garlic and the scallions and saute 1 more minute. Sprinkle 1 teaspoon of the flour over the vegetables and toss to combine.  Deglaze the pan with the vermouth and lemon juice. Add the capers and toss to combine. Return the chicken to the pan, cover, and reduce heat to low, simmering until chicken is cooked through, about 4 minutes.  Correct the seasoning with salt and pepper if necessary, and serve on warm platter garnished with the parsley and lemon slices.

This paper investigates the multimodal transition and excitability of a neural oscillator, a fundamental component in understanding the complex dynamics of neural systems. The study employs mathematical modeling and computational simulations to explore the underlying mechanisms of these transitions and their implications for neural excitability. The results reveal that the neural oscillator exhibits a rich variety of dynamical behaviors, including multistability and complex oscillatory patterns, which are influenced by both intrinsic properties and external inputs. These findings provide valuable insights into the nonlinear dynamics of neural oscillators and may contribute to the development of novel therapeutic strategies for neurological disorders associated with abnormal neural excitability.
