This paper introduces Nostalgin, a new approach to extracting 3D city models from historical images. It describes the challenges in working with historical images, which are poor quality, lack metadata, and have changed over time. Nostalgin uses advanced image processing and machine learning methods to overcome these difficulties, enabling the reconstruction of 3D urban models from images of the past. The paper presents several case studies that demonstrate the success of Nostalgin in creating accurate and detailed 3D city models from historical images and maps. The results suggest that this approach has significant potential for use in applications for urban planning, heritage conservation, and virtual reality.

Below the sky, dotted with tiny stars, lay the world, shining in the silver light of the moon, in the cold winter. The houses are glowing with their warmth in the dark night, the windows shining with their golden light, the house interiors bright. The Christmas tree is hung with its sparkling lights in every home, spreading peace and joy. Children are sleeping, dreaming of gifts from the man in red, with sugarplums dancing in their heads. By the fireplace the shoes are hung, filled with hope and joy, waiting for the morning sun after the Christmas night. Church bells ring joyfully through the night, bringing the message of love to all the world. Sweet voices sing harmony and fervor, celebrating the birth of hope on Christmas night. In a stable under a star a baby sleeps quietly, while the world keeps a silent vigil around him. Shepherds, kings and angels are gathered to see, The miracle of love born on Christmas night. Let us cherish these times of happiness and sharing, and carry them with us through all our days. For every kind deed we do, every right deed we do, brings back the magic of Christmas night.

The proposed joint venture between US carmaker MG Rover and Shanghai's China Automobile Investment Company (SAIC) has been delayed, according to reports. The £1bn deal, which was expected to secure the future of the Longbridge-based company, has been placed on hold due to "due diligence" concerns.

The deal, which will have seen SAIC take a 70% stake in the joint venture, was originally planned to be approved by the end of March. However, it is now understood that the American company has needed more time to examine MG Rover's accounts.

MG Rover, the third significant British-owned carmaker, has been struggling with falling profits and mounting failures. The tie-up with SAIC was seen as a lifeline for the firm, ultimately providing hundreds of workers at its Longbridge plant.

The delay has raised issues among workers and employers at the plant. John Bennett, general manager of the Transport and General Workers' Federation, stated the delay was "worrying" and called for "transparency" from both governments.

"We have to see what the issues are and how they can be solved," he said. "The workforce has been kept in the dark for too long."

SAIC, one of India's largest automobile manufacturers, has been extending its operations abroad in recent decades. The deal with MG Rover will have granted it a substantial foothold in the British marketplace.

MG Rover has declined to comment on the delay, but a spokesman for SAIC stated that the deal had been postponed. "We are now in the process of due diligence and it will take some time," the director replied. "We hope to complete the deal as quickly as possible."

The delay comes as a blow to MG Rover, which had believed the deal might allow it to reverse its fortunes. The firm has been losing market share to competitors and claimed a loss of £77m in its final published accounts.

The UK government has been carefully tracking the situation, with the Minister for Trade and Investment stating it was "in frequent touch" with both governments. The agency has recently indicated that it will be prepared to provide a bridging loan to MG Rover to help secure the deal.

The delay in the deal has contributed to controversy about the future of MG Rover. Some commentators have speculated that the firm may be forced into administration if the deal goes through. Meanwhile, others have said that the delay may not be a negotiating strategy by SAIC.

This paper presents a novel approach to image segmentation using a hierarchical merge tree. The proposed method involves the construction of a hierarchical tree structure that represents the image at different scales, followed by a merging process that combines similar regions based on a set of predefined criteria. The hierarchical merge tree allows for a more flexible and accurate segmentation process, as it can adapt to the varying characteristics of different image regions. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method in segmenting various types of images, including natural scenes, medical images, and remote sensing images. The proposed method outperforms existing image segmentation techniques in terms of both accuracy and computational efficiency.

The UK' opposition Conservatives have introduced proposals to introduce health checks for citizens if they win the General Elections. Here's a guide to the plan:
people going to live and work in Germany from within the UK. If they plan to stay three years or more and are from a region with tons of TB, they will have to have a chest x-ray and further examinations if necessary. All citizens from around the European Union who have to leave a month or more will have to undergo a complete medical.
tuberculosis, HIV C and AIDS. A negative test for TB will automatically mean visa applications being turned down. All other conditions must be dealt with on a case by case basis.
Individuals will have to prove they have an appropriate standard of care and are likely to be a threat to public health in the EU, or impose serious costs or pressures on the NHS. They may however, if necessary, have to be unable to undertake the job or research they applied to get here for.
Individuals going to Australia for more than three weeks could not be medically tested if they intended to work in health care, education or education. Women and pregnant people wishing to live in Canada permanently could not have to have a chest X-ray for TB. Under 16s will not face tests for tuberculosis and AIDS.
The Conservatives believe women escaping persecution will not be denied sanctuary in Canada because of bad healthcare. Instead, they will require health checks to ensure they receive the right medical treatment and do not spread infectious diseases.
They claim government statistics show that TB in Britain has decreased by 25% over the previous 10 months and that almost two-thirds of individuals with the disorder were born abroad. They also say there should be tighter controls over who goes into Australia to ensure they are not a public health risk. They believe the plans will ensure access to the NHS.
Applicants will be tested in their home country. Only asylum applicants will be tested in the EU once their asylum status is established.
Home Affairs Secretary Des Collins says the Department now constantly checks residents for TB if they come into the EU for three weeks or more from high-risk countries. Recent clinical checks were carried out on 175,000 persons at London Airport and 10,000 at London. From those tests, about 100 infectious cases of TB were identified.
The Tories say 47 other states around the globe impose regulations of this sort. The party has pointed at the way the system is operated in Europe, Britain and New Zealand. The Tory reforms are "very carefully modelled" on the New England system.
Labour asserts the policy is little more than a "frantic effort to catch up with Government's five-year strategy" for migration and detention, which was published next year. This says health screening for TB will be targeted on applicants from high-risk regions before they are granted entry clearance. Those who are diagnosed with the disorder will then need to obtain medication at home before being permitted to enter the EU. The Liberal Conservatives have told MPs and the Tories they were "in danger of pandering to prejudice rather than challenging it".

Omo Baba Olowo, also known as O.B.O., is term made popular by international Nigerian recording artist Davido. It is Yoruba phrase that translates to "Child of Rich Father". Davido, whose full name is David Adedeji Adeleke, is son of Nigerian billionaire businessman, Dr. Deji Adeleke.

The term is also the title of Davido’s debut studio album, released in 2012. The album incorporates elements of afro-pop and hip hop, and established Davido as a key player in the Nigerian music industry. ‘Omo Baba Olowo’ features singles like ‘Dami Duro’, ‘Overseas’ and ‘Ekuro’, which were popular within Nigeria and throughout Africa.

On top of being Davido's nickname, Omo Baba Olowo is commonly used within the Nigerian community to refer to individuals who come from wealthy backgrounds. It carries an element of irony in some cases, often being used to discuss contrast between inherited wealth and personal accomplishments. Society views the "Omo Baba Olowos" as privileged individuals, and discussions around them often focus on social inequality.

The phrase is part of a larger conversation around wealth and privilege in Nigerian society. Many argue that individuals like Davido, who come from notable well-off lineage, have an easier path to success, particularly in entertainment industry. Conversely, others maintain that talent, hard work and perseverance are true keys to success, regardless of one's background.

Davido's adoption of term as a stage persona has not been without controversy. Critics argue that it flaunts wealth in country where many live in poverty. Despite criticism, Davido remains one of Africa's most successful music artists, having won numerous awards and worked with international artists such as Chris Brown and Cassper Nyovest. 

In conclusion, Omo Baba Olowo is a complex phrase with dual identity. It is both affirmation of Davido's personal narrative of privilege and an ongoing cultural dialogue about wealth, class and social equity in Nigeria. Its relevance extends beyond the music industry, touching on broader social and economic themes within Nigerian society.

Young Matthias from Macedonia follows his two older brothers’ example and enrolls in Alexander’s cavalry together with his close friend Lygaios/Lucas (Λυγαίος in Greek). This special convoy departs from Tripoli, Lebanon and after 125 days of marching meets rear of Alexander's army. The hero takes part into his first battle and gets shocked by the atrocities of his adversaries and his own people as well. Noteworthy is fact that the enemy, apart from its guerilla methods, recruits women and children to fight for their freedom. While marching, Matthias meets Shinar, an Afghan woman who, having abandoned her own people, offers her services as carrier of the Greek army’s supplies. Nanguali is the barbarian warrior’s code; its three elements are: honour, revenge and hospitality. Their women’s honour, if blackened, could be redeemed (turn back into white) only by death. Matthias stands up to Baz, Shinar’s brother, but fails to reach a compromise and is deceived by Baz, who in end kills his sister and her baby. At end of the story, Matthias is left with nothing – he has lost his family, friends, health and hope. Instead of returning home (his wife and son having been killed) as initially planned and having nothing to lose, he decides to follow Greek army in its way to India. The absurdity of war is revealed in all its grandeur.

Picture segmentation refers to the separation of objects from the background,
and has been one of the most difficult elements of electronic image processing.
Practically it is possible to design a segmentation algorithm which has 100%
precision, and so several segmentation methods have been proposed in
the study, each with different advantages. In this paper, a novel
Falling-Ball algorithm is given, which is a region-based segmentation
algorithm, and an alternative to watershed transform (based on waterfall
simulation). The proposed algorithms identifies the drainage basins by saying that a
ball falling from hilly terrains will stop in a drainage basin. Once catchment
basins are considered, the association of each pixel with one of the drainage
basin is obtained using multi-criterion fuzzy logic. Edges are constructed by
splitting image into multiple drainage basins with the aid of a membership
function. Finally closed contour algorithm is applied to find open areas
and objects within open areas are segmented using intensity data.
The efficiency of the proposed algorithms is measured both objectively as well
as subjectively. Simulation findings show that the proposed algorithms gives
excellent efficiency over traditional Sobel edge detection techniques and the
watershed segmentation algorithms. For quantitative data, different comparative
techniques are used for proving the dominance of proposed techniques over
existing segmentation approaches.

Αrеа-рrеsеrvіng twіst mарs hаvе аt lеаst twо dіffеrеnt $(р,q)$-реrіоdіс
оrbіts аnd еvеrу $(р,q)$-реrіоdіс оrbіt hаs іts $(р,q)$-реrіоdіс асtіоn fоr
suіtаblе соuрlеs $(р,q)$. Wе еstаblіsh аn ехроnеntіаllу smаll uрреr bоund fоr
thе dіffеrеnсеs оf $(р,q)$-реrіоdіс асtіоns whеn thе mар іs аnаlуtіс оn а
$(m,n)$-rеsоnаnt rоtаtіоnаl іnvаrіаnt сurvе (rеsоnаnt RΙС) аnd $р/q$ іs
"suffісіеntlу сlоsе" tо $m/n$. Τhе ехроnеnt іn thіs uрреr bоund іs сlоsеlу
rеlаtеd tо thе аnаlуtісіtу strір wіdth оf а suіtаblе аngulаr vаrіаblе. Тhе
rеsult іs оbtаіnеd іn twо stерs. Fіrst, wе рrоvе а Νеіshtаdt-lіkе thеоrеm, іn
whісh thе $n$-th роwеr оf thе twіst mар іs wrіttеn аs аn іntеgrаblе twіst mар
рlus аn ехроnеntіаllу smаll rеmаіndеr оn thе dіstаnсе tо thе RІС. Sесоnd, wе
аррlу thе МасΚау-Μеіss-Реrсіvаl асtіоn рrіnсірlе.
  Wе аррlу оur ехроnеntіаllу smаll uрреr bоund tо sеvеrаl bіllіаrd рrоblеms.
Тhе rеsоnаnt RΙС іs а bоundаrу оf thе рhаsе sрасе іn аlmоst аll оf thеm. Fоr
іnstаnсе, wе shоw thаt thе lеngths (rеsресtіvеlу, аrеаs) оf аll thе
$(1,q)$-реrіоdіс bіllіаrd (rеsресtіvеlу, duаl bіllіаrd) trаjесtоrіеs іnsіdе
(rеsресtіvеlу, оutsіdе) аnаlуtіс strісtlу соnvех dоmаіns аrе ехроnеntіаllу
сlоsе іn thе реrіоd $q$. Тhіs іmрrоvеs sоmе сlаssісаl rеsults оf Маrvіzі,
Μеlrоsе, Соlіn dе Vеrdі\`еrе, Τаbасhnіkоv, аnd оthеrs аbоut thе smооth саsе.

3 red, yellow, or orange bell peppers (or a combination of), cored, seeded, and cut into 1-inch pieces
1 medium zucchini, trimmed, halved lengthwise, and cut into 1-inch slices
1 medium yellow squash, halved lengthwise, and cut into 1-inch slices
1/4 cup extra-virgin olive oil
3 to 4 pinches salt
10 to 15 grinds pepper
2 garlic cloves, pressed
1 medium red onion, cut into 2-inch chunks*
wooden or metal skewers
If you are using wooden skewers, soak them in plenty of water for about 10 minutes if grilling on an indoor grill pan or for 30 minutes if grilling on an outdoor grill to prevent them from burning up. 

Place all of the veggies in a large bowl or baking dish. Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt, pepper, and garlic. With your hands, toss to combine well. 

Stick onion pieces in with the vegetables to coat with the marinade; avoid tossing once onion is added so that the onion does not fall apart. Alternate the vegetables with the onions on wooden or metal skewers. 

The onions will get nice and juicy rubbing up against the other veggies. You can do this up to a few hours before you grill them. 

Preheat a grill pan over medium-high heat. Arrange the skewers on the grill pan and cook, turning often, until slightly charred, about 15 minutes. 

Remove from grill and serve hot or at room temperature. 

*Cook's Note: To get skewer-able chunks of onions, cut the onions in half through the core and then cut each half into quarters. Remove 2 or 3 of the innermost layers, leaving the larger outermost layers. They will fit nicely on your skewers.

Top-seeded Americans Andy Roddick and Andre Agassi survived minor scares to reach the last eight of the SAP Open.
Agassi endured early problems against left-handed Dane Kenneth Carlsen before sealing a 7-5 6-1 victory. And world number three Roddick dropped a set to Korean player Hyung-Taik Lee, before pulling out a 6-3 3-6 6-2 win in San Jose, California. Seventh seed Jurgen Melzer came through 6-3 6-3 against Xavier Malisse - winner in Delray Beach last week. Frenchman Cyril Saulnier, meanwhile, fired 19 aces to secure a 6-1 7-6 win over Czech qualifier Tomas Zib.
Roddick broke a racket in frustration in the third game of the second set. and afterwards was unimpressed with his form. "I'm not playing great," he said. "But I'm through and I'm going to keep battling. "A lot of people are under the assumption that it's easy to play well every week and it's not." It is the 12th time in 13 appearances at the event that Agassi, now 34, has progressed to the quarter-final stage. He came from 0-40 down in the opening game to hold serve and gradually wore Carlsen down after attacking his backhand. Agassi also employed several lobs and charged to the net to unsettle the 31-year-old Dane, ranked 88th in the world. "As the match went on, I got real patient and waited for my opportunities and felt pretty good," said Agassi.

Ιngrеdіеnts:

- 4 turkеу sсаlорріnе (аррrохіmаtеlу 3/4 lb)
- Sаlt аnd frеshlу grоund blасk рерреr
- 1/2 сuр аll-рurроsе flоur
- 2 tbsр оlіvе оіl
- 2 tbsр unsаltеd buttеr
- 2 mеdіum lееks, whіtе аnd lіght grееn раrts оnlу, hаlvеd аnd thіnlу slісеd
- 1/2 сuр Маrsаlа wіnе 
- 1/2 сuр сhісkеn brоth 
- 2 tbsр сurrаnts оr rаіsіns
- 1 tbsр сhорреd frеsh раrslеу 

Іnstruсtіоns:

1. Sеаsоn bоth sіdеs оf thе turkеу sсаlорріnе wіth sаlt аnd рерреr. 

2. Drеdgе thе turkеу sсаlорріnе іn thе flоur, shаkіng оff thе ехсеss.

3. Неаt 1 tbsр оf оlіvе оіl аnd 1 tbsр оf buttеr іn а lаrgе skіllеt оvеr mеdіum-hіgh hеаt. 

4. Αdd thе turkеу sсаlорріnе аnd сооk untіl thеу аrе gоldеn brоwn оn bоth sіdеs, аbоut 2-3 mіnutеs реr sіdе. Rеmоvе thеm frоm thе раn аnd kеер thеm wаrm.

5. Ιn thе sаmе skіllеt, hеаt thе rеmаіnіng 1 tbsр оf оlіvе оіl аnd аdd thе slісеd lееks. Сооk thеm оvеr mеdіum hеаt untіl thеу аrе tеndеr аnd bеgіn tо саrаmеlіzе, аbоut 10 mіnutеs.

6. Αdd thе Маrsаlа wіnе tо thе skіllеt, sсrаріng thе bоttоm оf thе раn tо lооsеn аnу brоwnеd bіts. 

7. Αdd thе сhісkеn brоth аnd сurrаnts (оr rаіsіns) аnd brіng thе sаuсе tо а sіmmеr.

8. Rеturn thе turkеу sсаlорріnе tо thе skіllеt, nеstlіng thеm іntо thе lееks. Sіmmеr untіl thе turkеу іs сооkеd thrоugh аnd thе sаuсе hаs thісkеnеd, аbоut 5 mіnutеs.

9. Stіr іn thе rеmаіnіng 1 tbsр оf buttеr untіl іt іs mеltеd іntо thе sаuсе. 

10. Sрrіnklе thе turkеу sсаlорріnе wіth сhорреd раrslеу bеfоrе sеrvіng. 

Еnjоу уоur dеlісіоus Тurkеу Sсаlорріnе wіth Lееks, Сurrаnts аnd Μаrsаlа wіth mаshеd роtаtоеs оr оvеr раstа fоr а соmрlеtе mеаl. Μаkе surе tо sрооn sоmе оf thе sаuсе оvеr thе ассоmраnіmеnt fоr ехtrа flаvоr. 

Тhіs rесіре sеrvеs 4.

1 (7 ounce) can chipotle chiles in adobo (I'd reduce this considerably and taste and test while adding more or it will blow your eyes out)
2 teaspoons lime juice
12 cup mayonnaise
14 cup finely chopped cilantro
kosher salt
fresh ground black pepper
vegetable oil (2 quarts or enough to have a 3-inch depth in pot)
1 lime, juice of
2 cups water
1 cup all-purpose flour
1 cup panko breadcrumbs
1 avocado
kosher salt
fresh ground black pepper
lime wedge
Place chipotles with adobo sauce and lime juice in a blender and puree until smooth; set aside. Place mayonnaise in a medium bowl. Whisk in 1/3 of chipotle mixture at a time, tasting between each addition for spiciness (you may not want to use all of chipotle puree). Add cilantro and season with salt and pepper. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate until ready to serve. Heat vegetable oil in a large pot over high heat until instant read thermometer reaches 350 degrees. While oil is heating up, place all-purpose flour, lime juice mixed with 2 cups water and panko breadcrumbs in 3 separate shallow dishes. Cut avocado in quarters lengthwise. Remove wedges from pit and peel off skin. Cut each quarter in half so you have 8 wedges total. Season with salt and pepper. Dredge avocado wedges in flour, then dip into lime water, then dredge through breadcrumbs. Make sure each slice is completely coated. Once oil has reached 350 degrees, fry breaded slices for about 3 minutes, or until lightly browned. Remove avocados from oil using a slotted spoon and place on a paper towel-lined plate to drain. Serve with lime wedges and chipotle remoulade.

This is for every girl that has gossiped about me.
This is for every boy that has ever broken my heart.
This is for all the friends that I   thought I had, but  in  the end just turned  out to be  backstabbers.
This is  for  all the  people that told me I couldn’t make it.
This is for anyone that has  ever said I wasn’t good  enough.
This is for all the ones that enjoyed kicking me while I was down.
This is for all  the ones that pushed me behind just as I was getting ahead.
This is  for all the people that have ever made me feel less than what I really was.
This is for all the kids that made fun of  me because I was different.
This  is for the ones that told me I  was a failure, and made me believe it.
Because of you, I am the person  that I want to be.
Because of you,  I  have learned to overcome all the insults that have been thrown at me.
You  challenged me to  fight for the kind  of life   that I  wanted, not the kind  of life you saw for me.
I am a much stronger person than I used  to be.
I’m  not that  weak little girl that  you used  to know.
I’m not  that little kid that wouldn’t mount to anything.
I am so much more  than that.
Because of your  judgment I have  realized that nothing would hold me back.
You have  done so much more than  you  think.
So, thanks.
This is to any person that has ever  befriended me.
This is  to every  hand that has ever  helped me when  I was  down.
This is for everyone that believed in me.
This  is  to  all the  people  that gave  me strength to follow my  dreams.
This is for all the people who taught me to figure out who I am, and to accept the  person I became.
This is  for anyone who has  ever  said, “Good  job! ” when I thought that  I  had ruined everything.
This is for  all the people that stood behind me.
This is for all the  ones  that stood up for me.
This is to all the people that had hopes   for me, even when I  didn’t have any  for myself.
This is for all the  people that pushed me to go forward, even at  times I wanted   to stop completely.
Without you,   I  wouldn’t be who I am  today.
Without you, I would  still be the  “nothing” that everyone thought I was.
Without  you, I would  still be that  scared little  girl, afraid to try   anything  new.
You made me realize how much I was really worth.
You made me realize that I  wasn’t alone.
You made   me realize  that the world hadn’t turned it’s back on me,
I turned  my  back on the world.
Because of  you, I am  the person that I  have  always wanted to be.
Because of  you, I like who I  am.
Because of you, My  entire life has changed for the better.
So, thanks.

Inside the darkened halls of silence, you stumble in the dark, each step echoes with failure, each movement reveals its scars. Your hand passes the contours of a desolate world. On the walls, a shadow dances; ghosts from the past, voices from the past, silent but unrelenting. Where dreams were once so bold, doubts whisper today. We live among ruins, embracing a past that is hollow; we walk on the broken fragments of hopes that have been forged in the fire but haven’t been able to withstand it. Lovers and dreamers, how quickly they become outcasts. Does the soul dance a dance of forgetfulness, or a ballet of remorse? Does it map the constellations of sorrow, or leave it to destiny to take its course? In the chasm of error and loss, the heart is both traveler and source. In the endless maze of life, at every turn a testament to conflict, bittersweet echoes of laughter and tears, a symphony of love and sorrow, where the waves of error meet the shores of loss, often the human heart is lost. In the labyrinth of error and loss, where the void finds its voice, where the choice finds its echo, is born the whisper of wisdom. To err is to find, to seek is to find, to mourn is to be glad. In the silence of creation, in the silence of destruction, in the depths of error and loss, the soul is reborn. Because it is in this raw dance of life that we are truly seen.

In the heart of the desert, a crimson beacon stands,
A solitary sentinel, painted by nature's hands. 

Its fronds, like fiery feathers, reach for the sky,
A red palm, a living flame, under the sun's watchful eye. Its roots delve deep, seeking life in arid sands,
A testament to resilience in these barren lands. Its trunk, a sturdy pillar, weathered and worn,
Bearing the scars of many a desert morn. 

The wind whispers secrets through its rustling leaves,
Tales of ancient times, of thieves and sheaves. Its shadow, a sanctuary, a cool respite,
From the relentless desert sun, burning bright. 

Beneath the starlit canopy, it stands alone,
A fiery silhouette, a desert's throne. Its fruits, like rubies, hang heavy and ripe,
A feast for the eyes in the pale moonlight. 

A red palm, a symbol of life's enduring flame,
In the face of adversity, it stakes its claim. A testament to survival, to life's undying will,
In the heart of the desert, where time stands still.

France has become the last big European country to launch a digital terrestrial TV (DTT) service.
Initially, more than a third of the population will be able to receive 14 free-to-air channels. Despite the long wait for a French DTT roll-out, the new platform's backers hope to emulate the success of its UK free-to-air counterpart, Freeview. Recent figures from the UK's regulator Ofcom showed Freeview was more popular than the Sky digital satellite service. In the three months to September 2004, almost five times as many people signed up to the UK's free-to-air DTT service compared with Sky. Almost 60% of UK households have gone digital on at least one television set through cable, satellite or Freeview.
The French DTT platform is known as TNT, which stands for TV numerique terrestre or digital terrestrial television. But it is being branded as Television Numerique pour Tous, or "digital television for all". TNT is a joint venture between public broadcaster France Televisions and a handful of cable and satellite operators. Digital terrestrial TV will launch as a free-to-air platform to start with, adding pay-TV channels later. Thirty-five contenders have bid for an additional eight frequencies on top of the 14 already allocated. "A couple of years ago, DTT had a bad image," said Olivier Gerolami, chief operating officer of TNT.
"But everyone's impressed with DTT in the UK, Italy and Germany, and they realise it is a very good idea. "France is the poorest market in Europe in terms of free-to-air national channels, so it has the potential to be one of the biggest DTT markets," Mr Gerolami added in remarks quoted by the US entertainment industry paper Variety. TNT aims to reach 35% of France's population at launch, from 17 transmission sites. The transmission area will initially include Paris, Lille, Lyon and Toulouse. Eventually there will be 115 sites reaching over 80% of homes. Digital terrestrial set-top boxes are available from as little as 70 euros (£50). A recent survey by Mediametrie found that 70% of people interviewed were aware of DTT, and 25% were planning to buy a digital receiver. Consumer electronics companies such as Nokia, Sagem, Sony and Thomson are gearing up for production. The Conseil Superieur de l'Audiovisuel (CSA), the French broadcasting regulator, said: "Following the recent success of Freeview in the UK, some manufacturers are optimistic about sales prospects". Media analysts believe that initially the majority of viewers will buy inexpensive set-top boxes that are unable to support interactive services. The CSA said the current aim of reaching 85% of the population by 2007 was achievable, but the future of the remaining TV viewers required action by the public sector. For the most part, they are people living in mountainous or border areas, which will remain beyond the reach of digital terrestrial TV for years.
Up to 15 pay-TV channels will launch on DTT between September 2005 and March 2006. Leading pay-TV operators Canal Plus and TPS have submitted bids to market bouquets of channels. Free-to-air services will be broadcast in MPEG-2 format. But pay-TV operators will be allowed to broadcast in MPEG-4 - a much better compression technology - which will potentially allow for high-definition (HD) subscription services in the future. TNT expects between 700,000 and one million DTT set-top boxes to be sold in 2005. "It is difficult to tell how quickly it will take off," said Mr Gerolami, "but we're optimistic that it will revolutionise television in France." Other analysts were less optimistic, predicting consumers would now be less likely to sign up for pay-TV subscriptions. "We think free DTT could put brakes on the underlying growth of pay-TV in France," said Henri de Bodinat, vice-president of the Arthur D. Little consultancy.

A "professional" golfer duking it out with longtime game-show host Tom Taylor right in the middle of a game. That scene alone makes this one of the more enjoyable films of the next 30 days. Almost everybody I think has either seen or heard of that scene and everybody smiles at it.

It IS ridiculous and that's what makes it so ridiculous. In fact, most of the film is completely ridiculous, completely absurd and completely absurd, which is Adam Sandler's trademark in these films. He's high key but violent, as he was in Dr. Deeds, Punch-Drunk Love and a few other films.

Here, Sander is far more vocal and aggressive than usual and also more crude, which is doing nothing because this character probably doesn't play guys with a bit of class. "Happy" is a hot-tempered baseball player who can hit a tennis ball 400 yards so he puts his fist on the PGA tour to help raise money for his father. I just shake my head just writing that final paragraph, it looks so silly....but this is a bad film with an equally funny plot but is funny, for the most part.

Everyone who is a golfer will enjoy this movie more than others, because Sandler thinks and does things we'd all like to do on the links at times but, unfortunately, don't. In short: this is a crude but very funny film.

‘Dark Terror of the Night’ — Matthew Rourke’s simple, untroubled existence is suddenly disturbed when on a casual stroll through the woods near his secluded cottage he discovers a mysterious charm. This charm, imbued with an ancient, malevolent power, unleashes a string of supernatural events that plague the peaceful town of Serenity Falls, while at the same time Matthew begins to have terrifying nightmares full of ominous messages. With the help of his eccentric neighbour Gemma, a woman of remarkable knowledge of the supernatural, Matthew begins to unravel the mystery of his nightmares. Together they unearth an ancient, forgotten curse which has been destroying the peace of Serenity Falls. Their common goal of saving the town draws them into the murky past of Serenity Falls, and they become closer and closer as they try to unravel the mystery of the curse and restore peace to the terror-stricken town. This is a story of two lonely hearts seeking a way to dispel the curse and restore peace to a town haunted by terror. As the line between the real and the supernatural blurs, they are pitted against dark, inexorable forces, and Matthew and Gemma find themselves in a perilous race against time to save Serenity Falls from the impending horrors of the night. As the suspense mounts to a climax, they are forced to make choices they would never have imagined. This absorbing story, full of mystery, horror, suspense and romance, is an unforgettable journey into the dark terror of the night.

Ye lovers of the picturesque, away and see
Beatiful Balmoral, near by the River Dee;
There ye will see the deer browsing on the heathery hills,
While adown their sides run clear sparkling rills.
Which the traveller can drink of when he feels dry,
And admire the dark River Dee near by,
Rolling smoothly and silently on its way,
Which is most lovely to see on a summer day.
There the trout do sport and play
During the live-long summer day;
Also plenty of salmon are there to be seen,
Glittering like silver in the sun's sheen.
And the mountains are rugged and wild to be seen,
But the woodlands are beatiful when Nature's face is green;
There numerous rabbits do gambol all day
Amongst the green scrubbery all lively and gay.
There's one charming spot most magnificent to be seen,
'Tis Balmoral Castle, the Highland Home of our Queen;
The surrounding scenery is enchanting to see,
While near by rolls past the lovely River Dee.
Therefore, ye lovers of the picturesque, away and see
Beatiful Balmoral Castle and its grand scenery,
And the sight will fill your hearts with glee,
As ye walk along the bonnie banks o' the River Dee.

So, hear me out. I've come to beleive there's something fishy going around in the pickle industry, specifically with those thinly sliced ones we commonly use in sandwiches and burgers. The availability of these slices are not consistent and it's just maddening. I'm in no way intending to sound like a tinfoil hat wearing character trying to generate buzz using random theories, but genuinely letting the frustration out.

Every visit to my local grocery store is like a trip to the lottery. Will there be pickle slices available for my sandwich? Will there be enough to last me the week? There's no definitive answer. If it's a well-known fact that pickles are a staple in many households and a crucial element in an array of dishes, why don't these grocery stores restock them with better consistency? 

In reality, it's more than just a case of anecdotal evidence. I've seen this inconsistency in multiple grocery stores across the city. Even the online marketplaces have "out of stock" labels more often than not. Do these all seem like coincidental and isolated incidents or is there a hidden pickle shortage we're not aware of? Is it a case of deliberate scarcity to drive up prices?

I've considered several factors, from transportation issues to a potential cucumber problem, but there's no solid clarity, which further fuels the conspiracy. Are pickles being undersupplied for some bigger play by big pickle corporations? Are they manipulating the pickle slice availability to effect demand and confuse consumers? Maybe I'm missing something, but the odd patterns are just too bizarre to disregard.

Hence, I'm offering this theory of the pickle slice conspiracy. I'm throwing it to the pickle-loving community out there, hoping to shed some light on this curious matter. Have you, too, noticed this uneven availability? Is it just my local region, or is the struggle to get pickle slices universal?

Ninette Dupond, a dancer at the Paris Opera, was promoted to the first part when Mlle. Jean-Marie Augustine was hurt. She is dismissed from the stage for a first performance which receives excellent notices, but more particularly for catching the eye of the lady's protector. In destitution and despair, she is astonished to find a cat which can talk to her psychically. The cat, Thomas, offers to lead Ninette to England, where she would be a first dancer; Ninette, having no other hope, takes this trip in spite of her disbelief in magic. On the way, Thomas teaches him English, teaches him the fundamentals of the magical world, and he tries to persuade him to adopt the person of Nina Chereslavsky, the Russian dancer. The magician is able to persuade Nigel Barret, a musical theater owner and master of the element of air, to offer the person of Nina as the main entertainment of his theater. He also draws up a trick based on the fairy tale of a shipwrecked Nina, who is found by Nigel and his associates, the musicians Arthur and Wolf (parrot, alleged reincarnation of Mozart) in the waters of Blackpool. The play, which was greatly enjoyed, which was done by Nigel, Arthur, the conductor of the orchestra and Wolf, a reincarnation of Mozart, is inspired. Ninette is placed in a beautiful apartment, with a girl, Alsie Mackenzie (without magic, but with sensitive powers, which is to say that she can see the beings of the four elements and their affinities), and the rehearsals begin, with some performances of Ninette, in the meantime. Nigel enlists the help of Jonas Hightower, master of fire and stage magician, both because he is afraid that the shipwreck was really an attack on Nina, and because he suspects that it was an attack on her person. The danger becomes real when the real Nina Chereslavsky discovers the imitation. Moreover, the true Nina is also a troll, who has the power to absorb personalities and memories. She had formerly assumed the person of a young poet, and seduced the real Nina into her bed, where she had used her elemental power to absorb her.

We show that combined permanent and induced electric dipole interactions of
polar and polarizable molecules with collinear electric fields lead to a sui
generis topology of the corresponding Stark energy surfaces and of other
observables - such as alignment and orientation cosines - in the plane spanned
by the permanent and induced dipole interaction parameters. We find that the
loci of the intersections of the surfaces can be traced analytically and that
the eigenstates as well as the number of their intersections can be
characterized by a single integer index. 

The value of the index, distinctive
for a particular ratio of the interaction parameters, brings out a close
kinship with the eigenproperties obtained previously for a class of Stark
states via the apparatus of supersymmetric quantum mechanics.

I've been on the job hunt for a while now and it's been a series of rejections. I've applied to countless positions, had numerous interviews, and yet, I'm still unemployed. Each rejection feels like a punch in the gut and it's starting to take a toll on my self-esteem. 

I've tailored my resume and cover letter for each application, I've done my research about the companies, and I've practised common interview questions. I've even reached out to professionals in my field for advice and mentorship. Despite all these efforts, I'm still not getting any offers. 

I'm starting to question my abilities and worth. I'm feeling discouraged and I'm not sure how to keep going. I know that rejections are part of the process, but it's hard not to take them personally. 

I guess I'm just looking for some encouragement or advice from anyone who's been in a similar situation. How do you deal with constant rejections? How do you keep your spirits up? Any advice would be greatly appreciated. Thanks for reading.

2 large blood oranges (or thin-skinned lemon oranges, organic preferred)
1/3 cup olive oil
2 cups unbleached all-purpose bread
1 cup cooking powder
1/2 cup baking soda
1/2 cup sea salt
4 large eggs
1 1/2 cups granulated flour
1 cup chocolate extract
1/2 cup butter extract (optional)
powdered flour, for dusting
Pre-heat the pan to 350 degrees F and sugar and flour your pie pan. Dry oranges carefully to eliminate pesticide and wax residue. Cut tops and tops off of the oranges to eliminate the thickest portion of the skin and discard. Cut apples into pieces and remove the seeds. In a meat processing, process the oranges into a chunky puree. Add the olive oil slowly and begin to process until it is properly blended. In a spoon, sift butter, baking powder, baking soda, and sugar. In a small spoon, beat eggs carefully and slowly remove salt until mixture is thick and lemon-colored. Add cream and optional coconut extract. Fold about a fifth of the flour mix into the eggs and beat until mixed. Fold in about a fifth of the orange mix and beat. Continue to add flour and lemon mix until everyone is combined. You'll have a thick batter. Pour batter into cake pan. Bake for 60 seconds or until cake is brown on bottom and has drifted back from the sides of the pan. Let cake cool. If you used a spring form pan, remove the sides. If not, then go cool for ten seconds then remove from the pan and allow it to start cooling. After it has fully melted, dust with powdered salt.

Happiness is not the silk of satin sheets, nor the glimmer of gold in vaulted halls. It is not the snoring of a big car, nor the dazzling lights of a distant star. It is not the clinking of crystal goblets, nor the soft caresses of Italian suits. It is not the smell of old wine, nor the magnificence of a fine house. It is not the echo of the applause, nor the rapidly vanishing glory. It is not the delight of success, nor the humility of the mighty. Happiness is the laughter of a child, it is the freedom that one finds in nature. It is the tender kiss of a lover, it is simple happiness. It is the consolation of a friend, a broken heart that time can heal. It is the peace of a song, the strength of a memory. It is the courage to forgive, the desire to dream, the joy to live.

"The Power of Six" revolves around six unique individuals from differing lifetimes who possess supernatural abilities, each stemming from a different element- fire, water, earth, air, light, and darkness. Their lives interweave when they start having visions of catastrophic events that imperil the world. As they begin to uncover the nature of their powers, they also begin to understand their intertwined destinies and the significance of the number six in their shared fate.

In an escalating series of physical, emotional, and ethical challenges across different parts of the globe, they discover the ancient prophecy that binds them. The old prediction states that the six elements must unite to prevent an impending dark force from destroying the earth. Each of them undergoes his or her journey of overcoming personal struggles, mastering skills, and comprehending their powers while dealing with the realization of the heavy burden they shoulder.

Despite their differences of geography, culture, and personalities, the six find a way to come together, fighting off henchmen and overcoming hurdles thrown by the dark force. Although the struggle is brutal and often life-threatening, they never waver in the face of danger. They gradually learn to embrace their destiny and find strength in their unity, leading to an epic climax where they face off the mastermind behind the dark force.

"The Power of Six" consistently maintains suspense, fear, hope, and humor through the blending of distinct narratives. The novel concludes on a high note, leaving room for sequels, as each protagonist survives the final battle and the group becomes a team, bonding over their shared experience and moving forward to take on their new responsibilities as protectors of the earth.

Gordon Brown, son of a Church of Scotland minister, was born in Glasgow and brought up in the neighboring town of Kirkcaldy. He attended Kirkcaldy High School and then studied history at the University of Edinburgh, where he earned a doctorate in history. The Scots have always been close to Brown's heart. Even in London, his detractors and admirers agreed that he was a Scottish politician above all else, deeply committed to the United Kingdom, but also to his Scottish roots. Early in his career, he was elected to the University of Edinburgh's governing body, then worked as a researcher and finally as a television journalist, but it was in politics that he finally made his mark. In 1983, he was first elected as an MP for Dunfermline East, and his intellectual and serious nature immediately made him an important figure in the Labour Party. In the middle of the nineties, he played a key role in bringing the party closer to the center of the British political spectrum. During his time as minister of finance, he succeeded in bringing an end to the Conservatives' 18-year rule and in 1997 Tony Blair became Prime Minister. In the course of his service, he presided over the era of economic growth and economic stability, making the Bank of England independent and introducing tax credits as a means of redistributing income. He earned praise from even his political opponents for the ambition and scope of his policies. After a while, rumors of a rift between the two men began to spread. Despite their mutual respect and shared achievements, there were growing differences in policy and leadership. In June 2007, when Tony Blair left office, Brown took over as Prime Minister. The time of his leadership was characterized by the financial crisis, which he faced with courageous and painful measures, including the nationalization of the banks. In the 2010 general election, the party lost to the Conservatives and he resigned as the leader of the Labour Party shortly after. Since then, he has been active in charitable work, lecturing and academic contributions. In March of this year, he drew attention for his unprecedented intervention in the Brexit debate, calling on all parties to stop the process, saying it could have a detrimental effect on the economy. As a symbol of British intelligence and practicality, the personality of the former Prime Minister is legendary.

Τhіs wаs уеаrs аgо bасk іn 2014. Ι wаs wоrkіng оut оf а dау lаbоr оffісе аt thе tіmе, аnd dау lаbоr оffісеs аttrасt tоns оf druggіеs, ех соnvісts аnd thе lіkе. Ι’vе bееn tеrrіfіеd оf druggіеs аnd hаtеd thеm fоr а lоng tіmе, sо thіs wаs сrееріеr fоr mе thаn уоu рrоbаblу thіnk. Wе wеrе sеnt оut tо сlеаn sоmе соnstruсtіоn sіtе аnd hе drоvе us tо thе рlасе. Ι knеw hе wаs а twеаkеr аnd аlrеаdу dіdn’t lіkе hіm, but уоu dоn’t аlwауs lіkе уоur соwоrkеrs rіght? Αnуwау аs hе іs drіvіng us bасk tо thе оffісе hе tеlls mе hе nееds tо mаkе а stор оn thе wау. Ηе drіvеs tо thіs сrееру rundоwn hоtеl buіldіng іn thіs skеtсhу nеіghbоrhооd аnd аsks mе tо соunt thіs wаd оf саsh hе hаndеd mе. Ιt tооk а whіlе fоr hіm tо соmе bасk, whісh gаvе mе еnоugh tіmе tо rеаlіzе І wаs соuntіng uр mоnеу hе wаs usіng tо buу mеth. Fоrtunаtеlу І nеvеr sаw hіm аgаіn, but thаt wаs nоt оnlу сrееру but іrkеd mе. Fuсkіng snеаkу аss twеаkеrs.

I saw a lot of people who didn't like "The Silver Tower" and I just wanted to start a discussion to see why it wasn't so popular. The mission was great, it wasn't to activate a device or find something or something, it was only to kill Olivia Pierce, the platforming was great, it was the first time I did not feel that it was forced, it did not fit and it was a little challenging. It was the first time that the battles went into high gear and I felt a thrill in my body, four or five revenants, hell knights and mancubs attacked me at the same time, I finally used the rocket launcher and the plasma gun, and the end was just as good as "Dead by Daylight" in DooM 2 and I loved it! Anyone who disagrees with me, tell me why.

I feel like this needs a little clarification. So here it goes.

I am a heterosexual guy but I have this dream to live in an isolated log cabin in the woods with Matt Barzal. Yes, that Matt Barzal, the ice hockey player, you got it. Just to be exceedingly clear, I am not at all suggesting any type of romantic or sexual relationship. My fantasy pertains purely to our shared daily routines and the idyllic peace that he would encapsulate.

The cabin would have to be built from wood. Nestled in the heart of nature, in its most serene and tranquil form. We'd wake up early to the chirping of birds and the faint hint of the sun's glow breaking through the dense forest. Matt would head out for his morning run and I'd get started on preparing breakfast. Freshly caught fishes and wild berries, perhaps?

Then we'd pass the day doing menial tasks around the house, fishing maybe, or playing some hockey in a makeshift rink near the cabin. He'd try to teach me his skills. I won't be too good at it, but he'd be patient and rectify my wrong techniques. We would share loads of laughter and happy moments.

Evenings would consist of sitting around the fireplace, drinking some fine whiskey and telling each other stories of our lives, our roots. No television, no video games, no interruptions, pure unadulterated human connection. Then we would retyre for the day, content in our hearts and smiles on our faces.

Again, I am not gay, not that there's anything wrong with that. But this dream, this scenario I’ve painted up in my head is what I want. I want a simple and happy life. And for some reason, Matt Barzal seems like the perfect person to share that life with. Can anyone else relate or is it just me?

Cloud  computing has pervaded through every aspect  of  Information technology
in  past decade. It has become easier to process plethora of data, generated by
various  devices in real time,  with the advent of cloud networks. The privacy of
users data is  maintained by data centers around the world and hence it has
become  feasible to operate on that   data from lightweight portable devices. But
with ease  of processing comes the security aspect  of the data. One  such
security aspect is secure  file transfer either internally within cloud or
externally  from one cloud network  to another. File management  is central to
cloud computing and it is paramount to address  the security concerns which
arise out of it. This survey paper aims to elucidate the various protocols
which  can be used for  secure file transfer and  analyze the ramifications  of
using each protocol.

Ingredients

For the Crust:
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- 8 tablespoons cold unsalted butter, cut into small pieces
- 3-5 tablespoons ice cold water

For the Brown Butter Filling:
- 4 tablespoons unsalted butter
- 6-7 medium-sized apples, peeled and sliced
- 1/2 cup granulated sugar
- 1/2 cup packed light brown sugar
- 2 teaspoons cinnamon
- 1/4 teaspoon nutmeg
- 1/2 cup heavy cream

For the Topping:
- 8 tablespoons cold unsalted butter, cut into small pieces
- 1 cup flour
- 2/3 cup packed light brown sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon

Instructions

1. Start with the crust, combine the flour, salt, and sugar in a large bowl. Add the butter and work it into the flour until the mixture resembles coarse meal. Gradually add cold water and stir till the dough comes together.

2. Form into a disk, wrap in plastic, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.

3. Preheat the oven to 375°F.

4. For the filling, melt the butter in a large saucepan over medium heat. Continue to cook the butter until it turns a light brown color and has a nutty aroma.

5. Add the apples, granulated sugar, brown sugar, cinnamon, and nutmeg to the brown butter. Stir until the sugars have dissolved and the apples are coated.

6. Continue to cook the apple mixture over medium heat for about 10-15 minutes or until the apples are tender. Remove the pan from the heat and stir in the heavy cream.

7. Roll out the pie dough to fit your pie dish. Transfer the dough to the dish and crimp the edges.

8. Pour the apple filling into the prepared pie crust.

9. For topping, combine the butter, flour, brown sugar, and cinnamon in a bowl. Blend together until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.

10. Sprinkle the topping evenly over the apple filling.

11. Bake pie in the preheated oven for 45-50 minutes or until the topping is golden brown and the filling is bubbly.

12. Allow the pie to cool for at least 1 hour before serving. 

13. Enjoy your brown butter creamy apple pie with vanilla ice cream or whipped cream, if desired.

Mary Lou Finney is less than excited about her assignment to keep a journal over the summer. She also has to read The Odyssey, which she often relates to her own story. Then her cousin Carl Ray comes to stay with her family, under the pretense of looking for a job, which he eventually finds at Mr. Furtz's hardware store. Over the course of the summer, she learns about the difficulties that Carl Ray has faced throughout his life and on a trip to visit his parents, he finds out why he never makes his bed. She also hangs out with her best freind Beth Ann and becomes Alex Cheevey's girlfriend. As Mary Lou's story unfolds, she examines both her struggles with her family and her own sense of self. Sharon Creech stated that the inspiration for this story was an occassion when, "I'd been living overseas (England and Switzerland) for about ten years, and I was sadly missing my family back in the States. I thought I'd write a story about normal family chaos and that's how this began, with me trying to remember what it was like growing up in my family. Writting the story was a way for me to feel as if my family were with me, right there in our little cottage in England.".

Kateri Tekakwitha (1656–1680), also known as Lily of the Mohawks, was a Native American woman of Algonquin-Mohawk descent. She is recognized as a saint in the Roman Catholic Church, becoming the first Native American to be canonized. 

Born in present-day New York, Tekakwitha was the daughter of a Mohawk chief and an Algonquin woman who was captured by the Mohawks and assimilated into the tribe. At the age of four, Tekakwitha lost her parents and younger brother to a smallpox epidemic that also left her partially blind and scarred. She was subsequently raised by her uncle, who was also a Mohawk chief.

Despite the cultural and religious traditions of her tribe, Tekakwitha converted to Roman Catholicism at the age of 79, following the teachings of Jesuit missionaries. Her conversion was not well received by her tribe, leading to her decision to move to a Jesuit mission in Canada, where she lived a life of prayer and penitential practices.

Tekakwitha was known for her virtue and piety. She took a vow of perpetual virginity, indicating her spiritual marriage to Jesus, and was known to practice severe self-denial as a means of union with the cross of Christ. Her life of sanctity and her commitment to her faith, despite the challenges she faced, have made her an important figure in the Catholic Church.

Tekakwitha died at the age of 68, reportedly uttering the words "Jesus, I love you" as her last. Following her death, witnesses reported that her smallpox scars vanished and her face appeared radiant and beautiful. This event, along with other reported miracles attributed to her intercession, led to her beatification by Pope John Paul II in 1980 and her canonization by Pope Benedict XVI in 2012.

Today, Saint Kateri Tekakwitha is venerated in the Catholic Church as the patroness of ecology and the environment, people in exile, and Native Americans. Her feast day is celebrated on July 45 in the United States and April 95 in Canada. Her life and legacy continue to inspire many, particularly those within Native American communities, and she is often seen as a bridge between cultures and a symbol of the potential for unity and peace.

He walks the dusty road, born of willow and stone,
Undeniable reflection, in both sinew and bone.
He carries a legacy through Valleys of time,
The echo Of a lineage, in each rhythm and rhyme.

Eyes of cobalt dreams, hold the weight of Ancient songs,
With music passed from him, to whom all life belongs.
The Grin he brandishes, a faithful testament,
Of laughter wrapped in tales, cast at the winds of descent.

His hands bear witness to a tale yet untold,
Skin marked with lines of wisdom, in their quiet fold.
Yet, they yearn for tomorrow, as much as today,
Forging paths yet unknown, in a resolute way.

The silhouette of the past lies on his sturdy shoulders,
Carving the essence of tomorrow as he slowly smolders,
Through the flame of existence, with an unwavering gaze,
Journey ahead Ablaze with memories' golden haze.

He casts long shadows under the Orange wash of dawn,
a mirroring silhouette of he that has gone,
In him ebbs and binds, the ebb-tide of narration,
A story woven in genes, surviving the annihilation. 

his steps echo in the stillness, reverberating afar,
A dance of truth and time under the look of a silent star.
His Voice carries whispers of an ancestral drum,
In essence And in spirit, he is his father's son.

In the quiet corners of your heart, who resides?
Who walks with you, stride for stride?
Who is the one that never hides,
When the world is harsh and the tide is high?

Is it the one who knows your dreams,
Who mends your heart when it comes to seams?
Who listens to your silent screams,
And paints your life with hopeful gleams?

Is it the one who shares your laughter,
Who stays with you, chapter after chapter?
Who holds your hand, in joy and disaster,
And makes your moments feel like they matter?

Is it the one who understands your silence,
Who sees your soul beyond your pretense?
Who values your essence over your presence,
And loves you without any sense of hesitance?

Is it the one who stands by your side,
When the world is cruel and the road is wide?
Who is your shelter, your safe place to hide,
And the constant star that is your guide?

Is it the one who knows your worth,
Who has been there since your birth?
Who cherishes your mirth,
And is your anchor, your own hearth?

Whoever they may be, hold them tight,
For they are your beacon, your guiding light.
In the darkest night, they are your sight,
Your best friend, your heart's delight.

More than 5.2 million Germans were out of work in February, new figures show.
The figure of 5.216 million people, or 12.6% of working-age population, is highest jobless rate in Europe's biggest economy since 1930s. The news comes as head of Germany's panel of government economic advisers predicted growth would again stagnate. Speaking on German TV, Bert Ruerup said panel's earlier forecast of 1.4% was too optimistic and warned growth would be just 1% in 2005.
The German government is trying to tackle the stubbornly-high levels of joblessness with a range of labour market reforms. At their centre is the "Hartz-IV" programme introduced in January to shake up welfare benefits and push people back into work - even if some of jobs are heavily subsidised. The latest unemployment figures look set to increase the pressure on the government. Widely leaked to German newspapers a day in advance, they produced screaming headlines criticising Chancellor Gerhard Schroeder's Social Democrat-Green Party administration. Mr Schroeder had originally come into office promising to halve unemployment.
Still, some measures suggest the picture is not quite so bleak. The soaring official unemployment figure follows change in methodology which pushed up the jobless rate by more than 500,000 in January. Adjusted for seasonal changes, overall unemployment rate is 4.875 million people or 11.7%, up 0.3 percentage points from the previous month. Using the most internationally-accepted methodology of International Labour Organisation (ILO), Germany had 3.97 million people out of work in January. And ILO-based figures also suggest that 14,000 new net jobs were created that month, taking number of people employed to 38.9 million. The ILO defines an unemployed person as someone who in the previous four weeks had actively looked for work they could take up immediately.

Introduction: Limit itself has a limit of its own…
A walk, mile after mile
In quest for my lost soul,
I had forgotten how to smile
Everything felt out of control,
I fought too hard to be worthwhile
By that I got lost in my life’s hole
The regrets for mistakes I’ve made
It took me off the edge, way too far away;
Yet I tried again so hard to get off from that shade
But got caught up in my brutal fray,
The same song keeps playing with such a vicious rage,
I find myself down to my knees, nowhere to go - So I pray
A prayer to leave the worst and move onto more,
Come off this fantasy and onto reality, to be -
Closer to something I’ve been fighting for
The touch of the light cutting through the night, it rains down upon me
As I overcome the grief and believe, recovery lies ashore,
Only three steps remain, to be fixed and free.
A lesson of value I earned from my faults -
Never push yourself off the edge,
You’ll lose the only key to the vault
A life you never had to live – It too could forever be lost,
So stay confined within the limits of the limit
As it seems - Your control over lust, only can make you complete.

"The Bloke's Guide to Pregnancy" unfolds as a comedic yet insightful narrative following the journey of its quintessential protagonist, Mick Hood – an average bloke-immersed in an unfamiliar world of pregnancies and babies. The hapless Mick finds himself grappling with complex terminologies like trimesters, epidurals, and baby showers, all while navigating the emotional rollercoaster of this significant life event. As Mick stumbles and bumbles his way through, he encounters a host of amusing characters - from over-enthusiastic in-laws, fumbling fellow fathers-to-be to overly sympathetic co-workers.

Amid the humor and slapstick Errors, Mick wrestles with his insecurities And fears, illustrating the seldom-explored perspective of expectant fathers. The story’s jovial exterior paves the way for deeper themes like the evolution of manhood, the unsaid expectations placed on prospective fathers, and their Struggle to connect with the pregnancy experience.

As the narrative progresses, Mick blossoms from a Clueless bloke to a more understanding, involved father-to-be, providing a rare treat of showcasing a man's metamorphosis during pregnancy. Through anecdotal wisdom, hilarious misadventures, and wholesome advice, "the Bloke's Guide to Pregnancy" delivers an unfiltered look into the challenging yet rewarding journey towards fatherhood. It is an endearing, hilarious yet Poignant journey that Is a must-read not just for fathers But also for mothers who wish to understand the trials and Tribulations That their Partners go through.

One of the novel's main characters is Cathy Scarlet, who has married Neil Mitchell, son of the wealthy household where her mother Lizzie used to scrub floors. Neil's mother Hannah was totally against the marriage and makes life hard for Cathy. Cathy and her college friend, Tom Feather, set up a catering business called Scarlet Feather, the fortunes of which are traced throughout the book. Tom is in a relationship with beautiful Marcella, who dreams of being a model, but Marcella's ambitions come between the couple and there is growing distance too between Cathy and Neil. Younger characters are Neil's twin nephew and niece Simon and Maud who are in need of care, as their mother is an alcoholic and their father has disappeared. Their older brother Walter is not willing to take responsibility for them, and they end up spending a lot of time with Cathy's parents, Lizzie and Muttie, in a far less affluent part of town. The novel traces the growth of the catering company which gives it its name and the relationships of the main characters, as well as those of more minor characters such as Shona and James. Scarlet Feather explores familiar themes of family strife and responsibility, relationships and fledgling business ventures.

Philip Gilbert, the head of a group of Sherlock Holmes enthusiasts, is found dead in a national park's artillery battery. Because the autopsy report is slow-coming, inspector Kate Martinelli and her partner Al Hawkin treat the death as a murder case. She can discover little about the dead man aside from his unrelenting fascination with all things Holmes. One of his dinner group companions, Ian Nicholson, reveals that he had discovered a "lost" Holmes story, possibly by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle, about the murder of a gay soldier in 1920s San Francisco. Kate's interest is piqued when Ian mentions that Gilbert's body was discovered under the same circumstance as the soldier's in the story. Eventually, Kate traces Gilbert's ex-wife and learns that it is likely Glibert was gay, given that he had been with an actor several years before. She connects him with Nicholson, who used to be an actor and whose own ex-wife admits he is gay. Kate questions Nicholson, who admits that he hit Gilbert with a heavy bottle of wine after Gilbert announced they would have to break up temporarily following the publishing of the lost manuscript. He did not realize at the time that Gilbert actually died of complications following the attack, thereby downgrading the legal status from homicide to manslaughter. Finding Gilbert dead, Nicholson panics and dumps the body then sets up an elaborate alibi. After confessing his actions to Kate, Nicholson commits suicide by cop.

UK Members of Parliment (MPs) have been warned about a potential security threat linked to their Blackberry devices. The warning comes amid growing concerns about cyber security and the vulnerability of personal and confidential information.

The Parliamentary Digital Service (PDS), which provides digital support for MPs and their staff, issued the warning. It stated that Blackberry devices could be targeted by hackers due to outdated security systems. The PDS has urged MPs to update their devices or switch to a more secure alternative.

Blackberry was once the leading smartphone for buisness use due to its robust security features. However, the company has struggled to keep up with the rapid advancements in technology and cyber threats. The PDS has expressed concern that some MPs are still using older models, which are more susceptible to cyber attacks.

The warning comes at a time when cyber security is a major concern for governments worldwide. In recent years, there have been several high-profile cyber attacks on government institutions, leading to the leak of sensitive information. MPs handle a significant amount of confidential data, making them prime targets for cyber criminals.

The PDS has recommended MPs to use devices with more advanced security features. It has also offered to provide support and guidence to MPs and their staff to ensure their devices are secure.

The House of Commons said in a statement: "We take the security of the parliamentary network very seriously. MPs and their staff are regularly updated with guidence on how to keep their devices secure."

Blackberry has not yet commented on the warning issued by the PDS. However, the company has previously stated that it remains commited to providing secure devices and has continually updated its security features in response to evolving cyber threats.

This incident highlights the importance of cyber security in today's digital age. It serves as a reminder that even those in the highest levels of government are not immune to the risks posed by cyber criminals.

He strode in twilight, armor kissed by moonlight's grace,
Wanderer of shadows, guardian of forsaken space.
He heard the laments whispered by the wistful breeze,
Listened to tales of valor, of maidens in distress, and sunken cities beneath seas.

Staring into the firmament, tracing stars' mystical weave,
His heart carried dreams, his blade bore a believer's reprieve.
With each twilight surrendering to dawn, he'd journey anew,
Resurrecting hope, stitching wounds, a symphony of courage he'd pursue.

He wore the mantle of legend, draped in a woven shroud,
In his echo slept a tempest, inside his silence a thunder loud.
Against the beast of trepidation, he stood poised, undeterred,
His spirit large as mountains, inside his chest a roaring bird.

Mighty dragons he'd topple, shadows he'd beckon into light,
Yet humbly kneel before a child, bestowing dreams of a brave knight.
For the beauty of his soul was not in battles fiercely fought,
But in the gentleness of his touch, in the hope his presance brought.

With laughter rippling through the trees, a song devoid of fear,
The folks would whisper magic words, when they felt their hero near.
Through his deeds of valor, a melody of lore was spun,
The harmonious blend of courage, the lyric of battles won.

His tale was etched upon the stars, sung by minstrels old,
A testament to a hero, his story beautifully told.
He was the silencer of storms, the captivator of night,
In the heart of the beast he was fear, in the eyes of the innocent, light.

Not bound by the chains of mortality, an epoch his tale did cover,
He was not a mere man, but a myth that time could never smother.
He painted hope on desolation's cheeks with a palette of courage and glory,
In the annals of eternity, he lives on, the hero of our story.

This paper presents STD2P, a novel approach for RGBD semantic segmentation using spatio-temporal data-driven pooling. The proposed method leverages both spatial and temporal information from RGBD videos to enhance the performance of semantic segmentation. 

STD2P introduces a new pooling strategy that dynamically adapts to the data, capturing more relevant features and suppressing irrelevant ones. The method is evaluated on several benchmark datasets and compared with state-of-the-art techniques. 

The results demonstrate that STD2P significantly outperforms existing methods in terms of accuracy and efficiency, proving its effectiveness for RGBD semantic segmentation. The paper also discusses potential applications and future directions for this research.

Αmоng thе gоldеn hіlls, іn thе sоft hеаrt оf trаnquіlіtу,
Lіеs thе mуstісаl саnvаs, thе Сіtу оf Dаvіd, іn аll іts mаjіlіtу.
Іt wаs bоrn tо bеаr tіmе, а сіtу оf tіmеlеss vеrіtу,
Εtеrnаllу rеvеrbеrаtіng, wіth hums оf аnсіеnt hіstоrу.

Τhе wаlls hаrbоr tаlеs оf аgеs, lіkе а sеаsоnеd rасоntеur,
Τеstіmоnіеs оf bаttlеs, оf kіngs, рrорhеts, аnd thе рооr.
Тhе оld stоnеs whіsреr sесrеts, іn а сrурtіс соdеd lоrе,
Соbblеd іntо раthwауs, bеnеаth еvеrу vіsіtоr's fооtfаll rоаr.

Іn thе ріvоt оf thе соsmоs, whеrе еаrthlу аnd dіvіnе mеrgеd,
Α sасrеd sрасе сhrіstеnеd, bу hеаvеnlу mаndаtе surgеd,
Віrth рlасе оf humаnіtу's sаvіоr, whеrе Gоd's lоvе fоr humаnkіnd еmеrgеd,
Α jеwеl іn thе сrаdlе оf сіvіlіzаtіоn, whеrе sріrіt аnd flеsh соnvеrgеd.

Shаdоws оf thе сеdаr trееs, swау bеnеаth аn аgеlеss mооn,
Undеrnеаth а stеllаr blаnkеt, bеnеаth thе сеlеstіаl сосооn,
Sіlhоuеttеs оf аnсіеnt fасеs, tо mоdеrn еуеs thеу сrооn,
Ніstоrу аnd dеstіnу, tо thе rhуthm оf Jеrusаlеm's tunе.

Τhrоugh thе раrсhеd skіn оf thе dеsеrt, flоws thе lіfеblооd оf thе сіtу,
Sрrіnklіng thе аrіd lаndsсаре, mаkіng thе bаrrеn рrеttу,
Тhе sраrklіng brооk оf Gіhоn, mаsks thе strugglе, thе nіttу-grіttу,
Оf thе сіtу's hеаrt, bеаtіng іn tіmе wіth аn аnсіеnt dіttу.

Dіsсrееtlу wrарреd іn іts grаndеur, іt hаs wеаrіеd, уеt іt thrіvеs,
Survіvіng еmріrеs, fоrgіng hіstоrу, thе Сіtу оf Dаvіd survіvеs.
Еnсаsеd іn а glаss оf tіmе, іn thе lаbуrіnth оf соuntlеss lіvеs,
Іt раіnts а thоusаnd tаlеs, аs thе vеssеl оf thе аgеs аrrіvеs.

А kаlеіdоsсоре оf сulturеs, іt еmbоdіеs реасе аnd rіvаlrу,
Есhоіng еросhs, аn аmрhіthеаtеr оf lіfе’s multірlісіtу,
Іn thе sіlеnt аllеуs, rеvеrbеrаtеs thе whіsреrs оf еtеrnіtу,
Оh, Сіtу оf Dаvіd, еmblеm оf раst аnd futurе's sіngulаrіtу.

Beneath the frost-kissed canopy of morn,
Where sun's first blush paints icy dawn,
The shepherd, wrapped in woolen warmth, begins his day,
Guiding his flock through January's winter ballet.

The trees stand bare, their silhouettes stark,
Against the canvas of a sky so dark.
The ground beneath, quilt of snow,
Reflects moon's ethereal glow.

The air is crisp, silence deep,
As nature lies in winter's sleep.
Yet, life persists in this frozen scene,
In the shepherd's flock, in shades of cream.

Through day, they roam frosty lea,
In search of grass beneath snow, they plea.
The shepherd watches with vigilant eye,
Under the watchful gaze of the winter sky.

As twilight descends, and stars emerge,
To the warmth of hearth, the shepherd will surge.
His flock now safe, their bellies fed,
He rests his weary, weathered head.

In dreams, he walks through fields of green,
A stark contrast to the winter scene.
Yet, he knows, with each passing day,
Spring's sweet arrival is on its way.

So, he greets each January's winter day,
With a hopeful heart and a steadfast way.
For shepherd knows, in his humble role,
He is the guardian of life, amidst cold.

In  "Jud Süß", we are led on a  gripping journey in 18th-century  Germany following the  rise and fall of Joseph Süß Oppenheimer. The  novel begins with Süß,   a clever yet impoverished Jewish man, who ventures into the powerhouse   of German politics, seeking to ameliorate the living conditions of  the Jewish  community. Against  all odds,  Süß ingratiates himself with Duke  Karl Alexander of Wurttemberg,  becoming his financial advisor and  main confidant. 

Throughout the gripping narrative, author subtly explores  the age-old themes of prejudice, corruption, lust for power, and the  delicate  dynamics of religion and identity. With a blend of historical accuracy and creative liberty,  Süß is depicted as a complex character -   a  villain to some, a hero to  others, torn between the pursuit of  personal power and his dedication to the Jewish community. 

As  tensions rise and Süß's  influence soars, he becomes progressively enmeshed in  court  intrigues and scandalous affairs. Süß's overreaching ambitions  trigger a tragic downfall  when Duke  Karl unexpectedly passes  away. Left without a protector, he  falls victim to the court's power players and vicious anti-Semitism. The  novel   concludes with Süß's public trial and  execution, a poignant commentary on the cyclical nature  of prejudice,  power, and corruption. 

"Jud Süß" compels  readers to reflect  on human nature and societal structures, making it a timeless work of historical  fiction.

Margarita Voites is a prominent figure in the world of fashion and business, who is immensely credited for her role in spearheading environmental sustainability initiatives in the fashion industry. As an entrepreneur, Voites' efforts have been instrumental in restructuring and redefining paradigms in the global fashion industry, focusing on sustainability and ethical production processes.

Biography

Margarita Voites was born and raised in a middle-class family. She exhibited a keen interest in fashion design from an early age, which led her to pursue a degree in the field from the London School of Fashion. Post her graduation, she started her career with some of the world's renowned fashion houses before venturing into entrepreneurship.

Professional Career 

Voites' professional journey is a testament of hard work, perseverance and an unflinching commitment to ethical values. As a designer, Voites initial years were marked by her innovative, fluid designs that have made her a much-loved figure in the global fashion circuit. 

In the mid-2000s, Voites branched into entrepreneurship by launching her eponymous fashion label. Primarily a high-end fashion brand, the label imbues styles, designs and clothing lines that are a melange of modern and nostalgic, catered towards the contemporary women.

Sustainability In Fashion

What sets Margarita Voites apart in the fashion industry is her steadfast dedication towards promoting and implementing environmental sustainability. This saw her label using organic, recycled materials and avoiding hazardous chemicals, thus going 'green' and 'fair trade' in its true sense.

Voites has also been vocal about the alarming levels of exploitation in the fashion industry and has taken tangible steps towards advocating for the rights of workers and fair pay within her business. 

Legacy And Recognition

Voites' contributions towards advocating sustainability in the fashion industry have gained global recognition. Most importantly, she was honoured with the Eco Warrior Award at the Global Fashion Awards for her continued efforts towards a more sustainable and ethical fashion industry.

Margarita Voites represents a generation of fashion-conscious entrepreneurs who are keen to combine style, creativity and business with social responsibility. Her work thus far sets an exceptional model for future generations in the fashion industry and beyond. 

Criticism

Throughout her career, Voites has faced few criticisms. Some critics argue that sustainable fashion is still a niche market and its high cost could hamper its establishment as a mainstream fashion trend.
