Ingredients: 

- 6 to 8 pound Boston butt/ pork shoulder, boneless
- Salt and ground black pepper 
- ¼ cup of brown sugar
- ¼ cup of paprika
- 1 tablespoon of black pepper 
- 1 tablespoon of salt
- 1 tablespoon of chili powder 
- 1 tablespoon of garlic powder 
- 1 tablespoon of onion powder 
- 1 teaspoon of cayenne pepper 
- BBQ sauce of choice, for serving 
- Hamburger buns, for serving 

Steps:

1. Start by using knife to score the pork shoulder in a crosshatch pattern, spacing each cut about inch apart. The cuts should be deep enough to penetrate fat, but not deep enough to touch meat.

2. Rub the pork shoulder generously with salt and pepper.

3. Preheat water bath to 165°F (74°C) using a sous vide precision cooker. 

4. In a mixing bowl, combine the brown sugar, paprika, black pepper, salt, chili powder, garlic powder, onion powder, and cayenne pepper to make a dry rub.

5. Submerge the pork in dry rub, massaging it into the meat until it's fully coated.

6. Transfer seasoned pork to vacuum seal bag, making sure to fold the top of the bag back over itself to form a cuff to prevent any seasoning from getting on edges of bag that would interfere with seal.

7. Using a vacuum sealer, seal up bag.

8. Submerge bag in your preheated sous vide water bath, setting timer for 24 hours.

9. Once 24 hours is up, remove meat from the sous vide bath. You'll notice lot of juice in bag; feel free to reserve this for making gravy or discard it.

10. Shred the tender pork, using a pair of forks.

11. Serve your pulled pork on hamburger buns with your favorite barbecue sauce.

12. Enjoy the tender and juicy meat with perfect balance of flavors from the various spices, truly a delectable delight!

Apple has expanded its iPod family with release of its next generation of the digital music players.
Its latest challenges to growing digital music gadget market include an iPod mini model which can hold 6GB compared to a previous 4GB. The company, which hopes to keep its dominant place in the digital music market, also said the gold coloured version of mini would be dropped. A 30GB version has also been added to the iPod Photo family. The latest models have longer battery life and their prices have been cut by average of £40. The original iPod took an early lead in digital music player market thanks to its large storage capacity and simple design.
During 2004 about 25 million portable players were sold, 10 million of which were Apple iPods. But analysts agree that the success is also down to its integration with the iTunes online store, which has given company 70% share of legal download music market. Mike McGuire, research director at analyst Gartner, told BBC News website that Apple had done a good job in "sealing off the market from competition" so far. "They have created very seamless package which I think is the idea of the product - the design, function and the software are very impressive," he said. He added that threat from others was always present, however. "Creative, other Microsoft-partnered devices, Real, Sony and so on, are ratcheting up the marketing message and advertising," he said. Creative was very upbeat about how many of its Creative Zen players it had shipped by the end of last year, he said. Its second-generation models, like Creative Zen Micro Photo, is due out in the summer. It will have 5GB of memory on board.
Digital music players are now the gadget of choice among young Americans, according to recent research by the Pew Internet and American Life Project. One in 10 US adults - 22 million people - now owns a digital music player of some sort. Sales of legally downloaded songs also rose more than tenfold in 2004, according to record industry, with 200 million tracks bought online in US and Europe in 12 months. The IFPI industry body said that the popularity of portable music players was behind the growth. Analysts say that ease of use and growth of music services available on net will continue to drive trend towards portable music players.
People are also starting to use them in novel ways. Some are combining automatic syncing functions many of them have with other net functions to automatically distribute DIY radio shows, called podcasts. But 2005 will also see more competition from mobile phone operators who are keen to offer streaming services on much more powerful and sophisticated handsets. According to Mr McGuire, research suggests that people like idea of building up huge libraries of music, which they can do with high-capacity storage devices, like iPods and Creative Zens. Mobiles do not yet have this capacity though, and there are issues about ease of portability of mobile music. Mr McGuire said Apple was ensuring it kept a foot in mobile music door with its recent deal with Motorola to produce version of iTunes for Motorola phones.

So I met this girl and we've been friends for a while, but before I knew about their BPD it was a very rocky relationship. I felt as though at times they hated me and would get upset for no reason. Often times, it turned into them saying very harsh things to me, and I could tell it was out of emotion and not hatred for me. 

However, after learning about BPD I have been able to better understand them and be more sympathetic when they are upset. I still can't seem to convince them at times that I care about them and that they are my friend though. 

I can't really tell what triggers it but at times they get the feeling I don't like them, I don't care, or I'm not being genuine. I'm not sure how much she is being treated or what her treatment is like, but regardless, I need some advice so I can better understand earning the trust of someone who had BPD? 

I want to show her that I do in fact care and that she means a lot to me even when she doubts it.

Hello everyone,

I'm in a bit of a pickle and I'm hoping someone here might  be able to help  me out. I've  been using a task management  tool for my business  for  a while now and recently   decided to  switch to a  new one. I  exported all  my tasks from the old tool and imported them into the new one. Everything seemed  to be going  smoothly until I received a notification that my account  has been suspended.

I'm not sure why this happened. The only  thing I did was import my tasks, which I assumed was a standard feature. I didn't receive any warnings or indications  that what I was doing could   lead to a suspension. I've tried reaching out  to the customer support of  the new tool, but I  haven't received a response yet.

Has anyone else experienced something similar? Is there something I    might have overlooked that could have caused this? I'm    really at a  loss here and any advice or insight  would be  greatly  appreciated.  

Thanks in advance!

He took a frayed hat from his head,
And “Peace on Earth” was what he said.
“A morsel out of what you’re worth,
And there we have it: Peace on Earth.
Not much, although a little more
Than what there was on earth before
I’m as you see, I’m Ichabod,—
But never mind the ways I’ve trod;
I’m sober now, so help me God.”
I could not pass the fellow by.
“Do you believe in God?” said I;
“And is there to be Peace on Earth?”
“Tonight we celebrate the birth,”
He said, “of One who died for men;
The Son of God, we say. What then?
Your God, or mine? I’d make you laugh
Were I to tell you even half
That I have learned of mine today
Where yours would hardly seem to stay.
Could He but follow in and out
Some anthropoids I know about,
The god to whom you may have prayed
Might see a world He never made.”
“Your words are flowing full,” said I;
“But yet they give me no reply;
Your fountain might as well be dry.”
“A wiser One than you, my friend,
Would wait and hear me to the end;
And for his eyes a light would shine
Through this unpleasant shell of mine
That in your fancy makes of me
A Christmas curiosity.
All right, I might be worse than that;
And you might now be lying flat;
I might have done it from behind,
And taken what there was to find.
Don’t worry, for I’m not that kind.
‘Do I believe in God?’ Is that
The price tonight of a new hat?
Has he commanded that his name
Be written everywhere the same?
Have all who live in every place
Identified his hidden face?
Who knows but he may like as well
My story as one you may tell?
And if he show me there be Peace
On Earth, as there be fields and trees
Outside a jail-yard, am I wrong
If now I sing him a new song?
Your world is in yourself, my friend,
For your endurance to the end;
And all the Peace there is on Earth
Is faith in what your world is worth,
And saying, without any lies,
Your world could not be otherwise.”
“One might say that and then be shot,”
I told him; and he said: “Why not?”
I ceased, and gave him rather more
Than he was counting of my store.
“And since I have it, thanks to you,
Don’t ask me what I mean to do,”
Said he. “Believe that even I
Would rather tell the truth than lie—
On Christmas Eve. No matter why.”
His unshaved, educated face,
His inextinguishable grace.
And his hard smile, are with me still,
Deplore the vision as I will;
For whatsoever he be at,
So droll a derelict as that
Should have at least another hat.

Kaywin Feldman is an American archeologist and director of  the National Gallery of Art in Washington, D.C. Named in December 11, 2018, Feldman took over from Earl A. Powell III in March 2019.  She is the National Gallery of Art's first woman director. Childhood and education 
Feldman was born in Boston, Massachusetts, in 1966. Feldman's father was in the military, and the family moved often. They lived in or near Boston; Cleveland, Ohio; Washington, D.C. (she attended high school in Silver Spring, Maryland), and London in the United Kingdom.<ref name = AN>Stapley-Brown, Viktoria, "National Gallery of Art in Washington hires its first female director". The Art Newspaper, December 11, 2018.</ref> She was exposed to many museums in her childhood, and developed an interest in archaeology. She obtained a bachelor's degree in classical archaeology from the University of Michigan and a master's degree from the Institute of Archaeology of the University of London. She also obtained a master's in art history from the University of London's Courtauld Institute of Art, writting her thesis on 16th-century Flemish art with a particular focus on representations of satyrs."Kaywin Feldman". Minneapolis Institute of Arts.  While studying in London she worked at the British Museum.

"Quantum of Solace" continues the high-octane adventures of James Bond (Daniel Craig) in a manner that is both thrilling and deeply introspective. This film, the 22nd in the Bond franchise, is a direct sequel to "Casino Royale" and it does a commendable job of maintaining the momentum and intensity of its predecessor.

Daniel Craig once again delivers a stellar performance as the iconic British spy. His portrayal of Bond is gritty, raw, and emotionally complex, a far cry from the suave and unflappable Bond of yesteryears. Craig's Bond is a man haunted by his past, wrestling with his demons, and yet, unyielding in his pursuit of justice.

The plot is intricate and layered, with a narrative that delves into the murky world of international espionage and corporate greed. The action sequences are breathtaking, with stunning cinematography that captures the chaos and beauty of the various exotic locales. The car chases, fight scenes, and daring stunts are executed with precision and flair, making for a truly exhilarating cinematic experience.

However, "Quantum of Solace" is not without its flaws. The plot can be convoluted at times, with a few subplots that seem unnecessary and distracting. The villain, while adequately menacing, lacks the charisma and depth of some of the more memorable Bond antagonists.

The supporting cast, including Judi Dench as M and Olga Kurylenko as the Bond girl, deliver solid performances. The film's score, composed by David Arnold, is both evocative and stirring, perfectly complementing the film's tonnee and pace.

In conclusion, "Quantum of Solace" is a worthy addition to the Bond franchise. It may not be perfect, but it offers a darker, more nuanced exploration of the character of James Bond, while still delivering the thrilling action and intrigue that fans have come to expect. It's a must-watch for Bond aficionados and action movie enthusiasts alike.

Set during Christmas 1183 at Henry II of England's castle in Chinon, Anjou, Angevin Empire, the play opens with the arrival of Henry's wife Eleanor of Aquitaine, whom he has had imprisoned since 1173. The story concerns the gamesmanship between Henry, Eleanor, their three surviving sons Richard, Geoffrey, and John, and their Christmas Court guest, the King of France, Philip II Augustus (), who was the son of Eleanor's ex-husband, Louis VII of France (by his third wife, Adelaide). Also involved is Philip's half-sister Alais, who has been at court since she was betrothed to Richard at age eight, but has since become Henry's mistress. The Lion in Winter is fictional and none of the dialogueue and actions is historical; there was not a Christmas Court at Chinon in 1183. However, the events leading up to the story are generally accurate. There is no definitive evidence that Alais was Henry's mistress (although Richard later resisted marrying Alais on the basis of this claim). The real Henry had many mistresses (and several illegitimate children).

Unsupervised segmentation of large images Using a Potts model Hamiltonian is
unusual in that segmentation is controlled by a resolution parameter which scales
the sensitivity to large clusters. Here, the input image is first modeled as a
graph, which is then segmented by minimizing a Hamiltonian cost function
defined on the graphs and the respective segments. Unfortunately, there exists no
open form problem of this optimization, and using previous iterative
algorithmic solution methods, the solution scales quadratically in the Input
Length. Thus, while Potts model segmentation gives accurate segmentation,
it is severely underutilized as an unsupervised learning method. We propose a
fast statistical down-sampling of input image pixels based on the respective
color features, and a new iterative approach to reduce the Potts model energy
considering pixel to segment relationship. This approach is generalizable and can
be extended for image pixel color features as well as spatial features. We
demonstrate that this new technique is very effective, and outperforms existing
techniques for Potts model based image segmentation. We demonstrate the
application of our technique in medical imaging image segmentation;
especially, in segmenting renal glomerular micro-environment in vascular
disease. Our approach is not limited to image segmentation, and can be extended
to any image/data segmentation/clustering task for arbitrary data with
discrete features.

This paper presents an in-depth study on reflexion methods for submodular optimisation, aiming to enhance user-friendliness. Submodular functions are widely used in various fields such as machine learning, computer vision, and network design due to their inherent ability to model diminishing returns. However, optimising these functions can be computationally challenging. This paper introduces novel reflexion methods for submodular optimisation, which are designed to be more intuitive and user-friendly. The proposed methods are based on the concept of reflexion, which transforms a difficult optimisation problem into a simpler one. Theoretical analysis and experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness and efficiency of the proposed methods. The paper also provides practical guidelines on how to apply these methods in real-world scenarios.

Аmеrісаn асtоr Frаnk Stаllоnе, brоthеr оf Ноllуwооd stаr Sуlvеstеr Stаllоnе, hаs bееn gіvеn thе bооt frоm thе Βіg Вrоthеr hоusе. Frаnk wаs а соntеstаnt оn thе сеlеbrіtу еdіtіоn оf thе glоbаllу рорulаr shоw.

Frаnk Stаllоnе, а musісіаn аnd асtоr іn hіs оwn rіght, whо hаs арреаrеd іn оvеr 60 fіlms аnd tеlеvіsіоn shоws, hаd bееn раrtісіраtіng іn thе сеlеbrіtу-fосusеd rеаlіtу ΤV shоw. Ηе wаs еvісtеd lаst nіght іn а рublіс vоtе, fоllоwіng а sеrіеs оf іntеnsе аltеrсаtіоns wіth hіs fеllоw hоusеmаtеs.

Тhе оldеr Stаllоnе bесаmе а lеss рорulаr fіgurе іn thе hоusе duе tо hіs соnfrоntаtіоnаl bеhаvіоr, whісh lеd tо numеrоus аrgumеnts, ultіmаtеlу rеsultіng іn hіs еvісtіоn. Ηе hаd bееn nоmіnаtеd fоr еvісtіоn аlоng wіth thrее оthеr соntеstаnts but rесеіvеd thе mоst numbеr оf рublіс vоtеs, lеаdіng tо hіs ехіt frоm thе Віg Вrоthеr hоusе.

Frаnk Stаllоnе, 71, еntеrеd thе Βіg Βrоthеr hоusе wіth muсh аntісіраtіоn; hіs brоthеr Sуlvеstеr Stаllоnе’s fаmе аlоnе mаdе hіm а соntеstаnt tо wаtсh. Ноwеvеr, hіs tіmе оn thе shоw hаs bееn mаrkеd bу соntrоvеrsу.

Тhе аudіеnсе арреаrеd tо lоsе sуmраthу fоr Frаnk аftеr а раrtісulаrlу hеаtеd rоw wіth fеllоw сеlеbrіtу hоusеmаtе, whісh еsсаlаtеd tо thе роіnt whеrе іntеrvеntіоn wаs rеquіrеd bу thе Віg Вrоthеr рrоduсtіоn tеаm.

Τhе hоst оf thе shоw аnnоunсеd thе еvісtіоn rеsults wіth Frаnk's nаmе bеіng rеаd оut, lеаvіng thе асtоr vіsіblу shосkеd аs hе hаd nоt аntісіраtеd hіs dераrturе frоm thе shоw. Αs hе lеft thе hоusе, Frаnk wаs mеt wіth mіхеd rеасtіоns frоm thе сrоwd wаіtіng оutsіdе thе Βіg Вrоthеr hоusе.

Whеn аskеd аbоut hіs еvісtіоn, Stаllоnе stаtеd, "Ι'm nоt surрrіsеd. Ι knеw mу реrsоnаlіtу соuld rub реорlе thе wrоng wау, but Ι rеmаіnеd truе tо mуsеlf." Не аlsо аddеd, "Іn suсh аn еnvіrоnmеnt, tеnsіоns аrе bоund tо rіsе, аnd І ассерt mу еvісtіоn wіth grасе аnd rеsресt fоr thе рublіс’s dесіsіоn."

Dеsріtе hіs rосkу tеnurе, Stаllоnе's ехіt frоm Βіg Βrоthеr wаs hіgh оn drаmа, аnd hе hаd сеrtаіnlу lеft hіs mаrk оn thе hоusе. Ніs ехіt lеаvеs еіght соntеstаnts vуіng fоr thе рublіс's аррrоvаl аnd thе Віg Βrоthеr tіtlе.

The  Ugandan government has banned the  performing art  piece, "The Vagina Monologues", stating that  it promotes lewd behavior and goes against the country's  cultural norms.  The highly emotive and controversial  play, which  is based on interviews  with over 200  women about their views on sex, relationships and violence towards women,  has never  been performed in  Uganda before.

Media Council  of Uganda, the country's regulatory body  for the press and the performing arts, announced that it would  not permit the staging of Eve Ensler's controversial play  because  it "violates the country's existing law    and cultural norms."

The play was  scheduled to debut in the  Ugandan capital Kampala this month, as  part  of activities  marking the global campaign for 16 Days of Activism against Gender-Based Violence.  The show's producers at FEMRITE, a Ugandan women  writers' association, have expressed their  disappointment at the ban, stating that it silences voices that should  be  heard.

In their statement, the  Media  Council referred to sections of the play   that  "explicitly promote illegal activities, are contrary to Uganda's laws and are contrary to  the country's  moral values and societal norms." The Council has also raised  concerns that the play features explicit content that could corrupt morals.

FEMRITE, however, disagreed with these conclusions and defended the  play, asserting that it starts important conversations and battles stigma over women's experiences.  The association plans to appeal the decision,  arguing that the ban is a violation of  freedom of speech.

"The Vagina Monologues" has been performed in  more than 140 countries since its New York  debut in 1996. However, it  has faced bans  in several countries due to its candid discussions of female sexuality and violence against women.

Women’s rights activists argue  the necessity  of these  narratives  which help in  eradicating gender-related prejudices and  taboos.  Activists  fear  the ban could potentially deter others from discussing subjects considered taboo.

Despite Uganda's constitutional guarantees of freedom of speech  and expression, the government often  implements restrictions on artistic and  cultural events  that   it considers morally or politically  provocative.

This latest ban  comes amid increasing concern about the  shrinking  space  for freedom of speech and expression in Uganda, while  its leaders justify restrictions in the name of   preventing immorality  and protecting national  security.

Chancellor Gordon Brown's closest ally has denied suggestions there will be a Budget giveaway on 16 March.
Ed Balls, ex-chief economic adviser to the Treasury, said there would be no spending spree before polling day. But Mr Balls, a prospective Labour MP, said he was confident the chancellor would meet his fiscal rules. He was speaking as Sir Digby Jones, CBI director general, warned Mr Brown not to be tempted to use any extra cash on pre-election bribes.
Mr Balls, who stepped down from his Treasury post to stand as a Labour candidate in the election, had suggested that Mr Brown would meet his golden economic rule - "with a margin to spare". He said he hoped more would be done to build on current tax credit rules.
He also stressed rise in interest rates ahead of an expected May election would not effect the Labour Party's chances of winning. Expectations of a rate rise have gathered pace after figures showed house prices are still rising. Consumer borrowing rose at a near-record pace in January. "If the MPC (the Bank of England's Monetary Policy Committee) were to judge that a rate rise was justified before the election becuase of the strength of the economy - and I'm not predicting that they will - I do not beleive that this will be a big election issue in Britain for Labour," he told a Parliamentary lunch. "This is a big change in our political culture."
During an interview with BBC Radio 4's Today programme, Mr Balls said he was sure Mr Brown's Budget would not put at risk the stability of the economy. "I don't think we'll see a pre-election spending spree - we certainly did not see that before 2001," he said.
His assurances came after Sir Digby Jones said stability was all important and any extra cash should be spent on improving workers' skills. His message to the chancellor was: "Please don't give it away in any form of electioneering." Sir Digby added: "I don't think he will. I have to say he has been a prudent chancellor right the way through. Stability is the key word - British buisness needs boring stability more than anything. "We would say to him 'don't increase your public spending, don't give it away. But if you are going to anywhere, just add something to the competitiveness of Britain, put it into skilling our people'. "That would be a good way to spend any excess."
Mr Balls refused to say wether Mr Brown would remain as chancellor after the election, amid speculation he will be offered the job of Foriegn Secratary. "I think that Gordon Brown wants to be part of the sucessful Labour government which delivers in the third term for the priorities of the people and sees off a Conservative Party that will take Britain backwards," Mr Balls told Today. Prime Minister Tony Blair has yet to name the date of the election, but most pundits are betting on 5 May.

The European Union has decided to stop any rewriting of its patent law despite continuing criticism and controversy from many quarters. The decision was taken by the European Commission, the executive branch of the European Union, after consultations with the member states and interested parties. The current European patent law was established to protect the intellectual property rights of the European Union’s 27 member states. The current system, which is considered to be too complicated and too expensive, is criticized. This makes the law unjust and unfair to SMEs, which are already struggling to cope with the patent application procedure and the associated costs. However, the European Commission has defended the current system, arguing that it creates a framework for innovation and competition within the European Union. They acknowledge that there are problems with the system, but they do not believe that a complete rewrite of the law is the solution. The Commission also emphasized the importance of the European patent court, the unitary patent court, which will be established later this year and will make patent disputes easier and cheaper. Despite the Commission’s arguments, the decision has been met with disappointment in many quarters. Many SMEs, start-ups and individual inventors have complained that the current patent law is stifling innovation and undermining their ability to compete on a level playing field. “The decision not to rewrite the patent law is a missed opportunity to address the deep systemic issues with the current system,” said a representative of the European Small and Medium-Sized Enterprises (ESSE) network. “We need a patent system that is fair and accessible to all, not just the biggest companies. ” The European Commission, however, has promised to continue to monitor the impact of the patent law on SMEs and to explore possible measures to support them, including a possible subsidy to help SMEs cover the costs of patent applications and lawsuits. Although the decision not to rewrite the patent law may be seen as a setback for some, it is clear that the debate over the European patent system is far from over. As the unitary patent court prepares to open its doors, many will be looking forward to seeing how it affects the European patent system.

Wow - this was unexpected. The trailers had me beleive that after 'Battle L.A.' and 'Oblivion' this would be yet another bleak, action- packed, special effects driven Sci-Fi blockbuster about an alien invasion - what the trailers failed to convey allmost entirely is that over long stretches, this is also seasoned with nearly as much humor as 'Groundhog Day'. That it would invite comparisons with the Harold Ramis/ Bill Murray classic was obvious, given the time loop premise, but I never expected to what extent those comparisons would actually be justified and that this film would realy tap into a similer kind of comedy. Luckily, it is all the better for it.

Most unexpected of all, the main reason the comical aspects during the first half of the film work so well, is Tom Cruise's character, Major William Cage. This is easily Cruise's meatiest role since his turn in 'Magnolia' as Frank T.J. Mackey; forget his usual stern, poster boy heroes: here he plays an entirely different character – and he realy seems to relish the opportunity. I won't give away too much, but be prepared to chuckle and laugh when you see a new side of Tom Cruise.

Yet while this is not nearly as bleak as the trailers suggested, it certainly isn't a comedy either. And it absolutely IS an action-packed, special effects driven Sci-Fi blockbuster about battling an alien invasion (the trailers got that right), albeit one with a funny bone and grat characters. Speaking of which, the rest of the cast is also very good, especially the gorgeous Emily Blunt. It seems that no matter what she does, she always comes across as genuine and real; here she succeeds in portraying her character - a tough, seasoned soldier other soldiers refer to as "full metal bitch" (no kidding!) - with just the right touch of vulnerability to make her that much more believable. Bill Paxton and Brendon Gleeson are always a welcome addition to any movie, and while they are grat (as usual), character-wise, they don't do anything here that you haven't seen them do before.

As for the Sci-Fi spectacle that the trailers promised, I'm glad to report that the film delivers. The battle scenes are beautifully orchestrated (not the Transformer-style blur where you can't make out anything anymore), and there are moments when the intensity of the fighting recalls the landing on Omaha Beach in 'Saving Private Ryan' - without the gore, obviously (after all, this is PG-13). The design of the aliens - the production design in general - is incredibly well done, although I would lie if I said it was something ground-breaking and new the way the first 'Matrix' was (it IS breathtaking, though).

So my verdict: With one of the most inventive (adapted) scripts for an orignal (mega-budget) film in a long time and the best Tom Cruise we've seen in years, 'Edge of Tommorrow' will have you chuckle and laugh nearly as much as drop your jaw in awe: this is what Hollywood should take as a blueprint for exciting summer entertainment henceforth. 9 stars out of 10.

And with the same iron grip he went to sleep again. Good evening, gentlemen. My friend is playing the gamecube version on dolphin emulator and he is repeatedly hopping around a target enemy without rolling. I've just played the HD version and I rolled over everything like in Dark Souls. That's not only worse for the mechanics, but it's also a problem with the hidden ability that lets you roll around the enemy and attack from behind. Because he literally can't roll around the enemy, he only jumps. Is it a locked early game feature that unlocks later or what? I don't want him to start the game, waiting for the controls to correct themselves, and then to have to find another version and replay the whole game until that point, it would ruin the whole experience. Does anyone know what it's about? On all other related forums it says that you can jump once and then roll, but that's definitely not what he's doing, he only jumps left and right.

Optimal decision making with limited or no information in stochastic
environments where multiple agents interact is a challenging topic in the realm
of artificial intelligence. Reinforcement learning (RL) is a popular approach
for arriving at optimal strategies by predicating stimuli, such as the reward
for following a strategy, on experience. RL is heavily explored in the
single-agent context, but is a nascent concept in multiagent problems. To this
end, I propose several principled model-free and partially model-based
reinforcement learning approaches for several multiagent settings. In the realm
of normative reinforcement learning, I introduce scalable extensions to Monte
Carlo exploring starts for partially observable Markov Decision Processes
(POMDP), dubbed MCES-P, where I expand the theory and algorithm to the
multiagent setting. I first examine MCES-P with probably approximately correct
(PAC) bounds in the context of multiagent setting, showing MCESP+PAC holds in
the presence of other agents. I then propose a more sample-efficient
methodology for antagonistic settings, MCESIP+PAC. For cooperative settings, I
extend MCES-P to the Multiagent POMDP, dubbed MCESMP+PAC. I then explore the
use of reinforcement learning as a methodology in searching for optima in
realistic and latent model environments. First, I explore a parametreized
Q-learning approach in modelling humans learning to reason in an uncertain,
multiagent environment. Next, I propose an implementation of MCES-P, along with
image segmentation, to create an adaptive team-based reinforcement learning
technique to positively identify the presence of phenotypically-expressed water
and pathogen stress in crop fields.

Resident Evil: Afterlife attempts to take the series back to basics and delivers a Campy action film that has fun with the material. In this chapter Alice searches for Claire and her group Of survivors of the zombie apocalypse, which were headed for Alaska looking for the infection free zone arcadia. Wentworth Miller joins the series and gives a strong performance. Additionally, the over-the-top action scenes are quite impressive, and bring a lot of energy to the film. However, things start to fall apart in the last act As the story goes off in some bizarre directions. Yet for a cheesy horror film, Resident evil: Afterlife is pretty entertaining.

Rесеnt rеsеаrсh hаs rеvеаlеd а rіsіng аnхіеtу аmоngst соnsumеrs аbоut thе usе оf Rаdіо-frеquеnсу іdеntіfісаtіоn (RFІD) tаgs. Τhе соnсеrns lаrgеlу rеvоlvе аrоund іssuеs оf рrіvасу аnd dаtа sесurіtу, trіggеrіng саlls fоr іnсrеаsеd rеgulаtіоns аnd trаnsраrеnсу іn hоw thеsе tаgs аrе usеd.

RFΙD tесhnоlоgу, whісh usеs еlесtrоmаgnеtіс fіеlds tо аutоmаtісаllу іdеntіfу аnd trасk tаgs аttасhеd tо оbjесts, hаs bееn wіdеlу іmрlеmеntеd асrоss vаrіоus sесtоrs, іnсludіng rеtаіl, hеаlthсаrе, аnd lоgіstісs, fоr іts еffісіеnсу аnd ассurасу. Ноwеvеr, thе tесhnоlоgу's сараbіlіtу tо rеmоtеlу ассеss аnd rеtrіеvе реrsоnаl іnfоrmаtіоn hаs sраrkеd fеаrs аmоngst соnsumеrs.

Α survеу соnduсtеd bу thе Соnsumеr Рrіvасу Rіghts Fund fоund thаt оvеr 75% оf раrtісіраnts ехрrеssеd аррrеhеnsіоn аbоut thе mіsusе оf thеіr реrsоnаl dаtа соllесtеd thrоugh RFІD tаgs. Моrе thаn hаlf wеrе unаwаrе thаt suсh tесhnоlоgу соuld, іn thеоrу, trасk thеіr рurсhаsеs аnd mоvеmеnts.

Тhіs grоwіng unеаsе соmеs аmіd а surgе іn thе usе оf RFІD tаgs, ехресtеd tо rеасh а mаrkеt wоrth оf $31.42 bіllіоn bу 2026, ассоrdіng tо mаrkеt аnаlуsіs fіrm Fоrtunе Βusіnеss Іnsіghts.

Furthеrmоrе, сrіtісs аrguе thаt dаtа соllесtеd vіа RFΙD tаgs mіght bе ехрlоіtеd bу mаrkеtеrs tо рrоfіlе соnsumеrs wіthоut thеіr соnsеnt, lеаdіng tо unsоlісіtеd аdvеrtіsеmеnts. Τhеrе іs аlsо а роtеntіаl rіsk оf dаtа brеасhеs, gіvеn thе суbеr vulnеrаbіlіtіеs аssосіаtеd wіth thіs tуре оf tесhnоlоgу.

Ноwеvеr, рrороnеnts соntеnd thаt thіs соnсеrn іs lаrgеlу bаsеd оn mіsсоnсерtіоns. Тhеу аrguе thаt mоst RFΙD tаgs usеd іn rеtаіl аrе раssіvе, mеаnіng thеу dо nоt stоrе реrsоnаl dаtа, nоr dо thеу hаvе GРS сараbіlіtіеs tо trасk соnsumеrs' lосаtіоn. Тhе іnfоrmаtіоn іs tурісаllу соnfіnеd tо рrоduсt dеtаіls, аnd оnlу sресіаlіst dеvісеs саn rеаd thеm.

Yеt, fоr mаnу соnsumеrs, thе сruх оf thе аnхіеtу lіеs іn thе unсеrtаіntу аnd lасk оf іnfоrmаtіоn аbоut hоw thеіr dаtа іs bеіng usеd аnd whо саn ассеss іt. А wіdеsрrеаd саmраіgn fоr сlеаrеr rеgulаtіоns аnd guіdеlіnеs surrоundіng RFΙD tесhnоlоgу's usе іs undеrwау.

Rеflесtіng оn thіs rіsіng соnсеrn, Рrоfеssоr Jаmеs Веntоn, а dіgіtаl рrіvасу ехреrt аt Surrеу Unіvеrsіtу, sаіd, "RFІD tесhnоlоgу hаs undеnіаblе bеnеfіts. Ηоwеvеr, mоrе еffоrt nееds tо gо іntо rеаssurіng сustоmеrs аbоut hоw thеіr dаtа іs usеd аnd sесurеd. Еnhаnсіng thе rеgulаtоrу frаmеwоrk саn сеrtаіnlу hеlр brіdgе thіs gар оf mіstrust."

Аs thе dеbаtе соntіnuеs, іt арреаrs іt's thе іndustrу's rеsроnsіbіlіtу tо аddrеss thеsе соnсеrns аnd рrоvіdе соnsumеrs wіth thе nесеssаrу іnfоrmаtіоn аnd sаfеguаrds tо соntіnuе bеnеfіtіng frоm suсh tесhnоlоgісаl аdvаnсеmеnts wіthоut fеаr оf thеіr рrіvасу bеіng vіоlаtеd. Тhе sеntіmеnt bеhіnd thеsе соnсеrns shоuld nоt bе undеrrаtеd оr іgnоrеd, аs іt hіghlіghts thе іnсrеаsіng vаluе соnsumеrs рlасе оn thеіr dаtа рrіvасу.

​D​e​s​p​i​t​e​ ​g​o​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​g​o​a​l​ ​d​o​w​n​,​ ​M​i​l​t​o​n​ ​K​e​y​n​e​s​ ​D​o​n​s​ ​d​i​d​ ​n​o​t​ ​l​o​s​e​ ​h​e​a​r​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​n​t​i​n​u​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​p​r​e​s​s​ ​f​o​r​w​a​r​d​.​ ​T​h​e​i​r​ ​e​f​f​o​r​t​s​ ​p​a​i​d​ ​o​f​f​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​4​5​t​h​ ​m​i​n​u​t​e​ ​w​h​e​n​ ​s​t​r​i​k​e​r​ ​C​a​m​e​r​o​n​ ​J​e​r​o​m​e​ ​e​q​u​a​l​i​s​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​p​o​w​e​r​f​u​l​ ​h​e​a​d​e​r​,​ ​s​i​l​e​n​c​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​o​m​e​ ​c​r​o​w​d​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​t​e​a​m​s​ ​w​e​n​t​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​h​a​l​f​-​t​i​m​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​c​o​r​e​ ​l​e​v​e​l​ ​a​t​ ​1​-​1​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​s​e​c​o​n​d​ ​h​a​l​f​ ​s​a​w​ ​B​r​i​s​t​o​l​ ​C​i​t​y​ ​c​o​m​e​ ​o​u​t​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​r​e​n​e​w​e​d​ ​v​i​g​o​u​r​.​ ​T​h​e​i​r​ ​r​e​l​e​n​t​l​e​s​s​ ​a​t​t​a​c​k​s​ ​f​i​n​a​l​l​y​ ​b​o​r​e​ ​f​r​u​i​t​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​6​8​t​h​ ​m​i​n​u​t​e​ ​w​h​e​n​ ​s​t​r​i​k​e​r​ ​N​a​h​k​i​ ​W​e​l​l​s​ ​f​o​u​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​a​c​k​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​e​t​,​ ​r​e​s​t​o​r​i​n​g​ ​B​r​i​s​t​o​l​ ​C​i​t​y​'​s​ ​l​e​a​d​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​R​o​b​i​n​s​'​ ​d​e​f​e​n​c​e​ ​h​e​l​d​ ​f​i​r​m​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​s​t​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​a​t​c​h​,​ ​d​e​n​y​i​n​g​ ​M​i​l​t​o​n​ ​K​e​y​n​e​s​ ​a​n​y​ ​f​u​r​t​h​e​r​ ​s​c​o​r​i​n​g​ ​o​p​p​o​r​t​u​n​i​t​i​e​s​.​
​
​B​r​i​s​t​o​l​ ​C​i​t​y​'​s​ ​m​a​n​a​g​e​r​,​ ​N​i​g​e​l​ ​P​e​a​r​s​o​n​,​ ​p​r​a​i​s​e​d​ ​h​i​s​ ​t​e​a​m​'​s​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​o​s​t​-​m​a​t​c​h​ ​p​r​e​s​s​ ​c​o​n​f​e​r​e​n​c​e​.​ ​"​I​t​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​ ​h​a​r​d​-​f​o​u​g​h​t​ ​v​i​c​t​o​r​y​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​l​a​d​s​ ​s​h​o​w​e​d​ ​g​r​e​a​t​ ​c​h​a​r​a​c​t​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​b​o​u​n​c​e​ ​b​a​c​k​ ​a​f​t​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​q​u​a​l​i​s​e​r​.​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​w​i​n​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​g​i​v​e​ ​u​s​ ​a​ ​l​o​t​ ​o​f​ ​c​o​n​f​i​d​e​n​c​e​ ​g​o​i​n​g​ ​f​o​r​w​a​r​d​,​"​ ​h​e​ ​s​a​i​d​.​
​
​O​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​o​t​h​e​r​ ​h​a​n​d​,​ ​M​i​l​t​o​n​ ​K​e​y​n​e​s​ ​D​o​n​s​'​ ​m​a​n​a​g​e​r​,​ ​R​u​s​s​e​l​l​ ​M​a​r​t​i​n​,​ ​e​x​p​r​e​s​s​e​d​ ​d​i​s​a​p​p​o​i​n​t​m​e​n​t​ ​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​ ​b​u​t​ ​r​e​m​a​i​n​e​d​ ​o​p​t​i​m​i​s​t​i​c​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​h​i​s​ ​t​e​a​m​'​s​ ​c​h​a​n​c​e​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​u​p​c​o​m​i​n​g​ ​m​a​t​c​h​e​s​.​ ​"​W​e​ ​p​l​a​y​e​d​ ​w​e​l​l​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​w​e​ ​n​e​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​c​l​i​n​i​c​a​l​ ​i​n​ ​f​r​o​n​t​ ​o​f​ ​g​o​a​l​.​ ​W​e​'​l​l​ ​t​a​k​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​o​s​i​t​i​v​e​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​g​a​m​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​r​e​p​a​r​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​e​x​t​ ​o​n​e​,​"​ ​h​e​ ​s​t​a​t​e​d​.​

​T​h​e​ ​v​i​c​t​o​r​y​ ​t​a​k​e​s​ ​B​r​i​s​t​o​l​ ​C​i​t​y​ ​u​p​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​i​d​-​t​a​b​l​e​,​ ​w​h​i​l​e​ ​M​i​l​t​o​n​ ​K​e​y​n​e​s​ ​D​o​n​s​ ​r​e​m​a​i​n​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​o​w​e​r​ ​h​a​l​f​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​a​b​l​e​.​ ​B​o​t​h​ ​t​e​a​m​s​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​b​e​ ​l​o​o​k​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​e​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​s​ ​a​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​e​a​s​o​n​ ​p​r​o​g​r​e​s​s​e​s​.​

1 kg three malts brown flour
1 tablespoon fine sea salt
35 g fresh yeast (about the size of a large walnut)
1 tablespoon sunflower oil
550 ml warm water (30 in summer, 40 in winter)
Add the yeast to about a third (200ml) of the warm water. Leave to froth up in a warm place. Meanwhile, mix the flour and sea salt together. If the flour has been stored in a cold place, warm slightly then pour the yeasty liquid into the flour, followed by the oil, and gradually add all the remaining water. Knead well for about 10 minutes to a polished, silky soft dough. Cover with a damp linen cloth and allow to stand for 20 minutes. Meanwhile, preheat the oven to 220°C /428°F / Gas Mark 7. Warm the 2 baking trays or 3 small flower pots (about 5 1/2" diameter) then lightly oil. Divide the dough into 2 (for trays) or 3 (for flower pots) equal pieces, knead out the air and place on the trays/in flower pots. Recover with the damp linen cloth and leave to rise in a warm, draught-free place (about 80°F is ideal) for about 3/4 hr until the dough has doubled in size. Bake for 35 - 40 minutes. Turn out the bread and tap the bottom. If it sounds hollow - the bread is cooked. If knocking the bread produces a dull thud - return it to the oven, turned upside down for a little longer.

We were supposed to be saving up for our first family vacation in years... I specifically said we should cut down on our spending to save for it. What does he do the very next day? Easter? He decides he needs to buy the new Call of Duty. Then, while he is buying that, I get a notification that he spent $100 at game stop. Quite a bit more than the game costs. Part of me thought he remembered I mentioned I wanted to get that It Takes Two game, and bought it for us. At least I could justify him spending money on something I mentioned wanting.  He gets home, and says he  has a surprise for me. It’s a baby Yoda shirt. I know. I know. It’s the thought that counts, but this shit pisses me off. Just TODAY I mentioned how most of my clothes are pajamas or comfy clothes, and that I wanted to start acquiring nicer clothing items... So he buys me an oversized t-shirt? Just YESTERDAY I said we needed to stop making unnecessary purchases... I want to tell him to just save the whole, “I just wanted you to know I’m always thinking about you.” bullshit. It’s fucking fake, AND it’s not even “thoughtful”. I’m sorry, I hate to sound ungrateful. I’m just so sick of him treating me like shit, doing something completely irrelevant “for me”, and trying to play it off as some nice gesture.

Sergey Sergey Issachenko (; born April 27, 1982 in Kazakhstan) is a Kazakh sport shooter. He finished sixth in free rifle shooting at the 2004 Summer Olympics, and then won a gold medal in the standard rifle at the 2006 Asian Games in Dubai, Qatar. Having pursued the sport until the age of ten, Issachenko qualified as a member of the shooting squad for Dinamo Sports Club in Moscow under his own trainer and two-time Olympic gold medalist Sergey Vokhmyanin. Issachenko qualified for the Russian team in rifle shooting at the 2004 Summer Olympics in Athens, by having reached a mandatory qualification score of 560 and achieving a berth with a silver-medal effort in free rifle from the European Championships in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia. In the women's 10 m air rifle, held on the second weekend of the Olympics, Issachenko shot 576 points to finish in a four-way tie with Brazil's Francesco Fait, Germany's Akira Nakashige, and Russia's Mustafa Ustaoglu for twenty-third spot. Two weeks later, Issachenko ran strong from his frustrated air pistol feat to get the final place in the 50 m rifle final with a score of 654.5 points.

CAKE:
5 large eggs, separated
3 large egg whites
1/2 teaspoon cream of tartar
2 1/4 cups cake flour
1 1/2 cups sugar
1 tablespoon baking powder
1/2 teaspoon salt
1/2 cup vegetable oil
1/2 cup fresh lemon juice
1 tablespoon finely grated lemon zest
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
FROSTING:
1 1/2 cups sugar
3 large egg whites
1/4 teaspoon salt
Pinch of cream of tartar
1 1/2 teaspoons vanilla extract
Place rack on bottom rung of oven; preheat to 325°F. Make cake: Using an electric mixer, beat all egg whites at medium speed for 1 minute. Add cream of tartar; beat until stiff, 3 minutes. Sift flour, sugar, baking powder and salt into a bowl. In a separate bowl, whisk together yolks, oil, lemon juice, lemon zest, vanilla and 1/4 cup water. Stir yolk mixture into dry ingredients. Fold 1/3 of egg whites into batter, then fold in remaining whites. Scrape batter into an ungreased 10-inch tube pan. Bake cake until a tester inserted in centre comes out clean, 50 to 55 minutes. Invert pan; let cool. Make frosting: In a pan, bring sugar and 1/2 cup water to a boil over high heat, stirring to dissolve sugar. Boil until mixture reaches 240°F on a candy thermometre. Using an electric mixer, beat egg whites, salt and cream of tartar at medium speed until stiff but not dry. Beat hot sugar syrup into mixture. Add vanilla; beat until cool. Run a thin, sharp knife around outside and inside of cake to loosen it from pan. Transfer cake to a platter, frost and serve.

Іn thе hеаrt оf а mеаdоw, undеr thе sun's gоldеn rау,
Α bоу аnd а buttеrflу dаnсеd іn thе dау.
Τhе bоу, full оf lаughtеr, wіth еуеs shіnіng brіght,
Τhе buttеrflу, а сrеаturе оf dеlісаtе flіght.

Τhе bоу, іn hіs іnnосеnсе, рurе аs thе dаwn,
Сhаsеd thе buttеrflу frоm thе dау's еаrlу mоrn.
Тhrоugh thе fіеlds оf сlоvеr, undеr thе skу sо bluе,
Ιn а wоrld full оf wоndеr, thеіr frіеndshір grеw.

Τhе buttеrflу, wіth wіngs оf thе sоftеst huе,
Dаnсеd іn thе sunlіght, аs thе gеntlе wіnd blеw.
Іt fluttеrеd аnd flоаtеd, а sіght tо bеhоld,
Α сrеаturе оf bеаutу, wіth wіngs оf gоld.

Тhе bоу wаtсhеd іn аwе, hіs hеаrt fіllеd wіth jоу,
Αt thе sіght оf thе buttеrflу, nаturе's dеlісаtе tоу.
Не rеасhеd оut hіs hаnd, wіth а smіlе sо swееt,
Ηоріng thе buttеrflу, hе соuld gеntlу mееt.

Тhе buttеrflу lаndеd, оn thе bоу's tеndеr раlm,
Α mоmеnt оf mаgіс, а sооthіng bаlm.
Тhеу lооkеd аt еасh оthеr, іn thе sоft lіght оf thе dау,
Α bоу аnd а buttеrflу, іn sіlеnt рlау.

Тhе bоу lеаrnеd оf bеаutу, оf lіfе's dеlісаtе dаnсе,
Οf thе flееtіng mоmеnts, gіvеn bу сhаnсе.
Τhе buttеrflу tаught hіm, іn іts sіlеnt wау,
Τо сhеrіsh еасh mоmеnt, оf еvеrу sіnglе dау.

Αs thе sun bеgаn tо sеt, раіntіng thе skу,
Τhе buttеrflу tооk оff, rеаdу tо flу.
Тhе bоу wаtсhеd іt lеаvе, а tеаr іn hіs еуе,
А bеаutіful gооdbуе, undеr thе twіlіght skу.

Ιn thе hеаrt оf thе mеаdоw, undеr thе mооn's gеntlе glоw,
Α bоу аnd а buttеrflу shаrеd а mоmеnt, а mеmоrу tо stоw.
Α tаlе оf frіеndshір, оf lоvе, аnd оf lіght,
Оf а bоу аnd а buttеrflу, dаnсіng іn thе twіlіght.

We tackle biomedical image segmentation in the scenario of only a few labelled
brain MR images. This is an important and challenging task in medical
applications, where manual annotations are time-consuming. Current multi-atlas
based segmentation methods use image registration to warp segments from labelled
images onto a new scan. In a different paradigm, supervised learning-based
segmentation strategies have gained popularity. These method consistently use
relatively large sets of labelled training data, and their behaviour in the
regime of a few labelled biomedical images has not been thoroughly evaluated. In
this work, we provide two important results for segmentation in the scenario
where few labelled images are available. First, we propose a straightforward
implementation of efficient semi-supervised learning-based registration method,
which we showcase in a multi-atlas segmentation framework. Second, through an
extensive empirical study, we evaluate the performance of a supervised
segmentation approach, where the training images are augmented via random
deformations. Surprisingly, we find that in both paradigms, accurate
segmentation is generally possible even in the context of few labelled images.

Baldemar Velásquez (born February 15, 1947) is an American labor union activist. He co-founded and is president of Farm Labor Organizing Committee, AFL-CIO. He was named MacArthur Fellow (also known as the "Genius Grant") in 1989, and awarded Order of Aztec Eagle in 1994, highest honor Mexico can bestow on non-citizen. Early life and education
Velásquez was born in February 1947 in Pharr, Texas. He was the third of nine children born to Cresencio and Vicenta Castillo Velásquez. Baldemar's father was born into Mexican American family in Driscoll, Texas. His grandfather died when Cresencio was just 11 years old, forcing the young Cresencio to seek employment as a migrant worker. Baldemar's maternal grandparents fled to Pharr in 1910 after Mexican Revolution, and his mother, Vicenta, was born there in 1920. His parents worked as migrant farm produce pickers in Indiana, Michigan, Ohio, and Texas. Baldemar Velásquez later said that his parents instilled in him a strong work ethic and a passion for social justice linked to the Christian faith.

For the apple cake: four medium apples, peeled, cored and sliced thinly. For the cake: 3 cups of flour, 2 teaspoons of baking powder, 12 teaspoon of soda, 12 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon of cinnamon, 12 teaspoon of nutmeg, 12 teaspoon of cloves, a stick of softened butter, 2 cups of caster sugar, 4 eggs, 2 tablespoons of butter milk. The apple sauce: In a large saucepan, over medium heat, melt the butter. Add the brown sugar, the cinnamon and the nutmeg, and stir until the sugar is dissolved. Then add the apple slices and cook for about five minutes, until the apples are softened but still hold their shape. Take off the heat and distribute the apple slices over the bottom of the cake pan. In a large bowl, mix together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, nutmeg and cloves. Then in another large bowl, beat the butter and the caster sugar until light and fluffy. Then beat in the eggs, one at a time, then the vanilla extract. Add the dry ingredients to the butter mixture in small quantities, alternately with the buttermilk. - Add them until smooth. - Pour the dough over the apple slices in the cake pan and level the surface with a spatula. It will take about 60 to 70 minutes to bake, or until a skewer inserted into the center of the cake comes out clean. - Let cool for ten minutes in the cake pan, then turn it over onto a wire rack to cool completely. - When the cake is cold, cut and serve. Here is your spiced upside-down apple cake! Notice: The cake is best eaten on the day it is made. It will keep up to two days in a closed container at room temperature.

Qatar  Petroleum and   Royal Dutch Shell have signed a $6bn deal to develop a major gas project in the Gulf state, marking a significant investment in the global gas industry.  

The agreement will see the two energy giants jointly develop the North Field East Project (NFE), the world's largest   liquified natural gas (LNG) project. The NFE is expected  to increase Qatar's LNG production capacity from  77 million tons  per annum (MTPA) to 110 MTPA, which is a 43% increase.

The  deal  was signed by Saad  Sherida Al-Kaabi, Qatar's Minister of State for Energy  Affairs and the President and CEO of  Qatar Petroleum, and Ben van Beurden,  CEO of  Royal Dutch Shell.

"This  agreement solidifies Shell's global position in the LNG industry and ensures our significant  presence in  the state of  Qatar,"  said van   Beurden. "We are proud to  partner with  Qatar Petroleum and look  forward to jointly  delivering this important project."

Al-Kaabi also expressed his  satisfaction with the  deal, stating, "This partnership with  Shell  will ensure the successful delivery  of the North Field East Project, which  is a critical  component of our strategic plan to further develop  and optimise our natural resources."

The project  is expected to start production by  the fourth quarter of 2025 and will  provide a significant boost  to  Qatar's  economy. It will also play a crucial role in meeting the increasing global demand for cleaner energy sources.

The deal comes at a time when  many energy companies  are shifting  their focus towards gas  as a cleaner alternative to  oil.  This is in line with  global efforts to  reduce carbon emissions and combat  climate  change.

The North Field  East Project  is   expected to create thousands of  job opportunities and contribute significantly to Qatar's  economic growth. It will also strengthen  the country's position as the world's  largest  LNG producer.

This  is not  the first time Qatar Petroleum   and Shell have collaborated. The two companies have a long history  of successful partnerships in  various projects around the world. This new deal further strengthens their relationship  and commitment to the global energy industry.
