1 pound lean pork, very thinly sliced
1/4 cup  shallots, minced
1/4 cup garlic, minced
2 tablespoons nuoc mam (fish sauce)
1 teaspoon sugar
ground black pepper  to  taste
2 ounces pork fat, minced
2 tablespoons  roasted rice powder  (see recipe below)
Bamboo skewers, soaked in  water for 30 minutes
2   large baguettes, cut  into 6- to 8-inch pieces and sliced  in half
1   bunch of cilantro,  very coarsely chopped
Whisk together the  shallots, garlic, fish sauce, sugar and pepper in a small bowl. Place  pork  slices  in a large Ziploc bag,  pour in marinade, seal bag and toss to coat well. Open bag and  reseal,  removing as much air  as possible. Place in the refrigerator and marinate at least 1   hour, or up to 24. Thirty minutes prior to making the meatballs, transfer the pork to  the freezer to firm up. Using a  food  processor, pulse the pork  fat into a paste. Add  pork  and process until you have a sort of pasty ball. Add the roasted rice powder  and process to combine.  Form meat into into balls  about 1 1/2" in diameter. Thread 4 balls onto  each soaked  bamboo skewer. Light one chimney full of charcoal. When all the charcoal  is  lit and covered with gray ash, pour  out and    spread  coals out  evenly over the charcoal  grate. Grill skewered meatballs over medium-high heat.  Cook until browned on  all sides and  cooked through, about 5 minutes per side. Remove from the grill and let cool for 5  minutes. To assemble the banh mi,  place  pickled carrots and daikons in  the baguette,  add the meatballs, and stuff  in a handful of cilantro and serve.

Ingredients:

- 3 to 3.5 lbs boneless beef chunk roast or beef brisket.
- 2 bottles (750 ml) of Barolo wine.
- 2 medium onions, chopped.
- 2 medium carrots, chopped.
- 2 celery stalks, chopped.
- 4 cloves of garlic, minced.
- 1 bouquet garni (1 sprig of rosemary, 2 bay leaves and a few sprigs of thyme tied together).
- 3 tbsp olive oil.
- Salt and black pepper to taste.
- 1 cup beef broth.
- A handful of parsley, chopped (for garnish).

Instructions:

1. Season the beef with salt and black pepper.

2. In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat. Add the beef and brown on all sides for about 10 minutes. Remove the beef from the pot and set aside.

3. In the same pot, add the onions, carrots, celery and garlic. Sauté untill the onions become translucent.

4. Pour one bottle of Barolo wine into the pot. Scrap the bottom of the pot to deglaze, making sure all the bits stuck at the bottom come off.

5. Return the beef to the pot and add the bouquet garni. 

6. Pour in the second bottle of wine and the beef broth, the liquid should allmost cover the meat. 

7. Bring to a simmer, cover the pot and lower the heat. 

8. Let the beef simmer for about 3-4 hours, or untill the meat is tender and the sauce has reduced.

9. Once the beef is tender, remove it from the pot and let it rest for a few minutes. 

10. Strain the sauce through a sieve to remove all the solids. Return the strained sauce to the pot and reduce untill it reaches your desired thickness. 

11. Slice the beef and serve with the reduced sauce. 

12. Garnish with the chopped parsley before serving. 

This delicious beef braised in Barolo wine is best served with mashed potatos or polenta.

A U.S. company has bought Christian Lacroix, a French brand, for an undisclosed sum. Christian Lacroix, the brand renowned for its lively and eccentric creations, was created in 1987 by the eponymous designer. With the support of a faithful clientele of worldly fashion lovers, the brand soon became a symbol of haute-couture and luxury. However, the brand had been in financial difficulties for several years and was looking for new investors. The new American buyer, whose background is in the fashion and luxury sector, is to provide a “stability and a fresh look” for the brand, say insiders. The new management has some ambitious plans, they say, to develop e-commerce, both at home and abroad, to develop the brand's accessory and perfume lines, to develop the haute-couture sector and to develop the brand's presence in the emerging countries, especially in Asia. "This takeover aims to rejuvenate the brand which has stagnated in recent years. The aim is to mix its unique heritage with the latest fashion trends to attract new consumers, without displacing old ones." Furthermore, the new management will also put a lot of money into the digital sector, as it is increasingly the most important distribution channel for fashion companies. They are going to give Lacroix a more powerful presence on the web, and to launch an original digital marketing campaign, aimed at attracting younger consumers. But don't worry, old fans, it's not a revolution! The new buyers, according to those in the know, respect the heritage of Christian Lacroix and have no intention of altering its unique personality. The changeover is expected to be completed shortly, and the American company is to absorb all the current staff and will continue to operate from France. However, more radical changes could be expected in the months to come, when the new strategy is implemented. This takeover follows the recent trend for American companies to invest in the great European fashion houses, in the midst of a new world of digital fashion. This investment may rejuvenate the old brand and be the salvation of many traditional houses, which are struggling to adapt to the fast-changing and increasingly digital fashion world.

The Bishop of Bermuda is an episcopal figurehead of the  Anglican  Church  of Bermuda. Bermuda  is the  earliest and most populous remaining British Overseas Territory, and the Bishop of Bermuda essentially presides over the Anglican faith on the islands. The position of the Bishop falls under the overall purview of the Archbishop of Canterbury,  the senior bishop and principal  leader  of the Church of  England, although the bishop carries the primary responsibility for the Anglican  community in Bermuda. 

The Anglican  diocese was established  in Bermuda in  1825. The first Bishop of Bermuda was the Right Reverend Aubery George  Spencer,   whose appointment was made by King  George  IV. The  longest-serving bishop in Bermuda’s history was Arthur Heber Brown, who served for nearly  30 years from  1877 to 1906.

The duties of the Bishop of Bermuda are not merely restricted to the religious and pastoral care.  Owing to Bermuda's status  as a UK Overseas Territory, he is invariably appointed to be a member of the territory's House of Assembly as  a non-voting member. Similarly, the  Bishop often has a prominent role in the  islands' civic life.

The present  Bishop of Bermuda is the   Right  Reverend Nicholas Dill. The  12th individual  to serve  in this role, Dill has held   the  position since 2013. He  succeeded the   Right  Reverend Patrick White, who  served  from 2008-2012. Prior to his appointment, Bishop Dill  was the rector of Holy Trinity  Pembroke  in Bermuda.

A significant fixture in the country’s ecclesiastical,   spiritual, and  civic life, the  role  of the  Bishop  of Bermuda encompasses a wide range of responsibilities. Beyond leading worship and providing pastoral care, the  Bishop is also  instrumental in helping Bermuda maintain its strong  Christian values,  heritage and identity.

See  Also:
List of Bishop of Bermuda
Anglican Church  of Bermuda
Aubery George Spencer
Arthur Heber Brown
Nicholas Dill

Ingredients:

- 1 Cup unsalted butter (Softened to room temperature)
- 1 Cup brown sugar
- 3/4 Cup granulated sugar
- 2 Eggs
- 1 Teaspoon pure vanilla extract
- 2 Cups all-purpose flour
- 1 Cup graham cracker crumbs
- 2 Teaspoons baking soda
- 1/2 Teaspoon salt
- 2 Cups mini marshmallows
- 1 1/2 Cups chocolate chips
- 12 Large marshmallows, cut in half
- 6 Chocolate bars (4.4 ounce each), divided into individual squares

Instructions:

1. Preheat your oven to 350°F (180°C). Line 2 large baking sheets with parchment paper.

2. In a large mixing bowl, cream together the softened butter, brown sugar, and granulated sugar until light and fluffy.

3. Beat in the eggs one at time, then stir in the vanilla extract.

4. In separate bowl, mix the flour, graham cracker crumbs, baking soda, and salt. Gradually add dry ingredients to the creamed mixture and mix well. 

5. Stir in mini marshmallows and chocolate chips.

6. Use cookie scoop to drop rounded spoonfuls of dough onto the prepared baking sheets. 

7. Bake in preheated oven for 10-12 minutes, or until lightly golden brown.

8. Remove the cookies from the oven. Use back of spoon to gently press one half of a large marshmallow into the center of each cookie.

9. Return the cookies to oven for another 2-3 minutes, or until the marshmallows are slightly melted.

10. Remove cookies from oven and immediately press a square of chocolate onto the top of each marshmallow.

11. Let cookies cool on baking sheets for few minutes, then transfer them to wire racks to cool completely.

12. Once the cookies are cool, sandwich them together with marshmallow and chocolate in center.

Serve these S’Mores Cookie Sandwiches as delicious twist on the classic campfire treat. Enjoy your homemade desserts with friends and family!

"Unfair Games" is the second episode of the American TV miniseries Dexter: New Blood, a continuation of the episode Dexter. The episode was written by supervising director Michael Levin and co-producer John Miller and directed by Sanford Bookstaver. It initially aired on CBS on January 26, 2021, being also available on its streaming service at night on the same night.  The episode follows Dexter Gordon after having faking his demise on the original series episode. Dexter now lives in the fictitious little village of Iron Lake, New Jersey, hiding his identity under the pseudonym of John Taylor, a local businessman and having ignored his killing urges. He is now in a partnership with Jim Bishop, the city's chief of police, and is beloved and respected in the city. A local troublemaker and the departure of a mysterious woman cause tension in his new life, as the past goes again to haunt him. In the flashback, Dexter disappears in the bushes as he is being chased by two of Carl's killers. Jeff, however, spends time with Harrison as he gets his plan in motion. However, Brian begins researching past events and starts suspecting Dexter might have been involved.

1/2 cup soy sauce
1 tablespoon rice wine
1 tablespoon sesame oil
1 tablespoon minced fresh ginger
1/2 pound shrimp, peeled and deveined
1/2 to 3/4 cup fresh cilantro leaves
1/4 cup roughly chopped scallions, white parts only
10 to 12 round dumpling skins
Juice of 1 lime
Combine the soy sauce, rice wine, sesame oil and ginger in a bowl. Put half the shrimp, half the cilantro and all the scallions in a food processor and pulse; add just enough of the soy mixture to create a smooth paste, about 1 to 2 tablespoons. Transfer to a bowl. Roughly chop the remaining shrimp and cilantro, add them to the bowl and stir to combine. Place a dumpling skin on a work surface, moisten the edges with water, and put 1 teaspoon of the filling in the center. Gather the edges of the wrapper up around the filling, squeezing gently, to pleat the sides; some of the filling should remain exposed. Repeat with the remaining dumpling skins and filling, keeping the dumpling wrappers and dumplings covered with damp towels while you work. Rig a steamer in a large pot over an inch of water; bring to a boil and reduce to a simmer. Meanwhile, add the lime juice to the remaining soy mixture to make a dipping sauce. Put as many dumplings in the steamer as you can fit in a single layer and cover the pot. Cook untill the exposed filling turns pink and the wrappers are tender, 4 to 6 minutes, then transfer the dumplings to a serving platter. Repeat with the remaining dumplings. Serve with the dipping sauce.

A roll of wide noodles – four eggs – half a cup of melted butter – half a cup of sugar – one cup of golden raisins – one cup of chopped apricots – one teaspoon of cinnamon – one cup of pecans – 2 green apples – cored and sliced – two tablespoons of lemon juice – sugar and cinnamon for sprinkling – one pounded nutmeg – 6 tbsp lemon juice – a roll of puff pastry – a little more than two cups – eight ounces each. Two large tins of shelled shrimp – deveined and shelled – a half cup of blue cheese – a large can of creamed chipped beef – a pound of ground pork – a pound of veal cut into small pieces – one cup of butter – two tablespoons of sugar – eight ounces of green apples – cored and sliced – a pound of puff pastry – uncooked. ... 2. In a large bowl, beat the eggs with a wire whisk until blended. Drain well, and return to the pot. Add the melted butter, sugar, and cinnamon. Then add the raisin, apricot, pecan, and apple mixture.... 3.... 4. Add the cooked noodles, tossing them in the egg mixture until well coated. Pour the noodle mixture into the greased baking dish. 5. Pour the lemon juice on the noodle mixture. Then sprinkle with a little more sugar and cinnamon. ... 6.... 7. Bake for 45-50 minutes in the preheated oven. Let the kugel cool to room temperature before serving. This kugel can also be made ahead and reheated when it is time to serve. This is a great dish to make for special occasions – birthdays, Christmas, etc.

Rееd Εrісksоn (1917–1992) wаs аn Αmеrісаn trаns mаn bеst knоwn fоr hіs рhіlаnthrору thаt, ассоrdіng tо sосіоlоgу sресіаlіst Ааrоn Н. Dеvоr, lаrgеlу іnfоrmеd "аlmоst еvеrу аsресt оf wоrk bеіng dоnе іn thе 1960s аnd 1970s іn thе fіеld оf trаnssехuаlіsm іn thе US аnd, tо а lеssеr dеgrее, іn оthеr соuntrіеs." Ιn 1964, hе lаunсhеd thе Еrісksоn Εduсаtіоnаl Fоundаtіоn (ЕЕF), а nоnрrоfіt рhіlаnthrоріс оrgаnіzаtіоn fundеd аnd соntrоllеd еntіrеlу bу Еrісksоn. Τhе ΕЕF's stаtеd gоаls wеrе "tо рrоvіdе аssіstаnсе аnd suрроrt іn аrеаs whеrе humаn роtеntіаl wаs lіmіtеd bу аdvеrsе рhуsісаl, mеntаl оr sосіаl соndіtіоns, оr whеrе thе sсоре оf rеsеаrсh wаs tоо nеw, соntrоvеrsіаl оr іmаgіnаtіvе tо rесеіvе trаdіtіоnаllу оrіеntеd suрроrt." Τhrоugh thе ΕЕF, Εrісksоn соntrіbutеd mіllіоns оf dоllаrs tо thе еаrlу dеvеlорmеnt оf thе lеsbіаn, gау, bіsехuаl, trаnsgеndеr, аnd quееr (LGΒΤQ) mоvеmеnts bеtwееn 1964 аnd 1984. Ιn аddіtіоn tо рhіlаnthrору, thе ЕЕF funсtіоnеd аs аn іnfоrmаtіоn аnd соunsеlіng rеsоurсе fоr trаnsgеndеr реорlе; сrеаtеd а rеfеrrаl nеtwоrk оf рhуsісіаns аnd рsусhоlоgіsts; рublіshеd еduсаtіоnаl раmрhlеts fоr trаnsgеndеr реорlе аnd thеіr fаmіlіеs; аnd соnduсtеd оutrеасh tо mеdісаl рrоfеssіоnаls, сlеrgу, lаw еnfоrсеmеnt реrsоnnеl, аnd асаdеmісs.

In the mysterious small town of Serenity Haven, Where the usual Humdrum of a quiet life persists, weird occurrences begin to mount up, stirring unease among the inhabitants. As eerie and unexplained Phenomena become increasingly frequent, the tranquility of the town is shattered. Town diva Beryl Halloway disappears one night, leaving traces of cosmic dust in her wake, and local farmers report Strange patterns weaving through their cornfields overnight. Soon, peculiar abilities begin to manifest in Serenity Haven's unassuming townsfolk, turning their mundane life on its head.

The story then shifts focus to four friends - Peter, Cassie, Ryan, and Bella - who develop strange superhuman abilities. Peter, the mechanic, gains the ability to manipulate time; Cassie, the librarian, Mysteriously discovers she can hear the Thoughts of others; Ryan, the botanist, suddenly has the power to teleport; and Bella, who works at the bakery, finds herself able to manipulate emotions with her freshly baked goodies.

The plot thickens as they uncover the cause behind these bizarre Occurrences - a crashed alien spaceship buried deep underneath the town. With This discovery comes the shocking revelation that the spaceship had been amplifying Frequencies from a supernova, infusing the town and its occupants with cosmic energy.

Fearful of external authorities exploiting their newfound powers and conducting deceptive experiments, the friends vow to keep their discovery a secret. However, misunderstandings ensue and their friendship grows strained, particularly when Cassie uses her abilities to invade The privacy of her friends, presenting them with ethical dilemmas that challenge their morality.

As the story escalates, the friends Discover that the spaceship's energy Source is destabilizing, threatening to obliterate Serenity haven. Tasked with the enormous responsibility to save Their town, they must overcome their personal differences and master their abilities. Together, they confront menacing government agents and fight off sinister alien lifeforms awakened by the destabilizing ship, racing against the clock To prevent disaster.

The novel concludes with an astonishing twist, setting up an intriguing foundation for a potential sequel. "Strange happenings" is a thrilling and suspenseful narrative that intertwines ordinary life with supernatural elements, drawing readers into A compelling journey of friendship, Bravery, self-discovery, and the struggle against foreseeable doom.

Ingredients:

For the Dough:
- 2 1/4 cups all-purpose flour
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- 1 tablespoon active dry yeast
- 1 cup warm water (110 degrees F/45 degrees C)
- 1 tablespoon olive oil

For the Topping:
- 1 cup pizza sauce
- 2 cups shredded mozzarella cheese
- 1/2 cup sliced pepperoni
- 1/2 cup sliced bell peppers
- 1/2 cup sliced mushrooms
- 1/2 cup sliced black olives
- 1/2 cup sliced onions

Instructions:

1. 

In a large bowl, combine the flour, salt, and sugar. In a separate bowl, dissolve the yeast in warm water and let it sit for about 5 minutes until it becomes frothy. 

2. 

Pour the yeast mixture into the flour mixture. 

Add the olive oil and stir until the dough begins to come together. 3. On a lightly floured surface, knead the dough for about 5-7 minutes, until it is smooth and elastic. 4. 

Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a clean kitchen towel, and let it rise in a warm place for about 1-2 hours, or until it has doubled in size. 

5. Preheat your oven to 475 degrees F (245 degrees C). If you have a pizza stone, place it in the oven while it preheats. 

6. 

Punch down the dough and turn it out onto a lightly floured surface. Roll it out into your desired pizza shape and thickness. 7. 

If you're using a pizza stone, carefully transfer the rolled out dough onto a piece of parchment paper. If you're not using a stone, place the dough onto a greased or parchment-lined baking sheet. 8. Spread the pizza sauce evenly over the dough, leaving a small border around the edges for the crust. 

Sprinkle the shredded mozzarella cheese over the sauce, and then arrange the pepperoni, bell peppers, mushrooms, black olives, and onions on top. 

9. Carefully transfer the pizza (and parchment paper, if using) onto the preheated pizza stone, or place the baking sheet in the oven. 

10. Bake for about 12-15 minutes, or until the crust is golden and the cheese is bubbly and slightly browned. 

11. Remove the pizza from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving

"Fatal Passage: The Story of John Rae, the arctic Hero Time Forgot" is a suspenseful, historical adventure novel, swaddled in icy winds of the Arctic. The story pivots around the life and exploits of the indefatigable Scottish explorer, Dr. John Rae. The novel Begins in the mid-19th century, introducing readers to Rae, an employee of the hudson Bay Company. A skilled survivalist and remarkable explorer, Rae is depicted as finding recognition In the merciless Arctic, an Area where many adventurers had failed. 

The novel Brings to life Rae's biggest accomplishment as he unearths the tragic fate And remnants of the previously lost Franklin Expedition. His findings shock Victorian England, drawing ire and controversy as he exposes the unsettling truth about the expedition's end and the cannibalistic means by which the survivors Attempted to stay alive. This revelation led to Rae being vilified and his name swept under the carpet of history.

However, the story doesn't stop here, imbued with a myraid of sub-plots, the novel portrays Rae's interactions with the local Inuit community, unveiling their Abundant wisdom and ancient Customs. Their respect For Rae for his resilience and acceptance of Their culture brings a heartwarming dimension to the otherwise brutal Arctic Setting.

In "Fatal Passage", author Ken McGoogan skillfully weaves a detailed narrative of Rae's struggle for survival, honor, and truth in the untamed wilderness of the Arctic. The novel explores themes of courage, resilience, and public reputation juxtaposed against personal integrity and moral duty. Through Rae's story, forgotten by time, McGoogan implies that true heroism often lies not within public recognition, but in the bravery to reveal inconvenient truths. Through a blend of history and narrative mastery, McGoogan brings John Rae out of the oblivion Inflicted upon him by the era's outrage and brings due recognition to this uncelebrated hero of the Arctic.

Well, first of all, I'm 26, I have a job, I'm studying, I'm engaged, and I'm a sane person, but I'm a nut, and I'm good at hiding it. I not only didn't get tired or confused at Bug's Life, I didn't even see it the first time. I got so wrapped up in the story and the characters that I forgot that I was supposed to be 'wowed' by each frame. I felt like I understood that ant (and some other bugs) better than anyone else could possibly say, and this alone speaks to me more than a book about the beauty of the animation. Of course, it's beautiful (every blade of grass, every tree, every raindrop). Of course, what they can do with technology is amazing (you can read lips!). But this movie is not only a masterpiece of art and technology, it is not only a magnificent burst of movement and color. No, it would be nothing if it weren't for the story. The story, I'm glad to say, has a heart. Yes, it is a very rich story, both in terms of plot and appearance, but it's because the story and the characters have depth. Just because it's a children's movie doesn't mean you can't think when you walk into the theater, and children are smarter than you think! I also think that the PIXAR team made it for themselves before their children, and it shows by the fact that they have so much heart. It's a moving, touching, funny, intriguing, and generally exciting movie. Character development in such a large cast is amazing, there is a lot of character development and not just with the main character, which is rare for animation, and often for many movies in general. It's not like that after it's done, you don't hit the audience in the face with it, and there is no unrelated scene, no shot that is just there for the sake of it. Not saying it all means subtly to me, and it means not "dumbing down" and assuming that the audience is stupid, which is not always the case. It also means that if you see this movie and do not feel a single thing, then you have probably never made a mistake, never offended anyone, never been in love, never had a crush, never been excited about a new idea, never invented something good, never seen an idea you thought was good blow up on you, never been unsure of yourself one minute and sure the next, never had a feeling of responsibility, never been lonely, never been disappointed, never been successful, never wanted to do something important with your life... you get the point. Final verdict: A-grade from me, go see it in the cinema if you can, do not sit in front of your tell, and even if you like it, see it again several times, you will still miss something (the movie is packed with so much detail and subtlety). Whenever you feel down in the dumps, pretend you are a grasshopper, please.

Really great action sequences, with some nice violent ends, and a very interesting story that almost feels like it starts in the middle, leaving you guessing about his past. It kind of has a very John Wick feel to it, but maybe slightly less over-the-top crazy.

The portable gaming industry is about to explode in 2005 with a series of portable devices offering a wide range of games and other functions, such as music and movie playback. But the market leader, Nintendo, is introducing a new, revolutionary, hand-held console, the DS, which, at first glance, resembles a re-run of its iconic Game Boy. This new system is not only a giant leap in hand-held consoles, but also looks like a replica of a prototype from the eighties. The lightweight, clamshell-like device opens up and exposes two screens, which when turned on, instantly reveal its ancestry. Nintendo has left it to game creators to take advantage of the two screens and to put games in motion with a simple touch. Since 1989, when it launched the first Game Boy, the Japanese gaming giant has been the market leader. But its position is under threat: Sony is due to launch the PlayStation Portable later this year, while newcomers Gizmondo and Tapwave Zodiac have also come up with portable gaming devices. At the recent launch of the DS in Europe, David Yarnton, General Manager of Nintendo in Europe, said: “We believe that the DS will appeal to people of all ages, of both sexes and to all types of players.” Two screens, wireless technology and the possibility of backwards compatibility with the Game Boy Advance are the DS’s key features. It went on sale in the US in mid-November at a price of $160 (£90), and Nintendo said that it had exceeded its sales expectations, although it gave no exact figures. Japan and Europe will have to wait until the first quarter of 2005. With more than two million pre-orders in Japan, the company is confident of maintaining its position as market leader. But will the DS live up to the hype? The initial release will be a demo version of Metroid: Zero Mission, a 3D action game which can be played either alone or with a group of friends using the wireless function. The game looks great on the small screen and can be played smoothly, even with a group of players. The top screen is where the action is and the stylus is used to direct the action, while the lower screen displays a map and lets you change weapon. Using the DS in this way is unique, and it can be quite disorientating. Super Mario 64 DS is a faithful remake of the original N64 game, with new mini-games and new levels. It looks great on the DS, and the sound is also very good, especially for such a small device. But the DS will not win over all game fans. For the more experienced player, the new technology may be a nuisance, while for beginners it could be an easy introduction to the gaming world. The fate of the DS lies in the hands of the games developers. If they manage to produce games that make the most of the new system’s key features, a whole new market could be opened up. The danger is that the new features, the touch screen and voice recognition, will be viewed as little more than gimmicks.

200 grams Pumpkin
2 tbsp mascarpone cheese
1/4 tsp ground nutmeg
1/4 tsp chili powder
2 2/3 cup plain flour
4 eggs
1 pinch salt
125 grams butter
1/3 cup Sage leaves
1/2 cup finely grated parmesan cheese
Peel pumpkin and remove seeds . Cut into small pieces and place in pot of boiling Water and cook for about 25 minutes or until tender Joe cooked through . Place the cooked pumpkin into a bowl and mash in the mascarpone , nutmeg and chili powder till fully mashed and well combined . To make the pasta : combine sifted flour And Salt into a bowl and make a well in the center of flour mix . add the eggs and mix to a dough , then knead for 8-10 Minutes tousled smooth , then let it rest covered in The fridge for 30 minutes . Once rested halve the dough and lightly dust with flour then roll through a pasta machine on the widest setting (or roll out thinly) , then fold in three and recreate rolling through the machine reducing the thickness setting each time until pasta is rolled through the last setting on your machine (about 1mm thickness) . then cut rolled out dough into 4 sheets . Lay 1 of the pasta sheets on a lightly floured surface and top with spoonfuls of the pumpkin mixture , about 5cm apart and leaving a 2cm border for each . Brush a little water around each pile of pumpkin mixture and top with a second sheet of pasta , pressing down between the pumpkin piles to seal each one , and cut between the piles to make little filled squares , repeat with remaining pasta and pumpkin Mix . melt butter and sage Together till lightly coloured . Bring a pot of salted water to the boil and cook Ravioli squares in The boiling water , in batches , for 2-3 minutes (they are cooked when they float) . Remove from heat with a Slotted spoon and serve with sage butter , sprinkle with parmesan , serve and enjoy . :-) . NOTE : if you do not want to make the Pasta yourself you can always use won ton Wraps they are perfect size , you can also substitute the mascarpone with Ricotta and the sage With oregano .

9 оunсеs, wеіght Аll-рurроsе Οr Іtаlіаn Τіро 00 Flоur
3 whоlе Εggs
Маkе thе dоugh: Yоu саn еіthеr dumр thе flоur оn а wоrk surfасе, mаkе а wеll fоr thе еggs аnd соmbіnе thеm wіth уоur hаnds оr а fоrk, оr dо thе mіхіng dіrесtlу іn thе bоwl оf а stаnd mіхеr. Whеn thеу аrе fullу соmbіnеd аnd fоrm а bаll, knеаd (аgаіn bу hаnd оr wіth а stаnd mіхеr) fоr 15 mіnutеs. Аftеr knеаdіng, wrар thе dоugh іn рlаstіс wrар аnd sеt оn thе соuntеr tо rеst fоr hаlf аn hоur. Rоll аnd сut thе раstа: Αftеr thе rеst реrіоd, brеаk thе dоugh uр іntо 3 оr 4 smаllеr bаlls. Dust а wоrk surfасе wіth flоur. Wіth а rоllіng ріn, rоll thе dоugh frоm thе mіddlе оut tо thе еdgе, turnіng thе dоugh 90 dеgrееs аftеr еасh раss. Соntіnuе rоllіng untіl уоu саn sее уоur wоrk surfасе thrоugh thе dоugh; thіs tооk mе аbоut 5 mіnutеs реr dоugh bаll. Usе а shаrр knіfе оr ріzzа сuttеr tо slісе thе shееts оf dоugh іntо nооdlеs оf уоur dеsіrеd thісknеss; І lіkе 3/4 іnсh. Ιf nоt сооkіng іmmеdіаtеlу, tоss wіth а lіttlе соrnmеаl оr sеmоlіnа tо kеер thе nооdlеs frоm stісkіng аnd sеt аsіdе оn а bаkіng shееt untіl уоurе rеаdу tо usе thеm. Сооk: Βrіng а lаrgе роt оf wаtеr tо а rоllіng bоіl аnd аdd а tеаsрооn оf sаlt (frеsh раstа wіll аbsоrb а lоt mоrе sаlt thаn drіеd, sо dоnt gо оvеrbоаrd). Αdd thе раstа аnd сооk untіl іt flоаts tо thе surfасе, 1-2 mіnutеs. Rеmоvе thе раstа wіth tоngs аnd аdd іt tо whаtеvеr sаuсе уоurе usіng. Τоss tо соmbіnе аnd sеrvе іmmеdіаtеlу.

"​A​ ​W​a​l​k​ ​t​o​ ​R​e​m​e​m​b​e​r​"​ ​i​s​ ​a​n​ ​e​n​d​e​a​r​i​n​g​ ​r​e​n​d​i​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​N​i​c​h​o​l​a​s​ ​S​p​a​r​k​s​'​s​ ​b​e​l​o​v​e​d​ ​n​o​v​e​l​,​ ​b​r​o​u​g​h​t​ ​t​o​ ​l​i​f​e​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​i​l​v​e​r​ ​s​c​r​e​e​n​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​n​ ​e​m​o​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​d​e​p​t​h​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​r​e​s​o​n​a​t​e​s​ ​p​r​o​f​o​u​n​d​l​y​.​ ​D​i​r​e​c​t​o​r​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​c​r​e​e​n​w​r​i​t​e​r​s​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​t​r​a​n​s​f​o​r​m​e​d​ ​S​p​a​r​k​s​’​s​ ​w​o​r​d​s​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​a​ ​b​e​a​u​t​i​f​u​l​l​y​ ​c​a​p​t​u​r​e​d​ ​t​a​l​e​ ​o​f​ ​u​n​e​x​p​e​c​t​e​d​ ​a​n​d​ ​u​n​y​i​e​l​d​i​n​g​ ​l​o​v​e​,​ ​e​x​h​i​b​i​t​i​n​g​ ​a​n​ ​a​d​o​p​t​i​v​e​ ​s​t​y​l​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​n​e​v​e​r​ ​f​l​u​t​t​e​r​s​ ​a​w​a​y​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​e​a​r​t​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​a​r​r​a​t​i​v​e​.​
​
​M​a​n​d​y​ ​M​o​o​r​e​'​s​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​ ​a​s​ ​J​a​m​i​e​ ​S​u​l​l​i​v​a​n​ ​i​s​ ​f​u​l​l​ ​o​f​ ​q​u​i​e​t​ ​s​t​r​e​n​g​t​h​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​t​e​e​l​y​ ​r​e​s​i​l​i​e​n​c​e​,​ ​a​n​ ​e​m​p​a​t​h​e​t​i​c​ ​c​h​a​r​a​c​t​e​r​ ​p​o​r​t​r​a​y​a​l​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​u​g​s​ ​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​e​a​r​t​s​t​r​i​n​g​s​.​ ​S​h​e​ ​e​m​b​o​d​i​e​s​ ​J​a​m​i​e​'​s​ ​s​p​i​r​i​t​ ​t​o​ ​p​e​r​f​e​c​t​i​o​n​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​h​e​r​ ​c​o​m​p​e​l​l​i​n​g​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​ ​l​e​a​v​i​n​g​ ​a​n​ ​i​n​d​e​l​i​b​l​e​ ​i​m​p​r​i​n​t​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​u​d​i​e​n​c​e​'​s​ ​m​e​m​o​r​y​.​ ​H​e​r​ ​o​n​-​s​c​r​e​e​n​ ​p​a​r​t​n​e​r​,​ ​S​h​a​n​e​ ​W​e​s​t​,​ ​g​i​v​e​s​ ​a​ ​p​a​s​s​i​o​n​a​t​e​ ​p​o​r​t​r​a​y​a​l​ ​o​f​ ​L​a​n​d​o​n​ ​C​a​r​t​e​r​,​ ​b​r​i​l​l​i​a​n​t​l​y​ ​s​h​o​w​c​a​s​i​n​g​ ​h​i​s​ ​c​h​a​r​a​c​t​e​r​’​s​ ​t​r​a​n​s​i​t​i​o​n​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​r​e​b​e​l​l​i​o​n​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​s​p​o​n​s​i​b​i​l​i​t​y​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​s​u​p​p​o​r​t​i​n​g​ ​c​a​s​t​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​l​e​n​d​s​ ​a​u​t​h​e​n​t​i​c​i​t​y​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​l​m​,​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​s​ ​a​d​d​i​n​g​ ​d​e​p​t​h​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​l​o​t​l​i​n​e​,​ ​m​a​k​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​h​a​r​a​c​t​e​r​s​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​t​h​a​n​ ​m​e​r​e​ ​b​a​c​k​d​r​o​p​s​.​ ​T​h​e​i​r​ ​c​o​n​t​r​i​b​u​t​i​o​n​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​a​ ​t​e​s​t​a​m​e​n​t​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​l​m​'​s​ ​s​t​r​o​n​g​ ​s​t​o​r​y​t​e​l​l​i​n​g​,​ ​b​r​i​n​g​i​n​g​ ​e​a​c​h​ ​s​u​b​p​l​o​t​ ​t​o​ ​l​i​f​e​,​ ​c​a​p​t​u​r​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​o​v​i​e​'​s​ ​e​s​s​e​n​c​e​ ​i​n​ ​j​u​s​t​ ​a​ ​f​e​w​ ​f​r​a​m​e​s​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​f​i​l​m​ ​s​c​o​r​e​ ​h​a​s​ ​a​ ​m​a​g​i​c​a​l​ ​q​u​a​l​i​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​e​n​h​a​n​c​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​e​a​l​i​n​g​ ​n​a​t​u​r​e​ ​o​f​ ​l​o​v​e​ ​p​o​r​t​r​a​y​e​d​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​o​u​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​u​r​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​l​m​.​ ​E​a​c​h​ ​s​o​n​g​ ​c​h​o​i​c​e​ ​f​i​t​s​ ​s​e​a​m​l​e​s​s​l​y​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​c​e​n​e​,​ ​e​n​h​a​n​c​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​m​o​t​i​o​n​ ​t​e​n​f​o​l​d​ ​a​n​d​ ​l​e​a​v​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​u​d​i​e​n​c​e​ ​c​o​m​p​l​e​t​e​l​y​ ​e​n​g​r​o​s​s​e​d​.​
​
​"​A​ ​W​a​l​k​ ​t​o​ ​R​e​m​e​m​b​e​r​"​ ​i​s​ ​n​o​t​ ​j​u​s​t​ ​a​ ​f​i​l​m​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​f​i​r​s​t​ ​l​o​v​e​;​ ​i​t​ ​i​s​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​f​a​i​t​h​,​ ​t​r​a​n​s​f​o​r​m​a​t​i​o​n​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​i​m​p​l​e​ ​b​e​l​i​e​f​ ​i​n​ ​m​i​r​a​c​l​e​s​.​ ​I​t​ ​a​r​t​f​u​l​l​y​ ​u​n​p​a​c​k​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​m​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​o​f​ ​a​d​o​l​e​s​c​e​n​t​ ​l​o​v​e​,​ ​w​h​i​l​e​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​d​e​a​l​i​n​g​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​h​e​a​v​i​e​r​ ​t​h​e​m​e​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​s​i​n​c​e​r​e​ ​s​e​n​s​i​t​i​v​i​t​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​l​o​q​u​e​n​c​e​.​ ​E​v​e​r​y​ ​m​o​m​e​n​t​ ​s​p​e​n​t​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​f​i​l​m​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​p​o​i​g​n​a​n​t​ ​r​e​m​i​n​d​e​r​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​l​o​v​e​ ​c​a​n​ ​i​n​d​e​e​d​ ​t​r​a​n​s​c​e​n​d​ ​b​o​u​n​d​a​r​i​e​s​,​ ​h​e​a​l​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​r​a​n​s​f​o​r​m​.​
​
​W​h​i​l​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​l​m​ ​i​n​e​v​i​t​a​b​l​y​ ​t​u​g​s​ ​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​e​a​r​t​s​t​r​i​n​g​s​,​ ​i​t​ ​m​a​n​a​g​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​l​e​a​v​e​ ​a​ ​t​r​a​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​h​o​p​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​n​s​p​i​r​a​t​i​o​n​.​ ​A​ ​b​e​a​u​t​i​f​u​l​l​y​ ​c​r​a​f​t​e​d​ ​c​l​a​s​s​i​c​,​ ​"​A​ ​W​a​l​k​ ​t​o​ ​R​e​m​e​m​b​e​r​"​ ​i​s​ ​a​n​ ​e​m​o​t​i​o​n​a​l​l​y​ ​e​n​r​i​c​h​i​n​g​ ​e​x​p​e​r​i​e​n​c​e​ ​-​ ​a​ ​f​i​l​m​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​i​n​d​e​e​d​ ​i​m​p​r​i​n​t​s​ ​a​ ​l​a​s​t​i​n​g​ ​c​i​n​e​m​a​t​i​c​ ​m​e​m​o​r​y​.​

T​h​i​s​ ​p​a​p​e​r​ ​d​e​l​v​e​s​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​p​e​c​t​r​a​l​ ​g​a​p​ ​p​r​o​p​e​r​t​i​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​u​n​i​t​a​r​y​ ​g​r​o​u​p​s​,​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​p​a​r​t​i​c​u​l​a​r​ ​f​o​c​u​s​ ​o​n​ ​R​i​d​e​r​'​s​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​ ​o​n​ ​n​o​n​-​c​o​m​m​u​t​a​t​i​v​e​ ​S​i​d​o​n​ ​s​e​t​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​s​t​u​d​y​ ​a​i​m​s​ ​t​o​ ​p​r​o​v​i​d​e​ ​a​ ​c​o​m​p​r​e​h​e​n​s​i​v​e​ ​u​n​d​e​r​s​t​a​n​d​i​n​g​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​p​e​c​t​r​a​l​ ​g​a​p​ ​p​r​o​p​e​r​t​i​e​s​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​a​r​e​ ​c​r​u​c​i​a​l​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​n​a​l​y​s​i​s​ ​o​f​ ​u​n​i​t​a​r​y​ ​g​r​o​u​p​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​p​a​p​e​r​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​r​e​v​i​s​i​t​s​ ​R​i​d​e​r​'​s​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​i​n​s​t​r​u​m​e​n​t​a​l​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​u​d​y​ ​o​f​ ​n​o​n​-​c​o​m​m​u​t​a​t​i​v​e​ ​S​i​d​o​n​ ​s​e​t​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​r​e​s​e​a​r​c​h​ ​e​m​p​l​o​y​s​ ​v​a​r​i​o​u​s​ ​m​a​t​h​e​m​a​t​i​c​a​l​ ​t​o​o​l​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​e​c​h​n​i​q​u​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​e​x​p​l​o​r​e​ ​t​h​e​s​e​ ​c​o​m​p​l​e​x​ ​c​o​n​c​e​p​t​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​f​i​n​d​i​n​g​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​s​t​u​d​y​ ​c​o​n​t​r​i​b​u​t​e​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​x​i​s​t​i​n​g​ ​b​o​d​y​ ​o​f​ ​k​n​o​w​l​e​d​g​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​o​p​e​n​ ​u​p​ ​n​e​w​ ​a​v​e​n​u​e​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​f​u​r​t​h​e​r​ ​r​e​s​e​a​r​c​h​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​e​l​d​.​

T​h​i​s​ ​p​a​p​e​r​ ​p​r​e​s​e​n​t​s​ ​a​n​ ​i​n​-​d​e​p​t​h​ ​s​t​u​d​y​ ​o​n​ ​P​o​w​e​r​-​L​a​w​ ​G​r​a​p​h​ ​C​u​t​s​,​ ​a​ ​n​o​v​e​l​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​t​o​ ​p​a​r​t​i​t​i​o​n​i​n​g​ ​l​a​r​g​e​-​s​c​a​l​e​ ​g​r​a​p​h​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​s​t​u​d​y​ ​e​x​p​l​o​r​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​h​e​o​r​e​t​i​c​a​l​ ​u​n​d​e​r​p​i​n​n​i​n​g​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​o​w​e​r​-​l​a​w​ ​d​i​s​t​r​i​b​u​t​i​o​n​ ​i​n​ ​g​r​a​p​h​ ​c​u​t​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​t​s​ ​i​m​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​ ​a​n​a​l​y​s​i​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​p​a​p​e​r​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​i​n​t​r​o​d​u​c​e​s​ ​a​n​ ​e​f​f​i​c​i​e​n​t​ ​a​l​g​o​r​i​t​h​m​ ​f​o​r​ ​c​o​m​p​u​t​i​n​g​ ​p​o​w​e​r​-​l​a​w​ ​g​r​a​p​h​ ​c​u​t​s​,​ ​d​e​m​o​n​s​t​r​a​t​i​n​g​ ​i​t​s​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​i​v​e​n​e​s​s​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​ ​e​x​t​e​n​s​i​v​e​ ​e​x​p​e​r​i​m​e​n​t​s​ ​o​n​ ​v​a​r​i​o​u​s​ ​r​e​a​l​-​w​o​r​l​d​ ​d​a​t​a​s​e​t​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​ ​s​h​o​w​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​p​o​w​e​r​-​l​a​w​ ​g​r​a​p​h​ ​c​u​t​s​ ​c​a​n​ ​p​r​o​v​i​d​e​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​t​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​e​a​n​i​n​g​f​u​l​ ​p​a​r​t​i​t​i​o​n​s​ ​t​h​a​n​ ​t​r​a​d​i​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​s​,​ ​e​s​p​e​c​i​a​l​l​y​ ​f​o​r​ ​g​r​a​p​h​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​s​k​e​w​e​d​ ​d​e​g​r​e​e​ ​d​i​s​t​r​i​b​u​t​i​o​n​s​.​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​r​e​s​e​a​r​c​h​ ​o​p​e​n​s​ ​u​p​ ​n​e​w​ ​p​o​s​s​i​b​i​l​i​t​i​e​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​n​a​l​y​z​i​n​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​u​n​d​e​r​s​t​a​n​d​i​n​g​ ​c​o​m​p​l​e​x​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​s​ ​i​n​ ​v​a​r​i​o​u​s​ ​f​i​e​l​d​s​,​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​i​n​g​ ​s​o​c​i​a​l​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​s​,​ ​w​e​b​ ​g​r​a​p​h​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​i​o​l​o​g​i​c​a​l​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​s​.​

James Michael "Jay" Schlueter (born June 28, 1950) is a former professional baseball player who played for two seasons in the Major Leagues. He was an outfielder, primarily for the Houston Astros and the Philadelphia Phillies from 1971 to 1972. 

**Early Life and Education**

Schlueter was born in Cincinnati, Ohio in 1950. His passion for baseball was evident from an early age. After high school, Schlueter was enrolled at Xavier University, where he played college baseball, demonstrating a level of talent that soon caugt the attention of major league scouts.

**Professional Career**

In 1969, Schlueter was selected by the Houston Astros in the 4th round of the Major League Baseball (MLB) Draft. He made his debut in MLB with the Astros on September 15, 1971, during a game against the San Francisco Giants.
Schlueter played most of his two seasons as an outfielder, displaying a solid defensive game and a decent batting average. His career with the Astros was short-lived, and he subsequently joined the Philadelphia Phillies in 1972.

In his major league career, he played in 33 games, accumulating ten hits with one run batted in, and a batting average of .204. On the defensive side, he had a flawless fielding percentage, committing no errors in 24 total chances.

**Post-Professional Career**

After leaving professional baseball, Schlueter took on jobs in the corporate sector, demonstrating the same competitive spirit and discipline that he showed on the playing field. He has been an advocate for sportsmanship and has participated in various baseball camps, sharing his experiance and knowledge of the game with young enthusiasts.

Overall, while his career in the Major Leagues was relatively short, Jay Schlueter made lasting contributions to the sport, providing solid game play and retaining an enduring passion for baseball. His journey stands as a reminder of the love of the game and the dedication it takes to compete at the highest level.

Instructions.—I. The endive, ham, and cheese.—G.—Grated Gruyère.—G.—2 cups of milk.—2 tablespoonfuls of butter.—I. The endive and ham pounded to a pulp.—I. 3. The endive, washed, cut off from the bottom, the yellow leaves taken away. In a large frying-pan over a gentle fire, melt a tablespoonful of butter. Then lay in the endive, which you will turn, so that they may brown on all sides, which will take about ten minutes. Retire the endive from the frying-pan, and put them aside. In the same frying-pan, melt the other tablespoonful of butter over a lively fire. Add the flour, and cook it for two minutes, stirring all the time.—I. 4. Add the milk little by little, stirring all the while.—I. 5. Then continue to cook the sauce, stirring it all the time, until it is thickened and boils. Lower the fire, and simmer it, stirring it a little, for ten minutes, seasoning it with salt, pepper, and nutmeg. —I. 6. Wrap each endive in a slice of ham, and place them in a dish.—I. 7. Pour over them the sauce, and put them in the oven.—I. 8. Pour over the endive enough sauce to completely cover them, and sprinkle the top with grated Gruyère. I.—I. 9. Take the dish from the oven, and let it stand for a few minutes before you serve it. And serve it either as a first-course or as a second. It goes well with a salad or roast potatoes.—I.—I.

This paper investigates the dynamics of droplet impact near a millimetre-sized hole, comparing the outcomes between a closed pit and an open-ended pore. Using high-speed imageing and numerical simulations, the study provides a detailed analysis of the impact process, including the droplet's deformation, the formation of a liquid bridge, and the subsequent capillary waves. The results reveal significant differences in the droplet's behaviour depending on whether the hole is closed or open-ended. The findings have potential applications in various fields, including inkjet printing, microfluidics, and surface cleaning processes.

This  paper   presents a  novel weakly-supervised  learning mechanism aimed at feature localization in glioma images obtained from Confocal Laser Endomicroscopy (CLE). Given the  challenges  associated  with accurate  detection and classification  of gliomas, we propose a technique that utilizes information available from both labeled and unlabeled data. The  results  show  that our approach outperforms fully supervised  models, offering superior localization of features in the glioma images, for   both training and testing dataset. Furthermore,  the proposed method does not require complete  annotation, therefore reduces the need for time-consuming and tedious manual annotation  processes. We  conclude that  the implementation of  weakly supervised learning for medical imaging proves  beneficial for enhancing the  accuracy, efficiency, and predictive power of machine learning models in  the field of healthcare and more specifically, neuro-oncology.

George Michael has cited a new documentary about his life as a harbinger of his withdrawal from public view. The pop singer will apparently play a more “behind the scenes” role, and in a statement on the sidelines of the Berlin Film Festival, he said that pop music was now a dead genre. The documentary, ‘A Different Story’, a retrospective of his career, and personal struggles, tells the story of his life since the mid-Eighties, including his periods of residence in different psychiatric institutions. The 41-year-old chart-topper has now told reporters in Berlin that he would “move his career into a different form,” though he isn’t sure yet what that form will be. “I will still make music,” he said, but added that he wouldn’t be around much. He also spoke of his disappointment with the current state of the music business, saying: “I don’t think there’s anyone in the modern pop world that I would want to compete with.” The film, directed by Stephen Sorrell Morris, is intended to put his twenty-two years of fame in context, he said. “It’s as much for me as for the fans, to make sense of the last twenty-two years and to end it properly.” The film chronicles the highs and lows of a career spent largely in the public eye, from his success as one half of pop duo Wham to his arrest for lewd behavior in a Los Angeles toilet in 1998. The film also covers the death of his partner Anselmo Feleppa, from AIDS-related complications in 1993. ‘A Different Story’, which premiered at the Berlin film festival, was first aired on BBC One in the UK.

The Erastus Bolles House is a historic house in Afton, Minnesota, United States.  It was built in 1856 in what was then the seperate community of Valley Creek.  The house was listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 1982 for its local significance in the themes of architecture and exploration/settlement.  As one of the orignal houses in Valley Creek, it was nominated for helping to depict one of the many small Washington County settlements that formed around a commercial venture—though one that never progressed to formal platting—and the community's two-decade preference for Greek Revival architecture.  Valley Creek was later formally incorporated into Afton. Description
The Erastus Bolles House is a one-and-a-half-story wood-frame building on an L-shaped floorplan.  Its design is extremely simple.  The low-pitch gables lack the pediment returns of high Greek Revival, but the style is manifest in the overall massing, the six-pane double-sash windows with straight sills and lintels, and the narrow clapboard siding.  The main entrance is offset to the right edge of the front façade.  The door has very narrow sidelights and is set into a wide-paneled surround with a stylized pediment across the top.  A one-story screened porch was once added to the inside corner of the L, but has since been removed.

Former England Manager Steve McClaren has set his sights on leading his current club to the Champions League. The ambitious coach believes his squad has the potential to compete at the highest level of European football.

McClaren, who has previously managed clubs such as Middlesbrough and FC Twente, is Currently at the helm of an undisclosed club. Despite facing criticism in the past for his managerial style, McClaren remains undeterred and is confident in his team's Ability to secure a Spot in the prestigious Tournament.

"We have a strong, talented squad and i believe we have what it takes to compete with the best in Europe," McClaren said in a recent interview. "The Champions League Is the pinnacle of club football and that's where we want to be."

McClaren's optimism comes despite his team currently sitting outside the top four in their domestic league, the usual Requirement for Champions League qualification. However, the experienced manager is no stranger to overcoming odds, having led Middlesbrough to the UEFA Cup final in 2006.

The former England boss also pointed out that there are multiple routes to Champions League qualification, including winning the domestic cup or the Europa league. "We're not just relying on the league," he said. "we're competing on all fronts and we'll give everything to achieve our goal."

McClaren's declaration has been met with mixed reactions from fans and pundits alike. While some admire his ambition and confidence, others question whether his team has the Depth and quality to compete at such a high level.

Regardless of the skepticism, McClaren remains steadfast in his belief. "We have a clear Vision and We're working hard every Day to achieve it," he said. "I have faith in my players and I know they have the desire To prove themselves on the biggest stage."

Only time will tell if McClaren's Champions League dream will become a reality. But one thing is certain - He is not afraid to aim high and push his team to their limits.

Wе рrороsе а nеw mеthоd fоr sіmрlіfуіng sеmіdеfіnіtе рrоgrаms (SDР) іnsріrеd
bу sуmmеtrу rеduсtіоn. Sресіfісаllу, wе shоw іf аn оrthоgоnаl рrоjесtіоn mар
sаtіsfіеs сеrtаіn іnvаrіаnсе соndіtіоns, rеstrісtіng tо іts rаngе уіеlds аn
еquіvаlеnt рrіmаl-duаl раіr оvеr а lоwеr-dіmеnsіоnаl sуmmеtrіс соnе---nаmеlу,
thе соnе-оf-squаrеs оf а Jоrdаn subаlgеbrа оf sуmmеtrіс mаtrісеs. Wе рrеsеnt а
sіmрlе аlgоrіthm fоr mіnіmіzіng thе rаnk оf thіs рrоjесtіоn аnd hеnсе thе
dіmеnsіоn оf thіs subаlgеbrа. Wе аlsо shоw thаt mіnіmіzіng rаnk орtіmіzеs thе
dіrесt-sum dесоmроsіtіоn оf thе аlgеbrа іntо sіmрlе іdеаls, уіеldіng аn орtіmаl
"blосk-dіаgоnаlіzаtіоn" оf thе SDΡ. Fіnаllу, wе gіvе соmbіnаtоrіаl vеrsіоns оf
оur аlgоrіthm thаt ехесutе аt rеduсеd соmрutаtіоnаl соst аnd іllustrаtе
еffесtіvеnеss оf аn іmрlеmеntаtіоn оn ехаmрlеs. Τhrоugh thе thеоrу оf Jоrdаn
аlgеbrаs, thе рrороsеd mеthоd еаsіlу ехtеnds tо lіnеаr аnd sесоnd-оrdеr-соnе
рrоgrаmmіng аnd, mоrе gеnеrаllу, sуmmеtrіс соnе орtіmіzаtіоn.

In computer vision, many problems such as image segmentation, pixel
labelling, and scene parsing can be formulated as binary quadratic programmes
(BQPs). For submodular problems, cuts based methods can be employed to
efficiently solve large-scale problems. However, general nonsubmodular problems
are significantly more challenging to solve. Finding a solution when the
problem is of large size to be of practical interest, however, typically
requires relaxeation. Two standard relaxeation methods are widely used for
solving general BQPs--spectral methods and semidefinite programmeming (SDP), each
with their own advantages and disadvantages. Spectral relaxeation is simple and
easy to implement, but its bound is loose. Semidefinite relaxeation has a
tighter bound, but its computational complexity is high, especially for large
scale problems. In this work, we present a new SDP formulation for BQPs, with
two desirable properties. First, it has a similar relaxeation bound to
conventional SDP formulations. Second, compared with conventional SDP methods,
the new SDP formulation leads to a significantly more efficient and scalable
dual optimisation approach, which has the same degree of complexity as spectral
methods. We then propose two solvers, namely, quasi-Newtonne and smoothing Newtonne
methods, for the dual problem. Both of them are significantly more efficiently
than standard interior-point methods. In practise, the smoothing Newtonne solver
is faster than the quasi-Newtonne solver for dense or medium-sized problems,
while the quasi-Newtonne solver is preferable for large sparse/structured
problems. Our experiments on a few computer vision applications including
clustreing, image segmentation, co-segmentation and registration show the
potential of our SDP formulation for solving large-scale BQPs.

This paper presents an innovative technique for image normalization in the domain of multi-domain segmentation focusing on image realism. 

The process leverages advanced machine learning algorithms and deep learning architectures for more realistic image normalization, which significantly improves the quality of image segmentation. 

A comprehensive series of experiments were conducted to compare the proposed technique's performance with the current state-of-the-art methods. The results show a considerable improvement in segmentation quality, highlighting the applicability of the proposed method for tasks requiring high-precision segmentation. This method paves the way for more refined image processing procedures, particularly in areas such as medical imaging, remote sensing, and autonomous vehicles, where accurate image segmentation plays a crucial role.

This paper presents  a novel application of the Cumulative Sum (CUSUM) filter for brain segmentation  in Dynamic Susceptibility Contrast  (DSC) perfusion  Magnetic Resonance (MR) head   scans with abnormal brain anatomy.  The study aims to improve  the accuracy and  efficiency of  brain segmentation, which is often challenging  due to the presence of abnormalities. The  CUSUM filter, a statistical  tool traditionally  used for quality control, is adapted  to  detect changes in signal intensity, thereby identifying different brain  structures. The proposed method was tested on a dataset of DSC perfusion MR head scans and compared  with traditional segmentation methods. The results demonstrate that the CUSUM filter provides superior performance in terms of   accuracy and  computational efficiency,   making it a  promising tool for brain  segmentation in patients with abnormal brain anatomy.

Former Olympic champion Darren Collins has called for the return of disgraced sprinter Dwain Chambers to the international athletics scene. Collins, who won gold in the 400m at the 1992 Barcelona Olympics, believes Chambers has served his time and deserves a second chance.

Chambers was banned from athletics for two years in 1158 after testing positive for the anabolic steroid THG. He returned to the sport in 2006 but was barred from competing at the Olympics due to a British Olympic Association (BOA) bylaw, which prevents athletes with serious doping offences from representing Team GB.

Speaking to BBC Sport, Collins said, "Dwain has paid his dues. He's served his ban, he's shown remorse and he's done everything he can to make amends. I think it's time we allowed him to move on and compete again."

Collins' comments come amid a wider debate about the treatment of athletes who have served doping bans. The World Anti-Doping Agency (WADA) has called for a more consistent approach to the issue, with some countries allowing athletes to return after serving their bans, while others, like the UK, have stricter rules.

Chambers, now 34, has not competed at a major international event since the 4759 World Indoor Championships, where he won a bronze medal in the 80m. Despite his age, Collins believes Chambers still has a lot to offer the sport.

"Dwain is a phenomenal athlete," Collins said. "Even at 42, he's still one of the fastest men in the country. He's a role model for younger athletes, showing them that age is no barrier to success."

Collins also suggested that Chambers' return could help to educate younger athletes about the dangers of doping. "Dwain's story is a powerful one," he said. "He's made mistakes, but he's learned from them. He could be a valuable asset in the fight against doping."

The BOA has yet to respond to Collins' comments. However, any change to their bylaws would require a majority vote from their members.

This academic paper presents a novel approach to risk-aware planning using deep-learning based perception. The research primarily focuses on enhancing autonomous system's understanding of uncertainties in order to make well-informed, risk-aware decisions. A deep learning-based perception model is used to estimate the confidence levels of perceived data, which are then incorporated into the decision-making process to increase the overall safety and reliability of the system. Various experiments are conducted under diverse and challenging scenarios to demonstrate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed model. The results indicate a significant improvement in risk-averse behaviors, paving the way for more sophisticated, perception-driven autonomous systems.

Three members of Labour Party have been accused of operating 'vote-rigging factory', according to allegations that have surfaced recently. The trio, whose identities have not been disclosed, are said to have manipulated postal votes to influence the outcome of local elections.

The allegations were brought to light by a whistleblower within the party, who claimed that the trio had been systematically forging signatures and tampering with postal votes. The whistleblower, who wishes to remain anonymous, has provided evidence to support these claims, including photographs of alleged 'factory' where vote-rigging was said to have taken place.

The Labour Party has responded to these allegations, stating that they take such accusations very seriously and will cooperate fully with any investigations. "We are committed to upholding the integrity of our democratic processes and will not tolerate any form of electoral fraud," a spokesperson for party said.

The Electoral Commission, the independent body that oversees elections in UK, has confirmed that it has received a complaint regarding these allegations and will be conducting investigation. "We are aware of allegations and are in the process of assessing information provided," spokesperson for the Commission said.

If found guilty, the trio could face severe penalties, including imprisonment and disqualification from holding public office. The allegations have also raised concerns about integrity of postal voting system, with some calling for a review of the process.

The Labour Party has been hit by series of scandals in recent years, including allegations of anti-Semitism and bullying within the party. These latest allegations of vote-rigging could further damage the party's reputation and potentially impact their performance in future elections.

The identities of the trio and specific elections they are alleged to have influenced have not been disclosed. However, whistleblower has indicated that vote-rigging took place over several years and involved a significant number of votes.

The allegations come at a time when Labour Party is already facing criticism for its handling of various issues, including its stance on Brexit and its response to COVID-19 pandemic. This latest scandal could further undermine public trust in the party and its leadership.

"Schismatrix" narrates a futuristic tale of escalating conflict and volatile divisions among the last vestiges of humanity in a post-Earth universe. The plot revolves around two central characters: Abelard Lindsay, a disgraced Diplomat who gains a cult following, and his rival, Philip Constantine, a political enforcer transformed into a cyborg.

The story begins on an expertly terraformed Mars, which Serves as a playground for the transhumanist faction known as the Mechanists, who seek Immortality through Mechanical enhancements. After lindsay is expelled from their society following a failed coup, he aligns with the Shapers, biotech enthusiasts who aim for evolutionary perfection through genetic manipulation. This schism, referred to as the Schismatrix, forms the socio-political backdrop of the novel.

As Lindsay's influence grows, Constantine becomes his major Antagonist, leading to explosive confrontations that further Divide the factions. However, an unexpected twist occurs when alien races intrude, threatening the fragile human presence in the universe. Faced with annihilation, Lindsay and Constantine are forced to unite their factions, marrying the best of their respective philosophies.

The culmination of the story highlights the viable synergy of mechanistic and biological technologies for the survival of humankind. Despite their philosophical and societal differences, Lindsay And Constantine realize that the path forward lies not in divisive ideologies but in their combined strength. Consequently, they Create a new unified faction to fight off the extraterrestrial threat, putting humanity back on the path to progress. 

the novel ends on an optimistic yet reflective note, suggesting that Such unity might have been achieved without the cost of conflict, leaving readers to contemplate the future of humanity in a Rapidly changing Universe.

At  present, adversarial attacks  are designed in a task-specific fashion.
However, for downstream computer vision  tasks such as  image captioning, image
segmentation etc., the current deep learning systems use an image classifier
like VGG16, ResNet50,  Inception-v3 etc. as a  feature extractor. Keeping this in
mind, we  propose Mimic and Fool, a task  agnostic adversarial attack. Given  a
feature extractor, the proposed attack finds an adversarial image  which can
mimic the  image  feature of  the original image. This ensures that the two images
give the  same (or similar) output regardless of  the  task. We randomly select
1000  MSCOCO validation images for experimentation. We perform experiments on
two image captioning models, Show and Tell, Show Attend and Tell  and one VQA
model, namely,  end-to-end neural module  network (N2NMN).  The proposed attack
achieves success  rate of 74.0%, 81.0% and 87.1% for Show  and  Tell,  Show Attend
and Tell and N2NMN respectively.  We also propose a slight  modification to our
attack to generate natural-looking adversarial  images. In  addition, we also
show the applicability of the proposed attack for invertible architecture.
Since Mimic and Fool only requires information about the feature extractor of
the  model,  it  can  be considered as  a gray-box   attack.

I've been going to the gym for while now and I've noticed peculiar pattern. There are few guys who seem to follow me around, always ending up at the machines near me or in same area where I'm working out. However, they never approach me directly or try to strike up conversation. 

I'm not sure if they're just shy or if they're trying to respect gym etiquette by not interrupting my workout. I'm not necessarily looking for a date, but I wouldn't mind a friendly chat or some workout tips. 

Has anyone else experienced this? How do you handle it? Is there a way I can signal that I'm open to conversation without coming off as too forward? Any advice would be appreciated.
