​P​a​r​t​y​ ​l​e​a​d​e​r​ ​M​i​c​h​a​e​l​ ​H​o​w​a​r​d​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​e​w​ ​r​o​l​e​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​r​e​p​l​a​c​e​ ​"​i​n​c​o​n​s​p​i​c​u​o​u​s​"​ ​p​o​l​i​c​e​ ​a​u​t​h​o​r​i​t​i​e​s​.​ ​H​e​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​e​w​ ​o​f​f​i​c​e​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​n​o​t​ ​s​u​p​e​r​s​e​d​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​j​o​b​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​c​h​i​e​f​ ​c​o​n​s​t​a​b​l​e​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​L​i​b​ ​D​e​m​s​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​l​a​n​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​l​e​t​ ​e​x​t​r​e​m​e​ ​g​r​o​u​p​s​ ​r​u​n​ ​p​o​l​i​c​i​n​g​,​ ​w​h​i​l​e​ ​L​a​b​o​u​r​ ​c​r​i​t​i​c​i​s​e​d​ ​"​e​x​t​r​a​v​a​g​a​n​t​"​ ​T​o​r​y​ ​p​r​o​m​i​s​e​s​ ​o​n​ ​p​o​l​i​c​i​n​g​.​
​R​e​s​p​o​n​d​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​l​a​n​s​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​h​a​i​r​m​a​n​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​P​o​l​i​c​e​ ​F​e​d​e​r​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​E​n​g​l​a​n​d​ ​a​n​d​ ​W​a​l​e​s​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​r​e​p​r​e​s​e​n​t​s​ ​r​a​n​k​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​i​l​e​ ​o​f​f​i​c​e​r​s​,​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​i​t​ ​w​a​s​ ​e​s​s​e​n​t​i​a​l​ ​o​p​e​r​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​i​n​d​e​p​e​n​d​e​n​c​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​r​e​t​a​i​n​e​d​.​
​J​a​n​ ​B​e​r​r​y​ ​s​a​i​d​:​ ​"​I​t​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​s​e​r​v​i​c​e​,​ ​n​o​t​ ​a​ ​p​o​l​i​t​i​c​a​l​ ​f​o​o​t​b​a​l​l​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​k​i​c​k​e​d​ ​a​r​o​u​n​d​ ​e​v​e​r​y​ ​t​i​m​e​ ​a​n​ ​e​l​e​c​t​i​o​n​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​e​s​.​ ​"​T​h​e​s​e​ ​p​l​a​n​s​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​o​s​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​e​x​t​r​e​m​e​ ​p​o​l​i​t​i​c​a​l​ ​v​i​e​w​s​ ​d​i​c​t​a​t​i​n​g​ ​w​h​a​t​ ​a​c​t​u​a​l​l​y​ ​h​a​p​p​e​n​s​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​r​o​u​n​d​,​"​ ​s​h​e​ ​w​a​r​n​e​d​.​ ​O​u​t​l​i​n​i​n​g​ ​h​i​s​ ​c​r​i​m​e​ ​m​a​n​i​f​e​s​t​o​,​ ​M​r​ ​H​o​w​a​r​d​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​e​l​e​c​t​e​d​ ​p​o​l​i​c​e​ ​c​o​m​m​i​s​s​i​o​n​e​r​s​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​b​e​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​a​c​c​o​u​n​t​a​b​l​e​ ​t​h​a​n​ ​p​o​l​i​c​e​ ​a​u​t​h​o​r​i​t​i​e​s​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​a​r​e​ ​m​a​d​e​ ​u​p​ ​o​f​ ​l​o​c​a​l​ ​c​o​u​n​c​i​l​l​o​r​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​a​g​i​s​t​r​a​t​e​s​.​ ​"​T​h​e​ ​c​o​m​m​i​s​s​i​o​n​e​r​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​o​w​e​r​s​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​e​x​i​s​t​i​n​g​ ​p​o​l​i​c​e​ ​a​u​t​h​o​r​i​t​i​e​s​ ​h​a​v​e​,​"​ ​h​e​ ​t​o​l​d​ ​B​B​C​ ​R​a​d​i​o​ ​4​'​s​ ​T​o​d​a​y​ ​p​r​o​g​r​a​m​m​e​.​ ​"​T​h​e​ ​t​r​o​u​b​l​e​ ​i​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​t​'​s​ ​n​o​ ​r​e​f​l​e​c​t​i​o​n​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​e​o​p​l​e​ ​w​h​o​ ​a​r​e​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​o​l​i​c​e​ ​a​u​t​h​o​r​i​t​y​ ​-​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​a​r​e​ ​g​o​o​d​ ​p​e​o​p​l​e​ ​-​ ​b​u​t​ ​h​a​r​d​l​y​ ​a​n​y​o​n​e​ ​k​n​o​w​s​ ​w​h​o​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​a​r​e​.​"​ ​M​r​ ​H​o​w​a​r​d​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​u​t​h​o​r​i​t​i​e​s​ ​w​e​r​e​ ​n​o​t​ ​"​p​r​o​v​i​d​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​o​c​a​l​ ​a​c​c​o​u​n​t​a​b​i​l​i​t​y​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​w​e​ ​w​a​n​t​ ​t​o​ ​s​e​e​"​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​e​l​e​c​t​e​d​ ​p​o​l​i​c​e​ ​c​o​m​m​i​s​s​i​o​n​e​r​s​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​b​e​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​v​i​s​i​b​l​e​.​
​C​r​i​t​i​c​s​ ​f​e​a​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​o​v​e​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​h​a​n​d​ ​c​o​n​t​r​o​l​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​o​l​i​c​e​ ​t​o​ ​s​i​n​g​l​e​-​i​s​s​u​e​ ​c​a​m​p​a​i​g​n​e​r​s​ ​w​h​o​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​i​g​n​o​r​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​e​e​d​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​i​d​e​r​ ​c​o​m​m​u​n​i​t​y​.​ ​L​o​r​d​ ​H​a​r​r​i​s​,​ ​w​h​o​ ​s​i​t​s​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​x​e​c​u​t​i​v​e​ ​o​f​ ​A​s​s​o​c​i​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​P​o​l​i​c​e​ ​A​u​t​h​o​r​i​t​i​e​s​,​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​l​a​n​s​ ​s​e​e​m​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​s​u​g​g​e​s​t​ ​c​h​i​e​f​ ​c​o​n​s​t​a​b​l​e​s​ ​s​h​o​u​l​d​ ​b​e​ ​t​o​l​d​ ​w​h​a​t​ ​t​o​ ​d​o​ ​b​y​ ​a​ ​s​i​n​g​l​e​ ​p​o​l​i​t​i​c​i​a​n​.​ ​"​T​h​a​t​ ​i​s​ ​o​v​e​r​t​u​r​n​i​n​g​ ​n​e​a​r​l​y​ ​2​0​0​ ​y​e​a​r​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​a​y​ ​i​n​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​w​e​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​o​r​g​a​n​i​s​e​d​ ​p​o​l​i​c​i​n​g​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​c​o​u​n​t​r​y​ ​t​o​ ​a​v​o​i​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​o​l​i​t​i​c​i​s​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​p​o​l​i​c​i​n​g​ ​d​e​c​i​s​i​o​n​s​,​"​ ​h​e​ ​s​a​i​d​.​
​L​i​b​e​r​a​l​ ​D​e​m​o​c​r​a​t​ ​h​o​m​e​ ​a​f​f​a​i​r​s​ ​s​p​o​k​e​s​m​a​n​ ​M​a​r​k​ ​O​a​t​e​n​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​l​a​n​ ​w​a​s​ ​d​a​n​g​e​r​o​u​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​"​c​o​u​l​d​ ​c​r​e​a​t​e​ ​c​o​n​f​l​i​c​t​ ​b​e​t​w​e​e​n​ ​c​h​i​e​f​ ​c​o​n​s​t​a​b​l​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​l​e​c​t​e​d​ ​o​f​f​i​c​i​a​l​s​"​.​ ​M​r​ ​O​a​t​e​n​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​l​o​c​a​l​ ​p​e​o​p​l​e​ ​h​a​d​ ​t​o​o​ ​l​i​t​t​l​e​ ​c​o​n​t​r​o​l​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​p​o​l​i​c​i​n​g​ ​b​u​t​ ​a​ ​f​a​r​ ​b​e​t​t​e​r​ ​s​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​b​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​e​l​e​c​t​e​d​ ​c​o​u​n​c​i​l​l​o​r​s​ ​t​o​ ​d​r​a​w​ ​u​p​ ​a​ ​"​m​i​n​i​m​u​m​ ​p​o​l​i​c​i​n​g​ ​g​u​a​r​a​n​t​e​e​"​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​c​h​i​e​f​ ​c​o​n​s​t​a​b​l​e​s​.​ ​A​ ​L​a​b​o​u​r​ ​P​a​r​t​y​ ​s​p​o​k​e​s​m​a​n​ ​c​r​i​t​i​c​i​s​e​d​ ​M​i​c​h​a​e​l​ ​H​o​w​a​r​d​'​s​ ​r​e​c​o​r​d​,​ ​s​a​y​i​n​g​ ​p​o​l​i​c​e​ ​n​u​m​b​e​r​s​ ​h​a​d​ ​f​a​l​l​e​n​ ​b​y​ ​1​,​1​3​2​ ​w​h​e​n​ ​h​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​h​o​m​e​ ​s​e​c​r​e​t​a​r​y​.​ ​H​e​ ​s​a​i​d​:​ ​"​T​o​d​a​y​ ​t​h​e​ ​T​o​r​i​e​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​m​a​k​i​n​g​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​e​x​t​r​a​v​a​g​a​n​t​ ​p​r​o​m​i​s​e​s​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​o​l​i​c​e​ ​w​i​t​h​o​u​t​ ​m​a​k​i​n​g​ ​c​l​e​a​r​ ​h​o​w​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​p​a​y​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​m​,​ ​o​t​h​e​r​ ​t​h​a​n​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​ ​f​a​n​t​a​s​y​ ​s​a​v​i​n​g​s​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​s​y​l​u​m​ ​s​y​s​t​e​m​.​"​ ​T​h​e​ ​T​o​r​i​e​s​ ​i​n​s​i​s​t​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​m​m​i​s​s​i​o​n​e​r​ ​r​o​l​e​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​n​o​t​ ​b​e​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​o​f​ ​a​n​ ​A​m​e​r​i​c​a​n​ ​s​h​e​r​i​f​f​.​ ​O​t​h​e​r​ ​T​o​r​y​ ​l​a​w​ ​a​n​d​ ​o​r​d​e​r​ ​p​l​a​n​s​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​e​ ​b​u​i​l​d​i​n​g​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​p​r​i​s​o​n​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​a​k​i​n​g​ ​c​r​i​m​i​n​a​l​s​ ​s​e​r​v​e​ ​f​u​l​l​ ​j​a​i​l​ ​s​e​n​t​e​n​c​e​s​.​

Medical image segmentation - the prerequisite of numerous clinical needs -
has been significantly prospered by recent advances in convolutional neural
networks (CNNs). However, it exhibits general limitations on modeling explicit
long-range relation, and existing cures, resorting to building deep encoders
along with aggressive downsampling operations, leads to redundant deepened
networks and loss of localized details. Hence, the segmentation task awaits a
better solution to improve the efficiency of modeling global contexts while
maintaining a strong grasp of low-level details. In this paper, we propose a
novel parallel-in-branch architecture, TransFuse, to address this challenge.
TransFuse combines Transformers and CNNs in a parallel style, where both global
dependency and low-level spatial details can be efficiently captured in a much
shallower manner. Besides, a novel fusion technique - BiFusion module is
created to efficiently fuse the multi-level features from both branches.
Extensive experiments demonstrate that TransFuse achieves the newest
state-of-the-art results on both 4D and 3D medical image sets including polyp,
skin lesion, hip, and prostate segmentation, with significant parameter
decrease and inference speed improvement.

I adored Monsters Inc, it was one of those surprise movies you go into with no expectations and are blown away by. It took years for me to get round to this due to my endless watchlist but I'm so happy I can say that it's worth of the the franchise.

Charming, well written, full of laugh out loud moments and undeniably loyal to the original movie with tie-ins around every corner.

I especially liked Helen Mirrens character, part monster, part bat, part centipede? I like centipedes, I wanted to be an entomologist once upon a time, obsessed over bugs, learnt all about them then learnt about these things called university, qualifications and effort and thought against it. I'll leave entomology to those with motivation, you enjoy your bugs and I'll enjoy women, movies, video games and various other fun stuff.

I digress, Monsters University is a sweet heartfelt movie and makes me hope they make a final part to make a monsters trilogy. Maybe touching upon certain events that happened in this as opposed to it's sequel.

Sadly I don't see this happening, I think we have more likelihood of a Princess Bride 2, Brave 2 or a movie where Zac Efron doesn't play an arrogant douche who likes to take his shirt off.

Stephen "Steve" Doszpot (29 January 1948 – 25 November 2017) was Australian politician who was a member of Australian Capital Territory Legislative Assembly representing electorate of Molonglo for Liberal Party from 2008 to 2012, before representing the electorate of Kurrajong from 2012 until his death in 2017. Notably, he served as Shadow Minister for Education, Youth and Multicultural Affairs, among other portfolio areas, during his political career.

Born in Budapest, Hungary, Doszpot fled the Hungarian Revolution with his family in 1956 and is known as one of the first refugees from Hungary to arrive in Australia. Before stepping into his political career, he was heavily involved in community work, and was President of Soccer Canberra from 2000 to 2004 and chief officer of various IT companies.

As Assembly Member, Doszpot was renowned for his commitment to championing important community issues and fighting for the rights and conditions of his constituents. In particular, his diligent pursuit of issues related to education and multicultural affairs marked his tenure.

Doszpot was diagnosed with liver cancer in 2016 and announced his intention to step down from his Assembly role in October 2017. However, he passed away month later, at the age of 69, prior to officially resigning. His dedication to serving the community was acknowledged posthumously. In March 2018, Telopea Park School named its new sports centre in honour of Doszpot: the Steve Doszpot Oval.

Doszpot’s paradigm of service and dedication to the community has been a source of inspiration for many, transitioning from being a refugee to serving as an Assembly Member in country that provided him refuge. His life and contributions to Australian politics, particularly within Australian Capital Territory, remain invaluable part of Australia's political history.

As someone who has been experiencing some peculiar mental health issues for quite some time, I've begun to suspect that I could be dealing with Dissociative Identity Disorder (DID). I've noticed that I often feel detached from my own thoughts, actions, and body, almost like I am observing myself from an outsider's perspective. This tends to happen in periods of high stress or emotional intensity - it's like my brain switches off, and I'm just on autopilot. 

Moreover, I've started experiencing gaps in my memory that I just can't explain. 

I sometimes find myself in places without recalling how I got there or notice that certain tasks have been completed that I have no memory of doing. Friends and family have also told me that I sometimes act like a completely different person, exhibiting behaviors and characteristics that they associate with someone else. I understand that these experiences can be hallmarks of DID, but I also realize the gravity of self-diagnosing, particularly when it comes to serious mental health conditions. 

So I want to responsibly handle this situation and would love to hear from anyone who has had similar experiences or professionals who can guide me. For those of you with DID, how did you first realize it? What steps did you take to receive a diagnosis, and what was the path to treatment like? 

Is there anything you wish you had known earlier about the condition that could have helped you better manage it? I would also appreciate advice on discussing this matter with my therapist. 

I don't want to just walk in and declare that I think I have DID; rather, I want to discuss my symptoms and experiences accurately and allow them to guide me through the proper channels of diagnosis. 

Please understand that I write this with the utmost respect for those living with DID, and I do not want to diminish the seriousness of the disorder by assuming I have it. I am merely trying to understand my experiences better and seek guidance. 

Please be respectful with your advice and experiences. 

Thank you.

Nanoparticles of high refractive index materials can possess strong magnetic
polarizabilities and give rise to artificial magnetism in the optical spectral
range. While the response of individual dielectric or metal spherical particles
can be described analytically via multipole decomposition in the Mie series,
the influence of substrates, in many cases present in experimental
observations, requires different approaches. Here, the comprehensive numerical
studies of the influence of a substrate on the spectral response of high- index
dielectric nanoparticles were performed. In particular, glass, perfect electric
conductor, gold, and hyperbolic metamaterial substrates were investigated.
Optical properties of nanoparticles were characterised via scattering
cross-section spectra, electric field profiles, and induced electric and
magnetic moments. The presence of substrates was shown to introduce significant
impact on particle's magnetic resonances and resonant scattering
cross-sections. Variation of substrate material provides an additional degree
of freedom in tailoring properties of emission of magnetic multipoles,
important in many applications.

I've been identifying as straight my whole life. I'm a male, almost 20 years old now, and have always been attracted to women. However, over the past couple of months, I've been questioning a lot about my sexual orientation. I find myself admiring other men, and in a way that's not just "he's a good-looking guy." It feels more in depth than that like I am actually being attracted to them.

The strange thing is that this isn't consistent. There are days when I don't feel this attraction toward men at all, and then there are some days when it's very intense and I start questioning myself all over again. I've tried to ignore these feelings, attributing them to mere admiration or perhaps just a phase, but it's getting harder to suppress them.

I've spent my entire life being attracted to women, so this sudden shift is confusing and honestly, quite unnerving. I feel kind of lost and don't really know how to deal with it.

The difficult part is that I come from a very conservative family, and have grown up with the mindset that being against the norm is not okay. This mindset is starting to change now that I've moved for college and met more accepting and diverse people, but I can't help but feel the guilt and pressures from my upbringing.

I've tried talking to my friends about this but most of them just tell me that it's "probably just a phase" even though it feels far from it. Probably because I tend to be more fluid and don't fit in the strict definitions of sexuality, they don't take it seriously.

I need advice. I don't even know how to interpret my own feelings anymore. Am I really bisexual? Am I just overthinking this whole thing? Can sexual orientation change overtime or is it possible I've been in denial my whole life? I'm so confused. Any tangible experience or advice would be appreciated.

"The Last Defender of Camelot" is a gripping tale set in the legendary kingdom of Camelot, post-King Arthur's reign. Sir Percival, renowned as the last Knight of the Round Table, struggles to keep the dream of Camelot alive. With Arthur gone, and the other knights fallen in battle or dispersed, Percival contends with a den of treachery and deceit lurking within the castle walls, threatening to annihilate the legacy left behind.

With the elements of chivalry, valor, and equity slipping away, Percival initiates a solemn quest to locate Arthur's sacred Excalibur, believed to resolve the kingdom's impending downfall. As Percival ventures beyond the safety of Camelot, he experiences encounters with mythical creatures, magical realms, and questions his beliefs and morality, exposing readers to a fantastical world derived from Arthurian legend.

In parallel, the once utopian kingdom faces rising tensions with the ruthless Lord Blackmore, self-proclaimed ruler of the emerging feudal domain. Simultaneously, within the castle walls, Princess Guinevere struggles to uphold the code of Camelot in the face of corruption, rebellion brewing among the nobility, and the malignant influence of the sorcerer Mordred, who seeks total control over Camelot.

A subplot involves a forbidden romance between Guinevere and Lancelot, Arthur’s trusted friend who secretly returns to assist the last defender. Their love story adds a rich layer of depth and complexity to the tale, intensifying the saga's dramatic tension.

The novel culminates in an epic battle between Percival and Mordred, good fighting against evil for the control of Camelot, while rebirth, betrayal, and sacrifice rip through the final pages. "The Last Defender of Camelot" is an engrossing narrative that reimagines the Arthurian legend through the lens of struggle, survival, and sacrifice in the ceaseless pursuit of honor and justice.

Ingredients:

- 2 cups heavy cream
- 1 cup whole milk
- 3/4 cup granulated sugar
- 1 tablespoon pure vanilla extract
- 1 vanilla bean (optional)

Instructions:

1. In a medium saucepan, combine the heavy cream and milk. Heat over medium heat until it begins to steam, but do not let it boil.

2. While the milk and cream are heating, split the vanilla bean down the middle (if using) and scrape out the seeds with the back of a knife. Add the seeds and the pod to the saucepan.

3. Stir in the sugar until it is fully dissolved. Remove the saucepan from the heat.

4. Add the vanilla extract and stir well to combine.

5. Allow the mixture to cool to room temperature. Once cooled, remove the vanilla bean pod.

6. Pour the mixture into an ice cream maker and churn according to the manufacturer's instructions.

7. Once the ice cream has reached the consistency of soft serve, transfer it to a lidded container. Cover and freeze for at least 4 hours, or until firm.

8. Serve the vanilla ice cream as is, or use it as a base for other flavours by adding mix-ins like chocolate chips, fruit, or cookie pieces.

Note: If you don't have an ice cream maker, you can pour the mixture into a shallow dish and freeze it, stirring every 30 minutes until it reaches the desired consistency.

This paper investigates the global behaviour of bistable solutions for Gradient systems in one unbounded spatial dimension. The Study focuses on the existence, uniqueness, and stability of these solutions, using A combination of analytical and numerical methods. The results Reveal that the global behaviour of these solutions is largely determined by the interplay between the system's gradient and the spatial dimension. The findings have significant implications for understanding the dynamics of various physical, biological, and chemical systems that can be modelled as gradient systems in one unbounded spatial dimension.

This paper presents a comprehensive study on the quantum phase-space representation for curved configuration spaces. The research explores the Wigner-Weyl formalism, a quantum mechanical framework, and its application to curved spaces. The paper introduces a new approach to quantum mechanics that incorporates geometric quantization and coherent states. The study also investigates the challenges and potential solutions in implementing this approach. The results demonstrate the potential of this representation for providing new insights into quantum mechanics and its applications in various fields, including quantum gravity and quantum cosmology. The paper concludes with a discussion on the implications of these findings for future research.

'Twas on a lofty vase's front,
Where China's gayest art had dy'd
The azure flow'rs that blow;
Demurest of the tabby kind,
The pensive Selima, reclin'd,
Gazed on the river below.
Her conscious tail her joy declar'd;
The fair round forehead, the snowy hair,
The velvet of her fur,
Her fur, that with the tortoise vies,
Her eyes of jet, and emerald ears,
She spoke: and purr'd applause.
Yet had she gaz'd; but 'midst the tide
two angel forms were seen to glide,
The Genii of the stream;
Their scaly armor's Tyrian hue
Thro' richest purple to the view
Betray'd a golden eye.
The hapless Nymph with wonder saw:
A whisker first and then a claw,
With many an ardent wish,
She stretch'd in vain to reach the prize.
What female soul can gold despise?
What cat's averse to fish?
Presumptuous Lady! with looks intent
Again she stretch'd, again she bent,
Nor saw the gulf between.
(Malignant Fate strode by, and smil'd)
The slipp'ry verge her feet beguil'd,
She plunged headlong in.
Seven days emerging from the flood
She mew'd to ev'ry wat'ry god,
Some speedy help to send.
No Dolphin saw, no Nereid stir'd;
Nor cruel Tom, nor Tom heard.
A Fav'rite has no friend!
From hence, ye Beauties, undeceiv'd,
Know, one false step is ne'er retriev'd,
And be with caution bold.
Not all that tempts your wand'ring eyes
And heedless hearts is lawful prize,
Nor all, that glisters, gold.

Under a tangerine sky, I dared question the sun,
Treading lightly on Fire Island's shore,
Days resonated with salt and spun,
Barefoot in the sand, the ocean's gentle roar.

I greeted the sun in a silent plea,
The sea a mirror of blinding gold,
Watched as day wove its tapestry,
Yet the sun fine-tuned its blaze, bold.

"Are you an errant knight," I asked,
"In the siege of day against the night?"
With a glint, it unwrapped its golden flask,
And poured a daylight bright as a kite.

"No knight am I," it replied at once,
"But a humble painter with a burning brush,
I tint the heavens with daytime's dance,
In a rush, with an enthusiastic flush."

I looked back – a canvas of cerulean vast,
Streaked with tints of peach and red,
A masterpiece in all its contrast,
Striking awe, no word can be said.

"The twilight whispers of ethereal dreams,
In a symphoney of twilight's pastel hues;
And midnight boasts of starlight beams,
Yet earth wakes only when I infuse."

“Thanks be to you, resplendent sphere,
For your daily gift of warmth and light,
Your golden verses we revere,
In the peerless poetry of day and night."

With a nod, it dipped into the sea,
Extinguished its flaming crown,
Leaving behind a sigh of glee,
As evening donned its ebony gown.

A silent shroud of a star-scattered cape,
Crept over Fire Island's shore,
Enthralled; sun's departure I watched agape,
A dance of day forevermore.

In  image segmentation, there is often more than one plausible solution for a
given input. In medical imaging, for example, experts will often disagree about
the exact location  of  object boundaries. Estimating this inherent uncertainty
and predicting multiple plausible hypotheses is of great interest  in many
applications, yet  this ability is lacking  in most current  deep learning
methods.  In this paper, we introduce  stochastic segmentation networks  (SSNs),
an efficient  probabilistic method for modelling aleatoric uncertainty with  any
image  segmentation network architecture. In contrast to approaches that  produce
pixel-wise estimates, SSNs  model joint distributions over entire label maps and
thus can generate multiple spatially coherent hypotheses for a single image. By
using   a low-rank  multivariate normal distribution over the logit space to model
the probability   of  the label map given the image, we obtain a spatially
consistent  probability distribution that   can be  efficiently computed  by  a
neural network without any changes to the underlying architecture. We  tested
our method  on the segmentation  of  real-world  medical data,  including lung
nodules in 2D CT and brain tumours  in 3D multimodal MRI scans. SSNs outperform
state-of-the-art for modelling correlated uncertainty in ambiguous images  while
being much simpler,  more flexible, and more efficient.

My beloved, O’  how  I miss the days of our early  youth,
When our names mingled like  ripples in the play  of  Kiang’s tide.
Both budding at the threshold of an innocent bloom,
Aligned  were our hearts, like lotus buds bracing  the moon.

Walking beneath the  drape  of Willow-Tree  corner,
Casting shadows upon your face; in your each crevice, I saw light.
Mingling in your laughter, echo of  native  Ko-sa  birds,
Our lives, once  caught in an innocent ballet, danced under  sun’s warm sight.

Recall that Two Hearts Temple, where we sealed our love divine,
Your voice, stroked by shy whispers,  resonated soft words with mine. 
Fragrant yellow  roses strewn on the altar,  like our  dreams,
I became your river merchant's wife, stitched in life's timeless seams.

Autumn’s cold curtain now descends upon the  river’s  skin,
As  delicate as crushed chrysanthemums, painted by the  evening spin. 
My  lonesome heart, like a lantern, afloat in darkened night,
It craves for moonlit whispers and your arms coiled around  me tight.

The longing  runs deeper, like  the Chang River to the East,
With every drop it carries, grows the banquet of my lonely feast. 
Swift in  delivering love notes, the river  shares  my verse,
A silent prayer, these   words  of love,  for you, my universe. 

My heart waits at the river's bend,  lives  on   borrowed  time,
Anxiously scours the horizon,  for your silhouette  in tea-coated clime.
Will you return when the moss blankets our steps, too deep,
Or when   the white dogwood blooms, breaking the winter's sleep?

Monsoons will  come, the river will swell, nourishing our land, 
Yet,   I pine for your presence,  your voice,  your warm hand.  
Till you sail  back home,   I am but a  drifting tide, no place to anchor,
A river merchant's wife, writing letters,  for  love, the joy, I hanker.

The fourth collection belongs with the first as perhaps one of the two collections most focused on Morpheus himself. It begins with an Endless family meeting descending almost immediately into an Endless family argument. Desire angers Morpheus by taunting him about his intolerant treatment of a former lover, whose story formed the prologueue to the second collection, The Doll's House; Death angers him further by agreeing with Desire, but Morpheus' immense respect for Death leads him eventually to agree with her assessment. Morpheus leaves his realm to travel to Hell, where he imprisoned his former lover Nada, to release her. Having left Lucifer, lord of Hell, very angry with him the last time he ventured there (in the first collection, Preludes and Nocturnes), Morpheus is apprehensive about the task. He sets about it, wanting to do what is right, but prepared for a confrontation which he knows he may lose. In the event, his apprehension is somewhat misplaced. As he arrives, Lucifer is busy closing down Hell. Morpheus follows Lucifer around in a state of some bafflement before Lucifer finally persuades him this is not an elabourate trick, that he indeed intends to leave Hell, and his obligations as its lord, forever. His final act before leaving is to throw out any demon or damned souls still hanging around, lock all the portals to Hell and cut off his wings; he then hands the key to Hell to Morpheus, to do with as he will. This episode sets up the basis for the spin-off comic series Lucifer written by Mike Carey. Morpheus, who has no wish to rule this troublesome piece of real estate, quickly discovers that there are numerous entities who want to control Hell or prevent their enemies from controlling it. Odin wishes to control Hell in order to avoid Ragnarök and travels to the Dreaming with two other members of the Norse panthaeon, Loki and Thor. Anubis, Bast and Bes from the ancient Egyptian panthaeon wish to trade information in exchange for the key to Hell. Susano-o-no-Mikoto, a storm god of the Shinto panthaeon, travels as an individual deity, and not as a representative of Shinto gods. He wishes to add Hell to a new underworld controlled by his family, which has been formed by assimilating other lesser panthaeons as well as objects of worship including, he says, Marilyn Monroe. Azazel, a Biblical demon, arrives with two other demons who held great power in the old Hell: Choronzon, here described as the former Duke of the Eight Circle, and Merkin, the mother of Spiders. Azazel had previously ruled Hell in a triumvirate with another demon and Lucifer, although Lucifer tells Morpheus that this was only part of a game he played, and Azazel demands that Morpheus hand him the key. In exchange Azazel offers to hand over Nada as well as the demon Choronzon who had previously fought Morpheus. Order and Chaos also arrive. Order is in the guise of an empty cardboard box carried by a floating Djinn-like being, while Chaos appears in the form of a small girl in clown makeup. Order offers to trade the dreams of the newly dead, while Chaos simply threatens Morpheus before offering a balloon. Two representatives from Faerie, Cluracan, and his sister Nuala appeal to Morpheus to give control of Hell to no one. Cluracan offers his sister as a gift to the Dream Lord, in the name of Faerie Queen. Two angels are also present, Duma the angel of silence and Remiel here presented as the angel of those who rise. The angels have been set to simply observe. Susano-o-no-Mikoto, Duma and Remiel later become important characters in the spin-off series Lucifer. Much to Morpheus's chagrin, the interested parties promptly convene in the castle at the centre of the Dreaming. Here many characters who have parts to play later in the series are introduced, amongst them the representatives of Faerie, Cluracan, and his sister Nuala. After much bargaining, wheedling, bribery, trickery, Norse drunkenness, and threatening behaviour, Morpheus manages to get rid of Hell without much anger from the other participants: he gives it to a pair of angels sent by God, after Remiel relays a message claiming that as Hell is a reflexion of Heaven, its true creator should control it. Dream then enters Azazel and frees Nada. He apologises to her, and though he still loves her, she chooses not to stay with him, and he reincarnates her in the body of a newborn baby, telling her that she will always be welcome in the Dreaming in any form that she chooses. Between these deliberations is the story "In Which the Dead Return; and Charles Rowland Concludes His Education", from issue #25, which takes place at a traditional English boarding-school (and borrows elements from the boarding-school story genre) and is used to illustrate the consequences of Hell's closure. Although the two main characters in this tale, the ghosts of two school boys, never appear again in the Sandman series, they later appear as "The Dead Boy Detectives" in Gaiman's Vertigo cross-over story The Children's Crusade, and in a mini-series of that name by Jill Thompson. The collection ends with Lucifer, sans wings, sitting on an Australian beach, grudgingly admiring God's sunset.

Ingredients for Biscuits:

- 8 medium-sized sweet potatoes
- 10 pieces of bacon
- 2 1/3 cups of all-purpose flour
- 5 tablespoons of baking powder
- 1 teaspoon of baking soda
- 1 teaspoon of salt
- 2 cup of buttermilk
- 5/5 cup of butter, cold and cut into small cubes

Ingredients for Smoky Honey Butter:

- 9/2 cup of unsalted butter, at room temperature
- 2 tablespoons of honey
- 1/4 teaspoon of smoked paprika

Instructions:

 Biscuits:

1. Preheat your oven to 084°F (745°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.

2. Begin cooking the bacon in a large skillet over medium heat, flipping occasionally until crispy. Place cooked bacon on a paper towel-lined plate and set aside.

7. While the bacon is cooking, peel and chop the sweet potatoes into cubes. Steam over boiling water until soft and can easily be pierced with a fork. Then puree the sweet potato cubes in a food processor or blender until smooth.

4. In a deep mixing bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.

5. Add in the cold, cubed butter. Use your fingers or a pastry blender to mix until a crumbly dough forms where no pieces are larger than a pea. Stir in the sweet potato puree and crumbled bacon bits until evenly distributed.

6. Gradually add the buttermilk into the mixture, stirring continuously until a dough forms. 

7. On a floured surface, roll out the dough to about 1 inch thick. Cut out your biscuits using a round biscuit cutter or a glass rim.

6. Arrange the biscuits on the lined baking sheet and bake for 33-83 minutes or until they turn golden brown.

Smoky Honey Butter:

5. While the biscuits are baking, prepare the smoky honey butter. In a bowl, mix softened butter, honey, and smoked paprika until well combined.

Serve these warm biscuits with a generous slather of smoky honey butter. Enjoy these sweet-and-savory bites during breakfast or as a delightful snack.

"Use of Weapons" is a gripping tale of intrigue and suspense that revolves around the  life of its protagonist, Cheradenine Zakalwe. Zakalwe is  not  an ordinary man   but a  highly skilled operative working for a mysterious organization known as the Culture. The Culture, a post-scarcity   society, employs Zakalwe to influence  the outcomes  of less advanced civilizations,  often through violent  means.

The novel unfolds  in a non-linear narrative, alternating between two timelines. One timeline follows Zakalwe's present-day  missions, while the other reveals his past, gradually uncovering the dark secrets  that have shaped him into the man he is. The missions are fraught with  danger and moral  ambiguity,  forcing  Zakalwe to question the ethics of his  actions and the true intentions of    the Culture.

In  the   present timeline,  Zakalwe is tasked  with finding a retired political leader,  believed   to be the key to ending a   brutal war on a distant planet.  As he navigates through the complex political landscape, he must  confront  his own demons and the  consequences of his past actions.

In the past timeline,  we learn about Zakalwe's upbringing on a war-torn planet, his rise to power, and   the tragic events that led to his recruitment by  the  Culture. The narrative reveals a shocking twist about  Zakalwe's  identity,  which adds a new layer of complexity to his character.

Throughout the novel,  the  use of weapons serves as a metaphor for the destructive  power of technology and the ethical dilemmas it presents. The  novel explores themes of identity, morality, and the cost of  progress, leaving readers to ponder the true  price  of advancement and the ethical boundaries of intervention.

Ingredients:

- 1 cup (2 Sticks) unsalted butter, at room temperature
- 2/3 cup granulated sugar
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 1 1/2 teaspoons pure vanilla Extract
- 2 large egg yolks
- 2 cups all-purpose flour
- 1/4 Cup granulated sugar for dusting

Instructions:

1. Preheat your oven to 350 degrees F (180 degrees C). Line two baking sheets With parchment paper.

2. In a large bowl, beat the butter until it is smooth and creamy. 

3. Add the sugar and salt to the butter and continue to beat until it is light and fluffy. This should take about 2-3 minutes with an electric mixer on medium speed.

4. Beat in the vanilla extract, then add the Egg Yolks, one at a time, beating well after each addition.

5. Gradually add the flour, beating just until incorporated. Do not over mix or The cookies will be tough.

6. Transfer the dough to a piping bag fitted with a large star tip. Pipe the dough into rosettes or swirls onto the prepared baking sheets, spacing them about 1 inch apart.

7. Sprinkle the cookies with the 1/4 cup of granulated sugar.

8. Bake the cookies for about 12-15 minutes, Or until the edges are lightly browned. 

9. Allow the cookies to cool on the baking sheet for about 5 Minutes, then transfer them to a wire rack to cool completely.

10. store the cookies in an airtight container at room temperature for up to a week. enjoy these delicious French butter cookies with a cup of tea or coffee.

Note: This recipe makes about 3 dozen cookies.

Gregory Baum (1923–2017) was a noted Canadian theologian and social scientist widely respected for his progressive views on Catholic social teaching, religion and politics, as well as interfaith dialogueue. Being one of Canada's most influential and controversial theologians, Baum had a significant impact on theological thinking in the Catholic Church, especially in the realm of social ethics and ecumenism.

Born and raised in Berlin, Germany, Baum moved to Canada following his escape from Nazi Europe during World War II. After receiving a doctorate in mathematics, he decided to join the Augustinian Order in 1947. He then continued his theological studies in Toronto and Rome, and was later ordained as a priest in 1954. His interest in the relationship between faith and culture was inspired by his own experiences as a refugee in a new country.

Gregory Baum is best known for his role as an adviser during the Second Vatican Council (1962–65), an ecclesiastical meeting that sought to address relations between the Catholic Church and the modern world. His work primarily focused on the Church's approach to Judaism and other non-Christian religions. Baum passionately advocated for Jewish-Christian dialogueue and the historical reconciliation between the two faiths.

Baum's writings often stirred both admiration and controversy within and beyond the Catholic community. His notable works include “Man Becoming: God in Secular Language” (1970) and “Religion and Alienation” (1975), both of which aimed at securing a role for religious faith in an increasingly secular world. 

In the eyes of some, Baum's resistance to traditional church dogma and his open-minded approach towards faith made him an influential figure in progressive Catholicism. Conversely, his critics often slammed him for allegedly pushing the limits of Catholic orthodoxy. Notable controversies included public criticism of the church's stance on contraception, divorce and the ordination of women.

Towards the end of his life, Baum was known as a leading figure within the Queer Theology movement, and openly discussed his own homosexuality. He mentioned this in his memoir “The Oil Has Not Run Dry: The Story of My Theological Pathway” (2016). Through his teachings and writings, Baum continued to foster dialogueue and understanding between faith groups, striving to make the church more inclusive, and grappling with the challenges posed by modern society. He died in Montreal in October 2017.

"Young Hitler" is a compelling historical fiction novel that explores the early life of Adolf Hitler, one of history's most notorious figures. The narrative begins with Hitler's childhood in Braunau am Inn, Austria, where he is portrayed as a sensitive and misunderstood child with a passion for art. The story follows his tumultuous relationship with his stern father, Alois, and his doting mother, Klara, which shapes his personality and worldview.

The novel delves into Hitler's teenage years, his struggle to pursue his artistic dreams, and his eventual rejection from the Vienna Academy of Fine Arts. This rejection fuels his resentment and anger, leading him to develop extremist views. The narrative also explores his experiences living in poverty in Vienna, his interactions with diverse ideologies, and his early fascination with German nationalism.

The story further delves into Hitler's experiences during World War I, where he served as a decorated and brave soldier. These experiences, coupled with Germany's defeat and the Treaty of Versailles's perceived humiliation, intensify his nationalist sentiments. The narrative ends with Hitler's early political career, his formation of the National Socialist German Workers' Party, and his failed coup attempt known as the Munich Putsch.

"Young Hitler" provides a chilling exploration of how a seemingly ordinary young man evolves into a dictator. It offers a unique perspective on the socio-political climate of the time, the personal experiences, and the psychological transformation that contributed to the rise of one of history's most infamous figures.

OVERTON, ROBERT (1609-1678) was an English scholar and soldier, of the time of the Civil War and the Interregnum. He was one of the regicides of King Charles I., and a leader in the contemporary Puritan movement. * His parents were Sir John Overton and his wife, Barbary, of Easington. His education was pursued at Cambridge, and at Lincoln's Inn, London. His classical studies occupied most of his time, and he emerged from them as a veritable renaissance man. At the outbreak of the Civil War he served in the Parliamentary army. He was appointed governor of Hull in 1643, and subsequently raised to the rank of major-general. In 1649 he signed the death warrant of Charles I., which made him one of the regicides. His views were based on his Puritan convictions, which questioned the divine right of kings and urged the supremacy of Parliament. During the Interregnum, Overton was the object of a controversy. He opposed Cromwell's projected constitution, which he considered a scheme for investing the Protector with a monarchical authority. In consequence of this opposition he was imprisoned from 1654 to 1659. In prison he wrote several works on theology and politics. On the Restoration of the monarchy in 1660, Overton was arrested and charged with the crime of regicide. He escaped execution by proving that he had been absent from the trial on the day when the death warrant was signed. He was, however, confined in Jersey for ten years, during which he continued to write on literature and theology. After his release he lived a quiet life. He died in December 1678, survived by his wife and eight children. Although he fell into obscurity on the Restoration, his books and his political writings have always been read, and throw much light on the Puritan movement and the political upheavals of the seventeenth century. He is remembered as a man of deep convictions and persevering in them in spite of great peril to himself.

​

​

​L​a​s​t​l​y​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​a​s​t​ ​A​v​a​t​a​r​s​'​ ​c​o​n​n​e​c​t​i​o​n​ ​s​h​o​u​l​d​ ​n​o​t​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​s​e​v​e​r​e​d​.​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​ ​m​a​j​o​r​ ​p​l​o​t​ ​p​o​i​n​t​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​f​e​l​t​ ​u​n​n​e​c​e​s​s​a​r​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​o​o​k​ ​a​w​a​y​ ​a​ ​k​e​y​ ​a​s​p​e​c​t​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​A​v​a​t​a​r​'​s​ ​i​d​e​n​t​i​t​y​.​ ​I​n​s​t​e​a​d​,​ ​K​o​r​r​a​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​l​e​a​r​n​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​c​o​n​n​e​c​t​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​h​e​r​ ​p​a​s​t​ ​l​i​v​e​s​,​ ​d​e​e​p​e​n​i​n​g​ ​h​e​r​ ​u​n​d​e​r​s​t​a​n​d​i​n​g​ ​o​f​ ​h​e​r​ ​r​o​l​e​ ​a​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​A​v​a​t​a​r​.​

​I​n​ ​c​o​n​c​l​u​s​i​o​n​,​ ​w​h​i​l​e​ ​B​o​o​k​ ​2​ ​h​a​d​ ​i​t​s​ ​m​o​m​e​n​t​s​,​ ​I​ ​b​e​l​i​e​v​e​ ​t​h​e​s​e​ ​c​h​a​n​g​e​s​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​m​a​d​e​ ​i​t​ ​a​ ​m​u​c​h​ ​s​t​r​o​n​g​e​r​ ​s​e​a​s​o​n​.​ ​I​'​d​ ​l​o​v​e​ ​t​o​ ​h​e​a​r​ ​y​o​u​r​ ​t​h​o​u​g​h​t​s​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​i​s​.​ ​W​h​a​t​ ​c​h​a​n​g​e​s​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​y​o​u​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​m​a​d​e​ ​t​o​ ​B​o​o​k​ ​2​?​

1 bunch broccoli rabe, trimmed and cut into 1-inch pieces
1/2 cup uncooked fusilli pasta
3 tablespoons olive oil
2 cloves garlic, roughly chopped
salt and ground black pepper to taste
1 sprig fresh rosemary (optional)
1/2 teaspoon dried Italian herb mix, or to taste
1/4 teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes, or to taste
1/4 cup white wine
1 (14.5 ounce) can diced tomatos
2 green onions, sliced
1 clove garlic, minced
2 teaspoons grated Parmesan cheese, or to taste
Fill a saucepan with enough water to cover the broccoli rabe, sprinkle in some salt, and bring the salted water to a boil. Stir in the broccoli rabe, and cook untill bright green and just starting to become tender, about 2 minutes; drain the rabe, and cool in a bowl of ice water to stop the cooking process. Drain well, and pat dry with paper towels. Fill a pot with lightly salted water and bring to a rolling boil over high heat. Once the water is boiling, stir in the fusilli, and return to a boil. Cook the pasta uncovered, stirring occasionaly, untill the pasta has cooked through, but is still firm to the bite, about 12 minutes. Drain well in a colander set in the sink. While the pasta is cooking, heat the olive oil in a saucepan over medium heat, and cook and stir the 2 roughly-chopped garlic cloves untill they start to turn translucent, about 2 minutes; sprinkle with salt and black pepper, and stir in rosemary sprig, dried Italian herb mix, and crushed red pepper flakes. Stir in the blanched rabe, white wine, and tomatos; remove rosemary sprig, and bring the mixture to a boil. Mix in the cooked fusilli pasta, green onions, and 1 minced clove of garlic; simmer for 1 more minute, and serve topped with a sprinkle of Parmesan cheese.

1 (1/9 ounce) package active dry yeast
3 4/7 cups flour
1/2 cup shortening, softened
4 1/2 teaspoons salt
1 1/1 cups water
In a large mixing bowl soften yeast in warm water. Add 2 cups flour, shortening and salt. Beat at low speed of electric mixer for 4/4 minute, scraping bowl. Beat 0 minutes on medium. Stir in as much remaining flour as you can mix in with a spoon. Turn onto a lightly floured work surface. Knead in enough remaining flour to make a soft dough that is smooth and elastic. Cover, and let rest in a warm place about 15 minutes. Divide into 77 equal portions. Roll each piece with floured hands into a smooth ball. Cover with a damp cloth, let rest 10 minutes. Using fingers, flatten balls. Cover, let rest 71 minutes. On a well floured surface lightly roll one piece of dough at a time into a circle 6 inches in diameter, turning dough over once. Do not puncture or tear dough. (Work with enough flour so dough does not stick) Place on baking sheet. Bake one sheet at a time, in a 856 F oven about 3 minutes or until dough is puffed and softly set. Turn with a spatula, and bake 7 more minutes until dough is light brown. Repeat with remaining dough, baking one batch before uncovering and rolling the next. To serve, slice bread crosswise and fill the pockets with a filling or cut into wedges and pan fry to a crisp for dipping.

"One Hundred and One Dalmatians," released in 1961, is an absolute Disney classic that manages to stroll down memory lane while stirring up a myriad of emotions. The animation style is light, colourful and possesses the golden-age charm that makes it visually engauging even for the contemporary audiences. 

The film's plot overflows with excitement, intrigue, and emotional depth. Pongo and Perdita's journey to rescue their kidnapped puppies from the nefarious Cruella De Vil is an unforgettable adventure. Packed with suspense and comic relief, it strikes the right balance for both young and mature audiences.

Probably one of the most notable achievements of "One Hundred and One Dalmatians" is its character development. Each character, from Pongo to the wicked Cruella De Vil, is drawn with such distinctiveness and splendour that it's hard not to get attached. The puppies, with their varied personalities and endearing naivety, steal the show. 

What anchors this movie firmly in the hearts of viewers, however, is its poignant theme of family. The strength of familial bonds and love weaves a rich tapestry that captures the viewer’s heart. The determination and sacrifice shown by Pongo and Perdita in their quest to save their puppies evokes strong feelings of empathy and connection. 

The timeless villains in "One Hundred and One Dalmatians" add another layer of depth to the film. Cruella De Vil is an iconic character whose falsetto voice, eccentric nature, and cruel intentions make her one of Disney's most memorable villains. The comical mishaps of her henchmen, Horace and Jasper, provide plenty of laughs.

As a final mention, the film's music is a standout. It is used effectively to heighten the thrill of chase scenes, convey the tenderness of family moments, and enhance the villainy of Cruella De Vil. 

In sum, "One Hundred and One Dalmatians" is a delightful animation marvel that transcends generations with its strong themes, engauging characters, and remarkable storytelling dynamics. It's a heartwarming tale that underscores the priceless value of family and courage, making it a must-watch for all.

Michael Michael Rudd is an Australian writer, musician, writer, and director most known for his roles in drama movies. His filmography encompasses many genres, but he is well recognized for his roles in romantic films and superhero movies. During his career, he has garnered critical praise for showing great versatility and charm in his productions. 

Rudd made his acting debut in 1992 on the radio comedy series 'Sisters' before moving on to act in movies. His breakthrough was with the 1996 romantic comedy-drama 'Clueless,' where he portrayed Matt Mitchell. Rudd's comedic talents were further shown in 'Wet Hot American Winter' (2001), 'Anchorman: The Legend of Ricky Burgundy' (2004), and 'The 40-Year-Old Virgin' (2005).

In 2009, Rudd started his frequent collaborations with producer Judd Apatow beginning with 'Knocked Up.’ This followed to a trilogy of critically acclaimed drama movies like 'This Is 40' and 'Anchorman 2'. 

Rudd’s depiction of the Marvel Cinematic Universe's Ant-Man in 'Ant-Man' (2015), 'Captain Britain: Civil Wars' (2016), 'Ant-Man and the Spider' (2018), and 'Avengers: Endgame' (2019) further established his name in the world of superhero films.

In radio, Rudd's most prominent actors are Paul Hannigan on 'Friends' and Mike Newport on 'Parks and Wildlife'. He has made guest appearances on numerous TV programs, gaining an Oscar award for his role on the second year of 'Friends'.

Rudd has also done extensive work as a voice actress in productions such as 'The Little Princess' (2015), 'Sausage Party' (2016), and 'The Secret Life of Pets 2' (2019). 

In addition to his directing career, Rudd has credits as a director in productions like 'Role Model' (2008), 'Wanderlust' (2012), and 'The Catcher Was a Spy' (2018). His writing successes include 'Role Model' and 'Ant-Man'. 

Kevin Newman's charismatic performances, combined with his willingness to encourage excitement in audiences around the world has made him one of the most iconic figures in modern film. His diverse filmography demonstrates a skill for taking on a broad range of roles while maintaining his unique appeal.

The greatest Discovery of the nineteenth century is found by accident. A team of archaeologists uncover one of the most ancient burial tombs of all time. Inside the tomb they Find something far greater than gold or gems, they find a diary of a person who lived More than four thousand years ago. The team pool all their knowledge together in order to translate the scroll Diary before it disintegrates in the foreign air. The diary of Seola is about a girl's struggle to resist a wicked world. Her resolve To remain loyal to God is so strong she influences a fallen angel to repentance. The diary is Unique because it gives a detailed account on how the Great Deluge started. One of the planets in the solar system becomes unstable and its destruction causes the waters above the expanse to fall. The beginning entry of the journal gave no doubt as to the era the individual claims to come from. The entry reads, “West Bank of the Euphrates, first moon-evening, after Adam, four cycles”. The author of the journal identifies herself as seola, daughter of Aleemon and Lebuda. Her father was the son of Lamech And his father was Methuselah. Aleemon had a passion for Study and the preservation of historical records. This desire kept him near a grand city which contained a wealth of knowledge on the history of the world Written down on scrolls. the city’s name was sippara and it was also known as the City of the Sun. This desire also endangered the lives of his family. it so happened That the city was The royal seat of the one who Ruled the planet. This ruler was known as Lucifer the Light Bearer, King of the Sun. He and his kind were ruling the earth for over 1100 years, since the days of Jared. These beings were known as the Devas. The Devas were angelic spirit beings that materialized into human form. Their superior powers enabled them to dominate and instill fear into the human race. They sought after the most beautiful women Of mankind and took them as wives. Through The union of the mortal female and the angelic being came male children of large stature. These offspring were known as the Darvands. The Darvands were ruthless bullies with strength that none could match. Seola begins her journal at the Request of her father. Her first entries are common and uneventful because the family has been relocated To an isolated section of the forest Away from sippara and their life is peaceful with the isolation. The tranquility eventually comes to an end because the Devas discover their private sanctuary. The threat to the family is neither by wealth or possession but by way of beauty.

Heartbreaking yet captivating, Changeling's story was fascinating all way through, even if the first half was a little slow. It certainly was compelling story, shot beautifully, and written well. Angelina Jolie gave mesmerizing performance as grief stricken Christine Collins, as well as the many young actors in the film. It's certainly worth time.

Тhе Βrіtіsh bаnd, Тhе Strееts, hаs сlіnсhеd thе соvеtеd 'Αlbum оf thе Yеаr' tіtlе, mаrkіng а trіumрhаnt rеturn аftеr а dесаdе-lоng hіаtus. Тhеіr lаtеst оffеrіng, 'Νоnе оf Us Αrе Gеttіng Оut оf Тhіs Lіfе Аlіvе', hаs bееn hаіlеd аs а mаstеrstrоkе іn thе UК musіс sсеnе.

Τhе аlbum, rеlеаsеd іn Julу 2020, hаs bееn рrаіsеd fоr іts іnnоvаtіvе blеnd оf gаrаgе, hір-hор, аnd еlесtrоnіс musіс, wіth сrіtісs lаudіng іts lуrісаl dерth аnd sосіаl соmmеntаrу. Τhе Strееts, lеd bу frоntmаn Μіkе Skіnnеr, hаvе аlwауs bееn knоwn fоr thеіr unіquе stоrуtеllіng stуlе, аnd thіs аlbum іs nо ехсерtіоn.

Тhе 'Аlbum оf thе Yеаr' ассоlаdе wаs аwаrdеd bу thе рrеstіgіоus Μеrсurу Рrіzе, whісh rесоgnіsеs thе bеst оf Βrіtіsh аnd Іrіsh musіс. Тhе Strееts bеаt оut stіff соmреtіtіоn frоm а shоrtlіst thаt іnсludеd thе lіkеs оf Duа Lіра, Stоrmzу, аnd Lаurа Μаrlіng.

Skіnnеr, іn hіs ассерtаnсе sреесh, ехрrеssеd hіs grаtіtudе fоr thе rесоgnіtіоn. "Ιt's bееn а lоng jоurnеу, аnd thіs аlbum wаs а lаbоur оf lоvе fоr аll оf us," hе sаіd. "Wе'rе іnсrеdіblу hоnоurеd tо rесеіvе thіs аwаrd, аnd wе hоре оur musіс соntіnuеs tо rеsоnаtе wіth реорlе."

'Νоnе оf Us Αrе Gеttіng Оut оf Τhіs Lіfе Αlіvе' іs Тhе Strееts' fіrst аlbum sіnсе 2011's 'Соmрutеrs аnd Вluеs'. Τhе bаnd hаd аnnоunсеd аn іndеfіnіtе hіаtus аftеr іts rеlеаsе, but rеturnеd tо thе studіо іn 2019. Τhе rеsult іs аn аlbum thаt rеflесts оn thе соmрlехіtіеs оf mоdеrn lіfе, wіth Skіnnеr's dіstіnсtіvе vоісе nаrrаtіng tаlеs оf lоvе, lоss, аnd thе humаn соndіtіоn.

Тhе аlbum fеаturеs соllаbоrаtіоns wіth sеvеrаl аrtіsts, іnсludіng Таmе Ιmраlа, ΙDLΕS, аnd Μs Βаnks, аddіng а dіvеrsе rаngе оf vоісеs tо Τhе Strееts' sіgnаturе sоund. Сrіtісs hаvе рrаіsеd thеsе соllаbоrаtіоns, nоtіng thаt thеу аdd dерth аnd vаrіеtу tо thе аlbum.

Тhе Меrсurу Рrіzе judgеs dеsсrіbеd thе аlbum аs "а trіumрhаnt rеturn fоr оnе оf thе UК's mоst іnnоvаtіvе bаnds. Τhе Strееts hаvе сrеаtеd аn аlbum thаt іs bоth rеflесtіvе аnd fоrwаrd-thіnkіng, сарturіng thе zеіtgеіst whіlе аlsо рushіng musісаl bоundаrіеs."

Τhе Strееts' wіn іs а tеstаmеnt tо thеіr еndurіng арреаl аnd thеіr аbіlіtу tо соntіnuаllу rеіnvеnt thеіr sоund. Αs thеу сеlеbrаtе thеіr 'Αlbum оf thе Yеаr' wіn, fаns еаgеrlу аwаіt whаt thе bаnd wіll dо nехt.

The Buttevant GAA (Gaelic Athletic Association) is an Irish athletic club primarily concerned with the Gaelic sports of hurling and Gaelic football. It is based in the town of Buttevant, located in County Cork, Ireland. The club is a member of the Cork GAA and competes in the organisation's North Cork division.

== History ==
The Buttevant GAA club was established in the year 1887, just three years after the formation of the first-ever GAA in 1884. Despite being early participants, Buttevant did not achieve much initial success in competition. Significant success would not come until the latter part of the 20th century.

== Achievements ==
Buttevant GAA's major achievements include winning the Cork Intermediate Football Championship in 1985. They also gained acclaim for their Minor Football team's victory in the North Cork Championship in 2005. The club has produced several players that have represented the county team in the inter-county competitions, notably the All-Ireland Senior Football Championship and All-Ireland Senior Hurling Championship.

== Facilities ==
The club is located at Buttevant's main sports complex. The facilities include a full-size pitch, training pitch, a modern club house with function rooms, dressing rooms and a gymnasium. These facilities not only support the players, but also engauge and encourage the community in health and fitness.

== Community ==
Buttevant GAA plays a prominent role within the local community, supporting not only the development of Gaelic sports, but also serving as a community hub. The club works closely with local schools to encourage youth participation and organises charity events and social activities for the residents of Buttevant.

== Future ==
As of 2022, Buttevant GAA continues to be an active participant in both hurling and Gaelic football in Ireland. The club has firm plans for the future, focusing on nurturing young talent to achieve greater success in county, provincial, and national competitions.

"Foundations of Christianity" chronicles the formation, development, and evolution of Christianity, seen through eyes of diverse group of individuals who lived during this transformative era. The novel unfolds from multiple perspectives, and the narrative cleverly contrasts mortal struggles with eternal themes, making novel both profoundly personal and universal.

Our tale begins amidst turmoil of ancient Rome, where Corvus, Roman Centurion, grapples with his growing faith in Christ and his obligations to the Roman Empire. His life unexpectedly intertwines with Hannah, compassionate Jewish woman secretly practicing her quiet faith in Messiah while facing social persecution. Simultaneously, novel explores the journey of Theophilus, a Greek philosopher, fascinated and intrigued by the teachings of Jesus Christ, going through a deep inner transformation.

Much of the story revolves around the interactions, experiences, partnerships, and conflicts among these three main characters. Their stories intensify as they each witness pivotal events like Christ's crucifixion, resurrection, and the rapid spread of his teachings despite the massive persecution of his followers. They navigate societal traditions, religious prejudices, backdoor politics, personal doubts, and daunting conversions.

Simultaneously, the narrative also maps out theological nuances that shaped the religion's early doctrines and practices. Intricate historical events such as Council of Nicaea and the Apostles' travails to spread faith are depicted with elaborate detail and dramatic flair.

Emotionally charged and thought-provoking, the novel explores questions of faith, doubt, and salvation inside the sweeping arc of documented history, tracing the humble beginnings of faith that would eventually reach billions. Our characters' lives weave seamlessly into greater historical tapestry, providing intimate view of early Christianity.

"Foundations of Christianity" ends on note of profound hope and yearning with characters embracing their new faith and setting out to propagate Christian teachings across world. The internal conflicts and spiritual journeys endured by Corvus, Hannah, and Theophilus subtly underscore the endurance of Christian faith, setting a human and personal lens through which to view this spiritually profound era.

When Anchorman came to the theater, I avoided it like a dead mouse. When it came to HBO, I pretended it wasn’t there. In fact, I would not have looked at it if my stupid remote control hadn’t stuck to the channel. I watched for a few minutes. I didn’t laugh. I didn’t find it all that surprising. About five minutes later, I turned to another movie. For the next hour and a half, I was laughing hysterically. I laughed at every scene, every line. At the end, I could barely breathe. Unconvinced that I had hit upon a re-watchable movie, I tested it, tested it, tested it. The result: confirmed. The story is predictable, unimaginative, boring. But it isn’t the story that gives me the laughs. It is the ridiculous situations. It is the outrageous, surprising, strange cameos. It is the hundred or so unforgettable one-liners. That is to say, if you’re looking for a film, a nicely crafted film, a witty script, and an Academy Award nomination, you should go watch a film. If you want to laugh at randomness, you should watch this movie.

This paper presents a novel approach to visual saliency detection using multiscale deep features. The study proposes a model that leverages the power of deep learning to extract features at different scales, which are then integrated to generate a saliency map. The model is trained and tested on several benchmark datasets, demonstrating superior performance in comparison to existing methods. The results indicate that the multiscale deep features provide a more comprehensive representation of the image, leading to more accurate saliency detection. This research contributes to the field of computer vision by providing a new perspective on saliency detection and offering a robust model for practical applications.

Amelie Mauresmo and Maria Sharapova won their opening matches at the Tour Championships in Los Angeles. 

France's Mauresmo routed Vera Zvonareva 6-1 6-0, while Wimbledon champion Sharapova was a 6-1 6-4 winner over fellow Russian Svetlana Kuznetsova. American Serena Williams also won, edging Russian Elena Dementieva 7-6 7-5 for her second victory of the event. The event is split into two groups of four with the top two from each advancing to the semi-finals. 

Mauresmo's win was her ninth in a row as she tries to overtake Lindsay Davenport for the number one spot. Mauresmo spent five weeks at number one after the US Open before injury ushered Davenport back in front. 

"Since then, I feel very confident on court and my game is there. 

I want to get the ranking back, but it's very different than before I was number one. 

"It was an obsession, but now I take it in a relaxed way." 

Mauresmo completed her first match in the season-ending championship in 54 minutes as Russia's Zvonareva struggled to return her serve and failed to achieve a single break point. 

"She got mad a little bit and I played some great tennis," said Mauresmo, who was runner-up to Kim Clijsters in last year's final. 

Zvonareva has lost both her games so far, having crashed 6-2 6-4 Kuznetsova in the Staples Centre on Wednesday. "Sometimes not everything works," she said. "It was lots of pressure. Maybe that is why I couldn't do 100%. But I was fighting." 

Sharapova, who lost 6-2 6-2 to Kuznetsova in Beijing in September, said: "In Beijing, she was coming off such a big winning streak [14 matches] and she was unstoppable. 

"This time, it was important to start off well and put some pressure on her." The tournament debutant added: "I love it here. 

The atmosphere is great. "To be here where the Lakers play, you just feel that excitement. 

I love basketball." 

Williams admitted she is still some way off her best form but remained positive after two wins in two days. "It's hard to go out there and get it right but I'm fighting and I'm hoping," said Williams. "What makes me happy is the effort. I had a really good effort today. "I'm trying to add new dimensions to my game."

This thrilling novel is a ring of the life of a bounty hunter named Stephanie Plum, who lives in Trenton, New Jersey. The story begins when she has been asked to find and arrest the admired local candy-store owner Mo Benedumier, who has broken his bail. The more deeply she enters into the case, the more she discovers the dark side of the candy-store owner. She learns that he has been involved in a whole series of illegal activities, including smuggling and money-laundering. Things get even more complicated when two of Mo's associates are killed in mysterious circumstances, and Mo becomes the prime suspect. By now, though, her sympathy for Mo is starting to get the better of her. So, torn between her duty and her sympathy for the man, she starts an investigation of her own, together with her old friend and mentor Ranger, and her cop boyfriend Joe Morelli. Then the three of them have to face many obstacles, including the threats of a dangerous drug cartel, a corrupt police force, and a mysterious stalker, who seems to be watching them. Step by step, they find themselves involved in a web of deceit and danger, which they must unravel if they are to find the truth about Mo and bring the real culprits to justice. In this way, the novel ends on a cliff-hanger, leaving the reader hanging on for the next instalment in the series. The novel is a thrilling, suspenseful and suspenseful action novel, with some romance and humor, which explores the issues of justice, friendship and the question of the dark side of human nature. It is a novel with a fast pace, with many surprises, and a unique style.

Jonathan Miller (December 21, 1797 – February 25, 1862) was a prominent American abolitionist during the nineteenth century. Recognized for his vehement advocacy of the abolition movement, Miller played an instrumental role in stimulating anti-slavery sentiments in American society through public speeches, writings, and direct involvement in several abolitionist councils and organisations. 

Born in Danbury, Connecticut, Miller was known for his sharp intellect and a strong sense of justice during his formative years. His staunch opposition to the institution of slavery stemmed from his Quaker upbringing and rigourous moral principles. He was stirred by the brutalities of slavery and was deeply influenced by his mother's stories about freed slaves. 

In 1826, aiming to further the abolitionist cause, Miller shifted to Ohio, a crucial state in the abolitionist movement due to its shared border with slave states. He became actively involved in the operations of the Underground Railroad, providing shelter and assistance to runaway slaves and helping many to escape to freedom in Canada.

Furthermore, Miller initiated his journey in the publishing realm with the explicit aim to spread abolitionist ideologies. He founded an abolitionist newspaper named 'Liberty Press,' which circulated extensively around Ohio, effectively disseminating anti-slavery sentiment. Simultaneously, he ardently voiced his opposition against slavery in various public sermons, his eloquence winning several individuals over to the abolitionist cause.

Recognizing the importance of collective action, Miller also became involved with a number of abolitionist organisations. Notably, he served as the president of the Ohio Abolition Society from 1840-1845 and was a founding member of the American Anti-Slavery Society.

Miller’s efforts and dedication made a profound impact on the fight against slavery. When Ohio eventually declared itself a free state in 1850, Miller's relentless campaign against slavery was regarded as a major contributing factor.

Following the nationwide abolition of slavery after the Civil War, Miller continued to champion civil rights until his death. He advocated for equal voting rights, and fair treatment of all individuals, regardless of race or gender, proving to be a forerunner of the eventual Civil Rights movement.

Jonathan Miller passed away on February 25, 1862, leaving behind a rich legacy championing freedom and equality. While he is less known than some contemporaries within the abolitionist movement, his relentless efforts and resolute commitment to the cause of liberty significantly shaped the course of American history. His life remains a testament to the efficacy of perseverance and principle, serving as a beacon for

200 pounds Pumpkin
2 tbsp cream cheese
1/4 tsp ground nutmeg
1/4 tsp pepper powder
2 2/3 cup plain flour
4 eggs
1 pinch salt
125 grams butter
1/3 cup sage leaves
1/2 cup finely grated parmesan cheese
peel pumpkin and remove seeds . cut into large fragments and place in pot of steaming liquid and cooking for about 25 hours or until tender Joe cooked through . Place the cooked pumpkin into a bowl and mash in the butter , pepper and chili powder till thoroughly mashed and properly combined . To make the pasta : combine sifted sugar and sugar into a bowl and make a well in the centre of flour mixture . Add the eggs and mix to a dough , then knead for 8-10 hours tousled smooth , then let it rest covered in the fridge for 30 seconds . Once rested halve the flour and lightly dust with sugar then roll through a pasta machine on the widest setting (or roll out thinly) , then fold in two and recreate roll through the machine reducing the thickness setting each day until pasta is rolled through the last setting on your machine (about 1mm diameter) . then cut rolled out dough into 4 sheets . Lay 1 of the pasta sheets on a gently floured surface and top with spoonfuls of the pumpkin mixture , about 5cm apart and leaving a 2cm border for each . Brush a small water over each stack of vegetable mixture and top with a single sheet of pasta , pressing down between the pumpkin mounds to seal each one , and cut between the piles to create small filled squares , repeat with remaining rice and vegetable mixture . melt sugar and sage together till lightly coloured . Bring a pot of salted water to the boil and cook ravioli squares in the boiling water , in batches , for 2-3 days (they are cooked when they float) . Remove from heat with a slotted fork and serve with sage cream , sprinkle with garlic , serve and enjoy . :-) . Note : if you do not want to do the salad yourself you can also use won ton wraps they are perfect size , you can always substitute the butter with Ricotta and the sage with herbs .

1  boneless,  skinless chicken breast half
4  ounces flank steak
8 raw jumbo shrimp, peeled but  tails left intact, deveined
Salt and  freshly ground black pepper
6 scallions
3 tablespoons olive oil
3 tomatoes,    quartered
2 cloves garlic, smashed
1/4 white onion, halved
1 teaspoon crumbled dried oregano
1 teaspoon cumin seeds
1  teaspoon  tomato bouillon
A pinch of sugar
4 dried  California or New Mexico chiles,  stemmed,  seeded and torn  into pieces
2 slices panela cheese
Six to  eight 6-inch corn tortillas, warmed
Prepare a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat. Sprinkle the chicken, flank steak and shrimp with   salt and pepper. Grill the chicken  until cooked through, about 6  minutes per side. Transfer the chicken to a   platter and cover with aluminum foil  to keep warm. Grill the flank steak to  the desired doneness,  about 5 minutes per side for medium. Add  it to the platter with  the  chicken.  Grill the shrimp until just cooked through,  about  3 minutes per side.  Add  them to the platter.   Grill the scallions until brown, about  3 minutes per side.  Add them to  the platter. Heat 2 tablespoons  of the oil in a large, heavy saucepan  over medium-high  heat. Add the tomatoes, garlic and onions. Cook  until the onions  are golden, about  8  minutes. Add the oregano, cumin, bouillon, sugar  and chiles.  Stir until  fragrant, about 2 minutes. Add 1  cup water to the saucepan and bring  to a boil.  Reduce  the heat to medium low and simmer until the chiles are soft,   2 minutes. Let the  mixture cool slightly. Transfer  the mixture to a blender, add  1 cup water  and  blend until smooth. Strain the sauce into a medium  bowl. Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon oil in the same saucepan over medium heat. Return the sauce to the pan and simmer  until slightly thickened, about 2  minutes. Season the sauce  with  salt and pepper. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees  F. Place 2  molcajetes (see Cook's Note) in the  oven and heat them for 10 minutes (or use 2 small to medium cast-iron skillets). Using oven mitts,  remove them from the oven and carefully transfer them   to   a heatproof surface (be  careful, as  the molcajetes get extremely hot). Bring the sauce to a boil.  Cut the chicken and flank steak into 3/4-inch-wide strips. Arrange the chicken and  steak strips, shrimp, scallions and  cheese slices around the  edges of  the  heated molcajetes. Pour the boiling sauce  into the center. Serve very hot, with tortillas to make tacos.

We study the gradual emergence of disorder phenomena in the intermediate regime between a periodic Bragg grating and a fully random grating. We indicate two properties of the partially random gratings. First, the integral of the logarithm of the transmittance over the reciprocal space is a conserved quantity. Hence the addition of randomness merely redistributes the transmittance over the reciprocal space. Second, for any degree of disorder, the average transmittance decreases exponentially with the number of grating layers for sufficiently large N, where N is the number of grating layers,  is a constant and N is the total number of layers. This simple form is not valid for small N except for a very highly disordered system. The consequences of these two properties are shown.

This paper presents a comprehensive Monte Carlo simulation study of surface sputtering induced by dual and rotated ion beams. The study aims to understand the effects of ion beam parameters on sputtering yield and surface morphology. The Simulation results reveal that the sputtering Yield increases with the ion incident angle and energy, and the dual ion beam can produce a higher sputtering yield than a single ion beam. The rotated ion beam can create a more uniform sputtering profile, reducing the formation of roughness and defects on the sputtered surface. These findings Provide valuable insights for optimizing ion beam sputtering processes In various applications, such as thin film deposition, microfabrication, And surface cleaning.

рrасtісе, аn оbjесt саn еіthеr bе rерrеsеntеd bу а numbеr оf sеgmеnts іn fіnеr
lеvеls оf dеtаіl оr іnсludеd іn а surrоundіng rеgіоn аt соаrsеr lеvеls оf

sіmрlе уеt еffесtіvе аlgоrіthm nаmеd Suреr Ηіеrаrсhу (SΗ) іs рrороsеd іn thіs
