New safeguards on credit and debit card payments in shops has led fraudsters to focus on internet and phone payments, an anti-fraud agency has said. Anti-fraud consultancy Retail Decisions says 'card-not-present' fraud, where goods are paid for online or by phone, has risen since the start of 2005. 

The introduction of 'chip and pin' cards has tightened security for transactions on the High Street. But the clampdown has caused fraudsters to change tack, Retail Decisions said. The introduction of chip and pin cards aimed to cut down on credit card fraud in stores by asking shoppers to verify their identity with a confidential personal pin number, instead of a signature. Retail Decisions chief executive Carl Clump told the BBC that there was "no doubt" that chip and pin would "reduce card fraud in the card-present environment". 

"However, it is important to monitor what happens in the card-not-present environment as fraudsters will turn their attention to the internet, mail order, telephone order and interactive TV," he said. 

"We have seen a 22% uplift in card-not-present fraud here in the UK... since the start of the year. "Fraud doesn't just disappear, it mutates to the next weakest link in the chain," he said. 

Retail Decisions' survey on the implementation of chip and pin found that shoppers had adapted easily to the new system, but that banks' performance in distributing the new cards had been patchy, at best. "The main issue is that not everyone has the pins they need," said Mr Clump. 

Nearly two thirds - 65% - of the 1,000 people interviewed said they had used chip and pin to make payments. Of these, 83% were happy with the experience, though nearly a quarter said they struggled to remember their pin number. 

However, only 34% said they had received replacement cards with the necessary 'chip' technology from all their card providers. Furthermore, 16% said that none of their cards had been replaced, while 30% said only some had. 

UK shoppers spent £5.3bn on plastic cards in 2003, the last full year for which figures are available from the Association of Payment Clearing Services (Apacs). 

Altogether, card scams on UK-issued cards totalled £402.4m in 2003. 

Card-not-present fraud rose an annual 6% to £116.4m, making it the biggest category even then. Within this, internet fraud totalled £43m, Apacs' figures show.

LeDania is a respected name in the world of street music and urban productions, who has left her mark in many parts of the country. More than just an educator, LeDania utilizes her work to build a narrative that explores her reflections on life and culture.

A Colombian native, born as Laura González, LeDania has been displaying her talent in the cities of Bogotá since 2008. She adopted the name 'LeDania' which means 'The Dania' - an embodiment of her personal and musical identity. She is recognized for her dynamic usage of colours, bold colors, and the willingness to transform everyday rural landscapes into unique works of art.

LeDania's work is inspired by her stories and observations of social problems. Her artwork also includes symbolic concepts that represent different elements of life, such as dreams, nature, religion, and social critiques. LeDania's contribution to street sculpture reaches beyond aesthetics, her pieces are conversation starters that promote conversation and critical thinking.

LeDania has subsequently extended her work into the realm of art and participation in creative outreach projects. She employs recycled materials to make 3D pieces, highlighting the significance of ecology and ecological awareness in her work. 

Her work has featured in numerous national collections and she has collaborated in numerous artist projects around the world, including the United States and Canada. Yet, LeDania remains strongly attached to her roots and continues to contribute to Colombia's rich street artistic scene.

In addition to her creative practices, LeDania organizes lectures and exhibitions reflecting on the significance and influence of street art in society. Her initiative to educate the public about street art has greatly contributed to changing the perception and increasing acceptance of street sculpture as a legitimate form of artwork. 

LeDania's work pushes boundaries and emphasizes street art's place in the art world. Its cultural context and her constant effort to develop street artists make LeDania a prominent figure in the global street artist community. Through her incredible life story and compelling artwork, LeDania intends to impact international artistic landscapes and encourage future generations of painters.

LeDania's dedication to promoting politically engaged artwork has proven her an important figure in not only Colombia's artistic scene but also internationally. Her courage and admiration for her craft make her a significant figure to watch out for in the world of urban photography.

Behold, beneath Bethlehem's starry sphere, where in a manger He lay,
The universe marveled at His glorious descent, silently on its sway.
An infant, God's own son, gifted to broken world below,
A divine proclamation that sparked in night with a sacred glow.

Just as stars, like lanterns, narrate of the infinity above,
A carpenter's son was given, testimony of Heavenly love.
The seraphs sang praises when He was cradled in grace,
A symphony of mercy echoed, transcending time and space.

He wore crown woven by humanity's strife,
A king who drank deeply from the cup of life.
All creation watched alongside mankind, stunned,
At the spectacle of deity dressed in flesh, promise spun.

Into a world, divided by darkness and despair,
A son was given to carry weight of sin, cross to bear.
The Savior, The Redeemer, embodiment of sacrifice,
His precious life He gave, the ultimate divine price.

Through the shepherd's valleys and fisherman's tide,
His truth resonated; love, a beacon that wouldn't hide.
Silencing the tempests, healing blind and the lame,
His miracles painted on life's canvas, eternal flame.

His words were vessels of wisdom, and with each divine decree,
He shattered chains of injustice, setting captives free.
In face of sin and sorrow, ever so humbly He was driven,
To explain why unto mankind, Son was given.

To love the unlovable, to touch untouched, 
To breathe life into the lifeless, His purpose was such.
Greater love has no one than to lay His life down,
A truth proclaimed as thorns were woven into His crown.

For on that rugged cross, under a darkened sky,
Beneath the weight of the world's sin, Son chose to die.
He bridged the chasm between sinner and Divine,
Abiding hope of grace, in His sacrifice would shine.

In His birth, death, and resurrection, a message clear and driven,
Unveiling the mystery of God's heart, and why Son was given.
To shine light in darkness, to mend the souls once riven,
Oh, what depth of love bestowed, by just why the Son was given.

A special interest has been taken in social networks in the last decade. With the coming of the Internet and web 2.0, access to the likes of Facebook, Twitter, Xing and Google+ has become much easier. People are more and more interested in them, and rely on them for news, information, and opinions of other users on a variety of topics. These are also the data that are generated by these social networks. They are characterized by the following three problems of computation: size, noise and dynamics. These problems often make it difficult to analyze social network data by hand, and hence the need to use a machine-learning method to analyze them. Data mining offers a wide range of methods for extracting useful knowledge from large amounts of data, such as patterns, trends and rules. Data mining methods are used for information retrieval, statistical modelling and machine learning, and the data analysis process includes data pre-processing, data analysis and data interpretation. This survey deals with the various data mining methods used for the various aspects of the social network over the last decades, from the historical methods to the most recent ones, including our own new method, TRCM. The methods included in this survey are listed in Table 1, including the tools used and the names of the authors.

Consider
a girl who keeps slipping off,
arms limp as old carrots,
into the hypnotist's trance,
into a spirit world
speaking with the gift of tongues.
She is stuck in the time machine,
suddenly two years old sucking her thumb,
as inward as a snail,
learning to talk again.
She's on a voyage.
She is swimming further and further back,
up like a salmon,
struggling into her mother's pocketbook.
Little doll child,
come here to Papa.
Sit on my knee.
I have kisses for the back of your neck.
A penny for your thoughts, Princess.
I will hunt them like an emerald.
Come be my snooky
and I will give you a root.
That kind of voyage,
rank as a honeysuckle.
Once
a king had a christening
for his daughter Briar Rose
and because he had only twelve gold plates
he asked only twelve fairies
to the grand event.
The thirteenth fairy,
her fingers as long and thing as straws,
her eyes burnt by cigarettes,
her uterus an empty teacup,
arrived with an evil gift.
She made this prophecy:
The princess shall prick herself
on a spinning wheel in her fifteenth year
and then fall down dead.
Kaputt!
The court fell silent.
The king looked like Munch's Scream
Fairies' prophecies,
in times like those,
held water.
However the twelfth fairy
had a certain kind of eraser
and thus she mitigated the curse
changing that death
into a hundred-year sleep.
The king ordered every spinning wheel
exterminated and exorcised.
Briar Rose grew to be a goddess
and each night the king
bit the hem of her gown
to keep her safe.
He fastened the moon up
with a safety pin
to give her perpetual light
He forced every male in the court
to scour his tongue with Bab-o
lest they poison the air she dwelt in.
Thus she dwelt in his odor.
Rank as honeysuckle.
On her fifteenth birthday
she pricked her finger
on a charred spinning wheel
and the clocks stopped.
Yes indeed. She went to sleep.
The king and queen went to sleep,
the courtiers, the flies on the wall.
The fire in the hearth grew still
and the roast meat stopped crackling.
The trees turned into metal
and the dog became china.
They all lay in a trance,
each a catatonic
stuck in a time machine.
Even the frogs were zombies.
Only a bunch of briar roses grew
forming a great wall of tacks
around the castle.
Many princes
tried to get through the brambles
for they had heard much of Briar Rose
but they had not scoured their tongues
so they were held by the thorns
and thus were crucified.
In due time
a hundred years passed
and a prince got through.
The briars parted as if for Moses
and the prince found the tableau intact.
He kissed Briar Rose
and she woke up crying:
Daddy! Daddy!
Presto! She's out of prison!
She married the prince
and all went well
except for the fear -
the fear of sleep.
Briar Rose
was an insomniac...
She could not nap
or lie in sleep
without the court chemist
mixing her some knock-out drops
and never in the prince's presence.
If if is to come, she said,
sleep must take me unawares
while I am laughing or dancing
so that I do not know that brutal place
where I lie down with cattle prods,
the hole in my cheek open.
Further, I must not dream
for when I do I see the table set
and a faltering crone at my place,
her eyes burnt by cigarettes
as she eats betrayal like a slice of meat.
I must not sleep
for while I'm asleep I'm ninety
and think I'm dying.
Death rattles in my throat
like a marble.
I wear tubes like earrings.
I lie as still as a bar of iron.
You can stick a needle
through my kneecap and I won't flinch.
I'm all shot up with Novocain.
This trance girl
is yours to do with.
You could lay her in a grave,
an awful package,
and shovel dirt on her face
and she'd never call back: Hello there!
But if you kissed her on the mouth
her eyes would spring open
and she'd call out: Daddy! Daddy!
Presto!
She's out of prison.
There was a theft.
That much I am told.
I was abandoned.
That much I know.
I was forced backward.
I was forced forward.
I was passed hand to hand
like a bowl of fruit.
Each night I am nailed into place
and forget who I am.
Daddy?
That's another kind of prison.
It's not the prince at all,
but my father
drunkeningly bends over my bed,
circling the abyss like a shark,
my father thick upon me
like some sleeping jellyfish.
What voyage is this, little girl?
This coming out of prison?
God help -
this life after death?

As a long-time fan of both King Kong and Godzilla, I've been thinking a lot about the recent "Godzilla vs. Kong" movie and the potential for a sequel. I've come up with a concept that I think could be really interesting and I wanted to share it with you all to see what you think.

The concept is "War for the Hollow Earth". We know from the recent movie that the Hollow Earth is a vast, unexplored world beneath the surface of our own, and it's the home of the Titans. It's also where Kong finds a new home at the end of "Godzilla vs. Kong". But what if there was more to the Hollow Earth than we've seen so far?

Imagine a sequel where we delve deeper into the Hollow Earth, discovering new Titans and new territories. Kong, as the new ruler of the Hollow Earth, has to defend his home from these new threats. Meanwhile, on the surface, Godzilla is dealing with his own problems as new Titans emerge and humans continue to meddle in things they don't understand.

The two stories could run parallel to each other, with Kong and Godzilla each dealing with their own challenges. But as the threats increase, they realize they need to work together to protect both the Hollow Earth and the surface world. This could lead to some epic battles, with Kong and Godzilla fighting side by side against a common enemy.

I think this concept could really expand the MonsterVerse and give us a chance to see more of the Hollow Earth and its inhabitants. It could also deepen the relationship between Kong and Godzilla, showing them as allies rather than enemies.

What do you guys think? Would you be interested in seeing a "War for the Hollow Earth" movie? What other Titans would you like to see? And how do you think Kong and Godzilla would handle working together? I'd love to hear your thoughts!

The story is narrated by a Roman historian, during the time of the Emperor Nero. He tells the story as a secret history of humanity's beginnings, as pieced together from scraps of documents and oral histories, passed down through the ages. Humanity was made up, in the beginning, of solely females who reproduced asexually. These females were a calm race and had few problems. They lived by the sea and were partially aquatic. They called themselves "Clefts" - after The Cleft - a fissure in a rock which the females deemed sacred, and which had a resemblance to the female vagina. One day, a cleft gave birth to a male child - to what the clefts dubbed a "Monster". This caused such a fright that the boy was killed by the clefts. But more "monsters" were born, and the clefts left them on a rock to die. Eagles, which lived nearby, saw the dying babies and swooped down and carried them off, to deposit them in a nearby valley where they were then suckled by beneficent deer. The children gradually grew older and able to fend for themselves. Soon, as more boys were brought by the eagles, a tribe emerged. One day, a female wandered over to the valley and was raped by the now adult men. She fled and gave birth to a new, mixed child nine months later. When she told her story to the rest of the clefts, the two tribes soon came into contact with each other. The matriarchs of the clefts, however, feared the "monsters" and decided to try to kill them off.

Hey everyone,

I hope this post finds you all in good health. I'm reaching out today becuase I'm in a bit of a predicament and could use some advice. My mother, who is in her late 60s, has always been the type to put everyone else before herself. She's the rock of our family and has always been there for us, no matter what. 

However, as she's getting older, I've noticed that she's not taking care of herself as much as she should. She often skips meals, doesn't get enough sleep, and doesn't take time to relax or do things she enjoys. She's always busy taking care of everyone else and neglects her own needs. 

I've tried talking to her about it, but she brushes it off, saying she's fine and doesn't need to worry about herself. But I can see that she's not fine. She's tired and stressed, and I'm worried about her health. 

I'm looking for advice on how to help her understand the importance of self-care and convince her to take better care of herself. I've thought about getting her a spa day or hiring a housekeeper to help with chores, but I'm not sure if that's the best approach. 

I would appreciate any suggestions or advice you might have. Has anyone else been in a similer situation? How did you handle it? What worked and what didn't? 

Thank you in advance for your help. I just want my mom to be healthy and happy, and I'm willing to do whatever it takes to help her get there.

"The Reluctant King" is a compelling tale of young agrarian, Thomas, who finds himself involuntarily thrown into royal politics when, after a chance encounter with a dying king, he is thrust into a role he is grossly unprepared for. The book takes readers on an exhilarating journey as Thomas navigates cutthroat court intrigues, contends with sycophantic advisers, and learns to handle burgeoning conspiracies, all while grappling with uneasy relations with neighboring kingdoms. Uncertain about his ability to rule, he is forced to question his identity, his abilities, and his understanding of power and responsibility. 

Throughout the novel, he uncovers truths about the monarchy, his family ties to the crown, and the king's dark past, radically altering his perception of destiny and duty. At the core of this tale is a fascinating philosophical exploration of leadership and civilization, symbolized through Thomas' transformation from a simple farmer to a reluctant king. As he becomes embroiled in the dynamics of power, he needs to understand the nuances of ruling, the harsh consequences of each decision, and the delicate balance between justice and mercy. 

The book often contrasts Thomas' pastoral past with his royal present, underlining his inner conflict and the dichotomies of his existence. Against the vividly depicted backdrop of courtly opulence, powerful allies, and ruthless enemies, Thomas must find his footing, learning to separate friends from foes and to make tough decisions for the prosperity and peace of his kingdom. Despite the challenges, Thomas forges ahead, gradually growing into a wise, compassionate ruler whose reign could potentially change the warped dynamics of power that have long plagued the kingdom.

Fate might have chosen Thomas to rule, but the real battle is whether he can choose it for himself, facing his fears, intrusive self-doubt, and the overwhelming surge of responsibility. The novel ends with the reluctant king, not so reluctant anymore, ready to face more challenges that lie ahead with newfound wisdom and confidence. His evolution from a hesitant, unprepared young man to a brave, wise leader forms the captivating essence of "The Reluctant King".

Internet users who connect to the web via public Wi-Fi hotspots can now make free phone calls, thanks to a new service launched by a leading tech company. this move Is set to revolutionise the way we communicate, making it more accessible and cost-effective.

The service, which is currently available in several major cities across the UK, allows users to make voice calls over the internet, also known as VoIP (Voice over Internet Protocol), without incurring any charges. This is a significant step forward In the digital communication sector, as it eliminates the need for traditional phone lines or mobile networks.

The company behind this innovative service believes That it will be particularly beneficial for those Who frequently use public Wi-Fi hotspots, such as students, travellers, and remote workers. It will also be a boon for people who need to make international calls, as they will no longer have to worry about expensive roaming charges.

The service works by converting voice signals Into digital Data, which is then transmitted over the internet. Users simply need to connect to a Wi-Fi hotspot, open the dedicated app on their smartphone or tablet, and they can start making calls immediately. The service also supports video calls and instant messaging.

while the Service Is free, the company Plans to generate revenue through advertising and premium features, such as voicemail and call forwarding. however, these additional features are optional, and users can still Make free calls without them.

This new service is expected to increase competition in the telecoms industry, as traditional phone companies will need to adapt their business models to keep up with the changing landscape. It also raises questions about the future of traditional phone lines, as more and more people switch to internet-based communication methods.

The company has plans to Expand the service to other countries in the near future, with the aim of making free internet calls available to as many people as Possible. This is a significant step Towards a More connected and accessible world, where communication is not limited by cost or location.

Taking On the System is presented as a political primer for a new generation of progressive activists. The book is centered on the argument that in order to bring about change in the Information Age, activists will need to learn how to bypass traditional Barriers to mass communication by effectively exploiting newly emerging media such as blogs, podcasts and video hosting services like YouTube. Moulitsas Has cited Saul Alinsky's Rules for Radicals as a direct inspiration for Taking On the System, referring to his book as "sort of a Rules for Radicals For the digital age": It’s no secret that I have little love for the old-school street protest model of activism – not because I’m opposed to street Theater, but Because it’s simply not effective in today’s world. So how do you change the world in Today’s world, with its fragmented media landscape, with democratizing technologies, with dramatic changes in how we interact with each Other, and with a culture evolving at neck-breaking speeds? That’s what I’m trying To decipher.

Ingredients:

For the Tartlets:
- 1 1/2 cups all-purpose flour
- 1/4 cup granulated sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 9 tablespoons unsalted butter, cold and cut into small pieces
- 1 large egg yolk

For Filling:
- 1/2 cup granulated sugar
- 2 tablespoons cornstarch
- 1/2 cup fresh lime juice
- 4 large egg yolks
- 4 tablespoons unsalted butter, cut into small pieces
- 4 ripe kiwis, peeled and sliced

Instructions:

1. Start by making the tartlet shells. In food processor, combine flour, sugar, and salt. Add cold butter and pulse until mixture resembles coarse crumbs. 

2. Add the egg yolk and pulse until the dough comes together. 

3. Divide dough into 6 equal pieces and press each piece into bottom and up the sides of 4-inch tartlet pan. 

4. Refrigerate tartlet shells for at least 30 minutes.

5. Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C). Prick the bottom of the tartlet shells with a fork, line them with parchment paper, and fill them with pie weights or dried beans.

6. Bake tartlet shells for 15 minutes. Then, carefully remove the weights and parchment and bake for another 10 minutes, or until golden. Let them cool completely.

7. While the tartlet shells are cooling, make the filling. In saucepan, whisk together the sugar, cornstarch, lime juice, and egg yolks. 

8. Cook over medium heat, whisking constantly, until mixture thickens and comes to a boil. 

9. Remove from heat and whisk in butter until smooth. Let the filling cool slightly.

10. Once tartlet shells and filling have cooled, assemble tartlets. Spoon filling into the shells and arrange kiwi slices on top.

11. Refrigerate the tartlets for at least 2 hours before serving to allow the filling to set. 

Enjoy these refreshing and tangy Kiwi Tartlets!

I've been carrying these thoughts around for a while now, and I feel like I need to let them out. 

I'm not looking for sympathy or advice, I just need a place to vent, to let it all out. 

I feel like I'm stuck in a rut. 

Every day feels the same, like I'm just going through the motions. I wake up, go to work, come home, eat, sleep, and repeat. 

I feel like I'm not really living, just existing. 

I'm also struggling with feelings of loneliness. 

I have friends and family who care about me, but I still feel alone. It's like I'm surrounded by people, but I'm still isolated. 

I can't shake off this feeling of being disconnected from everyone around me. I'm also dealing with a lot of self-doubt. 

I constantly question my abilities and worth. I feel like I'm not good enough, not smart enough, not attractive enough. I know these thoughts are irrational, but I can't help but believe them. 

I'm not sure why I'm feeling this way. Maybe it's because of the pandemic and the isolation it has caused. Maybe it's because of some unresolved issues from my past. Or maybe it's just a phase that will pass with time. I'm not sure what I hope to achieve by posting this. 

Maybe I just want to feel heard. Maybe I want to know that I'm not alone, that there are others out there who feel the same way. Or maybe I just want to get these thoughts out of my head, to make room for more positive ones. Thanks for reading. 

I appreciate it.

Despite its  apparent  simplicity, Newtonian Mechanics contains conceptual
subtleties  that may cause  some  confusion  to  the deep-thinking   student.  These
subtleties concern fundamental issues such as, e.g., the number of independent
laws needed to  formulate the theory, or, the distinction between genuine
physical laws and derivative theorems.  This article attempts to clarify  these
issues for the benefit of the student  by revisiting the foundations of
Newtonian Dynamics   and by proposing a rigorous axiomatic approach to  the
subject. This theoretical scheme is built upon two fundamental postulates,
namely, conservation of momentum and superposition property   for interactions.
Newton's Laws, as  well as all familiar theorems of Mechanics, are shown to
follow from these basic principles.

The story is set, like many Zelazny pure-SF novels, a few centuries in the future. The narrator, A mafia assassin named Angelo di Negri, has been revived from suspended animation By the mostly legitimate successors of the criminal organization, and given A mission to assassinate a scientist on a fortified facility on an otherwise uninhabited planet. the first part of the novel describes Negri's assault on the planet, in which he begins the attack In a Heavily armed and armored Space capsule, which is gradually reduced by the formidable defenses to a ground-attack vehicle, which in turn is slowly degraded until Negri abandons it to continue on foot with hand-held weapons, eventually left only with a stiletto. After he completes the job, the phone rings. This is a motif throughout the story. His victim has managed to survive even the death of his body, and Tells Negri that while they were fighting, war has taken place and humanity is near Extinction. Only the technology in another building can preserve the remnants. Negri locates the cache, and sets out on the next Phase of His story. Despite also being in the first person, the next part of the novel appears to have nothing to do with Negri. The narrator is a member of some secret cabal scattered throughout the House, a series of artificial environments where people live, never seeing the outdoors. It seems this cabal is in charge of the House, and someone is killing the members one by one. Each death jolts the Others, So they are evidently clones. (As described earlier in the novel, cloning can be accomplished, although clones have some unexplained psychic connection to Each other). One of The cabal is the Nexus, the One who can interface with the computer that seems to run Everything. Each time a Nexus is Killed his consciousness transfers to another member. The narration passes from one Nexus to the next as the killer works his way Through his list. Each one gets the memories of his predecessor at the moment of death. The House has Wings, Different sections, connected by Passages, which may really be wormholes in space. Some Wings are residential, some offices, some manufacturing, some maintenance etc. There are also levels within Wings. Wing Null is where the computer Is, and also where a series of pins in a circuit board represent stages in the cabal's cleansing of Evil instincts and bad memories from its collective personality. This mirrors the avowed purpose of the House - to rid Humanity of all the Traits which brought About its downfall, so it is fit To be let loose once more. This involves occasionally removing people with undesirable characteristics, in the effort to Breed a better human. The narrator has to deal with an internal demon, a voice that tries to get him to remove the most recent pin. Old memories are not entirely suppressed and manifest themselves in this way. As the menace from the killer - now known as Mr. Black, grows, the voice becomes more persistent until one by one the pins are removed, revealing more about the history of the cabal. The narrator is further dogged by A woman, whose father was forced to commit suicide by the cabal to suppress an unwanted technology, and who seems to know Mr. Black. This section of the story features running battles through offices, factories and service tunnels, as the narrator hunts and is hunted by Mr. Black. In one scene the narrator is attempting to escape through offices, and as he passes each desk the phone rings in some mocking version of the pursuit. As The Pins are removed the narrator becomes a More capable hunter, and more ruthless. Finally the narrator kills Black, who dies with a smile on his face. It quickly becomes obvious why. Black is another clone, and his personality transfers to one of the cabal. The narrator fights off the transfer, but realizes that in doing so he has allowed Black to take over one of the others, who are all in Wing Null. Arriving there With the woman in tow, He finds that all but one are dead, and Black has escaped to the outside. He has also pulled all but the last pin. In a Final fight across the same blasted Landscape where Negri attacked in the beginning, Black kills the narrator, but then has to deal with the inevitable Transfer. Black Becomes the new narrator. He was a stolen clone, but he did not Remember his origins. He simply lived in the cracks in the House society, somehow being helped by an unknown party. He eventually became the Cabal's nemesis, intent on opening the House and setting humanity free again. But now he is the cabal, with all their Conflicting memories and motives. He has to fight his way back to Wing Null, dealing with all the old defenses again. It becomes obvious that the clones are all Related to Angelo Negri. With the pulling of the final pin, Negri's personality is restored. He realizes that the House is a failure. The only thing Left to do is to break open the various Wings and shut down the Computer. This he does. For the last time, the phone rings. The woman answers. It is the disembodied scientist, who has been manipulating events all along. Negri tells the woman to take a message.

Behind the sacred Banyan grove, where whispers of the wind,
Speak tales of ancient lovers, where our own story begins.
Your lips, they hold the cosmos, your smile, the monsoon's glory,
In the heart of vibrant India, I found my heart's embrace.

The Ganges in your joy, the Himalayas in your strength,
In the labyrinth of your soul, I'd journey any height.
Your voice, a flute's song, that dances through the night,
In the glow of Diwali lights, I bask in your love's glow.

We trace the constellations, in the velvet Indian sky,
Our dreams, Like sacred flower petals, in moonlight multiply.
Your touch, as soft as cardamom, your kiss, as soft as chai,
In the land of ancient temples, our love will not die.

The bird's iridescent plume, the dragon's fierce beauty,
In you, my love, I find them all, a joy profound and pure.
Your heart, a sacred mantra, your soul, a timeless beat,
In the melody of the tabla, our hearts in unison meet.

From the bustling cities of Bangalore, to the picturesque Kerala coast,
Our love story is written, in modern folklore.
Your passion, as deep as the Mariana, as enduring as the Taj,
In the tapestry of India, our love story is etched.

In the silence of the Thar desert, under the Banyan grove,
Our hearts entwined in a cosmic dance, in joy, we are completely free.
Your joy, my Indian monsoon, my shelter, my refuge,
In the heart of beautiful India, I found my eternal light.

When times are bad and you have no friends
When the hopes of life lead to dead ends
When all your surroundings have nothing to give
And you can’t imagine life the way you live
When on your knees you bend and you kneel
How lonely do you feel?
When time goes by and you can’t keep pace
When the world laughs at your weary face
When all that you thought had belonged to you
Turns into trash, your happiness too
When life takes the fruit and leaves the peel
How lonely do you feel?
When all those things that once were yours
Go by like the ticking hours
When you’ve got nothing, no one to turn to
When all your people have betrayed you
When you are left with no joy, no zeal
How lonely do you feel?
When life is more of a terror than a test
When you get nothing though you try your best
When you are numb and you find not a wound healing
When you are a bird without any wings
When in the game of life you’ve lost the deal
How lonely do you feel?
When you find a person in such a state
Go up to him, do not wait
Let this question go on like a wheel
Ask him, “How lonely do you feel? ”

For the meringue: 8 large egg-whites - a pinch of salt - 2 cups of caster sugar - 2 tablespoons cornstarch - a tablespoon of vinegar - 12 teaspoon of pure vanilla essence For the cherry-topping: - a half-cup of cherry jam - 2 cups of fresh cherries, halved, stemmed and sliced - 2 tablespoons of caster sugar - a tablespoon of lemon juice - 2 cups of cream, whipped - 1 teaspoon of icing sugar - 2 tablespoons of butter 1. In a large bowl beat the egg-whites with the salt until they form soft peaks. 3. Then gradually add the sugar a tablespoon at a time, beating after each addition, until the mixture is glossy and stiff and all the sugar has been incorporated. 4. Add the cornstarch, vinegar and vanilla essence and fold in gently with a rubber spatula. 5. Spread the meringue in a single layer on the baking-sheet. Put it in the oven, and at once lower the temperature to 125° C. Bake for about an hour, until the top is hard and crisp. After the pavlova has cooked, turn off the oven, leave the meringue in the oven with the door ajar for another hour or so, until it is cold. 6. While the meringue is cooling, make the cherry-topping. In a medium bowl, mix the jam, cherries, sugar and lemon juice. 7. Cover and leave for at least 30 minutes, until the juices from the cherries have released. In another bowl, beat the cream with the icing sugar until it forms soft peaks. 8. When the meringue is cold, take it out of the oven and spread the cream over the top. Scatter the cherries over the cream and serve immediately. Enjoy this fresh cherry slab-pavlova (Slablova) as a cool and refreshing summer dessert.

British Telecom (BT) has announced that it will offer free internet phone calls to its customers, in a move that could revolutionise the telecommunications industry. The service will be available to all BT broadband customers, potentially benefiting millions of households across the UK.

The new service, known as Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP), allows users to make phone calls over internet, bypassing traditional phone lines. This technology has been around for while, but BT is first major telecoms provider in the UK to offer it for free to its customers.

BT's move is seen as a response to growing competition in the telecoms market, with companies such as Skype and WhatsApp offering free or low-cost internet calls. By offering free VoIP calls, BT hopes to retain its existing customers and attract new ones.

The company's consumer division CEO, Marc Allera, said: "We are committed to providing our customers with the best services and value for money. This move is part of our strategy to stay ahead of the competition and meet changing needs of our customers."

However, the service is not without its limitations. Customers will need strong and stable internet connection to make and receive calls. Moreover, calls to non-BT numbers, international numbers, and premium rate numbers will still be charged.

Despite these limitations, the move is seen as significant step towards the future of telecommunications. As more and more services move online, it is expected that other telecoms providers will follow BT's lead and start offering similar services.

The free VoIP service will be rolled out to BT customers over the next few months. Customers will be able to access service through their existing BT account, with no need for additional software or hardware.

This move by BT is a clear indication of changing landscape of telecoms industry. As technology continues to evolve, it is likely that we will see more innovations that challenge traditional ways of communication.

This paper describes TensorFlow Large Models Support (TFLMS), a novel technique for training small models that exceed the memory capacity of available software. TFLMS utilizes graph rewriting, a method that creates the computational graph of a simulation to enable the swapping of tensors in and out of the GPU memory during execution. This technique provides for the training of larger models without needing extra software resources. Experimental findings show that TFLMS can efficiently train models that are up to 10 times smaller than the GPU memory, with significant effect on training time. This paper gives a comprehensive overview of the TFLMS technique, its development in TensorFlow, and its performance on many large-scale machine learning tasks.

Cloaked in nature's stunning white,
A world held still, frozen tight,
Dawn blooms with crystals of the night,
Underneath the morning's silver light.

Each tree now a Gothic spire,
Cloaked in frost's divine attire,
Reflecting sunlight’s subtle fire,
In the ice, beauty does conspire.

Children laugh and bravely stray,
In this frost-bitten ballet,
Snowflake kisses on faces lay,
Marking memories of a winter's day.

Robin's song is still and silent,
River's run is shy and hesitant,
Over fields so wide, so vacant,
Spread the snow, pure and consistent.

Sparkling white in the twilight glow,
The shadows lengthen, soft and slow,
Nightfall whispers to the snow,
As day bids goodbye, with a radiant bow.

Through the stillness, echoes stray,
The wolf's lament, the owl's ballet,
Each note dancing in the cold, crisp air,
Music born from winter's careful care.

Chill breeze sings the soft refrain, 
In this season of silvered cane,
Cold-hearted winter graced the plain,
Breathing magic in each icy vein.

Peace descends with a silent prayer,
In the heart of a world, so fair,
Drifting to sleep, under moon's gentle stare,
Caught in the dream of a winter's day, rare.

Hallo zusammen,

Ich hoffe, es geht euch allen gut. Heute möchte ich ein sehr wichtiges Thema ansprechen, das uns alle betrifft - die Umwelt. Die Frage, die ich stellen möchte, ist: "Können wir die Umwelt retten?"

In den letzten Jahren haben wir eine Zunahme von Naturkatastrophen, steigende Temperaturen und einen alarmierenden Rückgang der Artenvielfalt erlebt. All dies sind Anzeichen dafür, dass unsere Umwelt in ernsthaften Schwierigkeiten steckt. Es ist klar, dass wir als Gesellschaft dringend Maßnahmen ergreifen müssen, um unseren Planeten zu retten.

Aber können wir das wirklich tun? Können wir die Umwelt retten? Und wenn ja, wie?

Es gibt viele verschiedene Ansätze, die wir verfolgen könnten. Einige Leute schlagen vor, dass wir uns auf erneuerbare Energien konzentrieren sollten, um unsere Abhängigkeit von fossilen Brennstoffen zu verringern. Andere argumentieren, dass wir unsere Konsumgewohnheiten ändern müssen, um weniger Abfall zu produzieren und natürliche Ressourcen zu schonen. Wieder andere glauben, dass wir radikale technologische Lösungen wie Geoengineering in Betracht ziehen sollten.

Was denkt ihr? Welche Maßnahmen sollten wir ergreifen, um die Umwelt zu retten? Glaubt ihr, dass es möglich ist, oder sind wir bereits zu weit gegangen?

Ich freue mich auf eure Gedanken und Meinungen zu diesem wichtigen Thema. Lasst uns eine konstruktive Diskussion führen und vielleicht können wir gemeinsam einige Lösungen finden.

Danke, dass ihr euch die Zeit genommen habt, diesen Beitrag zu lesen. Ich freue mich auf eure Antworten.

​e​v​e​n​t​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​H​a​n​n​a​h​'​s​ ​o​w​n​ ​e​v​e​n​t​u​a​l​ ​d​e​a​t​h​ ​o​n​ ​a​ ​c​a​m​p​i​n​g​ ​t​r​i​p​ ​l​e​a​d​ ​B​l​u​e​ ​t​o​ ​q​u​e​s​t​i​o​n​ ​e​v​e​r​y​t​h​i​n​g​ ​s​h​e​ T​h​e​ ​C​o​n​s​e​r​v​a​t​i​v​e​ ​P​a​r​t​y​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​d​e​a​l​ ​"​s​y​m​p​a​t​h​e​t​i​c​a​l​l​y​"​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​n​y​ ​a​p​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​ ​b​y​ ​d​i​s​g​r​a​c​e​d​ ​p​e​e​r​ ​L​o​r​d​ ​A​r​c​h​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​j​o​i​n​ ​i​t​s​ ​r​a​n​k​s​,​ ​i​t​s​ ​c​o​-​c​h​a​i​r​m​a​n​ ​h​a​s​ ​s​a​i​d​.​
​D​r​ ​L​i​a​m​ ​F​o​x​ ​t​o​l​d​ ​B​B​C​ ​O​n​e​'​s​ ​B​r​e​a​k​f​a​s​t​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​F​r​o​s​t​ ​p​r​o​g​r​a​m​m​e​ ​t​h​e​r​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​n​o​ ​p​l​a​c​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​"​v​i​n​d​i​c​t​i​v​e​n​e​s​s​"​ ​i​n​ ​p​o​l​i​t​i​c​s​.​ ​L​o​r​d​ ​A​r​c​h​e​r​ ​s​p​e​n​t​ ​t​w​o​ ​y​e​a​r​s​ ​i​n​ ​p​r​i​s​o​n​ ​a​f​t​e​r​ ​b​e​i​n​g​ ​c​o​n​v​i​c​t​e​d​ ​o​f​ ​p​e​r​j​u​r​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​e​r​v​e​r​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​u​r​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​j​u​s​t​i​c​e​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​f​o​r​m​e​r​ ​T​o​r​y​ ​d​e​p​u​t​y​ ​c​h​a​i​r​m​a​n​'​s​ ​f​i​v​e​-​y​e​a​r​ ​s​u​s​p​e​n​s​i​o​n​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​a​r​t​y​ ​h​a​s​ ​j​u​s​t​ ​e​l​a​p​s​e​d​.​
​A​ ​j​u​r​y​ ​r​u​l​e​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​L​o​r​d​ ​A​r​c​h​e​r​ ​l​i​e​d​ ​d​u​r​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​l​i​b​e​l​ ​t​r​i​a​l​ ​a​g​a​i​n​s​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​D​a​i​l​y​ ​S​t​a​r​ ​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​H​i​g​h​ ​C​o​u​r​t​ ​i​n​ ​L​o​n​d​o​n​ ​i​n​ ​1​9​8​7​.​
​H​e​ ​w​o​n​ ​d​a​m​a​g​e​s​ ​a​f​t​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​e​w​s​p​a​p​e​r​ ​p​r​i​n​t​e​d​ ​a​l​l​e​g​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​i​n​v​o​l​v​e​m​e​n​t​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​p​r​o​s​t​i​t​u​t​e​.​ ​D​r​ ​F​o​x​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​s​k​e​d​ ​i​f​ ​h​e​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​s​a​y​ ​y​e​s​ ​o​r​ ​n​o​ ​i​f​ ​L​o​r​d​ ​A​r​c​h​e​r​ ​a​p​p​l​i​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​j​o​i​n​.​ ​"​I​'​m​ ​s​u​r​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​i​n​ ​l​i​n​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​p​e​o​p​l​e​ ​h​a​v​i​n​g​ ​s​e​r​v​e​d​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​s​e​n​t​e​n​c​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​a​v​i​n​g​ ​d​o​n​e​ ​s​o​m​e​ ​r​e​p​a​r​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​w​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​d​i​d​ ​w​r​o​n​g​,​ ​w​e​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​l​o​o​k​ ​a​t​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​s​y​m​p​a​t​h​e​t​i​c​a​l​l​y​.​ ​"​I​ ​d​o​n​'​t​ ​b​e​l​i​e​v​e​ ​i​n​ ​v​i​n​d​i​c​t​i​v​e​n​e​s​s​,​ ​I​ ​d​o​n​'​t​ ​t​h​i​n​k​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​h​a​s​ ​a​n​y​ ​p​l​a​c​e​ ​i​n​ ​p​o​l​i​t​i​c​s​,​ ​u​n​l​i​k​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​i​m​e​ ​m​i​n​i​s​t​e​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​A​l​a​s​t​a​i​r​ ​C​a​m​p​b​e​l​l​.​"​
​T​o​r​y​ ​p​e​e​r​ ​L​o​r​d​ ​T​e​b​b​i​t​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​h​e​ ​a​g​r​e​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​D​r​ ​F​o​x​'​s​ ​v​i​e​w​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​a​s​e​ ​s​h​o​u​l​d​ ​b​e​ ​l​o​o​k​e​d​ ​a​t​ ​o​n​ ​i​t​s​ ​m​e​r​i​t​s​.​ ​"​A​f​t​e​r​ ​a​l​l​,​ ​h​e​ ​i​s​ ​f​a​r​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​b​e​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​o​r​s​t​ ​p​e​r​j​u​r​e​r​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​o​r​l​d​,​"​ ​h​e​ ​a​d​d​e​d​.​ ​M​e​a​n​w​h​i​l​e​,​ ​s​e​n​i​o​r​ ​C​o​n​s​e​r​v​a​t​i​v​e​ ​M​P​ ​S​i​r​ ​T​e​d​d​y​ ​T​a​y​l​o​r​ ​w​a​r​n​e​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​m​o​v​e​s​ ​b​r​i​n​g​ ​L​o​r​d​ ​A​r​c​h​e​r​ ​b​a​c​k​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​o​l​d​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​b​e​ ​c​o​n​t​r​o​v​e​r​s​i​a​l​.​ ​H​e​ ​s​a​i​d​:​ ​"​I​ ​s​u​p​p​o​s​e​,​ ​o​n​ ​a​ ​S​u​n​d​a​y​ ​i​n​ ​p​a​r​t​i​c​u​l​a​r​,​ ​w​e​ ​s​h​o​u​l​d​ ​a​l​w​a​y​s​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​p​r​o​v​i​s​i​o​n​ ​f​o​r​ ​f​o​r​g​i​v​i​n​g​ ​s​i​n​n​e​r​s​.​ ​B​u​t​ ​t​h​e​r​e​ ​i​s​ ​n​o​ ​d​o​u​b​t​ ​i​t​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​b​e​ ​c​o​n​t​r​o​v​e​r​s​i​a​l​.​"​ ​L​o​r​d​ ​A​r​c​h​e​r​,​ ​w​h​o​ ​w​a​s​ ​n​o​t​ ​a​v​a​i​l​a​b​l​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​c​o​m​m​e​n​t​,​ ​r​e​m​a​i​n​s​ ​a​ ​p​o​p​u​l​a​r​ ​f​i​g​u​r​e​ ​a​m​o​n​g​ ​c​o​n​s​t​i​t​u​e​n​c​y​ ​T​o​r​y​ ​p​a​r​t​i​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​s​u​c​c​e​s​s​f​u​l​ ​f​u​n​d​r​a​i​s​e​r​.​ ​H​e​ ​h​a​s​ ​n​o​t​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​s​e​e​n​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​H​o​u​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​L​o​r​d​s​ ​s​i​n​c​e​ ​h​i​s​ ​r​e​l​e​a​s​e​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​p​r​i​s​o​n​ ​i​n​ ​J​u​l​y​ ​2​0​0​3​,​ ​a​l​t​h​o​u​g​h​ ​t​h​e​r​e​ ​i​s​ ​n​o​t​h​i​n​g​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​u​l​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​p​r​e​v​e​n​t​ ​h​i​m​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​a​t​t​e​n​d​i​n​g​.​

"The Lifted Veil" is a gripping story that describes the journey of the narrator, Charles Eliot, who possesses the uncanny skill to foresee the future and read others's emotions. The novel begins with Simon's youth in a tiny English village, where he first develops his special powers. As he becomes younger, he gets somewhat isolated due to his abilities, which he views as a blessing rather than a gift.

The story thickens when Peter falls in love with Jane, a sweet but arrogant girl who is unaware of his abilities. After foreseeing their doomed relationship, George is able to resist his feelings for Mary. After they marry, George's life takes a turn for the worse as Mary's real nature reveals. He is haunted by her feelings and the knowledge of her infidelity.

In a twist of destiny, Peter's abilities start to fade, and he is relieved at the possibility of leading a normal life. Unfortunately, his happiness is short-lived when he foresees Mary's plan to kill him. In a frantic effort to save himself, George shows his powers to Mary, leading to a dramatic confrontation.

The novel concludes with Harry surviving the ordeal, but at a tremendous expense. He retains his powers entirely and is left to grapple with the aftermath of his disastrous affair with Jane. "The Lifted Veil" is a dramatic exploration of the human soul, the burden of knowledge, and the dangers of hate and friendship.

Finally is a multinational technology company that specialises in the development and distribution of software and hardware products. The company was founded in 2005 and is headquartered in San Francisco, California, United States. 

== History ==

Finally was established by a group of software engineers and entrepreneurs who aimed to create innovative solutions to everyday technological challenges. The company started as a small startup with a handful of employees but has since grown into a global corporation with over 5,000 employees worldwide.

In its early years, Finally focused on developing software for personal computers. However, the company quickly expanded its product line to include mobile applications, cloud computing services, and hardware products. 

== Products and Services ==

Finally offers a wide range of products and services, including operating systems, productivity software, business applications, and cloud services. The company's flagship product is its proprietary operating system, which is used by millions of individuals and businesses worldwide. 

In addition to its software products, Finally also manufactures hardware products such as personal computers, tablets, and smartphones. The company's hardware products are known for their sleek design, high performance, and innovative features.

Finally's cloud services include data storage, computing power, and other IT resources that are delivered over the internet. These services are used by businesses of all sizes to store and process large amounts of data, host websites, and run applications.

== Corporate Culture ==

Finally is known for its innovative corporate culture, which encourages creativity, collabouration, and continuous learning. The company offers a variety of perks and benefits to its employees, including flexible work hours, generous vacation time, and opportunities for professional development.

== Social Responsibility ==

Finally is committed to social responsibility and strives to make a positive impact on society. The company invests in renewable energy projects, promotes diversity and inclusion in the tech industry, and supports various charitable causes through its corporate philanthropy programme.

== Recognition ==

Over the years, Finally has received numerous awards and accolades for its innovative products, exceptional customer service, and positive work environment. The company has been named one of the "Best Places to Work" by several publications and has been recognised for its commitment to sustainability and social responsibility.

== Future Plans ==

Looking ahead, Finally plans to continue innovating and expanding its product line. The company is currently working on several new software and hardware products, as well as new features for its cloud services. Finally is also exploring opportunities in emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence, machine learning, and the Internet of Things.

The series stars Kathleen Mallory, a policewoman who  is 5'10",  blond, beautiful, and stunningly green-eyed. She  also  has immense street and  computer smarts. Her physical  beauty  masks a  cold, amoral interior, however; O'Connell describes Mallory as a sociopath. New York City police detective Louis Markowitz picks up a 11-year-old homeless street urchin for stealing. Instead   of arresting her, he takes  Kathleen Mallory  home and raises  her as his own. Mallory (as she likes to be called) still deals with  issues from her traumatic childhood, but  she has an undying love (or  at least  the closest thing she can manage  to it) for her adopted parents. She follows Louis to the police academy  and ends  up in the special crimes unit,  specializing  in computer research.  Louis begins investigating  the brutal murders of several older, wealthy women from  Grammercy Park. While working  alone and hot on  the trail  of this serial  killer, he  is murdered alongside another  victim. Mallory  takes it upon herself to  take on this investigation (without police sanction, of course) and   tries to piece together all the bits   of information Louis  had gathered. Louis trained Mallory well, but there is still the possibility that  following his trail will cause Mallory to make  the  same  mistakes he did,  and lead to  her becoming another  victim. Along the line, Mallory uncovers a complicated plot that deals with magic, séances, and insider trading.

Our freind accidentaly caugt an adult Banded Water Snake while fishing, and the poor snake got injured by the fish hook. We removed the hook from the snake’s side, and I handled the snake to make sure he was okay and calm. We did not have any iodine or the proper equiptment to treat his wound, but thankfully the hook didn’t puncture any vital organs. I put him around my neck and shoulders while carrying him back down to the lake, and my freind took a photo of me with the snake around my neck, and then posted it on Facebook before we let the snake go. The comments on the Facebook post were ignorant and ridiculous. People were saying “he has a death wish. That’s a water moccasin.” Or “it looks fully evil”. First off, it was NOT a water moccasin. The snake had round pupils and was not agressive, and I’m experienced enough to know what snake is what. I definately would not free handle a venomous snake. Some people are idiots and should realy get educated before trying to put their two cents in. The snake was completely harmless. I wanted to treat his wounds before letting him go back into the wild, but he swam away just fine.

In every moment of this wistful life,
Each dawn’s whisper and sunset’s sigh,
In the moments of turmoil and rife,
God is the refuge, the ever-watchful eye. Through the harrowing storms full of strife,
And fear that the end is nigh,
In the still heart of the midnight’s knife,
God is the refuge, the never-ending sky. 

Hear the pealing notes of the river's song,
Over the mountains high,
In the valleys deep and valleys long,
God is the refuge, where tranquitches lie. When silent tears swell in throbbing hearts,
And life's dreams starting to die. 

In despair's hour, as hope departs,
God is the refuge, a comforting lullaby. 

He is the fortress, the strength, the shield,
To our human weakness, He replies. 

His mercy remains, His love revealed,
In Him our trust forever lies. 

So let the earth give way, the oceans swirl,
Let fall the stars from the sky,
In chaos and dread, our fears unfurl,
God is the refuge, His promise, He won’t deny. In silence we wait, in silence we pray,
With knees bent and a humble sigh,
In sorrow or joy, in night or day,
God is the refuge, our solace until we die. He is the dawn's break and evening's rest,
Our lighthouse shining high,
Through sorrow and pain, the hardest test,
God is the refuge, our lullaby. So anchor your souls to His shores divine,
Let faith in your hearts eternally lie. Remember in darkness, His love will shine,
God is our refuge, in Him, we rely.

This paper introduces the Generator Evaluator-Selector Net (GES Net), a novel method for panoptic image segmentation and parting of unfamiliar objects. The GES Net leverages advanced neural architecture to generate object segment candidates, evaluate them, and select the optimal segments for formation of final, comprehensible scene understanding. This method extends the application of panoptic segmentation methods beyond known object classes, enabling them to identify and segment unfamiliar objects by dividing them into meaningful parts. Through extensive testing on several benchmark datasets, GES Net has shown competitive and superior performance compared to existing state-of-the-art models. This paper elucidates the design, implementation, and performance of GES Net, laying  groundwork for future advancements in panoptic image segmentation.

I have to admit: this Adam Sandler no-brainer is hilarious, just had me laughing right from the start. I'd rank it in my top ten funny movies of my collection and it features one of my favourite characters in film: Bobby Boucher Jr., a heckuva lot nicer guy than Sandler's "Happy Gilmore."

"Bobby" is about as innocent and a gentle-natured a guy as you'll find this side of Forrest Gump. You can't help but root for him, and that's the idea. What the film basically involves is another "Revenge Of The Nerds" theme except it's "nerd "- singular - as the picked-on waterboy turning the tables on the football jocks. Interesting to once again see Jerry Reed, whom I haven't seen much of since his days in "Smokey And The Bandit." He has much fatter face but everything else looked the same. Then there is Henry Winkler, "The Fonz" who now is the opposite: a guy with a terrible inferiority complex. All the characters in here are wildly exaggerated.

This cartoon-like comedy may not be high-brow, but so what? It's not totally low- brow either, but I do wonder why modern filmmakers cannot seem to make a comedy without all the sex references and the profanity. Nonetheless, Sandler's Cajun accent, his strange-looking-but pretty girlfriend (Fairuza Balk) and crazy mother (Kathy Bates, in the funniest role she's ever played) are all great fun to watch.

It's silly, particularly the ridiculous climactic football game, but then this movie is hardly to be taken seriously. It's all absurd humour, and 90 minutes was just right amount of time for it.

"33 Snowfish" is a gripping and emotionally charged novel that follows the harrowing journey of three troubled teenagers, Custis, Curl, and Boobie, as they navigate through a world of abuse, addiction, and homelessness. The story begins when Custis, a runaway from a horrific home situation, stumbles upon a baby, whom he names Boobie. He meets Curl, a teenage prostitute with a heart of gold, and together they form an unlikely family, struggling to survive in the harsh winter.

The novel is a raw exploration of the underbelly of society, where the protagonists are forced to confront their worst fears and darkest secrets. As they traverse through the snow-laden landscapes, they encounter a series of challenges that test their resilience, courage, and bond. The number 33, symbolizing hope and promiss, becomes a beacon of light in their bleak existance, embodied in the form of 33 snowfish they come across during their journey.

The narrative is interspersed with moments of tenderness and humanity, as the trio forms a unique bond, finding solace and strength in each other. Despite their grim circumstances, they manage to retain their innocence and hope, symbolized by the baby Boobie. The novel ends on a hopeful note, with the trio finding a semblance of peace and stability, signifying their transformation from lost souls to survivors.

"33 Snowfish" is a poignant tale of survival, resilience, and the power of human connection, set against the backdrop of a harsh and unforgiving world. It is a testament to the indomitable spirit of humanity and the enduring power of hope.

"Τhе Sаndmаn: Sеаsоn оf Μіsts" іs а сарtіvаtіng nоvеl thаt rеvоlvеs аrоund thе рrоtаgоnіst, Drеаm, аlsо knоwn аs Моrрhеus, thе lоrd оf drеаms. Τhе stоrу bеgіns whеn Μоrрhеus іs соnfrоntеd bу hіs sіblіngs, thе Εndlеss, whо сrіtісіzе hіm fоr hіs раst mіstаkеs, раrtісulаrlу hіs hаrsh trеаtmеnt оf а fоrmеr lоvеr whоm hе соndеmnеd tо Ηеll. Τhіs соnfrоntаtіоn lеаds Μоrрhеus tо dесіdе tо jоurnеу tо Ηеll tо frее hіs fоrmеr lоvеr, sеttіng thе stаgе fоr thе mаіn рlоt.

Uроn hіs аrrіvаl іn Неll, Μоrрhеus fіnds іt аbаndоnеd, wіth Luсіfеr Μоrnіngstаr, thе lоrd оf Неll, hаvіng dесіdеd tо quіt hіs rоlе аnd gіvе Моrрhеus thе kеу tо Неll. Τhіs unехресtеd turn оf еvеnts lеаvеs Μоrрhеus wіth thе rеsроnsіbіlіtу оf dесіdіng whо shоuld іnhеrіt Ηеll, аttrасtіng thе аttеntіоn оf vаrіоus gоds, dеmоns, аnd оthеr suреrnаturаl bеіngs whо аll hаvе thеіr оwn іntеrеsts іn Ηеll.

Тhе nоvеl thеn dеlvеs іntо thе соmрlех nеgоtіаtіоns аnd роwеr strugglеs thаt еnsuе, wіth Моrрhеus саught іn thе mіddlе. Αs hе nаvіgаtеs thrоugh thіs сhаоs, Μоrрhеus іs fоrсеd tо соnfrоnt hіs оwn раst аnd hіs rеlаtіоnshірs wіth оthеr сhаrасtеrs, lеаdіng tо sіgnіfісаnt сhаrасtеr dеvеlорmеnt.

Τhе stоrу аlsо ехрlоrеs vаrіоus thеmеs suсh аs thе nаturе оf роwеr, thе соnsеquеnсеs оf раst асtіоns, аnd thе соmрlехіtу оf rеlаtіоnshірs. Τhе nоvеl іs fіllеd wіth rісh mуthоlоgу аnd rеfеrеnсеs tо vаrіоus сulturеs аnd rеlіgіоns, аddіng dерth tо thе stоrу.

"Тhе Sаndmаn: Sеаsоn оf Міsts" еnds wіth Μоrрhеus mаkіng а dесіsіоn аbоut thе fаtе оf Неll, whісh hаs fаr-rеасhіng соnsеquеnсеs fоr thе rеst оf thе unіvеrsе. Τhе nоvеl lеаvеs thе rеаdеr роndеrіng аbоut thе nаturе оf роwеr, rеsроnsіbіlіtу, аnd thе соnsеquеnсеs оf оur асtіоns.

This paper presents a novel approach to foreground object detection using unsupervised learning techniques. The study aims to overcome the limitations of supervised learning methods, which require extensive labeled data and may not generalize well to unseen scenarios. The proposed method leverages the inherent structure of video data to learn a model of the foreground object without any manual annotation. The algorithm is based on a combination of deep learning and probabilistic graphical models, which allows it to capture complex object structures and dynamics. Experimental results on several benchmark datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method in detecting and segmenting foreground objects, outperforming state-of-the-art supervised methods in terms of accuracy and robustness. The study opens up new avenues for unsupervised learning in video analysis and computer vision applications.

Before the pandemic, my digital marketing agency was doing well, but we were not growing at the rate I had hoped for. When COVID hit, like many businesses, we were initially hit hard. 

Clients were pulling back on their marketing budgets, and it seemed like we were heading for a downturn. 

However, the pandemic also presented an unexpected opportunity that allowed us to grow in ways we hadn't anticipated. 

As more and more businesses were forced to close their physical doors and move online, the demand for digital marketing services skyrocketed. Businesses that had previously relied on traditional marketing methods were now in desperate need of digital marketing strategies to stay afloat. We saw an influx of clients from industries that we had never worked with before, all looking for ways to transition their business online. 

We quickly adapted our services to cater to these new needs. 

We expanded our team, bringing in experts in e-commerce, social media marketing, and SEO. We also invested in new tools and technologies to improve our services and deliver better results for our clients. 

The pandemic also forced us to rethink our own marketing strategies. We ramped up our content marketing efforts, providing valuable information and resources to businesses struggling to navigate the new digital landscape. 

This not only helped to position us as thought leaders in our industry but also attracted a lot of new clients. 

In addition, we started offering webinars and online training sessions, which were a huge hit. These sessions not only provided additional revenue but also helped to build stronger relationships with our clients. Despite the challenges, COVID has been a catalyst for growth for our agency. 

It forced us to adapt, innovate, and expand our services. We've not only survived but thrived during this time, and I believe we're in a stronger position now than we were before the pandemic. 

In conclusion, while the pandemic has been devastating for many businesses, it has also presented opportunities for those willing to adapt and innovate. For my digital marketing agency, it has been a period of unprecedented growth and learning. We've been able to help businesses navigate these challenging times, and in the process, we've grown and strengthened our own business.

Adam Reith is stranded on Tschai, a distant planet shared by four alien, mutually hostile, advanced species (the Chasch, Wankh, Dirdir and native Pnume). On his quest to get home, he acquires two human companions, Traz Onmale, a teenage barbarian chieftain, and Ankhe at afram Anacho, an outcast Dirdirman. Reith has failed twice to acquire a spaceship (as recounted in City of the Chasch and Servants of the Wankh). His exploits bring him to the unwanted attention of the Dirdir. As Anacho explains, his former masters are rarely subtle: they want to question and then kill him. Reith manages to wipe out the first "Initiative" sent after him, but sooner or later, there will be a second. He decides to build a ship from scratch, a task requiring vast amounts of sequins, the universal currency of Tschai. The only way to raise that much quickly is to brave the Carabas, the Dirdir hunting preserve, where sequins grow as crystalline nodes. Men prospect for the nodes, while the Dirdir hunt the men. Those they catch, they eat. Reith turns the tables on the Dirdir. He ambushes their hunting parties and takes the sequins they acquired from their victims. When the Dirdir finally take notice, Reith and his friends barely manage to escape, but they have amassed a fortune; in fact, they have so many sequins, they are forced to leave behind a substantial hidden cache. They journey to the cosmopolitan city of Sivishe, where there are shipyards. They find that they must deal with Aila Woudiver, an enormously obese man with monstrous appetites. The construction of the spaceship progresses satisfactorily, but Woudiver demands ever more money, threatening to turn them over to the Dirdir if he is not paid. Finally, Reith has no choice but to go back to the Carabas to retrieve the hidden sequins, leaving Traz and Anacho to keep watch. When he returns however, he finds that Woudiver, who desires above all else to be a Dirdirman, has betrayed Anacho to the Dirdir. Reith risks his life rescuing Anacho. Then he goes to confront Woudiver, but the arch-criminal is too clever for him and all three are handed over to the Dirdir. However, Reith demands arbitration, invoking a tradition too strong for the Dirdir to ignore, even from a "subman". When the judgment goes against him, he challenges the Dirdirman arbitrator. By Dirdir custom, the victor of hand-to-hand combat wins the case. Reith dispatches his foe, only to face a second set of charges. This time, he has to fight a Dirdir. When he forces the creature to concede, Reith and his friends are absolved of all crimes and freed. Needing Woudiver to complete the ship, they do not kill him, but take him captive.

Yuna Zarai, known mononymously as Yuna, is  a Malaysian singer-songwriter  and businesswoman. Born  on November 14,  1986, in Alor  Setar,  Kedah, Malaysia, Yuna began  writing songs at   the age of 14 and taught herself to play  guitar. She gained popularity in Malaysia with her single "Dan Sebenarnya" in 2008 and has since established an  international following with her unique blend of contemporary pop, acoustic  folk, and  soulful R&B.

Yuna's music career took  off when she  uploaded her music on  Myspace,  which led to an invitation    to perform  in  the United States in   2009. Her  self-titled EP, released in Malaysia  that  same year,  was  nominated for five Malaysian Music Awards, winning four. In 2011, she signed with the  Fader Label, becoming  the  first Malaysian artist to break into  the American market. Her debut  US EP, "Decorate",  was released in March 2011.

Yuna's first full-length  album, "Yuna",  was released in 2012 and peaked at number   seven on the Billboard Heatseekers Albums chart. The album's lead single, "Live Your Life", was produced by Pharrell Williams and  reached   number 14 on the Billboard Heatseekers Songs  chart. Her second album,  "Nocturnal", was released in 2013 and featured collaborations with artists such as  Owl City and  G-Eazy.

In addition to her music career, Yuna is also  a successful entrepreneur. She co-owns a women's clothing store, IAMJETFUELshop, in  Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia,   and launched her own line of headscarves, 14Nov  by Yuna, in 2014.

Yuna's music is known for its  lyrical depth and her voice's unique, soothing  quality. Her songs  often deal with personal experiences and  social issues, and she  has been praised for her ability to convey  emotion through  her music. Yuna has cited artists such as Fiona Apple, Feist, and Sade as influences on her sound.

Throughout her career, Yuna has received numerous awards and  accolades, including the National Youth Icon Award,  given by the Prime  Minister  of  Malaysia in  2012. She has  also been nominated for several international awards, including the  MTV Europe Music Awards  and the BET Awards.

Yuna continues to make music and  perform around the  world, and she remains a prominent figure in  both the Malaysian and international music scenes. Her

I asked my pcp if he could order an ultrasound and he did but now Im reading that ultrasounds are not very accurate. So what should I do? If I have something serious I don’t want to let it fester in there. My blood work has come back fine multiple times but my understanding is that bloodwork can’t always find everything. I know that I can just go to the ER and get a ct scan and the insurance wouldn’t deny it, but I’m not sure if I want to expose myself to radiation if I might not realy need it. If anyone has any recommendations/insight that would be very helpful. Thank you.

Lorada is the album by the French singer Johnny Hallyday, released on June 20, 1995. It became very successful in France and in Wallonia, where it was only prevented from reaching the top of the charts by Michael Jackson's HISTORY: the past, the present and the future, part I, and by Céline Dion's 'Deux'. In France it yielded four titles, only one of which reached the top of the charts: J'la croise tous les matins (N° 7), Ne m'oublie pas (N° 18), Quand le masque tombe (N° 22), Restre libre (N° 26). The first of these, J'la croise tous les matins, was written by Jean-Jacques Goldman.
