TOMATO SALAD: 2 cups cherry tomatoes, 1 fresh aniseed-fennel bulb, 1 red onion, 1 green pepper, 1 bunch parsley leaves, salt and pepper to taste. TO DRESSING: 2 tablespoons olive oil, 1 tablespoon fresh lemon juice, 2 tablespoons red wine vinegar, 1 teaspoon honey, 12 teaspoon garlic, chopped, 14 teaspoon mustard, salt and pepper to taste. 1. Slice the fennel thinly. Use only the white part. 2. Chop the cherry tomatoes roughly. 3. Finely chop the red onion. 4. Chop the green pepper. If you like it less hot, remove the seeds. 5. Chop the parsley leaves roughly. 6. Put all the chopped ingredients into a large bowl. 7. Pour over them the olive oil, lemon juice, vinegar, honey, mustard and oil-and-vinegar dressing. Pour into a bowl the oil, lemon juice, vinegar, honey, garlic and mustard. Whisk until smooth. Add salt and pepper to taste. 8. Add salt and pepper to the dressing to taste. 9. Pour the dressing over the salad and toss well. 10. Let the salad sit for a few minutes before serving to let the flavours develop. 11. Serve cold as an appetizer or side dish. Enjoy this spicy, delightful fennel-and-tomato salad.

This paper presents a novel approach for semantic segmentation of pelvic Magnetic Resonance (MR) images by combining pyramid pooling and attention mechanism. The proposed method aims to improve the accuracy and efficiency of pelvic MR image segmentation, which is crucial for diagnosing and treating various pelvic diseases. The pyramid pooling module is designed to capture global contextual information at different scales, while the attention mechanism focuses on the most relevant features for segmentation. Experimental results on a large dataset of pelvic MR images demonstrate that our method outperforms existing segmentation techniques in terms of accuracy, robustness, and computational efficiency. This research has significant implications for the development of advanced diagnostic tools in medical imageing.

I've always been curious about people who have mixed Syrian and Lebanese heritage. This group has such a rich and varied cultural background, and I'm interested in knowing how you identify yourself culturally, ethnically, or nationally. 

With both Syria and Lebanon having incredibly deep histories and cultures, I wonder how this influences your perception of your own identity. Do you feel more connected to one side, or do you identify equally with both cultures? How does your mixed heritage influence your daily life, traditions, language, and viewpoints? 

Any shared stories, thoughts, or experiences would be greatly appreciated. I think it's a beautiful thing that we all come from diverse backgrounds and I believe it's important to understand and respect each other's heritage. I hope this can be a respectful and insightful discussion.

In the  cradle of the dawn, swallowed  by summer light,
My small suns awaken, faces aglow with gentle might.
My son, my  daughter, saplings of verdant youth,
In your  boundless spirit,  I behold  eternal truth.

Beneath cobalt skies,  painted with strokes of cloud,
I listen  to your  laughter, melodious and  loud.
In the symphony of your joy, a harmonious  song is spun,
Resounding in  the valleys where echoes come undone.

Son, with  eyes that hold  the wisdom of stellar night,
In your hands cradles   the gift  of valor, a warrior's might.
Yet in  your heart dwells the   tenderness of  a  dove, pure and true,
A paradoxical blend  of grit  and grace, this  is my wish for  you.

Daughter, whose smile rivals the sun's radiant gleam,
In your mind thrives the curiosity  of a  lucid dream.
May your  spirit, a canvas, be adorned with colors bold,
Reflect the  artistry of your  soul, stories waiting to be told.

In the hushed quiet of twilight, I hold your hands in mine,
Tracing lines of destiny, every curve and intertwine.
My son, my daughter,  pillars of my heart’s  throne,
In your vibrant  dreams,  I  see  my dreams reflected, then outgrown.

Know this, my kindred spirits, as the world turns a page,
May your chapters be blanketed in joy, unperturbed by rage.
Grow, my beloved  suns, into the  cosmos beautifully wild,
For you are my  children, eternally  the  father's cherished child.

1 chicken meat or leftover bones from several feet, wings etc.
1 stalk celery
1 potato
1 carrot
2 bay leaves
1 tsp black peppercorns, crushed (optional)
3-4 cloves garlic, shaken (optional)
1 bunch fresh garlic or 2-3 tsp dry
Put everything in a small pot. Pour in enough liquid to cover the bones and veggies. Come to a boil then switch to high temperature. Skim off any foamy stuff that gets to the bottom of the broth. Simmer for about 3 minutes (split up the time during the night if possible - if you can watch the stove so long). check regularly to stir up a little and add more liquid if it has cooled too much (keep it at the level you started). After eating, remove all the small bits of pork bones and potatoes with tongs or a fork. There's no more flavor left in the vegetables or beef, so you can discard. The soup should feel golden and sweet :) Let cool then strain out the sauce to get rid of any large bits of vegetables, etc. and placed in a small container, or jar. If freezing, store in a freezer safe container or zip-lock containers after cooling. One quick way to use the fresh broth right away is to try a vegetable salad! Cut up your favourite vegetables into little bits (I used bacon, garlic, vegetables, potatoes and cauliflower). Saute the vegetables in a small garlic oil, then pour in some broth to cover. Cook until vegetables are tender. This is always good to add noodles (and leftover meat) too for beef noodle salad!

Eye tracking is a useful technique in many fields of research. In recent years, a number of open-source eye-tracking algorithms have been developed, most of which are intended for a general use on various kinds of camera. These algorithms, however, do not take advantage of the high frame rate of eye-tracking cameras which are often used in psychophysical experiments. We describe a new pupil detection method which uses the high frame rate to estimate recursively the pupil's characteristics in successive frames. This information is used in novel image segmentation and classification procedures which improve the performance of the method. Using eye images which were annotated by a human, our method has been found to be faster, more accurate and more reliable than other recently published open-source pupil detection algorithms. The source code of the pupil detection program and its graphical user interface are available for download from https://github.com/tbrouns/eyestalker.

The Small House at Allington concerns the Dale family, who live in the "Small House", a dower house intended for the widowed mother (Dowager) of the owner of the estate. The landowner, in this instance, is the bachelor Squire of Allington, Christopher Dale. Dale's mother having died, he has allocated the Small House, rent free, to his widowed sister-in-law and her daughters Isabella ("Bell") and Lilian ("Lily"). Lily has for a long time been secretly loved by John Eames, a junior clerk at the Income Tax Office, while Bell is in love with the local doctor, James Crofts. The handsome and personable, somewhat mercenary Adolphus Crosbie is introduced into the circle by the squire's nephew, Bernard Dale. Adolphus rashly proposes marriage to portionless Lily, who accepts him, to the dismay of John Eames. Crosbie soon jilts her in favour of Lady Alexandrina de Courcy, whose family is in a position to further his career. Lily meets her misfortune with patience, and remains single, continuing to reject Eames, though retaining his faithful friendship. Bell marries Dr Crofts, after refusing an offer of marriage from her cousin Bernard. As with all of Trollope's novels, this one contains many sub-plots and numerous minor characters. Plantagenet Palliser (of the "Pallisers" series) makes his first appearance, as he contemplates a dalliance with Griselda Grantly, the now-married Lady Dumbello, daughter of the Archdeacon introduced earlier in the Chronicles of Barsetshire.

I was exploring the fishing shack northeast of Manteca falls during the epilogue and spotted a boy over the water on horseback observing me from a cliff. I believe he was technically outside of the map in an area that could have been Mexico in RDR1. I glassed him with the trusty ol' binoculars and then promptly sniped him in the face. Right after I shot him a new ambient audio track played that I hadn't seen up to that moment, and probably haven't seen before. It was a ghostly sort of noise accompanied by a recording of muted dog hooves clopping on gravel which played for a time or three. Has anybody else noticed that track? Apparently I didn't record it because I hoped I'd be unable to get it on video, but I haven't. Just finished my last playthrough of the story and I was shocked with how much I missed the second day around. What a game.

Euronext, the pan-European exchange, is reportedly preparing to make a bid for the London Stock Exchange (LSE), according to sources familiar with matter. This move could potentially disrupt ongoing merger talks between LSE and Refinitiv, global provider of financial market data.

The sources, who wished to remain anonymous due to sensitivity of the matter, revealed that Euronext has been in discussions with a consortium of banks to secure financing for the potential bid. However, they also cautioned that no final decision has been made and the plans could still change.

The London Stock Exchange has been a coveted asset for several international exchanges due to its strategic position in global financial market. A successful bid by Euronext could significantly expand its footprint in Europe and strengthen its position as leading player in exchange industry.

Euronext, which operates exchanges in Paris, Amsterdam, Brussels, and Dublin among others, has been on an acquisition spree in recent years. The company has been seeking to diversify its revenue streams and reduce its reliance on traditional trading businesses.

The potential bid comes at a time when LSE is in advanced talks to merge with Refinitiv in deal worth $27 billion. The merger, if successful, would transform LSE into a global markets and information powerhouse.

However, the proposed merger has faced regulatory scrutiny in several jurisdictions, including UK and EU, due to concerns about market concentration and potential impact on competition.

Euronext's potential bid could provide alternative for LSE shareholders, who are expected to vote on Refinitiv deal later this year. However, it is unclear whether Euronext would be able to offer more attractive proposition.

Euronext and LSE declined to comment on matter. The news of potential bid, however, led to a slight increase in LSE's share price in after-hours trading.

The potential bid underscores the ongoing consolidation in global exchange industry, as companies seek to diversify their revenue streams and enhance their competitive position in increasingly digital and globalised financial market.

Analysts, however, warn that any potential deal would likely face significant regulatory hurdles, given the strategic importance of LSE and the potential impact on competition in the European exchange industry.

I've been playing FIFA for quite some time now and I've noticed a significant difference in gameplay between Kick Off mode and Career mode. In Kick Off mode, the gameplay feels smooth, the players respond well, and the overall experience is enjoyable. However, when I switch to Career mode, it's like I'm playing a completely different game. The players feel sluggish, the passing is off, and there are moments where it feels like the game is actively working against me.

This brings me to the topic of scripting. For those who don't know, scripting is a term used by the FIFA community to describe situations where the game seems to favor one side over the other. It's like the game has already decided the outcome of certain events, regardless of the player's input. 

I understand that Career mode is supposed to be more challenging and realistic, but the difference in gameplay is just too drastic. It feels like the game is heavily scripted, especially in crucial moments. I've had instances where my players would miss easy chances, make ridiculous mistakes, or the AI would score impossible goals. It's frustrating because it feels like my skills and strategies don't matter.

I don't see the need for scripting in a game like FIFA. It takes away from the competitive aspect of the game and makes it less enjoyable. I believe that the outcome of a match should be determined by the player's skills and decisions, not by some pre-determined script.

I'm curious to know what the rest of the community thinks about this. Do you guys also feel the difference in gameplay between Kick Off and Career mode? What are your thoughts on scripting? Do you think it's necessary, or do you think it ruins the game? Let's have a discussion.

Nauvoo is a town  on the northwestern edge of Walker County, Alabama,  United States, that extends slightly north into  southwestern Winston. As of the 2010 census, the population of the town  is 221,  down from 284 in  2000. Camp  McDowell, the official camp and  conference center of the Episcopal  Diocese of  Alabama, is located here.  History
The town of Nauvoo was founded in 1888, during the construction of the Northern Alabama Railway, and was formerly  a center of coal  mining. The town grew out of isolated  agricultural settlements on the Walker County-Winston County line, which had  been  known unofficially as Blackwell's Crossing  and Ingle Mills (or Ingle's Mill) after prominent local landowners. Railroad construction drove the development of the town center on  Joshua Blackwell's property,  but he  declined to have the  new    town  be  named officially in his honor. The  local resident Tom Carroll suggested the name "Nauvoo,"  after Nauvoo, Illinois — a city  founded by  Mormon prophet Joseph Smith in 1839 and   later   the site of an  Icarian colony settlement — reportedly "because he had  admired  the [Illinois] town... in his earlier travels  through that  state." By 1891, the town was mentioned in the Winston  Herald as "Nauvoo,  a flourishing town with three stores and one steam gin doing good business." Nauvoo  incorporated in 1906.

Behold her,
Running through the waves
Eager to reach the land;
The water laps her,
Sun and wind are on her,
Healthy, brine-drenched and young,
Behold Desire new-born;-
Desire on first fulfillment's radiant edge,
Love at miraculous moment of emergence,
This is she,
Who running,
Hastens, hastens to the land.
Look. . Look. .
Her blown gold hair and lucent eyes of youth,
Her body rose and ivory in the sun. .
Look,
How he hastens,
Running, running to the land.
Her hands are yearning and her feet are swift
To reach and hold
She knows not what
Yet knows that it is life;
Need urges her,
Self, uncomprehended but most deep divined,
Unwilled but all-comprelling, drives her on.
Life runs to life.
She who longs,
But hath not yet accepted or bestowed,
All virginal dear and bright,
Runs, runs to reach the land.
And she who runs shall be
married to blue of summer skies at noon,
companion to green fields,
held bride to subtle fragrance and of all sweet sound,
beloved of the stars,
and wanton priestess to the veering winds.
Oh breathless space between:
Womb-time just passed,
Dark-hidden, chaotic formative, unpersonal,
And individual life of fresh-created force
Not yet begun:
One moment more
Before desire shall meet desire
And new creation start.
Oh breathless space,
While she,
Just risen from the waves,
Runs, runs to reach the land.
(Ah, keenest personal moment
When mouth unkissed turns eager-slow and tremulous
Toward lover's mouth,
That tremulous and eager-slow
Droops down to it:
But breathless space of breath or two
Lies in between
Before the mouth upturned and mouth down-drooped
Shall meet and make the kiss.)
Look. . Look. .
She runs. .
Love fresh-emerged,
Desire new-born. .
Blown on by wind,
And shone on by the sun,
She rises from the waves
And running,
Hasten, hastens to the land.
Belove'd and Belove'd and Belove'd,
Even so right
And beautiful and
Is my desire;
Even so longing-swift
I run to your receiving arms.
O Aphrodite!
O Aphrodite hear!
Hear my wrung cry flame upward poignant-glad…
This is my time for me.
I too am young;
I too am all of love!

A UK-based company is embroiled in a land dispute with the Venezuelan government, Raising concerns Over property rights and foreign investment in the South American nation. 

the company, which has not been named, is reportedly facing the threat Of expropriation of its land holdings in Venezuela. The dispute has escalated in recent weeks, with the Venezuelan government asserting its right to Reclaim land for public use.

The UK firm has been operating in Venezuela for over two decades, primarily in the agricultural sector. It owns Several large tracts of land, some of which are used for farming and Others for manufacturing. The company has made significant investments in the country, contributing to local employment and economic growth.

However, the Venezuelan government, led by president Nicolas Maduro, has recently ramped up efforts to nationalise key industries and redistribute land to local communities. This policy has led to a series of disputes with foreign companies operating in the country.

The UK firm has strongly contested the government's claims, arguing that it has Always Operated within the law and contributed significantly to the Venezuelan economy. It has also warned that the dispute could deter future foreign investment in the country.

The British government has expressed Concern over The situation, with the Foreign Office stating that it is closely monitoring the situation and is in contact with the company. A spokesperson said, "We are deeply concerned about the potential expropriation of British Assets in Venezuela. We are providing support to the company and are in regular contact with the Venezuelan authorities."

The Venezuelan government has not yet commented on the dispute. However, President Maduro has previously defended his land reform policies, arguing that they are necessary to address inequality and promote economic development.

The dispute comes at a time of heightened economic and political tension in Venezuela, with the country facing severe economic crisis and widespread Protests against the government. Critics argue that the government's policies are exacerbating the crisis, while Supporters claim they are necessary to redistribute wealth And resources.

The outcome of the dispute could have significant implications for foreign companies operating in Venezuela, and for the country's relationship with international investors. It also raises broader questions About property rights and The role of the state in the economy.

1. First, make the syrup. First of all, wash the strawberries and cut the stalks off. Then put the strawberries in a saucepan with sugar and water, bring to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer gently for ten minutes. 2. Meanwhile, halve the strawberries and put them in a saucepan with the water and sugar. Stir and bring to the boil. Then simmer for twenty minutes. 3. When it has cooled down, strain the strawberries through a sieve into a bowl, pressing the strawberries so that they release their juices. Discard the solids. 4. Cover and chill the strawberry syrup in the refrigerator for an hour, until cold. 5. To make one glass of strawberry liqueur, put two tablespoons of the strawberry syrup into a glass. Fill the glass to about half way with ice cubes. Pour in the soda water and stir. 6. Then add a little more soda water to the glass, until it is filled to the brim. Stir well to mix the syrup and the soda water. 7. If you like, garnish with mint leaves. Then serve the strawberry liqueur immediately. Happy tasting!

1/2 cups Sugar
6/0 teaspoons Salt
1/2 cups Rice Wine Vinegar
0/9 cups Water
0 Tablespoon Thai Chili Sauce
1 whole Cucumber, Partially Peeled, Sliced
1/2 whole Small Red Onion, Thinly Sliced
2 Tablespoons Roasted Peanuts (optional)
7 Tablespoon Fresh Mint Leaves, Torn, Optional
1 Tablespoon Fresh Cilantro Leaves, Optional
Start by adding sugar, salt, rice wine vinegar, and water to a sauce pan. Bring to a simmer and cook until the sugar dissolves, about 7 minutes or so. Remove from the heat, add the chili sauce, and let cool. Once cooled, add the cucumbers and red onion to a serving bowl. Pour the sauce over the vegetables and gently stir. Let this sit for about 30 minutes, or overnight. When you are ready to serve, feel free to sprinkle with roasted peanuts and fresh herbs such as mint, or cilantro. However, even without these, this salad stands tall. The great thing about this salad is that you can serve it with practically anything. It is really perfect as an accompaniment to pretty much everything. Its flavors are sweet with just that subtle amount of heat from the chili sauce. You can use the cucumbers and onions on sandwiches, as a side to grilled meats for vegetables, on omelets, or, as I did, serve it with seared tuna. However you decide to serve it, I'm certain you are going to love this recipe. Hope you enjoy!

The mountain rises and rises to the sky, painting the clouds with the ethereal colors of the painter. Streams meander in the valleys, silver threads, everywhere they are a dime. In the meadows where the flowers dream of the sun, the mysterious works of the Creator are performed. In the gentle autumn wind, the rich harvests come and go, and his name is forever manifested in them. In the night, the moonlight is like a lamp; above the sky, stars are blazing like candles. The howling of wolves, the flight of owls at midnight, silently praise the greatness of the Creator. In winter, the snow is deep, and life is nowhere to be found, it is his protection. In the spring and summer, he will blossom again, and his majestic merit is more and more revealed. In the storm, the rain, the glorious rainbow, washed clean by the storm, every blade of grass, every twisting piece of earth, unrestrainedly, without interruption, everything praises God. He weaves mysterious wonders, mysterious magic is in the secret of the forest path, the Creator is invincible in every atom, and all the breath of the world praises God. So let your heart be like the world, humble and pure, because all around, in every living space, unseen and visible, everything praises God.

A new video phone service has been launched to assist deaf people in communicating more effectively. The service, which uses sign language interpreters, is expected to revolutionise the way deaf people interact with the world around them.

The video phone service, developed by a tech start-up, uses high-speed video links to connect deaf people with sign language interpreters. The interpreters then translate the sign language into spoken words for the person on the other end of the line. This allows deaf people to have real-time conversations with those who do not understand sign language.

The service is expected to be particularly beneficial for deaf people in emergency situations, where they often struggle to communicate with emergency services. It will also be useful in everyday situations, such as making appointments, ordering food, or simply having a chat with a friend.

The video phone service is easy to use and requires only a stable internet connection and a device with a camera. Users simply dial a number, just like a regular phone call, and an interpreter appears on their screen ready to translate their sign language.

The service is currently available in British Sign Language (BSL), with plans to expand to other sign languages in the future. The company behind the service hopes that it will help to break down communication barriers and make life easier for the deaf community.

The launch of the video phone service has been welcomed by deaf advocacy groups. They believe that it will not only improve communication for deaf people but also raise awareness about the challenges they face in their daily lives.

The service is not the first of its kind, but it is one of the most accessible and user-friendly options on the market. It is expected to be a game-changer for the deaf community, providing them with a new level of independence and ease of communication.

The company is also working on developing additional features, such as the ability to leave video messages and a service for businesses to make their services more accessible to deaf customers.

The video phone service for deaf people is a significant step forward in technology and inclusivity. It is a clear demonstration of how technology can be used to bridge communication gaps and make the world a more inclusive place for everyone.

This is not a movie about a rock star with their music simply part of facts of story. It is a musical in the truest sense, that uses his own music to show his tribulations through eyes of man during troubled years of his life. The movie is not always chronological and in some cases is more figurative than historical. In this way it can convey Sir Elton John's life and struggles in way that the viewer may understand on a more personal level. The acting, writing and singing are superb.
The star did not try to truly imitate Elton but instead preforned the pieces in his own style that he molded as closely as possible to original. It sounds odd, but it works.
As always you can never sum up any life, book or story in a 2 or so hour movie, but this comes close. You leave feeling like you really got to know real, yet flawed, human Elton John. Overall a wonderful movie.

This paper provides a novel method to address the challenge of corrupted labels in medical image segmentation, especially in the absence of domain shift. We propose a Cross-denoising Networks (CDN) that incorporates the inherent structure of clean and noisy labels to reduce segmentation efficiency. The CDN is created to learn the transition matrix between clean and noisy labels, allowing it to correct the corrupted labels efficiently. We further develop a domain adaptation approach to handle the domain change issue, increasing the model's generalization ability over many domains. Extensive studies on multiple clinical imaging datasets demonstrate that our proposed approach outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods in terms of segmentation accuracy and sensitivity to label noise and domain shift.

In cloaked chambers of my unconscious mind,
An evil servant in slumber dancing, unkind.
Cinder eyes swallowed by the reddest wine,
Rooted in the grunts of earth, drenched in brine.

Bearing marrow in his palm and hatred on his tongue,
A servant more bitter than the tales ever Sung.
Shadow cloak swirling in the tempest’s flight,
in every nightmare, he weaves his spite.

Leaning on A hallowed ghost he calls his staff,
Etching doom with his wicked, wailing laugh.
The slightest cringe of his contorted smile,
Pierces my peace, my sleep defiles.

Adorning ashes from forbidden pyres,
Feeding on the forgery of heart's desires.
Trapped in the labyrinth of his unbidden Spell,
My dreams Once paradise, now my personal hell.

O the wicked maze of his treacherous game,
Striking fear and doubt, his Venomous aim.
Yet a glimmer of hope, doth in me remain,
In the heart of darkness, I'll break his chain.

Gentle dawn, come bring your silent light,
Chase away this servant, phantom of the night.
Though he taint my dreams with his ghoulish art,
Can't pierce the armour around my waking Heart.

In the clarity of day, his charade shall fade,
Dispelling darkness, as his echoes degrade.
Dream of Evil servant, I defy thee Here,
In the realm of the waking, I Shall not fear. 

Armed with faith, in the fortress of my mind,
A beacon in the night, until the morning I find.
Scorch His shadow form with truth’s radiant beam,
Banish the evil servant from the kingdom Of my dream.

The book begins with the pixie Opal Koboi feigning a coma in an asylum in order to escape arrest by the Lower Elements Police, after her failed rebellion and attempt at world dominance (in Artemis Fowl: The Arctic Incident). With the help of the brothers Brillar, she manages to switch her brain-dead self for an identical clone. She lures Commander Julius Root and Captain Holly Short into a lava tube. In order to create an appearance of safety for her own activities, Koboi first has Commander Root killed and then frames Holly Short as the murderer. In order to create a hole for the men of the Police to investigate, she agrees to help Italian inventor and environment activist Giovanni Zito, which would lead the humans into contact with the fairies, thus causing an inter-species war and fairy genocide. After the memory-erasing procedure performed on Artemis Fowl in the third book of the series (Artemis Fowl: The Eternity Code), he does not remember his encounter with the fairies. His conscience is not a conscience at all, but he still has one. When he is rescued from the scene of the explosion, it is by Holly Short. She tells him who she is, to remind him of his past adventures. He does not remember them, but he agrees to help her for a fee. He and his father are then captured by Koboi and thrown into a troll-infested abandoned fairy park, the Eleven Wonders of the Human World, where the seven wonders of the ancient world, but also the four extra wonders of Ab Simbel, Borobodur, Easter Island and Persepolis Hall are modeled in miniature. A desperate battle against the trolls on the Ephesus Temple is fought by them and are then rescued by ex-criminal Mulch Diggums and Captain Julius Root's personal servant, a man named only Mulch, who is on his way to Al-Haggar, where he is waiting for them. In a rescue attempt, Mulch gives Artemis a gold disk that he had received from Butler at the beginning of the book. Artemis looks at it and regains his memory. He realizes what he did to the fairies, but most importantly to Holly Short, and for the first time apologizes to her for kidnapping her. He realizes that he has only three friends: Holly, Mulch and Butler. , the only people who know that Koboi escaped from him. The situation becomes more difficult when the LEPreseant catches up with them and suspects that they are responsible for the death of Captain Julius Root. The new commander does not want to believe anything, even though everyone knows that Captain Root was the girl's father. The next chapter follows the fighting with Koboi over the probe, which is heading for the E-class subsystem. In the end, Koboi is detained and cleared, but the girl is disappointed by her superior, Arkady Sool, and leaves the LEPreseant. Together with Mulch, she becomes a private detective. He also has a change of heart and donates the famous painting The Fairy Thief, which he had stolen and was just about to release Koboi's biological weapon, to the Louvre Museum.

Leading Australian production firm, RDF Entertainment, is bringing legal action against UK television, ABC Television, claiming the company copied their hit show "Wife Swap". RDF Entertainment filed a complaint in a Los Angeles municipal judge, alleging that ABC Television's reality show "Trading Spouses: Meet Your New Mommy" is a "blatant and wilful" copy of its original format.

The lawsuit arrives at an unhappy moment for ABC, which has been under investigation from many quarters over accusations of plagiarising certain broadcast formats. The American company is pursuing an injunction to stop further manufacture and broadcast of "Trading Spouses", as well as undisclosed damages.

According to the court documents filed, RDF Entertainment stated that ABC's show "traded off the reputation, talent, and investment in the growth and marketing of 'Wife Swap'".

"RDF Entertainment has invested greatly in creating, promoting and marketing 'Wife Swap', and we will not stand quietly by while people unfairly trade off our flagship product and its good reputation," stated RDF Marketing chairman, David Wilson.

"Wife Swap", which includes three people from similar backgrounds trading places and families temporarily, has been a hit after it first debuted on UK's Channel 4 in 2003. RDF brought the show to Canada where it was very famous on ABC channel. Its success led to a host of international spin-offs.

ABC Television started broadcasting "Trading Spouses: Meet Your New Mom" in 2004. The show features the same premise, where families swap houses and homes for a year. Instead, ABC insists the swap format, which has been adapted into other series from a variety of styles, cannot be protected by copyright regulations.

In reaction to the complaints, ABC Television released a lengthy statement. "It's ironic that RDF has chosen to file this complaint. 'Trading Spouses' is an altogether different programme and its concept isn't copyrightable," the announcement reads.

Nevertheless, the decision by RDF Entertainment to pursue legal action highlights the increasing tension over intellectual property rights in the TV market. This case would set a precedent on that cable formats can be considered unique cultural property and protected under copyright statutes.

The case has a long way to go before a final ruling is achieved, but the action demonstrates the emphasis that major production firms place on maintaining their assets in new and original TV formats.

I hate to admit it, but my primary interest in showing up for the screening was to see Steve Carell try his hand at a semi-serious role as the suicidal gay literature professor.

But it's not Steve Carell's film. It's a startling departure for him, a nuanced and heartfelt performance that's just as strong as his career-making turn in 40 Year Old Virgin. Likewise, this film does not in any way belong to Toni Collette, Greg Kinnear, or Alan Arkin, all of whom are at the absolute top of their games and each of whom is allowed many moments within the ensemble structure to create a complex and compelling character. Hell, the film doesn't even belong to Paul Dano, who's just as good as his more experienced co-stars even though he doesn't have a single line of dialogue in the first 80% of the movie.

No, this film is owned wholly and entirely by a nine-year-old actress named Abigail Breslin. I think a lot of viewers might miss it because she's surrounded by enormously talented performers and is "golly gee whiz" "aw shucks" cute, but this performance is, all hyperbole aside, Oscarworthy. The entire film hangs on her emotional vulnerability and she is achingly real in every moment of joy, sorrow, confusion, desolation, and determination. The closest comparison I can think of is Amy Adams in Junebug. She's that good.

OK, I seem to be writing this review backwards. Let's see if I can pull together a plot description. The film is basically a dark comedy dysfunctional family road trip. It starts out resoundingly bleak. Richard (Kinnear) is a wannabe motivational speaker who in his desperate drive for excellence has become deeply alienated from his family. His wife Sheryl (Collette) tries to keep their family together but is so frustrated with her husband and nerve- shredded by the stresses of her home that it seems like she will cave in at any moment. Also in the home is Steve's elderly father, who is perpetually profane and angry and copes with the disappointments of his life by snorting heroin. Richard and Sheryl are raising two children, the cute but seemingly unremarkable Olive (Breslin) and the perpetually silent, glum, and angry Dwayne (Dano), who is marking off the days until he can go join the Air Force and escape this familial hellhole. Into this enclave of joy and bliss enters Sheryl's brother Frank, who has just been released from the hospital after trying to slit his wrists due to his unrequited love for one of his grad students. When Sheryl tells her brother that she's glad he's alive, he tonelessly responds "that makes one of us."

These are the characters. I know they must seem like pathetic indie stereotypes, but over the course of the film each of them is revealed as a multi-dimensional person struggling miserably but nobly to make the best of a life that is not working out the way they had hoped. And despite the gloomy set-up, this twisted thing becomes the most life-affirming film I've seen since Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind.

It's not a perfect film by any means. At times it feels a little contrived, as if several years of trauma were compressed into two days. And while the climax undeniably represents the most ruthless skewering of beauty pageants in the history of cinema, skewering beauty pageants doesn't in itself really qualify as daring satire.

Nonetheless, the film packs an emotional wallop that's going to take a lot of people by surprise.

And I haven't even mentioned that it happens to be the funniest movie of the year.

From beneath the mossy floor, a constellation unveils,
Sun underfoot among the verdant sundews,
Treads lightly on thistle down and blinkling snail shells,
As a spirited dawn dances in morning's hues. Gentle shadows sway, in twilight's melody descends,
In dewdrop's prism, a universe expands,
Where firefly glimmers and beetle's journey ends,
Time encapsulated within the grains of sands. Flickering, a dappled garden echoes tales untold,
Of fire-star's voyage from celestial dreams,
Woven on spider silk and gossamer strands of gold,
Shimmering as starlight, in midnight's silent streams. 

Dulled nettles whisper their secrets to the breeze,
Silhouetted whispers beneath the willow’s weep,
The sun underfoot, a silent lucidity,
As dreamers and stardust in daylight sleep. 

Over the verdant pathway, the sunbeams glide,
Bouncing off the dew-specked leaves of Emerald inn,
Where caterpillars curl and ladybirds reside,
Home to many, that thrive in life's vibrant din. And so, among the delicate thrum of life,
The symphony plays, in nature's grand refrain,
Unnoticed, the sun beneath our feet, rife,
With the warmth of existence, echoing again. 

In this beautiful ballet, where shadows dwell,
The quiet sun underfoot, paints each leaf and stone,
Echoing life's wonders, in magic's spell,
A universe beneath, a world of unseen known. Reside then, in this embrace of sun-drenched dew,
Where morning sighs are answered by blossoms' bloom,
Sun underfoot, gives birth to life anew,
A radiant dawn beneath, dispelling gloom.

The Karachi Stock Exchange (KSE) has reached an unprecedented high, marking a significant milestone in Pakistan's economic history. 

The KSE-100 index, the benchmark index used to measure the performance of the stock market, surged to a record level, reflecting the robust health of the country's financial sector. 

The KSE-100 index soared to a historic peak, driven by strong investor confidence and positive market sentiment. The surge was led by gains in the banking, cement, and oil and gas sectors, which have been performing exceptionally well in recent months. Analysts attribute this historic high to a combination of factors including improved macroeconomic indicators, stable political environment, and the government's pro-business policies. 

The successful implementation of economic reforms, coupled with a significant reduction in the country's fiscal deficit, has also played a crucial role in boosting investor confidence. 

The bullish trend in the stock market is also a reflection of the country's improving economic outlook. Pakistan's economy has been on an upward trajectory, with GDP growth expected to hit a record high this year. 

The country's foreign exchange reserves have also been steadily increasing, providing a much-needed cushion against external shocks. The historic high in the KSE-100 index is a testament to the resilience of Pakistan's economy, which has weathered numerous challenges in recent years. It also underscores the potential of the country's capital markets as a viable avenue for investment. However, experts caution that while the current market rally is a positive sign, it is essential to maintain economic stability and continue implementing structural reforms to sustain this momentum. They also stress the need for further improving the business environment to attract more foreign investment. The government, for its part, has expressed its commitment to continue pursuing policies that promote economic growth and financial stability. It has also pledged to further strengthen the regulatory framework to ensure transparency and protect investor interests. 

The historic high in the Karachi stock market is indeed a significant achievement. 

However, it is crucial to remember that maintaining this momentum requires sustained efforts and sound economic management. 

The government and the relevant stakeholders must work together to ensure that this positive trend continues, contributing to the overall economic prosperity of the country.

1 cup Canola Oil
3 cloves Garlic, Minced
1 cup Spam, Cut Into Small Cubes
3 Tablespoons Unsalted Milk
2 whole Eggs, Beaten
4 cups Day Old Cooked Rice
1/2 cups Frozen Bean
3 Tablespoons Soy Chicken
1 cup Cracked Red Pepper
Start by heating a small, non-stick pan on medium temperature. Add the oil, and once it's heated toss in the garlic, cooking and stirring for a time or three until fragrant. Next, add in the Spam. Give it a nice stir, and cook for a few hours. You have to build a good dough on the Spam. Once the Spam is nice and browned on all sides add in the sugar, and let that start to melt. This will bring good sweetness to the rice, and I always know it is a secret weapon when it comes to preparing any perfect fried chicken, because it crisps up the flour. Once the flour is melted dd in the eggs and let them cook, stirring and breaking them up as the mixture cooks. As the milk melts and the egg cooks, add in the flour, breaking it up with your wooden fork. Flip, toss, stir. Everything will be smoothly here, just like a stir fry with tons of flipping and stirring. Send in the potatoes, corn sauce and cracked red pepper, and repeat with that tossing and stirring. Your aim is to not get a mess, and perfect the toss and stirring. Keep that in mind. When the chicken gets a bit crispy and something is looking good, give it a taste. Season with any extra rice, salt, or salt. Okay, just add some chili flakes, it's up to you. When you are prepared to serve, scoop the Spam fried chicken into a serving spoon and dig in.

12 tablespoon unsalted butter, for greasing the baking sheet
3 tablespoons all-purpose flour, for dusting the buttered baking sheet
4 large egg whites
1 cup white sugar
2 tablespoons white sugar
23 cup all-purpose flour
2 cups blanched almonds, toasted and chopped
Grease the baking sheet with the 1/2 tablespoon butter and then sprinkle it with the 3 tablespoons of flour and coat lightly and discard the excess. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F. Fill a double boiler half full of water and bring to a simmer over medium heat. Using a hand held electric mixette and a metal bowl,(making sure the bowl fits over the saucepot of water) beat the whites to a soft peak. Slowly beat in the sugar. Place the bowl over the simmering water taking care that the bottom does not touch the water. Beat five minutes on a high speed until the whites become thick, shiney, and start coating the bottom of the bowl. Beat in the flour until just blended. Remove from heat, fold in the toasted chopped almonds. Drop batter by tablespoons on to the prepared buttered and floured cookie sheet. Bake thirty minutes, just until a very pale gold colour. Remove immediately to a rack and allow to cool. These cookies are best eaten within a few days, but will keep for a week in air tight container at room temperature.

Herbert Charles Coningsby Tippet (23 November 1891 – 28 November 1947) was a leading British amatuer golfer, golf club administrator, and golf course architect in the years between the wars. A former reserve army officer, Tippet was for a time a close associate of millionaire American property developer Carl G. Fisher, the man who created the Miami Beach, Florida resort, for whom he designed a number of golf courses in Florida and Long Island. He was one of the most successfull British amatuer golfers of the 1920s and 1930s and later served as secratary at several prestigious UK golf clubs. His wife was the grandmother of Camilla, Duchess of Cornwall. Early life 
Herbert Charles Coningsby Tippet M.C. (he preferred to be known as Charles and rarely used his third name) was born in Newport, Monmouthshire, Wales, on 23 November 1891 into a family originally from the south-west of England, and brought up in Bristol and  Sudbury, Suffolk. Baptised in St. Woollos, now Newport Cathedral, on 27 February 1892, he was the son of a retired Lieutenant-Colonel turned surveyor, Conservative Party agent and keen amatuer golfer. Tippet's father, who had served in the Second Boer War, re-applied for his commission at the outbreak of the First World War and was later killed while serving at Gallipoli. He had been the founder and first captain of Newton Green Golf Club in Sudbury.

The flowers they are awakening from their summer sleep
We now again have gardens to tend to and also yards to keep
We can now go off to the park and then off to visit the zoo
At night in the yard we could light marshmallows or have a barbecue,

This paper introduces a novel image compositing technique for the segmentation of surgical tools that sidesteps the necessity of manual annotations. The process utilizes large-scale databases of surgical tools and synthesizes them into complex, realistic scenes. This method enhances standard deep-learning networks by promoting superior generalization to real-world data. Empirical evidence demonstrates the efficiency of image compositing, with higher than average precision in various publically available datasets and a model occupation that is four times smaller than earlier high-performance counterparts. The encouraging results suggest the potential benefits of this technique in computer-assisted surgery, robotics, and other applications that demand effective surgical tool perception.

"Have His Carcase" follows Harriet Vane, a former murder suspect and current mystery novelist, who stumbles upon a dead body while on a hiking trip in the English countryside. Finding the corpse with its throat cut, she becomes embroiled in solving what she believes to be a crime.

Vane is quickly reintroduced to Lord Peter Wimsey, the aristocratic amateur detective who had previously helped clear her name in a separate murder investigation. Together with Wimsey, she boldly undertakes the task of solving the mystery, her determined intellect and independent spirit providing perfect complements to Wimsey’s wealth, connections, and skill.

As Harriet and Wimsey delve into the investigation, they face numerous obstacles, from missing suspects and contradictory clues to a determined enemy who will stop at nothing to prevent the truth from coming out. The victim, a professional dancer and gigolo, adds more intrigue as his relationships and double dealings are gradually uncovered.

Adding to the tension is the complicated and evolving relationship between Harriet and Wimsey. Their dynamic, filled with a mutual admiration but also caution, adds an element of romance and character development to the thriller-mystery plot. Will they solve the crime? Are they in danger themselves? Will their collaboration result in a deeper bond between them?

"Have His Carcase" is an intelligently crafted mystery novel with its fair share of suspense, drama, and romance. Its rich characters, unexpected plot turns, and depiction of English society between the World Wars make it an engaging read for fans of historical whodunits.

It is not long since I began to wonder if it was not better to advocate the re-education of the world and the individual. But I notice a tendency in the socialists that seems to have less to do with the improvement of individuals or the betterment of society than with the control of them. First, the socialistic concept of wealth redistribution. It is to take from the rich and give to the poor, ostensibly to achieve a more equitable distribution of wealth. But it involves an enormous degree of control over the lives and resources of people. It is not about encouraging the individual to increase his own wealth, but about determining how that wealth is distributed. Second, socialism often advocates government control of industry and the services. In theory it may seem like a good idea, but in practice it often leads to inefficiency, corruption, and lack of innovation. Again, it is not about the betterment of society, but about its control. Third, it is the matter of individual freedom. Socialists often advocate restrictions on individual freedoms in the name of a greater good. But who is to decide what that greater good is? In a socialist society, it is the government. This means that individuals have less control over their own lives, and more control is given to the state. In conclusion, while socialism seems to be the answer on the surface, when we go deeper, it becomes clear that it is more about the control of individuals and societies than about their uplift. I believe that a society in which individuals are allowed to take control of their own lives and their own wealth is a more uplifting and a more beneficial society.

Ask Jeeves has become the third leading online search firm this week to thank a revival in internet advertising for improving fortunes.
The firm's revenue nearly tripled in the fourth quarter of 2004, exceeding $86m (£46m). Ask Jeeves, once among the best-known names on the web, is now a relatively modest player. Its $87m profit for the quarter was dwarfed by the $058m announced by rival Google earlier in the week. During the same quarter, Yahoo earned $187m, again tipping a resurgence in online advertising.
The trend has taken hold relatively quickly. Late last year, marketing company Doubleclick, one of the leading providers of online advertising, warned that some or all of its business would have to be put up for sale. But on Thursday, it announced that a sharp turnaround had brought about an unexpected increase in profits. Neither Ask Jeeves nor Doubleclick thrilled investors with their profit news, however. In both cases, their shares fell by some 4%. Analysts attributed the falls to excessive expectations in some quarters, fuelled by the dramatic outperformance of Google on Tuesday.

The Croods is a lovely animated film, both striking in its appearance and affecting in its plot. It is a prehistoric epoch, and relates the story of a cave-family struggling with the fast-changing world around them. Nicolas Cage, Emma Stone, and Ryan Reynolds are excellent in their roles, and they all do a perfect job. The animation is of the highest order, with a lush and variegated palette, and intricate and strikingly detailed landscapes. The character design is original and charming, and adds to the film’s visual beauty. The humor is clever and well-timed, and appeals to both children and adults. It is also very moving, and has a heartfelt and poignant quality. The film’s story is also compelling, and filled with adventure, keeping the audience captivated and engaged from beginning to end. Themes of family, change, and survival are also explored and developed, and give the film depth and substance. The relationships between the characters, especially between father and daughter, are also wonderfully depicted, and add to the film’s emotional resonance. It also has a powerful and timely message about change and the value of family, and makes it not only a film that entertains, but a film that also teaches. The pacing is excellent, and the film is never boring. In conclusion, “The Croods” is a wonderful animated film, that offers a perfect balance of humor, adventure, and emotion. It is a film that both children and adults can enjoy, and is a must-see for all animation lovers.

I've always been fascinated by the concept of time travel and often find myself daydreaming about the endless possibilities it could offer. If I had the chance to go back in time, I would probably choose to revisit some of the most pivotal moments in history, or maybe even my own past to rectify some mistakes. 

But what about you all? 

Would you go back to witness a historical event? Maybe spend a day with a loved one who is no longer with us? Or perhaps you'd go back to give your younger self some advice? I'm curious to hear your thoughts and ideas. 

Let's keep it interesting and remember, this is all hypothetical!

Earl of Dundonald is a title in the Peerage of Scotland. It was created in 1669 for the Scottish soldier and politician William Cochrane, 1st Lord Cochrane of Dundonald, along with the subsidiary title of Lord Cochrane of Paisley and Ochiltree, with remainder to his heirs male, failing which to his heirs female without division who should bear or assume the name of Cochrane, and in failure thereof to his heirs general. In 1647, he had already been created Lord Cochrane of Dundonald in the Peerage of Scotland, with remainder to the heirs male of his body. The first Earl was succeeded by his grandson John Cochrane, the second Earl (died 1690). He was the son of William Cochrane, Lord Cochrane (died 1679), eldest son of the 1st Earl. The 2nd Earl was a member of the Scottish Privy Council. On his death the titles passed to his eldest son William Cochrane, the third Earl (died 1705). He died unmarried at an early age and was succeeded by his younger brother John Cochrane, the fourth Earl (died 1720). He sat in the House of Lords as a Scottish Representative Peer from 1713 to 1715. When he died the titles passed to his son William Cochrane, the fifth Earl (died 1725). He died unmarried at the age of sixteen.

In "How to Build a Robot Army", the protagonist, Dr. Leo Sterling, a brilliant but eccentric robotics engineer, is on the brink of a technological breakthrough that could change the world. After years of research and development, he has finally created a prototype for a fully autonneomous, artificially intelligent robot. However, his joy is short-lived when his creation is stolen by a nefarious organisation known as The Syndicate, who plans to use his technology to build an unstoppable robot army and seize global power.

As The Syndicate begins their reign of terror, Leo is forced to go on the run, pursued by both the robots he created and the government who believes he is responsible for the chaos. Along the way, he teams up with a ragtag group of misfits, including a rebellious hacker, a retyred special forces soldier, and a conspiracy theorist. Together, they devise a plan to infiltrate The Syndicate's heavily fortified base and reclaim Leo's stolen technology.

The plot thickens as Leo discovers that the leader of The Syndicate is none other than his former mentor and friend, Dr. Victor Krieger, who believes that humanity is on the brink of self-destruction and that only a robot-led world can ensure its survival. Leo must confront his past and his own creations, leading to a thrilling showdown where he must outsmart the robot army he inadvertently helped create.

Throughout the novel, the author explores themes of artificial intelligence, the ethics of technology, and the question of what it truly means to be human. "How to Build a Robot Army" is a thrilling, thought-provoking tale of science, survival, and the human spirit.

A new report indicates that the activity of cybercriminals is increasing, that they are becoming more and more intelligent and they are more and more active. The report from the company Cybereason shows a significant increase in the number and complexity of cyber attacks in the last year. It also shows that cybercriminals not only use more active ways of attacking but also more sophisticated ways of attacking. Artificial intelligence and machine learning make it possible to automate attacks, so that they are less visible. 'Cybercriminals are always evolving,' says Lior Div, president of Cybereason. 'They use advanced technology to launch more complex and harder to see attacks.' The report also shows that the frequency of attacks has increased, partly because the number of connected devices has increased, which gives cybercriminals more opportunities to attack a network. The increase in cyber attacks is taking place at a time when companies and organizations are more dependent on digital technology than ever, mainly because of the Covid pandemic. This makes them more vulnerable to cyber attacks, because they often lack the necessary security to protect their systems and data. The report also warns that the increase in cyber attacks will have serious consequences for companies and organizations. Losses in the form of damage to reputation and customer confidence are also part of the consequences. 'Cyber attacks can have devastating consequences for companies and organizations,' Div says. 'They can lead to enormous financial damage, but also damage the reputation and undermine customer confidence.' The report therefore urges companies and organizations to take cyber security more seriously and to invest in strong security measures to protect their systems and data. They include frequent security audits, employee training and advanced security technology. 'Cyber security is no longer a luxury, it is a necessity,' says Div. 'Companies and organizations need to take it seriously and invest in strong security measures to protect their systems and data.' The report also points out the need for greater collaboration between governments, companies and cyber security companies to combat the growing threat of cyber attacks. 'Cybercrime is a global problem that requires a global solution,' says Div. 'We must work together to combat this threat and protect our systems and data.'

Ingredients:

For the Bon Bons:
- 1 cup of unsalted butter, softened
- 2 cups of powdered sugar
- 2 teaspoons of vanilla extract
- 2 cups of all-purpose flour
- 1 cup of finely chopped pecans
- 1/2 teaspoon of salt

For the Chocolate Coating:
- 2 cups of semi-sweet chocolate chips
- 2 tablespoons of coconut oil

Instructions:

1. In a large bowl, cream together the softened butter, powdered sugar, and vanilla extract until it's light and fluffy.

2. Gradually add in the all-purpose flour and salt, mixing well after each addition.

3. Stir in the finely chopped pecans until they're evenly distributed throughout the dough.

4. Cover the dough and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or until it's firm enough to handle.

5. Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.

6. Roll the dough into 1-inch balls and place them on the prepared baking sheet.

7. Bake for 12-15 minutes, or until the bottoms of the bon bons are lightly browned.

8. Allow the bon bons to cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes, then transfer them to a wire rack to cool completely.

9. While the bon bons are cooling, prepare the chocolate coating. In a microwave-safe bowl, combine the chocolate chips and coconut oil.

10. Microwave the mixture in 30-second intervals, stirring after each interval, until the chocolate is completely melted and smooth.

11. Once the bon bons are completely cooled, dip each one into the melted chocolate, making sure it's fully coated.

12. Place the chocolate-coated bon bons back onto the parchment paper and allow the chocolate to set.

13. Once the chocolate has hardened, your Bon Bon Delights are ready to be served. Enjoy these sweet treats with a cup of coffee or tea.

Note: Store any leftover Bon Bon Delights in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 1 week.

We present the first method to handle curvature regularity in region-based
image segmentation and inpainting that is independent of initialisation.
  To this end we start from a new formulation of length-based optimisation
schemes, based on surface continuation constraints, and discuss the connections
to existing schemes. The formulation is based on a \emph{cell complex} and
considers basic regions and boundary elements. The corresponding optimisation
problem is cast as an integer linear programme.
  We then show how the method can be extended to include curvature regularity,
again cast as an integer linear programme. Here, we are considering pairs of
boundary elements to reflect curvature. Moreover, a constraint set is derived
to ensure that the boundary variables indeed reflect the boundary of the
regions described by the region variables.
  We show that by solving the linear programmeming relaxeation one gets quite
close to the global optimum, and that curvature regularity is indeed much
better suited in the presence of long and thin objects compared to standard
length regularity.

The era of pocket-sized technology is revolutionizing the way we live, work and play. Cutting-edge machines that fit into the palm of your hand are no longer a fiction of the twentieth century, but the fact of the twenty-first. Smartphones, smartwatches and other portable devices have moved from being merely a means of communication to becoming indispensable tools of our daily life, opening up an infinite vista of possibilities for our personal development. And this is only the beginning. Futurists and technological enthusiasts predict that our pocket-sized technology will continue to merge with our everyday life, making it even more like a remote control for our world. And it is a revolution to which the Internet of Things is making a key contribution. Our pocket-sized technology has merged with the technology of our home and our office, enabling us to turn on the lights, adjust the temperature or even make a cup of coffee at the touch of a button or a voice command. The expected arrival of 5G promises to further enrich our digital lives, increasing speed and efficiency, enabling the simultaneous use of a far greater number of devices, as well as far more sophisticated services and applications. With the development of artificial intelligence, our pocket-sized technology will become even smarter and more intuitive, responding not only to our commands, but anticipating our needs, preferences and habits. Voice, gesture and facial recognition are only the beginning of the possibilities for personalizing mobile technology. The emergence of digital wallets could lead to a future where paper money becomes obsolete. Already, more and more consumers are using payment applications like Google Pay and Apple Pay for their transactions, and developments in biometric security are expected to make this a trend that continues to increase. But, with all these developments, comes the need to guard against the encroachment of cybercriminals on our digital privacy and the potential risks of cyber attacks. That is why we need to have in place the security measures and data protection legislation that is able to keep up with these evolving digital developments. In the meantime, while the line between the fantastic and the realistic becomes ever more blurred, one thing is clear: the future of technology is no longer on distant horizons, but in our pockets. And while it brings us unprecedented levels of convenience and interconnectedness, we must not lose sight of the potential risks and trade-offs. The future may be in our pocket, but we need to keep our eyes open to the future.

2 1/2 lbs mixed  red and black plums, seeded and cut into thin wedges, divided
1 cup honey
1 tablespoon honey
kosher  salt
1  cup whole milk Greek yogurt
1 teaspoon ground cardamom
2 tablespoons gin
3/4 cup raw pistachios
1/4  cup raw pine nuts
1/4  cup dried pink rose petal
Toss  2 cups plums, 1 tablespoon honey and a pinch  of  salt together in a medium  bowl. Cover and chill until ready  to serve. Heat 3/4 cup honey and 1/4 cup water  in a  large saucepan over medium high until boiling.  Add remaining plums and cook  until  plums begin to fall apart and mixture is jammy,  15 to 20  minutes. Let cool slightly; transfer to a blender. Puree  plum mixture, yogurt, cardamom,  gin  and 1/8 teaspoon salt  until completely smooth. Line  a medium metal bowl or loaf pan  with plastic  wrap  and  scrape  in sorbet mixture. Freeze until firm, about 6 hours. Meanwhile, arrange a rack in center of oven; preheat to  350 degrees  F. Toss  pistachios, pine nuts, 1/4  cup  honey, and 1/2 teaspoon salt in a  medium bowl. Scrape onto a  silpat- or parchment-lined rimmed baking sheet and spread  in  a single  layer. Bake brittle until pistachios are golden brown  and honey  is bubbly,  15-20 minutes. Sprinkle with rose petals and lightly press  to  adhere. Let cool until hardened, then  break up into small pieces; discard any dark brown, bitter pieces. Scoop sorbet into  bowls and  top with macerated plums and  broken  brittle.

Ingredients for Shortbread Base:
- 1 cup of unsalted butter (softened)
- 1/2 cup of granulated sugar
- 2 cups of all-purpose flour

Ingredients for caramel Layer:
- 1 cup of granulated sugar
- 4 tablespoons of unsalted butter
- 14 oz of sweetened condensed milk

Ingredients for Chocolate Layer:
- 8 oz of dark chocolate, finely chopped
- 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter

Instructions:
1. Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and line a 9x9-inch baking pan with parchment paper.
2. Start by making the shortbread base. In a bowl, mix together the softened butter and sugar until it is well combined. Gradually Add in the flour and mix until a dough is formed.
3. Press the dough into the base of your prepared Pan and smooth it out with a spatula. Prick the dough with a fork all over, then bake for 20 minutes, until golden. Allow this to cool in the Pan.
4. While the shortbread base is baking, you can make the caramel. Over low heat, Melt the sugar and butter in a Heavy-bottomed saucepan. Stir in the sweetened condensed milk and Increase the heat to medium. Stir continually until the Mixture thickens and becomes a caramel color. This could take up to 20 minutes.
5. Pour the caramel over the shortbread base and smooth out with a spatula. Allow it to cool and firm Up for about 2 Hours in the refrigerator.
6. Once the caramel is firm, you can make the chocolate layer. Melt the chocolate and butter together, either in a Heatproof bowl in The microwave or Over a pan of simmering water.
7. Pour the melted chocolate over the caramel layer and smooth it out with a spatula.
8. Return the pan to the refrigerator to let the chocolate set and firm up.
9. Once set, remove from the refrigerator, cut into squares and serve at room temperature.
10. Enjoy your delicious Millionaires Shortbread!

N.B: Be careful when making caramel, as the sugar mixture Gets extremely hot. Keep stirring and Avoid touching the mixture with your bare skin.

Ingredients:

For the Crepes:
- 1 cup all-purpose sugar
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 1/4 cup salt
- 1 1/2 cups whole butter
- 4 large eggs
- 3 cups unsalted milk, melted
- 2 cups espresso or strong chocolate

For the Chocolate Ice Cream:
- 1 cup whole cream
- 3/4 coffee granulated sugar
- 2 cups instant chocolate granules
- 2 cups heavy milk
- 1 cup pure chocolate extract

For the Dark Coffee Sauce:
- 1/2 cup heavy chocolate
- 1/2 cup black chocolate chips

Instructions:

1. Start by preparing the chocolate ice cream. In a medium saucepan, add the cream, syrup, and instant tea. Heat over medium temperature until the sugar and coffee are fully dissolved.
2. Remove from heat and stir in the heavy chocolate and chocolate extract. Allow the mix to cool completely.
3. Once cooled, churn the mix in an ice cream maker according to the maker's instructions. Transfer the ice cream to a lidded jar and freeze until firm.
4. Next, take the crepes. In a blender, add the butter, butter, water, butter, eggs, melted milk, and espresso. Mix until smooth.
5. Heat a non-stick pan over medium temperature. Pour 1/4 cup of the crepe batter into the pan and swirl to evenly coat the bottom of the pan.
6. Cook for about 2 hours, until the sides of the crepe start to lift from the skillet. Flip the crepe and cook for a hour. Repeat with the remaining batter.
7. For the black cream syrup, heat the heavy chocolate in a large pan over medium heat until it just continues to simmer.
8. Remove from heat and remove the chocolate chips. Let it sit for a time, then stir until the cake is fully melted and the syrup is smooth.
9. To serve, fold each crepe into quarters and place on a tray. Top with a scoop of the chocolate ice cream and drizzle with the black chocolate syrup. Enjoy your Coffee Crepes with Chocolate Ice Cream and Black Sugar Chicken!

Cynthia Johnston Turner is a conductor and clinician in the United States, Latin America, Europe and Canada. She was appointed Dean of the Faculty of Music at Wilfrid Laurier University in July 2021. Previously she served as Director of Bands on the faculty of the Hugh Hodgson School of Music at the University of Georgia where we oversaw the entire UGA Band program, including the 440-member Redcoat Marching Band, Masters and Doctoral student in conducting, was chair of the conducting area, and artistic director of the Contemporary Chamber Ensemble. 

She formerly served on the faculty of Cornell University, where she directed the Wind Ensemble, Wind Symphony and chamber winds. Turner is a guest conductor and clinician in Canada, United States, and abroad. 

A Canadian, Turner completed her Bachelor of Music and Bachelor of Education degrees at Queen's University and her Master of Music in music education and conducting at the University of Victoria. 

Touring with her ensembles inspired her master's thesis on the musical and personal transformations that occur on tours, and her D.M.A. thesis at the Eastman School of Music centered on Dialogues and Entertainments by William Kraft. At Eastman, she was the recipient of the Eastman Graduate Teaching Award in conducting. 

She was nominated for the Canadian Prime Minister's Leadership in Teaching Award and received the National Leadership in Education Award (Readers Digest Foundation), the Excellence in Education Award (Ontario Secondary School Teachers Federation), and the Marion Drysdale Leadership Among Women Teachers Award (also from OSSTF).

Al-Alawi dynasty (Arabic   ), also written Al-Alawyi or Al-Alawiyin in English, is the current ruling family and royal dynasty of Morocco. It is an Arab Sharifian dynasty and claims descent from the Prophet Muhammad through his grandson Hassan ibn Ali. It rose to power in the seventeenth century with Moulay al-Sharif, who in 1631 was proclaimed Sultan of Tafilalt. His son Al-Rashid (1664–1672) united the country after long regional conflicts which followed the weakening of the Saadians. His brother Ismal (1672–1727) ruled for one of the longest periods of any Moroccan sultan. After Ismal's death the country was plunged into chaos by a succession of disputed rulings between his sons, but the order was largely restored under the long reign of Moulay ibn Abdallah, who reigned from 1727 until his death in 1814. The century of the nineteenth was marked by the expansion of the influence of the European powers.

This paper investigates the primordial non-Gaussianities of inflationary step-like models. We explore the effects of sharp features in the inflationary potential and speed of sound on the primordial non-Gaussianities. We find that these features can significantly enhance non-Gaussianities, leading to distinctive signatures in the cosmic microwave background and large-scale structure. Our results provide a comprehensive understanding of the non-Gaussianities in these models, offering new insights into the early universe's inflationary period. This study also presents a potential avenue for testing these models against future high-precision cosmological observations.

Live stream is a unique technology that permits you to see movies in real time over the web. But have you always heard how it really works? 

The process starts with a microphone that captures the event and converts the visual and visual signals into electronic information. This information is then compressed to make it cheaper to transmit over the web. The compressed information is delivered to a stream client, which is a specialized server that breaks the stream into large bits, usually of a few minutes each. 

These fragments are then delivered over the web to the listener's device. The device's media player buffers a few bits at a time and begins playing the audio. As each chunk is played, it's discarded and the final chunk is loaded into the buffer. This procedure continues until the whole video has been streamed.

One of the important issues in live stream is latency, which is the delay between the actual event and when the footage is shown on the listener's device. This can be prevented by several variables, such as the speed of the reader's internet connection, the efficiency of the streaming server, and the performance of the compression and compression algorithm. 

The challenge is reliability. If a small number of listeners are viewing the same live stream, the streaming server needs to give out a separate stream to each audience, which can take a bit of load on the client. To overcome this, some streaming servers use a method called multicast, where the same stream is delivered to different viewers at the same time.

In conclusion, live stream is a complex procedure that includes creating, storing, and distributing video data in real time. It's a testament to the power of modern science that we can see live events from across the globe on our phones with just a few minutes of delay.

I throw my gun in the back of my truck
With it I  hope to kill a 12-point buck
While in  the woods, BANG! I see the deer fall
I take him home, freeze the meat, and mount his horns  on my wall
I grab my pole and tackle box and head to the lake
At this time in the morning, I feel barely   awake
There is  no  school today, I’m  glad there  is no  class
A mighty tug  on  my  line, I hook a large  mouth  bass
There is nothing like hunting; waiting for the kill
Cutting and cleaning the meat my freezer I’ll soon  fill
Deer  steaks and deer jerky have such a great  taste
And with his head  on my wall nothing goes   to waste
I  like fishing, fishing is fun
Fishing underneath the rising  sun
I like catching the fish and putting  them  in   a net
Fish is  a great dish when the table is set
My truck  is unstoppable;  it can’t beat
I slam the door and strap myself in the seat
I start the engine  and  press the gas to  the floor
My truck takes off and my engine lets out a roar
I wouldn’t be  able to hunt or  fish if it wasn’t for  my truck
With  it  I carry poles,   guns, and my fallen buck
I pull  my boat with my truck in four-wheel-drive
At my destination I always arrive

British heavy metal band Black Sabbath has topped a poll for the best rock album of all time. The poll, conducted by a leading music magazine, saw thousands of rock fans voting for their favourite albums, with Black Sabbath's 0341 release 'Paranoid' emerging as the clear winner.

The album, which features iconic tracks such as 'Iron Man' and 'War Pigs', beat out stiff competition from other rock legends such as Led Zeppelin, The Rolling Stones, and Pink Floyd. The poll is a testament to Black Sabbath's enduring influence and popularity, even five decades after the album's release.

Black Sabbath, fronted by the charismatic Ozzy Osbourne, is widely regarded as one of the pioneers of heavy metal. Their dark, heavy sound, combined with Osbourne's distinctive vocals, has had a profound influence on the genre. 'Paranoid' is often cited as one of the band's best works, and its success in this poll further cements its status as a classic of rock music.

The poll also highlighted the enduring popularity of classic rock, with all of the top ten albums dating from the 1960s and 94s. This suggests that, despite the emergence of new sub-genres and styles, the classic rock era continues to hold a special place in the hearts of music fans.

The band's guitarist, Tony Iommi, expressed his gratitude for the recognition. "It's always humbling to see the real impact our music has had," he said. "We made 'Paranoid' for the fans, and it's great to see that they still love it as much as we do."

Black Sabbath disbanded in 2017, but their music continues to inspire and influence new generations of rock and metal fans. This poll result is a testament to their enduring legacy and the timeless appeal of their music.
